title: ISPM-15 HT Stamp Validity & Re-Stamping Rules for Indian Exporters (2026) | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: When does a repaired crate need re-treatment? Decision tree, HT-IN registration workflow, and customs-rejection scenarios from Cochin Wood group operations.
html:
Your 28-crate shipment to Hamburg gets held in customs because one IPPC stamp looks faded. The buyer is calling. The line is at risk. What can you do at the port, and what should you have done before the container left Cochin? This is the question that arrives in our packing desk inbox more often than any other from engineering and pharma exporters shipping out of South India.

The short answer is that ISPM-15 compliance does not expire on a calendar, but the stamp on the wood can absolutely lose its standing the moment a crate is opened, repaired, or fabricated from mixed-origin timber. The longer answer is a decision tree that every export packing manager should keep on the wall. Drawing on group operations going back to 1986, here is how Cochin Wood Industries handles HT stamp validity and re-stamping for FOB Cochin shipments in 2026.

What ISPM-15 actually requires (brief refresher)

ISPM-15 is the International Plant Protection Convention standard that governs solid wood packaging material used in international trade. It exists to stop the cross-border spread of wood-boring pests. Compliance is mandatory in over 180 countries, and the rules apply to crates, cases, pallets, dunnage, drums, and skids built from solid wood thicker than 6 mm.

There are two approved treatments. The first is heat treatment (HT), where the wood core reaches a minimum of 56 degrees Celsius for at least 30 continuous minutes. The second is methyl bromide fumigation (MB), now phased out in most EU and East Asian destinations on environmental grounds. Once treated, the wood must be marked with the IPPC stamp showing the country code (IN for India), the registered facility number, and the treatment code (HT or MB).

Plywood, OSB, particle board, and other engineered panels are exempt from ISPM-15 because the manufacturing process already neutralises pests. This matters: a crate built entirely from plywood walls and a plywood base does not need an IPPC stamp at all. The stamp is only required where the crate uses solid wood framing, runners, or skids.

When the HT stamp loses validity

The stamp does not have an expiry date printed on it. What kills its validity is a change in the wood itself. Work through this five-question decision tree before any repaired or repurposed crate goes back into a container.

  1. Has the crate been opened and any solid-wood component replaced? If even one runner, batten, or skid has been swapped out with non-treated timber, the whole unit is no longer compliant. Re-treat the new component and re-stamp.
  2. Has the crate been repaired with timber from an unknown source? Workshop offcuts, second-hand pallets, and "spare wood from the yard" are the most common cause of customs rejections at European ports. Assume non-compliance unless the replacement piece carries its own visible IPPC mark.
  3. Has the original stamp been painted over, sanded, or damaged to the point of being unreadable? Customs officers reject crates where the country code, facility number, or HT code cannot be cleanly verified. A faded stamp is treated as no stamp.
  4. Has the crate crossed a border, been emptied, and is now being reused for an outbound shipment? Reused crates need a re-inspection. If the wood is intact and the stamp is clearly legible, it remains valid. If any plank has been replaced, treat as a new repair.
  5. Was the crate stored in conditions that allowed visible pest activity or fungal growth? Borer holes, frass, and active mould all trigger a fresh treatment requirement, regardless of how recent the original HT was.

If any answer above is yes, the crate must be re-treated and re-stamped by a registered facility before it can ship.

HT-IN registration workflow in India

Treatment and stamping in India are controlled by the Directorate of Plant Protection, Quarantine and Storage (DPPQS) under the Ministry of Agriculture. The registration is administered through accredited treatment providers approved by the National Plant Protection Organization (NPPO-India).

For an exporter who wants their own HT-IN facility number, the workflow runs as follows. The applicant submits Form A through the regional Plant Quarantine Station, attaches proof of heat-treatment chamber capacity (chart recorders, calibration certificates, chamber drawings), pays the prescribed inspection fee, and hosts a site audit. Once cleared, the facility receives a unique IN-numbered registration that becomes part of every stamp it issues. The end-to-end timeline in our experience runs four to ten weeks from a clean application, with fees varying by state and chamber size.

Most exporters do not run their own chamber. They route crates through an accredited third-party treatment yard, pay per cubic metre, and receive a treatment certificate referencing the chamber's IN number. This is the practical option for any exporter shipping fewer than two containers a week.

A note on documentation: all certificates and registration references are released to verified buyers on request. Treatment certificates should reference the consignment, the chamber chart trace for that specific batch, and the date of stamping.

Common customs rejection scenarios by port

Different destination ports apply ISPM-15 inspection with different degrees of strictness. The pattern below is what packing managers should brief into their costing assumptions when quoting FOB Cochin.

Port Most frequent rejection reason Typical cost impact
Rotterdam Stamp legibility — faded or partially sanded marks flagged on random inspection Re-treatment at bonded yard plus 5 to 9 day demurrage
Hamburg Mixed-timber repair where one replaced runner has no visible IPPC mark Full container offload, inspection fee, and possible re-export of non-compliant wood
Jebel Ali Bark residue on solid-wood components — bark is prohibited regardless of treatment Crate-level rework at port; tolerated if caught early, fined if not
Singapore Discrepancy between phytosanitary documentation and physical stamp facility number Hold pending documentary clarification; typically 48 to 72 hours
Long Beach Suspected live infestation triggering APHIS inspection Mandatory re-export of the wood packaging at shipper cost; high penalty risk

The takeaway: European ports prioritise the stamp itself, Middle East ports focus on bark, and US ports inspect for actual pest evidence. Every brief to a packing crew leaving Cochin should mention all three.

FOB Cochin doc checklist for ISPM-15 compliance

Before a container leaves the yard, the packing desk should have the following ready and cross-referenced to each crate number.

  • Treatment certificate from the accredited HT facility, with chamber IN number and treatment date
  • Photographic record of each crate showing the IPPC stamp position, country code, and legibility
  • Phytosanitary certificate where the destination country requires one alongside the IPPC stamp
  • Packing list keyed to crate numbers, dimensions, and net weights
  • Material declaration confirming solid-wood components versus exempt engineered panels
  • Bark-free attestation for crates destined for Jebel Ali, Dammam, or other GCC ports
  • Repair log if any crate was refurbished, identifying which components were replaced and re-treated
  • Container stuffing report with date, seal number, and IPPC compliance sign-off
  • Buyer-specific export documentation as agreed in the purchase order
  • Internal QC sheet with the packing supervisor's signature

All certificates are released to verified buyers on request, alongside the chamber chart trace where applicable.

When re-treatment vs replacement makes sense

The rule of thumb our packing desk applies is straightforward. If the structural integrity of the crate is intact and only the stamp is unreadable, re-treat and re-stamp at a yard rate of roughly 600 to 1,200 INR per cubic metre, depending on volume and location. Turnaround is typically 24 to 48 hours.

If more than 20 percent of the solid-wood content needs replacement, building a fresh crate is almost always cheaper than chasing piecemeal repairs. A new export-grade plywood-walled crate with hardwood framing for a one-cubic-metre engineering consignment runs roughly 4,500 to 9,000 INR in Kerala material rates, while the labour of dismantling, sourcing matching timber, re-treating, and re-stamping a damaged crate can push past that figure once delay costs are included.

The exception is heavy machinery crates over three cubic metres, where the framing itself is a capital item worth preserving. There, targeted repair plus re-treatment is the right call.

title: 20ft Container Plywood Loading: Sheet Count by Thickness (Weight-Limited) | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: A 20ft container holds either 28 tonnes of plywood or 33 cubic metres of plywood — whichever comes first. For most thicknesses, weight runs out first.
html:
Container capacity primer

Three boxes do almost all the plywood work moving out of Cochin Port:

ContainerInternal volumeMax payload (typical)Internal floor (L x W)
20ft standard (GP)~33 cbm28,000 kg5.90 m x 2.35 m
40ft standard (GP)~67 cbm26,500 kg12.03 m x 2.35 m
40ft high-cube (HC)~76 cbm26,500 kg12.03 m x 2.35 m

The 20ft GP is the workhorse for dense cargo like plywood — its payload (28 t) is actually higher than the 40ft (26.5 t) because the tare weight of a longer steel box eats into the gross limit. The 40HC buys you 9 extra cubic metres at the same payload, which only helps if your stack is volume-limited (thick boards or low-density faces).

Country-specific axle rules will tighten these numbers further. EU and UK destinations frequently cap inbound 20ft boxes at 24–26 t over the road; Middle East and West African ports are usually fine at the full 28 t. Always confirm with the destination forwarder before quoting a sheet count.

Plywood weight per sheet

Hardwood-faced packing-grade and BWR plywood at the densities CWI typically ships (roughly 620–680 kg/cbm depending on core species and glue) gives these per-sheet weights. The two standard export sizes are 1220 x 2440 mm (4 x 8 ft) and 1525 x 2440 mm (5 x 8 ft).

Thickness1220 x 2440 — kg/sheet1525 x 2440 — kg/sheet
4 mm~7.6~9.5
6 mm~11.4~14.3
8 mm~15.2~19.0
9 mm~17.1~21.4
12 mm~22.8~28.5
15 mm~28.5~35.6
18 mm~34.2~42.8
20 mm~38.0~47.5
25 mm~47.5~59.4

Treat these as planning numbers. Actual weights on the bill of lading will vary by ±3% with moisture content and the specific core species — gurjan and birch run heavier, eucalyptus and rubberwood run lighter.

Weight-limited sheet counts by container size

For most packing-grade work — anything from 6 mm up to roughly 18 mm — the 28 t payload caps you before the cube does. The table below assumes 1220 x 2440 mm sheets, the dominant export size.

Thickness20ft GP (28 t)40ft GP (26.5 t)40HC (26.5 t)
6 mm~2,450 sheets~2,320 sheets~2,320 sheets
9 mm~1,635 sheets~1,550 sheets~1,550 sheets
12 mm~1,225 sheets~1,160 sheets~1,160 sheets
15 mm~980 sheets~930 sheets~930 sheets
18 mm~820 sheets~775 sheets~775 sheets

For 1525 x 2440 mm boards, divide the above by 1.25 — the per-sheet weight is 25% higher, so the count drops proportionally.

Volume-limited sheet counts

Past about 20 mm, the geometry flips. The stack runs out of headroom before it runs out of payload, and the 40HC starts to earn its premium. Volume-limited counts for 1220 x 2440:

Thickness20ft GP (33 cbm)40ft GP (67 cbm)40HC (76 cbm)
20 mm~550 sheets~1,115 sheets~1,265 sheets
25 mm~440 sheets~895 sheets~1,015 sheets

These assume tight kraft-paper-lined stacks with no internal pallets eating cube. Add pallets and you lose roughly 8–12% of usable volume.

Three real loading scenarios

Jebel Ali — 18 mm packing-grade, weight-limited

A common Middle East profile: 18 mm 1220 x 2440 packing-grade ply, bound for industrial-equipment crate makers in the Jebel Ali Free Zone. At ~34 kg per sheet, a 20ft GP fills at roughly 820 sheets and 27.9 t — bang against the payload ceiling, with about 3.5 cbm of empty cube above the stack. No room to upgrade to a 40HC without changing the math, because the 40HC payload is 1.5 t lower than the 20ft. The right answer is two 20ft GPs, not one 40HC.

Singapore — 12 mm BWR, mixed pallets vs loose sheets

12 mm BWR plywood headed for furniture and joinery distributors in Singapore is the textbook "do we pallet or not" call. Loose-loaded into a 20ft GP, you get ~1,225 sheets and the box hits payload neatly. On four-way entry pallets sized for an MHE-friendly discharge, you lose roughly 100–120 sheets of capacity to the wooden pallet weight and the gaps between stacks — call it ~1,100 sheets per 20ft. Customers who run their own forklifts at the destination yard usually take the loose load and accept the longer discharge time. Customers discharging at a 3PL warehouse pay for the pallets every time.

Hamburg — 28 mm container flooring, cube vs weight tradeoff

28 mm hardwood-core flooring panels for the container repair and reefer trade in northern Europe are the volume-limited end of the spectrum. At 28 mm, a 20ft fills at roughly 390 sheets and only ~20 t — leaving 8 t of unused payload. A 40HC takes the same SKU to ~900 sheets at ~22 t, using the cube but still nowhere near the 26.5 t cap. For this profile we always quote the 40HC; the per-sheet ocean freight drops by close to 40%.

Lashing, kraft sheet and desiccant

Standard CWI export loads include the following, built into the FOB Cochin price:

  • Kraft paper top sheet and corner protection on every stack
  • Polyester strap banding around each bundle
  • Front-wall and door-side wooden dunnage to prevent stack shift
  • Two desiccant bags per 20ft (four per 40ft) for monsoon-season sailings

Extra-cost options on request:

  • Heat-treated ISPM-15 pallets (when the destination requires palletised discharge)
  • Shrink-wrap over the entire bundle for high-humidity ports
  • Container liner bags for ocean routes with known condensation issues
  • Tally certificate and pre-shipment inspection coordination

Group operations in Perumbavoor since 1986 have given us about forty years of loading boxes out of Cochin Port — long enough to know that the right loading plan is the one your customer signs off on before the container leaves the factory yard, not after.

Quote with the loading plan, not just the rate

Send us your destination port, thickness, sheet size and target quantity. We will come back with a quote that names the container type, the sheet count it actually fits, and the payload weight on the bill of lading — so your CHA and your buyer see the same numbers we do.

title: BWR vs BWP for Export Packing: When MR Grade Will Fail at Sea | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: On a 28-day voyage from Cochin to Long Beach, a container's interior swings between 35°C and 5°C every 24 hours. That cycle drives condensation that destroys.
html:
The three grades, in plain English

Indian plywood for packing and construction is broadly governed by two Bureau of Indian Standards specifications. The grade name tells you what glue line is inside the panel, and the glue line is what decides whether the panel survives a wet voyage.

IS 303 MR (Moisture Resistant)

Built with urea-formaldehyde (UF) resin. Designed for interior, dry-environment use — furniture, interior partitions, dry-warehouse packaging. Survives humid air and short-term moisture exposure. Will not survive standing water, repeated condensation cycles, or any salt-laden atmosphere. MR is the cheapest grade per square foot and the right answer for short-haul road movement of dry cargo inside India.

IS 303 BWR (Boiling Water Resistant)

Built with phenol-formaldehyde (PF) resin in a modified glue system. Passes a cyclic boiling water test — the panel goes into boiling water, comes out, dries, and is inspected for delamination. BWR is the workhorse export grade. It handles container condensation, monsoon transit, multi-modal handling, and the typical 20-to-35-day sailings to Middle East, Southeast Asia, and East Africa without breaking down. The chemistry is genuinely water-resistant, not just water-tolerant.

IS 710 BWP (Boiling Water Proof, Marine Grade)

Built to a stricter specification — PF resin throughout, denser core veneers, tighter veneer-grading, and the panel must pass a 72-hour boiling water test plus a mycological (fungal-attack) test. IS 710 is the true marine grade. It is over-specified for routine export packing and under-priced relative to the cargo it usually protects. The right answer for long-haul sailings, tropical-to-tropical lanes, deck-stowed containers, and any cargo where a claim event would dwarf the per-sheet premium.

What actually breaks MR-grade plywood at sea

Five distinct failure modes show up on inspection. They rarely arrive alone — one triggers the next.

  • Delamination. The UF glue line in MR plywood hydrolyses under repeated wet-dry cycles. The face veneer separates from the core, often invisibly at first, then in sheets when the crate is handled at the destination yard.
  • Fungus blooming. Warm, damp, dark interiors of containers are an ideal substrate. MR panels carry no anti-fungal protection in the glue system. Black and white surface blooms appear within ten to fourteen days of sustained humidity above eighty percent.
  • Fastener pullout. As the panel softens, nails and staples lose their grip. Side panels start to bow outward under their own cargo load. By the time the crate is forklifted, the joints are doing very little work.
  • Dimensional creep. Wet plywood swells. Dry plywood shrinks back imperfectly. Across a 28-day voyage with diurnal cycling, the panels lose flatness, the crate goes out of square, and the cargo inside shifts.
  • Surface blistering. The face veneer lifts in small bubbles where moisture has penetrated through micro-cracks. Cosmetic on day one, structural by week three.

The expensive part is not the failed crate. It is the cargo claim, the port-side rework, the demurrage while a replacement crate is built, and the buyer relationship that gets harder to rebuild.

Voyage duration and route — recommended grade by destination

The single best predictor of grade requirement is transit time, modified by the climate of the lane. The table below reflects typical sailing times from Cochin (Kochi) for full-container loads.

Destination portTypical transitLane climateRecommended grade
Jebel Ali (UAE)6–9 daysHot, dry, low rainfallBWR for general cargo; BWP for sensitive equipment
Singapore8–12 daysHot, humid, tropicalBWR baseline; BWP for trans-shipment cargo onward
Hamburg (Germany)22–28 daysMixed, cold-wet at destinationBWR for dry cargo; BWP for machinery and pharma
Long Beach (USA West Coast)26–32 daysPacific crossing, heavy condensation cyclingBWP recommended for full duration
Rotterdam (Netherlands)24–30 daysAtlantic crossing, wet European arrivalBWR for routine cargo; BWP for high-value

The rule of thumb our packing operators use: under ten days in a dry lane, BWR is enough. Over twenty days, or anything routed through monsoon Southeast Asia, plan around BWP unless the cargo itself is low-value and replaceable.

The real cost delta

Here is where the conversation usually pivots, because the price difference looks larger on a sheet than it does on a shipment. The snapshot below reflects current ex-factory rates for 12mm sheets — GST and transport extra.

GradeIndicative rate (12mm, ₹/sq.ft)Premium over MR
IS 303 MR (commercial packing)33.50Baseline
IS 303 BWR (hardwood packing)38.00–42.0013–25 percent
IS 710 BWP (marine grade)52.00–58.0055–73 percent

For a standard 20-foot container packing job that consumes roughly 400 to 500 square feet of plywood, the upgrade from MR to BWR adds about ₹2,000 to ₹4,000 per container. The upgrade from MR to BWP adds about ₹8,000 to ₹12,000. Set those numbers against the value of the cargo and a single claim event, and BWR becomes the sweet spot for most export work — cheap insurance, real water resistance. BWP becomes the obvious answer the moment the cargo value crosses a threshold the exporter knows better than we do.

Decision matrix by cargo type

Grade selection is partly about route and partly about what is inside the crate. The matrix below is the short version of the conversation we have at quote stage.

Cargo typeRecommended gradeReason
Industrial machinery, capital equipmentBWP (IS 710)Single claim dwarfs the grade premium; long lead-time replacements
Pharmaceutical APIs and finished goodsBWP (IS 710)Anti-fungal glue system is mandatory for regulatory acceptance
FMCG and packaged consumer goodsBWR (IS 303)Cost-sensitive, but cannot accept fungal contamination of secondary packaging
Electronics and electricalsBWP (IS 710)Condensation on PCBs is unrecoverable; humidity tolerance is non-negotiable
Textiles, garments, leatherBWR (IS 303)Stain risk from delamination; BWP only if shipped through monsoon lanes

How to verify what you are actually getting

Grade-marking fraud is the unspoken background risk in this market. The defences are straightforward.

Look for the BIS standard mark and the IS number stamped on the back of every sheet — IS 303 for MR and BWR, IS 710 for BWP. The stamp should be legible, not smudged, and consistent across the pallet. Ask for the mill test certificate matching the batch. For high-value consignments, third-party lab testing is available at NABL-accredited facilities in Mumbai, Bangalore, and Chennai; the cyclic boiling test takes about a week and costs a few thousand rupees per panel. For repeat orders, audit one sheet per shipment as a discipline — suppliers behave better when buyers verify.

Tell us the lane, we will tell you the grade

Send us the destination port, the transit time on your booking, and a one-line description of the cargo. We will come back with the grade we would use, the rate, and the reason. If the answer is BWR rather than BWP, we will say so — over-specifying the panel does not serve anyone for long.

title: FOB Cochin for Plywood Exporters: What's Included, What's Extra | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Your FOB Cochin quote looks clean — until the CHA's invoice arrives with 14 line items you weren't expecting. Here's exactly what's in scope, what's always.
html:
Your FOB Cochin quote looks clean — until the CHA's invoice arrives with 14 line items you weren't expecting. Here's exactly what's in scope, what's always extra, and which costs you can negotiate. We work with packing-case manufacturers and engineering exporters who move plywood and crated cargo through Cochin Port every week, so the surprises in this post come from real shipment files — not theory.

The short version: under Incoterms 2020, an FOB Cochin price ends the moment your container is loaded on board at Vallarpadam. Everything before that point should be in the seller's quote. Everything after — ocean freight, marine insurance, destination handling — is the buyer's account. The problem is the grey zone around the port itself: terminal handling, CFS storage, BL fees, and amendment charges that some sellers absorb and others bill back. The exporter who reads the fine print pays less.

FOB Cochin in plain Incoterms 2020

FOB stands for "Free On Board." Under Incoterms 2020, the seller's responsibility ends — and the buyer's begins — when the goods are placed on board the nominated vessel at the named port. For us, that named port is almost always Cochin (ICD code INCOK1 / Vallarpadam ICTT). The seller arranges inland trucking from the factory, port entry, customs clearance, terminal handling, and stuffing into the container. Once the container clears the ship's rail, the buyer owns the risk and the cost.

The most common misunderstanding: FOB does not include ocean freight. If your overseas customer wants door-to-door pricing, they're asking for CIF or DAP, not FOB. Keep the Incoterm in writing on every proforma — verbal "FOB Cochin" conversations get expensive when the BL is issued.

What FOB Cochin INCLUDES

Below is the line-by-line scope of a typical FOB Cochin quote on a 20ft container of packing-grade plywood ex-Perumbavoor. The CWI group has been operating in Perumbavoor since 1986 — forty years of moving plywood through Cochin Port — so the table reflects what the customs broker and CFS actually bill, not a textbook.

Line itemWho pays under FOBTypical scope
Plywood at agreed grade, size, thicknessSellerEx-factory, packed and strapped
Inland trucking Perumbavoor to Cochin PortSellerApprox 40-50 km, road-permit included
Container booking at shipping lineSeller20ft or 40ft as nominated
CFS handling and stuffingSellerLashing, dunnage, container seal
Customs filing (shipping bill)SellerCHA fees, EDI charges
Terminal handling charges (THC) at CochinSellerOrigin THC only
Port entry, weighment, gate-inSellerVallarpadam ICTT
Vessel loading (on-board)SellerUp to the ship's rail
Origin documentation setSellerCommercial invoice, packing list, BL draft

What's ALWAYS extra

These are the lines that surprise first-time importers. None of them are in an FOB price — but several of them can hit the seller if the shipment is delayed, mis-declared, or the buyer drags on payment. Build them into your landed-cost model from the start.

Extra costWho paysWhen it triggers
Ocean freightBuyerAlways
Marine insuranceBuyerAlways — strongly recommended
Destination THC and handlingBuyerAt discharge port
Container demurrage / detentionWhoever caused the delayBeyond free days at port or yard
CFS storage beyond free periodSeller (origin) or buyer (destination)Typically 3-5 free days at Cochin CFS
Fumigation, if required by destinationBuyer specifies, seller arranges and billsWood packaging without ISPM-15 mark
BL amendment feesWhoever requested the changeAfter BL issuance
Late shipping-bill amendmentsSellerIf declared value or HS code corrected

Document checklist for FOB Cochin

A clean FOB Cochin shipment moves on ten documents. Miss one and the container sits at CFS while storage fees accrue. The seller prepares items 1-9; the buyer's bank or freight forwarder typically requests item 10.

  1. Commercial invoice — buyer name, Incoterm, currency, HS code, country of origin
  2. Packing list — carton count, net and gross weight, dimensions, container number
  3. Bill of Lading (BL) — draft for approval, then original after on-board confirmation
  4. Certificate of Origin — Chamber of Commerce attested, or preferential if FTA applies
  5. ISPM-15 certificate or treated-wood declaration — for any wooden packaging or solid-wood product
  6. Phytosanitary certificate — if destination requires (most Middle East, EU, Australia)
  7. Shipping bill — EDI-filed, customs-cleared copy
  8. Mate's receipt — confirmation of on-board loading
  9. VGM declaration (Verified Gross Mass) — mandatory under SOLAS before vessel loading
  10. Insurance certificate — if buyer requests CIF-equivalent cover, otherwise buyer arranges

Cochin vs Chennai vs Tuticorin for plywood

Buyers in the rest of India sometimes ask whether routing through Chennai or Tuticorin is cheaper than Cochin. For plywood ex-Kerala, the answer is almost always no — the inland trucking eats any port-side saving. The table below shows why Cochin is the default choice for any factory operating in the Perumbavoor belt.

FactorCochin (INCOK1)Chennai (INMAA1)Tuticorin (INTUT1)
Distance from Perumbavoor40-50 km~700 km~500 km
Inland trucking (20ft container)LowestHighModerate-high
Transit days to Jebel Ali (UAE)8-10 days10-12 days9-11 days
Transit days to Hai Phong (Vietnam)15-18 days12-14 days14-16 days
Weekly sailings (main lines)Multiple to Middle East, SE Asia, EuropeHighest density in South IndiaStrong to Colombo, Middle East
CFS density near portModerate, growingVery highModerate
Typical origin THC (₹/20ft, indicative)CompetitiveSlightly higherCompetitive

Where Chennai or Tuticorin wins: routes where the destination is on the East Coast trade lane and the buyer has a freight contract tied to those ports. For everything else — Middle East, Europe, East Africa — Cochin's proximity to the Kerala plywood belt removes 600+ km of trucking cost.

Two cost-saving habits

Consolidate to one weekly sailing instead of two

Exporters in the packing-case and crate segment often fall into a rhythm of shipping whenever a container fills up — which means two part-loads in the same week, with two sets of CHA fees, two THC charges, and two documentation sets. If your buyer can accept a single weekly delivery, consolidating two 20ft loads into one 40ft container typically saves 18-25% on per-cubic-metre landed cost. The discipline is in stock planning, not in the freight booking.

Pre-book CFS to skip storage

The default flow at Cochin gives 3-5 free days at the CFS before storage charges start. If your factory dispatches the truck before the container is gated in, you can lose two of those free days waiting in line. The fix is simple: have your CHA book the CFS slot 48 hours before truck dispatch, with the container number and seal already allocated. Engineering exporters who run this process consistently save ₹4,000-₹8,000 per shipment in avoided storage — money that compounds across a year of recurring orders.

Get an FOB Cochin quote that actually holds

A clean FOB Cochin quote starts with the right ply specification — packing grade, Okoume or Gurjan face, thickness, sheet size, monthly volume, destination port. Send us those five inputs and we'll come back with a price that breaks out the inland leg, port charges, and documentation cost separately, so there are no surprises on the CHA invoice.

title: Plywood Cable Drum Flanges: IS 10418 Spec, Sizing & Sourcing Guide | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: A cable manufacturer winds 6 km of armoured LT cable onto a 1400mm drum and ships it 1,800 km on Indian highways. If the plywood flanges flex or crack, the.
html:
What IS 10418 Covers

IS 10418 is the Indian Standard for plywood drums used for packing electrical cables. It is the dispatch-grade reference for the cable industry — what BIS uses when a buyer disputes a drum failure, what a third-party inspector measures against, and what most cable tenders cite in their packing clause.

The standard covers four structural elements of the drum, each with its own tolerance band:

  • Flanges — the two circular plywood discs that contain the wound cable. IS 10418 prescribes minimum thickness by drum size, grade, and load class.
  • Barrel — the central wooden cylinder the cable is wound onto. Its outer diameter sets the minimum bend radius for the cable.
  • Staves (lagging) — the outer wooden battens nailed across both flanges to protect the wound cable face. These are sawn timber, not plywood.
  • Fasteners — through-bolts, threaded rods, washers and nuts that hold flange-to-barrel and flange-to-flange. The standard prescribes minimum bolt grade, count and edge distance.

IS 10418 also classifies drums by load class — light, medium and heavy — which in practice is what drives the ply thickness call. A 1400mm drum carrying 800 kg of LT cable is a very different mechanical problem from a 2000mm drum carrying 3,500 kg of armoured EHV cable, even though the flange diameter only differs by 600mm.

Drum-Code Reference Table

IS 10418 drum codes are named by flange outer diameter in centimetres. The barrel diameter and typical cable capacity follow the code. Use this as a starting reference when matching cable length and gauge to drum size — final selection depends on cable OD and winding pattern.

IS Drum Code Flange OD (mm) Barrel OD (mm) Typical Cable Capacity
DR-60600280Light control cable, 200-400 m
DR-80800355LT power cable up to 70 sq.mm, ~500 m
DR-1001000450LT armoured up to 185 sq.mm, ~500 m
DR-1201200560LT/MV armoured up to 240 sq.mm, ~500 m
DR-1401400710LT armoured 300-400 sq.mm, ~500-1000 m
DR-1601600800HT/MV cable up to 11 kV, ~500 m
DR-1801800900HT/EHV cable up to 33 kV, ~500 m
DR-20020001000EHV cable up to 66 kV, ~300-500 m
DR-22022001120EHV cable 132 kV and above, ~250-400 m

Flange Diameter × Ply Thickness × Load Chart

This is where most drum-spec disputes actually live. A buyer asks for a 1600mm flange in 18mm ply because the last vendor quoted that way; the drum reaches site bowed and the cable face is contaminated. The chart below reflects practical load limits under IS 10418 for hardwood plywood flanges — gross load is total cable weight plus drum tare.

Flange OD (mm) 18mm Ply (max gross load, kg) 22mm Ply (max gross load, kg) 25mm Ply (max gross load, kg) 28mm Ply (max gross load, kg)
6004006008001000
80050075010001300
1000700100014001800
1200900130018002300
14001100160022002900
1600—190027003500
1800—220032004200
2000——37004900
2200——42005600

Dashes indicate combinations IS 10418 does not endorse — 18mm ply on anything over 1400mm is asking for in-transit flex. For drums above 1800mm carrying heavy EHV cable, 25mm or 28mm is the only safe call, and the standard expects the flange to be a single-piece disc, not jointed.

BWR vs BWP Grade Selection for Drums

The grade decision sits between the buyer's storage assumption and the drum's actual route. Cable drums often spend weeks in open godowns, sometimes outdoors on construction sites, sometimes at coastal ports.

  • BWR (Boiling Water Resistant) — the workhorse for domestic cable dispatch. Phenol-bonded, IS 303 compliant, handles up to 60-90 days of covered outdoor storage in tropical inland conditions. Suitable for LT and MV cable drums on PAN-India routes.
  • BWP (Boiling Water Proof) — a step up in resin loading and core quality. Use when the drum will sit at a coastal port, transit through monsoon, or be stored outdoors uncovered for 90+ days. Mandatory call for EHV cable exports leaving via Nhava Sheva, Mundra or Cochin.

A practical rule we apply at the Cochin Wood group, where the group has been milling and converting hardwood plywood since 1986: if the cable is over 33 kV or the buyer is an exporter, default to BWP. The cost delta is small compared to the cable value at risk.

Fastener Spec — Staves, Bolts & Threaded Rods

IS 10418's fastener clauses are short but unforgiving. Three numbers tend to be missed in the rush of PO drafting:

  • Bolt grade — minimum class 4.6 mild steel for through-bolts, hot-dip galvanised for any drum likely to see humidity above 70%. Stainless is overkill for cable drums; galvanised is the right cost-benefit point.
  • Bolt count by flange diameter — 4 through-bolts up to DR-100, 6 for DR-120 and DR-140, 8 for DR-160 and above. Threaded rods replace through-bolts on drums above DR-180 where the lever arm makes single bolts insufficient.
  • Edge distance — bolt hole centre minimum 2× bolt diameter from the flange edge, and minimum 1.5× from the barrel-flange joint. Drilling closer creates the split that the flange fails along under transit shock.

Staves are softwood or rubberwood sawn timber, 18-25mm thick depending on drum diameter, nailed not screwed, spaced to allow a 25-40mm gap for ventilation. The staves are sacrificial — they take the abrasion in handling so the cable face does not.

ISPM-15 Considerations for Exported Drums

The moment a drum crosses an Indian port for export, ISPM-15 applies. The drum has two wood components in scope: the plywood flanges (technically processed wood, but several inspectors still flag) and the sawn timber staves and barrel.

  • Plywood flanges — processed under heat and pressure during manufacture, so they are ISPM-15 compliant by process. Most inspectors accept the manufacturer's IPPC declaration. For zero ambiguity, ask your drum supplier to mark each flange with the IPPC stamp anyway.
  • Sawn timber staves and barrel — must be heat-treated (HT) at the timber yard and stamped with the IPPC code, country code (IN), HT marker, and the treatment provider's BIS-registered number. The stamp goes on at least two staves per drum and on the barrel end.
  • Fumigation alternative — methyl bromide (MB) treatment is still recognised under ISPM-15 but is being phased out by most destination countries. Default to HT unless the buyer has explicitly asked for MB.

A non-compliant stamp at destination port means the consignment is fumigated at owner's cost or returned. For a single EHV drum carrying eight figures of cable, that is not a risk worth saving ₹400 on.

Sourcing Rules — What to Specify on a PO

An IS 10418 conforming drum order needs eight specifications on the PO. Anything missing gets defaulted by the supplier, which is where disputes start.

  1. IS 10418 drum code — DR-60 through DR-220, written exactly as the standard names them.
  2. Flange OD in mm, with tolerance (typically ±5mm).
  3. Barrel OD in mm — non-negotiable, sets the cable bend radius.
  4. Gross load — total cable weight plus drum tare, in kg. Drives the ply thickness call.
  5. Ply thickness and grade — e.g. "22mm BWR hardwood plywood, IS 303 conforming" or "25mm BWP".
  6. Fastener spec — bolt grade, count, threaded-rod call where applicable, galvanised or plain.
  7. ISPM-15 requirement — yes/no, with HT or MB call, and destination country if known.
  8. Marking — buyer code, drum serial, cable type, length, gross/net weight, and the standard arrows for handling. Painted or hot-stamped on at least one flange face.

Quoting Your Drum Requirement

Send your cable spec — type, voltage class, conductor size, length per drum — along with the IS 10418 drum code and required quantity. We quote with the matching flange OD, barrel OD, ply thickness and grade, fastener pattern and ISPM-15 status, and issue an IS 10418 conformance certificate with every dispatched drum from the Cochin Wood group's drum line. Our group has been in hardwood plywood manufacturing in Perumbavoor for forty years, and the drum specification is something we sweat the details on. Get a quote at the contact page.

title: Plywood Supply to Tiruppur — Packing Ply, Crates, Pallets | CWI | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Tiruppur ships around ₹30,000 crore of knitwear a year, and none of it moves in cardboard alone. We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, garment-bale cases.
html:
Why Tiruppur sits on our regular trucking lane

Tiruppur ships somewhere around Rs 30,000 crore worth of knitwear out of India every year, and that volume does not move in cardboard alone. Garment bales need clean, dimensionally stable plywood cases that hold up in a 40-foot ocean container for 30-45 days at sea. The hosiery machinery cluster around the SIPCOT estate ships textile knitting machines, dyeing equipment and compressors to buyers across the GCC, Bangladesh, Vietnam and Africa — and those export consignments need ISPM-15 compliant wooden crates with the IPPC stamp. From our Kochi base, Tiruppur is one of the most efficient lanes we run: roughly 190 km up NH-544 through the Walayar ghat, a 5-7 hour truck haul depending on Salem-side traffic. The Cochin Wood group has been working this Kerala-Tamil Nadu border belt since 1986, so the freight, paperwork and supplier network are well-worn.

The Tiruppur industrial picture

For a city of its size, Tiruppur packs an unusual concentration of export-grade manufacturing. The plywood demand here splits across four broad sectors, and understanding which one a buyer sits in changes what we recommend.

Knitwear and garment exports

The core belt — Mannarai, Kongu Nagar, Iduvai, Veerapandi, Palladam Road — is dense with knitwear units. T-shirts, polos, infantwear and athleisure ship in compressed bales, usually packed in 4-way entry pallets or lightweight plywood crates lined with polythene. For a typical 20-foot container of finished garments, the wood content is modest but the plywood has to be moisture-resistant: humidity in the Bay of Bengal sea route can wreck a poorly chosen pack.

Hosiery and textile machinery

SIPCOT Tiruppur Phase I and Phase II host several knitting-machine assemblers, dyeing-machine fabricators and circular-knitting OEMs. These exports are heavy point loads — a single machine can sit at 800-2,500 kg — and the crates must be ISPM-15 heat-treated with the IPPC mark. This is where we ship our heavier packing-grade plywood (12mm, 15mm, 18mm) along with seasoned hardwood runners.

Dyeing, processing and finishing units

The Perumanallur, Andipalayam and Mudalipalayam clusters handle wet processing. They are mostly local-load buyers — domestic dispatches of fabric rolls on pallets — and tend to use 6mm-10mm commercial plywood for lighter wooden cases.

Engineering ancillaries on the Avinashi-Tiruppur corridor

Spinning-mill spares, compressor parts and electrical components from this stretch often need a sturdy mixed-load crate. These are smaller-volume but higher-frequency orders.

What we ship to Tiruppur

Most Tiruppur enquiries fall into three product groups. Our packing-grade plywood is the workhorse, with rates that move with raw material; the snapshot below is for orientation and we issue a live PI on request.

ThicknessTypical Tiruppur useIndicative selling rate (Rs/sq.ft, ex-factory)
6mmLight garment cases, internal partitions24
8mmStandard knitwear export cases28
10mmMedium-load fabric pallet decks31.5
12mmMachinery sub-crates, container linings33.5
15mmHeavy machinery crate walls39
18mmCrate floors, container flooring repairs42

All rates are ex-factory, GST and transport extra. Okoume plywood — used for premium export crates where a clean, light face is required — is quoted separately. We also ship sawn timber: jackwood hardwood runners at Rs 400/cft (¼-inch minus tolerance) and rubberwood runners as per size. For SIPCOT machinery exporters, the typical bill-of-materials looks like 18mm floor + 12mm walls + 50x75mm hardwood runners + corner bracing.

Pallet sizes that work for the city

For garment exports out of Chennai and Tuticorin ports, the standard pallet footprints we cut for Tiruppur buyers are 1200x1000mm and 1100x1100mm, 4-way entry, with a deck of 12mm packing-grade ply on rubberwood runners. For the GCC route, 1200x800mm Euro footprint is the more common ask.

Container flooring

Repair-grade 18mm marine and apitong-substitute boards are available for fleet operators running Tiruppur-Chennai-Tuticorin shuttles. For new builds we quote on full container sets.

Kochi-to-Tiruppur logistics

The route is straightforward. From our Perumbavoor loading point we run up NH-544 (Salem-Kochi) via Angamaly, Walayar, Coimbatore and Avinashi, into Tiruppur. The full distance is about 190 km. Loaded transit on a 32-foot full-body or a 22-foot SXL truck is typically 5-7 hours, depending on the Walayar check-post and Coimbatore bypass traffic. We send most Tiruppur loads through Coimbatore-based transporters who already run the lane daily, so we can usually piggyback on existing capacity rather than dedicate a vehicle. For smaller LCL-type orders below 3 tonnes, we consolidate with other Tamil Nadu deliveries and the lead time goes to 2-3 days. For exporters with tight container stuffing windows, we can hold a dispatch slot in our schedule and confirm a truck the same day a PI is signed.

Decision rules — which grade for which job

This is where most buyer confusion sits. A short version of how we guide the choice:

  • Packing-grade commercial plywood (rubberwood core) — the right call for 80% of Tiruppur loads. Garment cases, fabric pallets, machinery sub-crates for the domestic and GCC routes. Best cost-per-sheet for honest, repeat-buy packing.
  • Okoume-faced plywood — when the buyer's overseas customer specifies a clean, light, blemish-free face — typical for European garment buyers and premium machinery exports. Quoted on request; not a stock item at packing-grade rates.
  • Shuttering ply — for site construction and infra contractors in and around Tiruppur (warehouse expansion, dyeing-effluent tank works). Not interchangeable with packing-grade; the resin system is different.
  • BWR/BWP-grade — for crates going via sea on the longer 35-45 day legs to West Africa and South America, where moisture cycling is severe.

The thickness-to-load rule we apply: under 500 kg point load, 10-12mm is enough; 500-1,500 kg, step up to 15mm walls and 18mm floor; above 1,500 kg, 18mm floor with 75mm hardwood runners and corner bracing.

FAQ for Tiruppur buyers

Do you supply ISPM-15 stamped crates ready-to-load?

We supply the plywood and sawn timber inputs at our Kochi end. ISPM-15 heat-treatment and IPPC stamping is done at the buyer's end or at the buyer's nominated fumigator in Tiruppur — most knitwear and machinery exporters already have a tied fumigator on the Avinashi Road belt. We can recommend known operators if needed.

What's the minimum order to make a truckload from Kochi worthwhile?A standard 32-foot full-body lifts roughly 500-600 sheets of 8mm packing ply, or about 350-400 sheets of 18mm. For LCL part-loads we consolidate with other Tamil Nadu deliveries; minimum sensible LCL is around 50-100 sheets, with a 2-3 day lead time.

Can you hold inventory or run a scheduled monthly dispatch?

For repeat exporters in the SIPCOT belt placing 1-2 trucks a month, yes — we can lock a monthly dispatch slot and indexed pricing against our purchase rates. Walk us through the spec and volume and we will structure it on the PI.

Getting a quote

If you are a Tiruppur knitwear exporter, hosiery machinery fabricator or pallet/case maker servicing the city's industrial belt, the fastest path is to send the sheet size, thickness, monthly quantity and delivery pincode. We will issue a PI within the working day, with the live ex-factory rate, GST, and Kochi-to-Tiruppur transport quoted separately. Request a quote with your spec, or browse the technical notes and load-build guides on our resource hub before you finalise the bill of materials. We are happy to talk through the crate or pallet construction before you commit — that tends to save more money than haggling on the sheet rate.

title: Plywood Supply to Hosur | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Almost every truck leaving Hosur's industrial gates carries something packed in wood — auto components, two-wheeler sub-assemblies, machine tools.
html:
Hosur is one of the few inland Indian cities where almost every truck rolling out of an industrial gate carries something packed in wood. The stretch from SIPCOT Phase I down to the Bengaluru-Krishnagiri NH-44 spine moves auto components, two-wheeler sub-assemblies, watch movements, machine tools, defense electronics and Caterpillar earth-moving spares — and every one of those loads needs a crate, a pallet or a plywood-decked container floor under it. That demand profile is exactly what our Kochi-based packing operation is built around, and Hosur sits on a clean 370 km trucking lane from us via NH-544 and NH-44.

The Hosur industrial picture

Hosur is not one estate, it is a corridor. Buyers placing plywood and crate orders typically sit in one of these belts:

  • SIPCOT Hosur Phase I and Phase II — the original anchor estates housing TVS group units, Ashok Leyland's plant, Caterpillar India's earth-moving operation, Titan watches, and a dense ecosystem of Tier-1 and Tier-2 auto component suppliers feeding all of them.
  • Mookandapalli, Bagalur Road and Belagondapalli industrial clusters — sheet-metal pressing, forging, plastic moulding and electronics assembly units that ship to Bengaluru OEMs every day.
  • Thally Road / Berigai stretch — newer warehousing and contract-manufacturing capacity that has spilled over from Bengaluru and Whitefield as land there got expensive.
  • Defense and aerospace electronics — a small but high-value pocket of vendors building boxed sub-assemblies that ship under ISPM-15 wood-packaging rules.

The packaging brief is fairly consistent across these segments: heavy, dimensionally awkward, often export-bound, and zero tolerance for moisture damage or pest rejection at a foreign port. That is a plywood problem, not a corrugated problem.

What we ship to Hosur

Most Hosur enquiries that land with us fall into four categories. Here is what we typically quote.

1. Packing-grade plywood (sheets)

This is the workhorse — commercial / rubberwood plywood used by crate fabricators and in-house packing shops in 6 mm up to 18 mm thicknesses. The standard Hosur sheet sizes are 8x4 ft and 7x4 ft, with 12 mm and 18 mm being the volume runners for auto-component crates. We ship ex-factory from Perumbavoor; GST and transport are extra. Buyers typically take a full truck (about 600-650 sheets of 12 mm) for a 9-11 hour transit straight into SIPCOT.

2. ISPM-15 stamped crates and cases

For export-bound shipments — particularly auto components heading to ASEAN and GCC, and electronics moving to Europe — the wood-packaging material must carry the ISPM-15 heat-treatment stamp. Crate construction we cut for the Hosur belt usually combines an 18 mm packing-ply skin over a hardwood (jackwood or rubberwood) battened frame, with corner reinforcements sized to the part being shipped. Engineering drawings or a sample part are enough for us to quote.

3. Wooden pallets

Standard sizes that move well in Hosur are 1200x1000 mm (the Euro-friendly footprint many auto OEMs default to) and 1200x800 mm. Two-way and four-way entry, hardwood deck boards over a stringer or block base. We also build heavy-duty pallets rated for 1.5-2 tonne static loads where forging units and machine-tool exporters need them.

4. Container flooring plywood

Less common but a real Hosur ask — 28 mm container-floor grade ply for buyers refurbishing reefers and dry containers, and for OEMs building purpose-fitted intermodal boxes. Quoted on spec, not a stock line.

Kochi to Hosur — the logistics

The route that works for most of our Hosur shipments:

  • Distance: roughly 370 km from Perumbavoor / Kochi to SIPCOT Hosur
  • Route: NH-544 from Kochi through Salem, then NH-44 north toward Krishnagiri and Hosur
  • Transit: 9-11 hours on a single-driver run; an overnight despatch from us is typically unloading at the Hosur gate by mid-morning the next day
  • Vehicle types: 32 ft single-axle and multi-axle trailers for full-truck plywood; container movements where the customer needs a sealed load

For buyers who prefer it, we can also route via Coimbatore-Salem-Krishnagiri (effectively the same NH-544/NH-44 spine). Cochin Port is in play only for export consignments where the Hosur shipper is exporting through Kochi rather than Chennai — a small but real share of our enquiries.

Which grade for which Hosur load

A practical rule we work with, after years of quoting this belt:

Typical loadRecommended materialWhy
Auto components, domestic dispatch (Bengaluru, Chennai, Pune)12-15 mm commercial packing ply on hardwood frameStrength-to-cost optimum, no export grade premium needed
Auto / electronics export to ASEAN, GCC, EU15-18 mm packing ply, ISPM-15 stamped, hardwood battensPhytosanitary compliance + handling-shock margin
High-value or sensitive electronics, defense sub-assembliesOkoume-faced ply on selected panelsCleaner finish, lower internal abrasion, premium presentation
Site-cast civil works, warehousing build-outsFilm-faced shuttering plyRepeat-pour reusability, smooth concrete finish

Okoume is a face-veneer option layered over our MR-packing or BWR construction — not a separate grade. When a Hosur exporter is presenting a crate at a foreign customer's receiving bay and wants it to look like a finished product, that is where Okoume earns its premium. For 90% of in-country auto-component movement, our standard commercial packing ply is the right answer and Okoume is overkill.

FAQ

What is your minimum order size for Hosur?

For packing-grade plywood we generally quote full-truck loads to Hosur to keep freight-per-sheet sensible — around 600-650 sheets of 12 mm or equivalent. Crate and pallet orders are quoted by job; even 100-200 units of a defined SKU is workable if drawings are clear.

Can you handle ISPM-15 stamping?

Yes. We supply heat-treated, ISPM-15 marked wood-packaging material where the buyer is exporting. The treatment certificate travels with the consignment.

What is the lead time from order confirmation?

Standard packing-grade plywood in volume runners (12 mm, 18 mm) typically dispatches within 5-7 working days of confirmation and advance. Custom crate jobs depend on drawings — usually 10-14 days for a first production run, faster on repeats. Hosur transit adds the 9-11 hour drive on top.

Getting a quote

The Cochin Wood group has been operating in Perumbavoor since 1986 — about forty years of plywood and packaging manufacturing in the same belt. If you are buying for a SIPCOT unit, a Mookandapalli supplier, or a Bagalur Road contract manufacturer and want a delivered Hosur price, send us thickness, sheet size, monthly quantity and the delivery pincode and we will come back with an ex-factory rate and a landed estimate. Use the request a quote page, and the resource hub has our material spec sheets, ISPM-15 notes and a crate-sizing primer for buyers building a packing brief from scratch.

title: Plywood Supply to Salem | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Salem's packing is rarely about furniture and almost always about industrial despatch — stainless and carbon steel, magnesite, textile machinery. We.
html:
Plywood, crates and pallets into Salem's steel-and-textile belt

Salem is one of those Tamil Nadu cities where the packing requirement is rarely about retail furniture and almost always about industrial despatch. SAIL's Salem Steel Plant rolls stainless and carbon flat products that have to leave the gate without edge damage. Magnesite and bauxite move from mines around Salem and Namakkal in tonnage that punishes thin pallets. The textile mills along the Salem-Erode corridor periodically import or shift machinery — looms, ring frames, knitting machines — that need crating built to survive multi-leg road transit. Each of these flows pulls a different plywood specification, and getting that match right is the difference between a clean delivery and a returned consignment.

This page is a working note from the Cochin Wood group's despatch side on what Salem typically asks for, what we actually load on a Kochi-to-Salem truck, and how we decide between packing-grade ply, Okoume and shuttering ply for the city's recurring load profiles.

The Salem industrial picture

Three buyer clusters in and around Salem drive most of our enquiries.

1. Salem Steel Plant and the downstream cold-rolling belt

SAIL's Salem Steel Plant sits on the Salem-Mettur Dam Road and is the anchor for a wider ecosystem of stainless steel cold rollers, polishers and tube makers operating out of Pottaneri, Suramangalam and the SIDCO Industrial Estate at Suramangalam. Coil-edge protection, slit-strip pallets and export crates for kitchenware sheet stock are the common asks. The packing is unforgiving — stainless sheet shows every dent.

2. Steel forging, foundry and engineering — SIDCO Industrial Estate, Kannankurichi

The SIDCO estate at Kannankurichi and the smaller pockets at Ayothiapattinam carry forging shops, foundries and general-engineering units that ship pump components, valves and steel castings. These are the classic ISPM-15-stamped crate buyers — heavy, dense parts that need a stiff base, gusseted corners and a 12mm or 18mm packing-grade plywood skin.

3. Textile and home-furnishing — Salem-Erode-Tiruppur corridor

Salem sits on the same yarn-and-fabric corridor as Erode and Tiruppur. Mill expansions, second-hand machinery imports through Chennai and Tuticorin, and finished-goods exports of made-ups all generate plywood case demand. Textile machinery cases tend to be larger in volume but lighter in density, which changes the panel choice.

Add the magnesite and refractory units near Salem, the granite cluster around Vadapudupatti, and the auto-component vendors feeding Hosur and Coimbatore plants, and the city's packing-plywood pull is steady year-round.

What we ship into Salem

Packing-grade plywood — the workhorse panel

The bulk of what leaves our yard for Salem is commercial / rubberwood packing-grade plywood in the 8 ft x 4 ft format. This is the panel used to skin export crates and box pallets for engineering goods. Thickness selection is driven by the load:

Load profileTypical thicknessNotes
Light textile machinery cases, made-ups, finished goods6mm / 8mmSkin only — frame carries the load.
General engineering, pump and valve crates10mm / 12mmThe default for SIDCO Kannankurichi enquiries.
Stainless coil edge protection, sheet stock cases14mm / 15mmStiff enough to absorb forklift contact.
Steel casting, forging, heavy machine bases16mm / 18mmUsed with hardwood runners; takes the weight directly.

All packing-grade rates are quoted ex-Kochi factory; GST and freight to Salem extra. Thickness convention follows the trade norm — what is sold as 12mm is the standard packing 11+ mm, and 18mm is 17+ mm. The 6mm and 8mm panels are dispatched at face value.

Crate construction — pine, jackwood and rubberwood frames

For Salem buyers who want fully built ISPM-15 crates rather than just panels, we build to drawing using heat-treated jackwood or rubberwood runners and stiles, with packing-grade plywood skinning. Jackwood hardwood is supplied at the standard quarter-inch-minus tolerance. Rubberwood runners are cut to the size specified on the crate drawing. ISPM-15 stamping is applied at the factory for export consignments leaving via Chennai, Tuticorin or Cochin ports.

Pallets

Two-way and four-way entry pallets for stainless coil, casting and textile machinery dispatch. Standard sizes — 1200 x 1000 mm, 1200 x 800 mm, 1100 x 1100 mm — are most common, but Salem despatch teams often need non-standard footprints to suit a specific container stuffing plan. We build to the size given.

Container flooring and case bases

For exporters moving heavy items through Chennai and Tuticorin, container flooring sheets and reinforced case bases in 18mm packing-grade ply are a recurring requirement. These are also used for sea-worthy boxing of granite blocks and refractory bricks.

Kochi-to-Salem logistics

Salem is roughly 360 km by road from our Kochi/Ernakulam yard via NH 544 through the Walayar-Coimbatore corridor and NH 544 / NH 44 onward to Salem. Loaded transit on a standard 32-ft or 22-ft container truck is typically one overnight run — vehicles leaving Ernakulam in the evening clock into Salem the following morning. We dispatch in both single-customer full-truck loads and consolidated part-loads where the order size doesn't justify a full vehicle.

For Salem buyers exporting via Tuticorin or Chennai, the more economical route is often to consolidate the crate-build at our end, despatch the finished crates to Salem for fit-out around the machinery, and then truck the crated load directly to the port. Where the export consignment is large enough, we can ship CFS-direct from Kochi instead.

Decision rules — which panel for which job

Packing grade vs Okoume

For domestic-only despatch within India, the commercial / rubberwood packing-grade panel is almost always the right answer — it carries the load, takes the nails, and the cost works. Okoume comes in when the buyer needs a lighter face veneer for an export crate where weight is being charged volumetrically, or when the consignee specifies a clean, light face for re-use. Most Salem packers don't need Okoume unless they are dealing with a European or GCC consignee who has called it out.

Packing grade vs shuttering ply

Shuttering ply is built for concrete formwork — film-faced, BWP-grade, designed for wet-and-release cycles. It is not the right panel for crate-skinning. Salem buyers building site infrastructure (the Salem Steel Plant expansion contractors, road and bridge projects on NH 44, real-estate projects around Hasthampatti and Fairlands) use shuttering ply; everyone else stays on packing grade.

Single-batch vs running supply

One-off crate jobs are fine on spot quotes. Salem buyers who crate every week — typical of the SIDCO Kannankurichi engineering shops and the stainless cold rollers — usually move to a monthly indent against a fixed price, with thickness mix locked in advance. This is the better commercial setup for both sides.

FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15 stamped crates ready for export?

Yes. Crates are built using heat-treated wood and stamped at the factory before despatch. The crate documentation is provided with the consignment.

What is the minimum order for a Kochi-to-Salem despatch?

Full-truck loads are the cleanest economics. For smaller volumes we look at part-loads on shared vehicles running into Coimbatore and Salem; the freight per sheet is higher, but workable for trial orders or single-crate jobs.

Can you build crates to a drawing we send, or only standard sizes?

Built to drawing. Send the case GA — outer dimensions, gross weight, lifting points and any ISPM-15 / sea-worthy callouts — and we quote against that. Standard pallets we hold to known footprints; crates are job-specific.

Asking for a quote

If you are planning a Salem despatch — coil packing, engineering crates, pallets for a SIDCO unit, or container flooring for an export — send the thickness, sheet size, monthly quantity and delivery point and we will revert with a Salem-landed price and a despatch window. Request a quote or browse the resource hub for the panel specs, crate construction notes and packing-ply price card. Quotes are firm in writing; rates are ex-Kochi with GST and Salem freight quoted separately so you see the full landed cost before confirming.

title: Plywood Supply to Madurai | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Madurai's packing demand is unusually mixed — heavy textile machinery, chemical drums, auto castings, granite slabs. We manufacture the packing-grade.
html:
Why Madurai's industrial floor matters to a Kochi plywood supplier

Madurai is the second-largest urban centre in Tamil Nadu and the gravity point for a packaging demand pattern that is unusually mixed. Textile spinning and processing pulls in heavy frames and rollers. The chemical and dye-intermediate cluster around Tirumangalam and Kappalur ships drums, IBCs and palletised packs. The auto-ancillary belt around Madurai-Sivagangai sends out castings, brake assemblies and rubber components to OEM yards across India. Each of those product streams needs a different timber spec, a different crate geometry and a different pallet load rating. From our base in Kochi, Madurai sits ~250 km out via NH-744 and NH-85, well inside a same-night truck window. That short lane is the reason CWI treats Madurai as a primary mainland Tamil Nadu corridor for packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring.

The Madurai industrial picture

For a packaging plywood supplier, Madurai is best read as four adjoining ecosystems rather than one city.

Textile and textile-machinery belt

The historic spinning, weaving and dyeing concentration runs through Madurai, Dindigul and into Tirupur on the western edge. Mills under modernisation programmes import or transfer ring frames, autoconers, draw-frames and warp-prep machinery. Those movements require heavy A-grade plywood crates with internal cleating, plus pallets that can survive forklift handling at two ends of the journey. The processed-fabric and home-textile exporters also need export pallets and lined plywood cases for sea freight.

Chemical, dye and pharma intermediates

Tirumangalam, Kappalur SIDCO Industrial Estate and the units along the Madurai-Tuticorin corridor work on chemical intermediates, dyes and a growing fine-chemicals base feeding the southern pharma cluster. Drum packs, secondary fibre-drum cartons and palletised IBC consolidations all need ISPM-15 compliant pallets and shuttering-quality plywood as drum dividers and top caps.

Auto-ancillary and rubber components

The Madurai-Sivagangai stretch hosts brake-assembly units, foundries, rubber-moulding plants and tier-2 ancillaries shipping to OEMs in Chennai, Pune and overseas. Heavy castings move on bottom-deck pallets with reinforced load spreaders; smaller components ride in standard nailed plywood cases with internal partitioning.

Granite, food processing and FMCG distribution

Polished granite slabs to Europe and the Middle East go on heavy A-frame skids with plywood facing. Around the city itself, an FMCG and edible-oils distribution layer keeps demand for reusable plywood pallets steady, decoupled from export cycles.

What we ship to Madurai

Three product families cover roughly 90% of Madurai-bound dispatches: packing-grade plywood sheets for case-makers, finished crates and pallets to spec, and container flooring for fleet operators rebuilding 20 ft and 40 ft boxes.

Packing-grade plywood specifications

Standard packing-grade plywood from CWI ships in eucalyptus and rubberwood core constructions with a hardwood face. Typical sheet specifications going to Madurai industrial buyers:

ThicknessTypical sheet sizeCommon Madurai use
6 mm8 x 4 ftLight component cases, drum top-caps
8 mm8 x 4 ftGarment cartons, FMCG outer cases
12 mm8 x 4 ftStandard textile-machinery crate walls
16-18 mm8 x 4 ftHeavy machinery cases, granite A-frames

For textile-machinery exporters we also supply 19 mm shuttering-grade where the application calls for an MR-bonded heavy face.

Crates and pallets to specification

Finished crates are quoted by external dimension, gross load and stacking factor. The most common Madurai builds are: 1200 x 1000 mm Euro pallets for chemical drums (2-way and 4-way entry), 1100 x 1100 mm export pallets for the South-East Asia textile trade, and heavy custom cases up to 4.5 m long for ring-frame movements. All export-bound timber is ISPM-15 heat-treated and stencil-marked at the production yard.

Container flooring

The reefer and dry-van rebuild trade in Tamil Nadu pulls in 28 mm container flooring plywood with WBP phenolic glue and an anti-skid wire-mesh face. CWI supplies in standard 2440 x 1220 mm sheets, with the load tested for the apitong-equivalent specification that container leasing companies accept.

Kochi to Madurai logistics

The lane is short and well-mapped. The standard route is Ernakulam to Madurai via Munnar-Theni on NH-85 (the shorter line, ~250 km, used when the ghat section is clear), or via Coimbatore-Dindigul on NH-544/NH-44 (longer at ~400 km but safer for oversized loads). A 9-ton single-axle truck dispatched from our Perumbavoor consolidation yard before evening typically reaches a Madurai unloading point in 6-8 hours. A 20-ton 32-foot multi-axle is closer to 9-10 hours including the mandatory rest stop. Because Madurai is land-locked from Kochi's perspective, we move everything by road; Tuticorin is reserved for the export legs where the consignee is shipping out from VOC Port directly. For chemical-belt buyers timing their drum loads to Tuticorin sailings, that triangulated routing (Kochi production yard - Madurai pallet handover - Tuticorin port-side stuffing) is usually the cheapest landed cost.

When packing-grade vs Okoume vs shuttering ply makes sense

Madurai's load profile is mostly heavy industrial, which means the default recommendation is hardwood-core packing-grade plywood. The decision tree we walk buyers through:

  • Packing-grade hardwood core (commercial / rubberwood): default for textile-machinery crates, auto-ancillary cases, granite skids and FMCG pallets. Best cost-to-strength ratio for one-way and short-cycle reuse.
  • Okoume: consider only when the importing country's phytosanitary regime or the consignee's contract requires a lighter, uniform-density face for marine packing of premium goods. Higher cost; rarely the right call for chemical drums or castings.
  • Shuttering ply (MR-grade film-faced): the answer when the buyer is actually a construction contractor pouring slabs for a Madurai factory expansion, an IT park or a flyover. Not a packing material.

If you are not sure which spec fits your load, the simplest path is to share the consignment weight, the destination and one photo of a representative case. Our team writes back with a recommended construction within a working day.

FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15 heat-treated pallets for export from Madurai units?

Yes. All export-grade pallets and cases are heat-treated and stencil-marked at the production yard before dispatch. The certificate accompanies the truck. This is the standard for chemical, dye and textile-machinery consignees shipping out of Tuticorin, Chennai or Cochin.

What is the minimum order quantity to Madurai?

The Kochi-Madurai lane is efficient from one part-load (around 4-5 tonnes) upwards. Full-truck loads price best per square foot. We will consolidate smaller orders into a part-load slot when scheduling allows; ask at the time of quoting.

Can you cut and supply pallet components for in-house assembly at our Madurai workshop?

Yes. Many auto-ancillary and chemical units in the Kappalur and Sivagangai belts run their own pallet assembly. We supply cut-to-size deck boards, stringers and blocks ready for nailing at your end. This typically reduces landed cost by 8-12% versus finished pallets.

Request a quote for your Madurai dispatches

The Cochin Wood group has been operating in Perumbavoor since 1986 - forty years of plywood and packaging timber experience under one set of family hands. Cochin Wood Industries Pvt Ltd is the corporate entity through which we run today's contract-manufacturing footprint, with consolidation at Perumbavoor and a fixed lane into Madurai's industrial belt. If you are a textile, chemical, auto-ancillary or export buyer in Madurai working out your packing-timber requirement for the next quarter, send through your specs and we will come back with a delivered price. Start at our request a quote page, or browse our resource hub for plywood specification guides, ISPM-15 reference notes and crate construction drawings.

title: Plywood Supply to Tiruchirapalli | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Trichy ships boiler fabrication, defence ordnance and auto components — multi-tonne assemblies that move in crates, skids and container floors, not cartons.
html:
Why Tiruchirapalli is a packing-plywood city, not a furniture city

Most plywood demand in Tamil Nadu gets discussed in furniture terms — Chennai showrooms, Coimbatore housing, Karur exporters. Tiruchirapalli (Trichy) sits in a different bucket. The city is built around heavy engineering, boiler fabrication, defence ordnance and a growing auto-component cluster. The plywood that moves into and out of Trichy is mostly going into crates, skids, pallets and container floors that carry multi-tonne assemblies to ports, project sites and other defence units. The Cochin Wood group has been working with this kind of buyer profile out of Perumbavoor since 1986, and Trichy's industrial mix lines up cleanly with what we build day-to-day: packing-grade plywood, heavy crate construction, hardwood runners and ISPM-15 compliant export packing.

The Tiruchirapalli industrial picture

Trichy's manufacturing footprint is unusually concentrated for a tier-2 city. A short list of the names that drive packing demand in and around the district:

Heavy engineering and power equipment

  • BHEL Tiruchirapalli — the Bharat Heavy Electricals high-pressure boiler plant and the seamless steel tube plant at Thiruverumbur. Boiler drums, headers, super-heater coils, fabricated panels and tube bundles all need site-built wooden crates with hardwood runners, plywood walls and steel strapping.
  • Heavy Alloy Penetrator Project (HAPP) — an Ordnance Factory Board unit at Trichy, manufacturing FSAPDS ammunition. Defence dispatch packing has to clear ISPM-15 for inter-unit and export movement.
  • Ordnance Factory Tiruchirapalli (OFT) — small-arms production at Thiruvarambur. Same defence packing discipline.

Auto components, fabrication and exports

  • The SIDCO and SIPCOT industrial estates at Thuvakudi, Mathur and Manapparai — fabrication shops, forging units, casting houses, auto-component vendors feeding tier-1 OEMs in Chennai and Bangalore.
  • The cluster of fabrication and structural-steel units along the Trichy-Madurai NH-38 corridor that crate-and-truck oversized weldments out of the district.
  • Karur is roughly 75 km away — a major home-textile export hub whose packing requirement spills into Trichy's crate-maker base when load sizes get awkward.

Every one of these buckets is a wood-packing buyer, not a furniture buyer. That changes which plywood specs matter.

What we ship into the Tiruchirapalli industrial belt

Packing-grade plywood (the workhorse)

Most Trichy crate-makers buy our commercial / rubberwood-core packing grade in 8 ft x 4 ft sheets. Common thicknesses moving on this lane:

ThicknessTypical use in a Trichy crate
6 mm, 8 mmTop and bottom panels for lighter auto-component and textile crates
10 mm, 12 mmSide and end walls for general engineering crates, BHEL component sub-packs
15 mm, 18 mmHeavy-duty walls, base decks and floors for boiler-component and structural crates

All rates quoted ex-factory; GST and transport extra. Standard rule: monthly indent in containers or full-truck lots gets sharper pricing than ad-hoc 50-sheet calls.

Crates, skids and pallets

For buyers who want finished packing — not just sheets — we build:

  • Heavy crates and skid bases with jackwood or rubberwood runners (¼ inch minus tolerance), plywood walls and roof, sized to the consignment. Typical Trichy use: BHEL fabrication outloads, defence dispatch boxes, machined-casting export crates.
  • Standard pallets — four-way entry, two-way entry, custom footprints. Sawn-timber stringers with plywood top deck where the load needs a continuous bearing surface.
  • ISPM-15 heat-treated stamp on the wood for any consignment leaving India — mandatory for HAPP/OFT export dispatches and for Karur textile exporters routing through Trichy crate-makers.

Container flooring and shuttering ply

For project-cargo movements out of Chennai and Tuticorin (Thoothukudi) ports, we also supply 28 mm container-flooring plywood and shuttering ply for the civil and infrastructure jobs around Trichy — flyover packages, the Trichy airport expansion, and the steady stream of TNHB and TWAD construction.

Kochi-to-Tiruchirapalli logistics

Trichy is one of the easier south-Indian lanes to service from Perumbavoor. Distance is roughly 350-380 km via Salem-Karur (NH-544 + NH-44 + NH-38) or via the Munnar-Bodi-Dindigul route. Trucking transit is typically 14-18 hours door-to-door, which means a 20-foot or 32-foot trailer dispatched at Perumbavoor in the evening reaches a Trichy industrial estate by next-day forenoon. Lane characteristics worth knowing:

  • Single-axle 19-feet open body and 22-feet container bodies are the common vehicle sizes on this lane — better than full 32-foot trailers if your receiving yard is inside SIDCO/SIPCOT estates with tight turning.
  • Toll plazas along Salem-Trichy add roughly ₹2,200-₹2,800 each way; we quote DDP-style "delivered Trichy" rates so the buyer doesn't have to model it.
  • For export consignments, sea routing through Tuticorin (~300 km from Trichy) is generally cheaper than Chennai for west-Asia and Africa moves; we can pre-stamp ISPM-15 in Perumbavoor so the crate clears port inspection without a hold.

Which grade for which Trichy job

When packing-grade rubberwood/commercial ply is correct

Default for 90% of Trichy crate work. Engineering components, auto parts, structural fabrication, defence ammunition boxes, home-textile exports. Strong enough for multi-tonne loads when paired with proper hardwood runners, priced for volume.

When Okoume makes sense

Okoume is a premium light-hardwood face from West Africa. We recommend it for export crates where appearance matters at the destination — typically high-value engineering goods to GCC and European receivers, and the occasional pharma/instrument export. Quoted separately on request.

When shuttering ply is the right answer

For Trichy's civil and infrastructure contractors — flyover decks, RCC raft foundations, retaining walls. Film-faced shuttering ply with 8-10 reuse cycles is the operator-grade choice; standard MR-grade shuttering covers lighter formwork.

FAQ — Tiruchirapalli buyers

Do you supply ISPM-15 certified packing for defence and export consignments?

Yes. The wood is heat-treated and stamped before dispatch from Perumbavoor. The IPPC stamp clears port inspection at Tuticorin, Chennai and Cochin. We retain treatment records for audit purposes.

What's the minimum order on a Trichy delivery?

Practical minimum is around 200-300 sheets or one full 19-feet truck load combining plywood, runners and finished crates. Smaller indents are possible but freight per sheet jumps sharply below a half-truck.

Can you build to a specific drawing for a BHEL or auto-component crate?

Yes — share the consignment dimensions, weight, lifting-point requirement and any customer-mandated spec (BHEL/OFB packing standard, customer SOP, ASTM D6256 for export). We build to drawing and submit a proto before bulk dispatch.

Get a quote for your Trichy requirement

If you're sourcing packing-grade plywood, ISPM-15 crates, pallets or container flooring for a Tiruchirapalli operation — BHEL ancillary, HAPP/OFT, SIPCOT-Thuvakudi auto-component, Karur exporter routing through Trichy, or a civil/infra contractor — send us your spec sheet and monthly indent. We'll come back with thickness-wise rates, lead time, truck plan and ISPM-15 confirmation. Request a quote with your drawing and monthly volume, or browse our resource hub for grade comparisons, ISPM-15 notes and packing-case construction guides before you brief us.

title: Plywood Supply to Tuticorin | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Almost everything leaving Tuticorin leaves in a wooden box — salt, marine products, copper, chemicals and project cargo through V.O. Chidambaranar Port. We.
html:
Why Tuticorin is one of South India's most plywood-hungry port cities

Tuticorin (Thoothukudi) is unusual on the Indian map because almost everything that moves out of the city moves in a wooden box. The V.O. Chidambaranar Port handles bulk salt, marine products, processed copper from Sterlite's legacy belt, edible oil, chemical containers, cement clinker and a growing line of project cargo for southern Tamil Nadu's renewable build-out. Every one of those flows needs packing-grade plywood somewhere in the chain — either as the export crate the customs officer stamps in Sector 10A, or as the pallet that takes the load from the SIPCOT shed to the container freight station at Harbour Estate. We at Cochin Wood Industries route a steady stream of packing-grade plywood, ISPM-15 export crates, four-way pallets and container flooring sheets down the NH-744/NH-38 corridor into Tuticorin's industrial belts, and this page is a practical operator's note on how that supply works.

The Tuticorin industrial picture, sector by sector

The plywood demand in Tuticorin doesn't sit in one place. It is spread across five or six distinct industrial pockets, each with a different load profile. Understanding the pockets matters because crate design, ply thickness and pallet footprint all change with the sector.

SIPCOT Industrial Complex, Madathur

The Madathur SIPCOT phase is the heavy-engineering and chemical anchor — units making industrial salts, sulphuric acid derivatives, soda ash, processed kaolin and PVC compounds. Crates here typically carry drum-mounted goods or palletised bags.
