Floor planks and side walls run on 12 mm and 18 mm BWP packing ply because the loads are corrosive-adjacent and the customs route is direct-port-export.

V.O. Chidambaranar Port and the CFS belt

The container freight stations clustered around Harbour Estate and Mullakkadu need pallet stock more than crate stock. The pallet footprint is mostly 1100 x 1100 mm and 1200 x 1000 mm because outbound flows hit GCC ports (Jebel Ali, Sohar) and South-East Asia. Top and bottom decks are 12 mm packing ply; stringers are pine or rubberwood scantling.

Tuticorin Marine Products cluster (Therespuram, Threspuram, Vembar belt)

Frozen seafood exporters need light, hygiene-compliant export crates and slip sheets. The marine products belt is where our 8 mm and 10 mm rubberwood-core packing ply moves in volume — it goes into the reefer container as box bottoms and partition panels.

Sterlite-legacy copper and metals downstream

Copper rod, cathode and downstream fabricators in the Madathur and Pandarampatti belts buy thicker BWP for heavy crating — typically 18 mm with battens for cathode bundles, and container flooring sheets when project cargo needs an internal load-distribution platform.

Renewable and wind-component vendors

The wind belt south of Tirunelveli, which routes through Tuticorin port, occasionally needs oversized project crates for blade fittings, nacelle sub-assemblies and inverter cabinets. These are one-off jobs but heavy on 18 mm BWP and IS 710-grade boards.

What we ship into Tuticorin

The Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing in Perumbavoor since 1986, and the catalogue we serve to Tuticorin is the one shaped by forty years of group manufacturing into the export-packing trade. Three product families do almost all the work in this city.

Packing-grade plywood (commercial and rubberwood-core)

This is the volume product. EX-factory rates as of mid-2026 — GST and transport extra — are: 6 mm Rs 24, 8 mm Rs 28, 10 mm Rs 31.5, 12 mm Rs 33.5, 14 mm Rs 36, 15 mm Rs 39, 16 mm Rs 39.5, 18 mm Rs 42 per sq ft. Standard sheet 8 x 4 ft. For Tuticorin port-direct export crates, the 12 mm and 18 mm grades carry most of the load.

Okoume plywood

Premium export-grade with Okoume face veneer over our packing-grade or BWR core. Used where the buyer's overseas customer specifies a clean face (cosmetics, electronics, certain Middle-East food categories). Priced on enquiry. Note: Okoume is a face-veneer option layered on the MR-Packing and BWR grades, not a separate grade — clarify with our quoting team if you're spec'ing it.

Shuttering plywood (IS 710 BWP)

For Tuticorin's construction belt — port expansion contractors, the warehouse build-out around Eppodumvendran, hotel and residential projects on the ECR side. 12 mm and 18 mm shuttering ply with film face when the buyer wants 8-10 pour repetitions.

Sawn timber, runners and pallet stock

Jackwood runners at Rs 400 per cft (¼ inch minus tolerance) and rubberwood runners priced by size. We also supply ISPM-15-compliant heat-treated pallet kits when the buyer wants the crate built at our Perumbavoor floor rather than locally.

Container flooring panels

For project cargo moving through V.O.C. Port — 28 mm phenolic-bonded flooring panels in container-pitch widths. Niche, but the wind-component and copper-cathode shippers ask for it regularly.

The Kochi-to-Tuticorin logistics lane

The road run from Perumbavoor to Tuticorin is one of the cleaner south-Indian trucking lanes. The standard route is Perumbavoor — Muvattupuzha — Thodupuzha — Kumily — Theni — Madurai — Tirunelveli — Tuticorin, roughly 470 km on NH-744 (the Kochi-Dhanushkodi corridor) and NH-38 (the Madurai-Tuticorin stretch).

LegDistanceTypical transit
Perumbavoor to Madurai~290 km7 hours
Madurai to Tuticorin (SIPCOT)~175 km3 hours
Total Kochi to Tuticorin port~470 km14-16 hours

A 20-ft full-truck load lifts roughly 600 sheets of 12 mm packing ply. Bigger 32-ft single-axle bodies lift around 1,000 sheets. We dispatch from Perumbavoor by evening, the truck crosses the Walayar check-post overnight, and material is on the SIPCOT customer's floor by the second morning. For port-direct deliveries to CFS yards near Harbour Estate, we can route via the Karaikudi-Aruppukottai bypass and shave roughly an hour off peak-monsoon transit.

Decision rules: which grade for which Tuticorin load?

  • Salt, chemical and corrosive-adjacent goods — go BWP (boiling-water-proof) at 12 mm or 18 mm. The marine-grade glue line is non-negotiable when the crate sits dockside before lift.
  • Marine-products export (frozen seafood) — 8 mm or 10 mm packing ply for box bottoms; partition panels at 6 mm. Light, food-contact-safe coating optional.
  • Copper cathode, metal fabrications, project cargo — 18 mm BWP with battened crate construction and 4-way pallet base.
  • Outbound general cargo to Jebel Ali / Sohar via VOC Port — Okoume-faced packing ply if the consignee specifies, otherwise straight rubberwood-core packing grade.
  • Port-expansion and warehouse contractors — IS 710 shuttering ply; film-faced if the contractor wants pour-repetition.

Frequently asked questions from Tuticorin buyers

Do you supply ISPM-15-stamped export crates ready to load?

Yes. Heat treatment and the ISPM-15 stamp are done at our Perumbavoor facility before dispatch. Buyer gets a phytosanitary-compliant crate landed at SIPCOT or the CFS yard.

What is the minimum order quantity for a Tuticorin run?

For pure plywood sheet supply, one truck (~600-1,000 sheets depending on body size). For built crates and pallets, the MOQ is lower because we consolidate with other Tamil Nadu-bound consignments.

Are rates inclusive of GST and freight to Tuticorin?

No. Card rates are EX-factory Perumbavoor. GST 18 percent and freight to Tuticorin are extra. We share a delivered-to-SIPCOT or delivered-to-Harbour-Estate quote on the proforma invoice once thickness, sheet count and delivery dock are confirmed.

Get a Tuticorin-delivered quote

If you are crating salt or chemicals for export, palletising marine products for the reefer line, or sourcing IS 710 BWP for a port-side contractor, send us your thickness mix, monthly volume and the SIPCOT or CFS dock you want it delivered to. We will turn around a proforma invoice with the Kochi-to-Tuticorin freight built in. Request a quote from our sales team, or browse the resource hub for spec sheets on BWP grades, Okoume face veneers and ISPM-15 crate construction. Our quoting team in Ernakulam handles Tuticorin enquiries directly — no distributor layer between the factory floor and the buyer's purchase manager.

title: Plywood Supply to Karur | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Karur sits at the centre of one of India's densest home-textiles export clusters — bedlinen, towels and made-ups headed to the US, EU and Gulf. We.
html:
Why Karur is on our weekly truck list

Karur sits at the centre of one of India's densest home-textiles export clusters. Bedlinen, terry towels, kitchen linen, table linen and made-ups roll out of mills around the Karur-Pugalur-Aravakurichi belt every week, headed to ports at Tuticorin, Chennai and Cochin and onward to the United States, the European Union, the United Kingdom and the Gulf. With Tamil Nadu home-textiles exports tracking around the Rs 8,000-crore mark in recent years and Karur contributing the lion's share, every consignment needs a wooden bale-crate, a heat-treated pallet, or container dunnage that won't fail in transit. That packaging demand is what brings the Cochin Wood group's trucks up the NH-544 / NH-83 corridor every week.

The Karur industrial picture

Karur's packaging demand isn't one homogeneous market. It splits across four overlapping pockets that buy plywood in different formats:

The home-textiles export belt

Concentrated around Thennilai Road, Andankoil East, Pasupathipalayam and the Karur-Erode Road, this is the heart of the bedlinen and terry-towel industry. Compressed fabric bales need plywood top/bottom caps and edge protectors; full-container shipments need 1140 mm x 1140 mm export pallets and 9 mm to 12 mm plywood load-floor.

SIDCO and SIPCOT estates

The SIDCO Industrial Estate at Thalapatti and the SIPCOT Industrial Complex at Pugalur house a mix of engineering, auto-component and chemical units. These plants need ISPM-15 stamped crates for heavier machinery exports, not just bale-grade ply.

Bus-body and automotive cluster

Karur has a long-standing bus-body building reputation, with workshops along the Karur-Trichy NH route. They consume 18 mm and thicker shuttering and commercial plywood for internal panels, jigs and form-work.

Aravakurichi paper and packaging sub-cluster

The Aravakurichi-Kulithalai stretch carries paper mills and converters who blend corrugated outers with wooden pallets and bottoms for heavy loads. This is a mixed-material zone and we typically supply the wooden-pallet side, not the corrugated side.

What we ship into Karur

Packing-grade plywood (the workhorse)

For bale capping, edge protectors and inner box construction, the Cochin Wood group's MR / packing-grade ply is the default. Standard thickness ladder, ex-factory rates as of June 2026 (GST and freight extra):

ThicknessTypical use in KarurRate (Rs / sq.ft)
6 mmTop/bottom caps on fabric bales24.00
8 mmLight bale crates, partition boards28.00
10 mmMedium crates, container side-lining31.50
12 mmContainer flooring, mid-weight crates33.50
15 mmHeavy export crates, machinery boxes39.00
18 mmBus-body internals, form-work, pallet decks42.00

Sheet size standard 8 ft x 4 ft (2440 mm x 1220 mm); we also cut to 7 ft x 4 ft and 6 ft x 3 ft for fabric-bale customers who want minimum trimming waste.

Okoume plywood for premium export crates

For export consignments where the crate itself has to look the part — automotive jigs, mill-direct premium-bedlinen shipments to the EU, machinery for the GCC — Okoume face-veneer plywood gives a clean, light-coloured finish. Priced on enquiry; lead time from Kochi is typically 5 to 7 days for trailer-load quantities.

Ready ISPM-15 crates and pallets

For SIPCOT and SIDCO buyers exporting under wood-packaging rules, we supply ISPM-15 heat-treated and HT-stamped crates and pallets from registered facilities. Standard export pallet sizes we move regularly to Tamil Nadu:

  • 1200 mm x 800 mm (Euro pallet — EU-bound textiles)
  • 1200 mm x 1000 mm (UK / general export)
  • 1140 mm x 1140 mm (container-optimised, 20 per 20 ft container)
  • 1100 mm x 1100 mm (US / ISO standard)

Container flooring and dunnage

12 mm to 18 mm packing-grade ply rip-cut into 1220 mm wide strips for false-flooring inside containers; 100 mm x 100 mm and 75 mm x 75 mm sawn-timber dunnage in rubberwood or jackwood for blocking and bracing.

Sawn timber

Jackwood runners at Rs 400 / cft (quarter-inch-minus tolerance) for crate framing; rubberwood runners priced by size for lighter bale crates.

Kochi to Karur: the route and the economics

The lane is roughly 280 km from Perumbavoor / Kochi to Karur via NH-544 (Salem road) and NH-83. A loaded trailer leaves the factory in the evening, clears the Walayar check-post, runs through Coimbatore and reaches Karur in 8 to 10 driving hours — most consignments unload by the next morning. We typically run:

  • Full trailer (32 ft / 16 MT) — best economics for bulk plywood sheets, around 800 to 1,000 sheets per trailer depending on thickness.
  • Container truck (20 ft / 9 MT) — for ISPM-15 crate kits and mixed loads with pallets.
  • Part-loads via Coimbatore-Karur consolidators — for smaller monthly off-takes; transit adds about 24 hours.

Because Karur is a regular lane for us, freight cost per sheet stays competitive against North-Kerala-based suppliers despite the longer distance — the trip is direct, the road is good, and the return-leg from Karur often carries textiles back towards Cochin Port, which helps trucker pricing.

Which grade for which load?

Use packing-grade (MR) plywood when

  • The crate is single-trip, port-to-port
  • You are capping or boxing fabric bales, light home-textile cartons
  • Budget per crate matters more than aesthetics

Use Okoume face-veneer plywood when

  • The end-customer sees the crate (premium brand exports)
  • You need a lighter, cleaner-grain face on the outer skin
  • Crate is for high-value machinery, automotive jigs, art / display goods

Use shuttering / 18 mm commercial plywood when

  • The job is bus-body internals, jigs, fixtures or re-usable form-work
  • The crate must survive 3-plus trips
  • You are building pallet decks for heavy machinery exports

FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15 certified crates ready for export?

Yes. We work with registered HT-treatment facilities and stamp crates with the IPPC mark before dispatch to Karur. Documentation accompanies the consignment so it clears customs without query at Tuticorin or Chennai.

What is the minimum order quantity to Karur?

Economical MOQ is a part-load of about 200 sheets or a full pallet-kit consignment. We also consolidate smaller monthly off-takes for Aravakurichi and Karur-town customers via our Coimbatore part-load partner.

Can you cut sheets to non-standard sizes for fabric-bale caps?

Yes. We cut to size at the factory before loading — the Karur bedlinen and terry-towel sector regularly takes 1200 mm x 600 mm and 1500 mm x 750 mm caps to suit specific bale presses. Cutting charge is nominal and reduces wastage at your end.

Request a quote

For Karur-based packing-case makers, exporters and SIPCOT / SIDCO buyers, we hold week-on-week trailer slots on the Kochi-Karur lane. Share your thickness, sheet-size, monthly volume and unloading address and we will return a written quotation, ex-factory and delivered-Karur, the same day. The Cochin Wood group has been operating in Perumbavoor since 1986 — forty years of group manufacturing experience now backs every consignment that leaves our gate. Request a quote or browse our resource hub for crate specs, ISPM-15 guidance and material selection notes.

title: Plywood Supply to Sivakasi | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Sivakasi packs roughly 80% of India's fireworks, a large share of its safety matches and a dense stream of offset print work — and almost all of it leaves.
html:
Why Sivakasi runs on careful packing

Sivakasi is one of the few towns in India where the packing list matters as much as the product list. The cluster pushes out roughly 80% of the country's fireworks, a very large share of safety matches, and a heavy stream of offset-printed cartons, calendars and labels for FMCG brands across the country. Every one of those outputs has to leave town on a truck — and almost every one of them needs a wooden crate, pallet or board behind it. That is the conversation we have with buyers in Sivakasi every week, and it is why the Cochin Wood group, with operations in Perumbavoor since 1986, has built a steady Tamil Nadu lane out of Kochi.

The Sivakasi industrial picture

The town sits in Virudhunagar district, about 80 km from Madurai. Industry is concentrated in a handful of belts that each have their own packing logic. If you supply plywood into Sivakasi, you need to understand those belts before you quote, because the crate spec follows the cargo.

Fireworks belt — Anaikuttam, Vembakottai, Thiruthangal

The fireworks units are clustered south and west of the town along the Anaikuttam, Vembakottai and Thiruthangal stretches, with hundreds of licensed factories operating under PESO rules. These units ship Class-3 and Class-4 explosives to dealers nationwide. The packing requirement is unusually strict: outer wooden cases that resist crush, sealed lids, no free play between the inner cartons and the case wall, and lumber that does not splinter sharply on impact. A second crate failure in transit is not just a refund — it is a regulatory incident.

Matchstick and safety-match cluster

Safety match production is spread between Sivakasi town, Kovilpatti and the smaller satellite units around Sattur. The cargo is lighter than fireworks but very dense per pallet, and the destinations are largely domestic distributors plus some export through Tuticorin. The typical ask is plywood for case lids, pallet decks and corner protection — not high-grade material, but consistent thickness and consistent moisture content.

Offset printing and packaging cluster

Sivakasi has more than 400 offset and flexo printing units, several of them running Heidelberg, Komori and KBA presses. When a press is upgraded, retired or relocated, it leaves on a custom crate. New machinery from Europe arrives in heavy-duty ISPM-15 cases that the local fabricators then refurbish or rebuild. Plywood for printing-machinery cases needs to be flat, dimensionally stable and rated for the load — this is where our 18 mm and 16 mm boards move best.

What we ship to Sivakasi

The Sivakasi lane is dominated by three SKUs out of our range. The mix shifts month to month depending on whether the buyer is a crate fabricator, a direct exporter or a printing-machinery mover.

Packing-grade plywood — the workhorse

For fireworks cases, matchstick cartons and general crating, the standard ask is commercial rubberwood-core packing-grade plywood in 8 ft x 4 ft sheets. Thicknesses move as follows:

ThicknessTypical Sivakasi end-use
6 mmInner partitions, carton stiffeners
8 mmLight crate sides, match-case lids
10-12 mmFireworks outer case walls, general crating
14-16 mmPallet top decks, heavier export crates
18 mmPrinting machinery cases, pallet stringers

All boards leave the facility ex-factory; GST and transport are quoted separately. Buyers typically pull 6-8 trucks a month against repeat orders, and pricing follows the standard CWI commercial card with no carve-outs for the lane.

Crates and pallets — built or supplied as components

Many Sivakasi crate makers prefer to fabricate locally and just buy the panel material from us. For buyers who want finished crates, we ship knocked-down case kits cut to the customer's drawing — sides, top, bottom, internal partitions and a separate hardwood frame. Standard pallet footprints we ship are 1200 x 1000 mm (export), 1200 x 800 mm (Euro) and 1100 x 1100 mm (domestic FMCG). ISPM-15 heat-treatment is arranged on request for the export crates.

Container flooring and shuttering ply

For 20 ft and 40 ft container reefer and dry-van rebuilds — a smaller but steady demand from Tuticorin-bound exporters using Sivakasi as a consolidation point — we supply 28 mm container-grade flooring panels. The same buyers occasionally take BWR shuttering ply for in-plant civil works. Shuttering ply is also used by the building contractors expanding the new warehousing parks along the Madurai-Tuticorin NH-38 corridor.

Kochi to Sivakasi logistics

The road distance from our Perumbavoor consolidation point to Sivakasi is roughly 430 km via Kambam-Theni-Madurai (the Bodi-Theni ghat route) or about 470 km via the longer Kanyakumari side. We default to the Theni route because it shaves 6-8 hours off the round-trip and avoids the Coimbatore congestion.

  • Transit time: 14-18 hours door-to-door for a single-driver 20-ton truck; 10-12 hours with a relay driver.
  • Vehicle: 32 ft single-axle for full loads (~440 sheets of 12 mm); 24 ft for half-loads.
  • Cut-off: trucks dispatched by 14:00 IST from Perumbavoor reach Sivakasi industrial estates by next-morning unloading.
  • Port option: for export buyers, factory-stuffed containers can clear through Tuticorin (V.O. Chidambaranar Port), ~150 km from Sivakasi, instead of the longer Kochi-port route.

For most buyers, road from Kochi is cheaper and faster than road from Yamunanagar or Kandla. That is the structural reason this lane works.

Which grade for which Sivakasi load

The decision is rarely about "best ply." It is about matching the board to the cargo and the journey. A few operator rules we apply when quoting:

  • Use packing-grade commercial ply for fireworks cases, matchstick cartons, domestic FMCG pallets and short-haul crates. This is 90% of the Sivakasi volume.
  • Use Okoume-faced packing ply only when the buyer needs a cleaner face for stencilling, brand printing or export presentation — typical for premium-segment exporters and certain printing-machinery cases bound for Europe.
  • Use BWR shuttering ply for civil works on warehouse expansions, not for cargo crates. It is a different product and customers sometimes confuse the two.
  • Use 28 mm container-grade flooring only for reefer or dry-van floor rebuilds. Do not substitute structural ply.

Frequently asked questions

Can you supply fireworks-grade crates that meet PESO transport rules?

We supply the plywood panels and case kits to the buyer's drawing. PESO compliance — explosive class marking, lid sealing, certification — is handled by the fireworks unit itself or its packing contractor. Our role is to deliver clean, defect-free boards with consistent thickness and stable moisture so the case fabricator has no surprises.

What is the minimum order quantity for a Sivakasi delivery?

A full 32 ft truckload is the most cost-efficient drop. For smaller buyers we run consolidated loads where two or three units in the same industrial belt share a truck — most often a Sivakasi-Sattur-Kovilpatti combined drop.

How are payments handled for new buyers?

First order is typically 100% advance against proforma invoice, then we move repeat customers to a partial-advance + balance-against-LR-copy model after 2-3 clean cycles. GST invoicing is standard.

Get a quote for your Sivakasi requirement

If you run a fireworks unit in Anaikuttam, a matchstick line in Sattur, a printing press in Sivakasi town or a packaging exporter using Tuticorin, the Cochin Wood group can quote a delivered price within the same business day. Share the thickness, sheet size, monthly quantity and delivery pin code, and we will revert with rates, a load plan and the lane economics. Request a quote or browse the resource hub for grade selection guides, ISPM-15 notes and packing-case drawings.

title: Plywood Supply to Abu Dhabi | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Between the Khalifa Industrial Zone (KIZAD), the Mussafah vendor belt and the offshore service yards, Abu Dhabi generates a constant flow of project cargo.
html:
Why Abu Dhabi's industrial belt pulls steady plywood demand from Kochi

Abu Dhabi is not just the federal capital of the UAE — it is the operational backbone of the country's oil, gas, and heavy-engineering economy. Between the Khalifa Industrial Zone Abu Dhabi (KIZAD), the Mussafah industrial belt, and the offshore service yards feeding ADNOC's upstream and downstream operations, the emirate generates a constant flow of project cargo, modular equipment, and spare-parts crating that all has to leave the country in compliant, ISPM-15-marked wooden packaging. Kochi sits roughly 2,000 nautical miles away across the Arabian Sea — close enough that container economics work in our favour and far enough that local Indian plywood from Perumbavoor reaches Khalifa Port at delivered costs that Southeast Asian suppliers cannot match on lead time. That equation is why the Cochin Wood group has shipped packing-grade plywood, knocked-down crate kits, and pallet stock into the Abu Dhabi corridor for years now, and why this guide unpacks how that supply chain actually runs.

The Abu Dhabi industrial picture

Before talking specifications, it helps to understand which industrial estates and sectors actually generate the plywood demand. Abu Dhabi is geographically vast, but the packaging consumption is concentrated in a few belts.

KIZAD and Khalifa Port

KIZAD is one of the largest integrated trade and logistics zones in the region — petrochemicals, metals, polymers, EV components, pharmaceuticals, food processing, and a growing cluster of heavy fabrication. It sits adjacent to Khalifa Port, which has the deepest draft of any port in the UAE and is geared for ULCS class vessels. Manufacturers inside KIZAD generate export cargo year-round that requires bulk packing-grade plywood for crating finished modules, machinery skids, and pipe spools.

ADNOC upstream and downstream

The Abu Dhabi National Oil Company and its operating subsidiaries — onshore, offshore, refining, gas processing — drive a heavy spare-parts and turnaround economy. Valves, pumps, instrumentation, drill-bits, and high-pressure tubulars all move in custom-built wooden crates with ISPM-15 stamps. The vendor ecosystem in Mussafah and Musaffah West (M-zones) is where most of this crating is fabricated, and it runs on continuous plywood supply.

Mussafah and ICAD

The Industrial City of Abu Dhabi (ICAD I through IV) hosts steel fabrication, building materials, automotive workshops, and contract manufacturers. This belt absorbs a lot of pallet stock and shuttering ply — pallets for inbound and outbound logistics, shuttering for the construction projects still active across Saadiyat, Yas, and the inland Al Ain corridor.

What we ship into Abu Dhabi

The product mix into Abu Dhabi is more focused than what we ship into, say, a furniture market. Operators here are buying wood as an industrial input, not a finished good, so specification and consistency matter more than aesthetics.

Packing-grade plywood

The workhorse SKU. Standard sheet size 8 ft x 4 ft (2440 x 1220 mm), thicknesses from 6 mm up to 18 mm, MR-bonded for moisture resistance through humid sea transit. Common moves into Abu Dhabi:

  • 12 mm and 15 mm — crate walls and tops for medium machinery and ADNOC spare-parts cases
  • 18 mm — base boards and skid surfaces under heavy oil-and-gas equipment
  • 9 mm and 6 mm — light internal partitions, dunnage, and inner blocking

Where the buyer needs higher dimensional stability or a denser face — particularly for high-value modules where surface marking matters — we move them to Okoume face veneers on the same MR-packing or BWR construction. Okoume is not a separate grade; it is a face-veneer option that gives a smoother, more uniform skin for premium crates.

Built-up crates and knock-down kits

For Abu Dhabi customers who want to skip the in-house fabrication step, we ship pre-built crates or knock-down kits cut to the project's bill of materials, with cleats, corner blocks, and lid panels labelled per case. This works well for one-time project cargo out of KIZAD where the receiver does not want to keep idle carpentry capacity.

Pallets

Standard sizes that move well into the UAE:

PalletFootprintTypical Abu Dhabi use
Euro1200 x 800 mmFMCG, pharma inbound into KIZAD
ISO standard1200 x 1000 mmGeneral industrial, ADNOC vendors
Custom heavy-duty1200 x 1200 mm and upOil-gas tooling, drilling spares

Container flooring

For specialised reefer and dry-box refurbishments handled by container-leasing yards in the Khalifa Port hinterland, we supply 28 mm container flooring boards in BWP construction — a niche but recurring line.

Kochi-to-Abu Dhabi logistics

The maritime lane is straightforward and one of the reasons Kerala-origin plywood is cost-competitive into the UAE. Cargo is consolidated at our Perumbavoor / Kochi loading point, trucked to Cochin Port (or Tuticorin / Mundra depending on the line), and sailed to Khalifa Port or Jebel Ali. Common parameters:

  • Sea distance: roughly 2,000 nautical miles Kochi to Khalifa Port
  • Transit time: typically 8 to 12 days port-to-port, depending on the carrier and transhipment
  • Container modes: 20 ft and 40 ft dry containers; FCL is the standard for plywood given the cube efficiency
  • Documentation: commercial invoice, packing list, BL, COO, phytosanitary or ISPM-15 certification where applicable, plus any FTA preference certificates

For project crates and pallets, ISPM-15 heat-treatment marking is non-negotiable at UAE customs, and we ship pre-marked stock so the receiver does not have to re-treat at destination.

Decision rules — which product fits which Abu Dhabi load

A practical decision matrix for buyers in the emirate:

  1. If you are crating standard industrial machinery, valves, pumps, or spare parts for ADNOC vendors, fabrication shops, or general KIZAD manufacturers — our MR-packing-grade plywood at 12 mm to 18 mm is the right specification. Cost-efficient, stable, ISPM-compliant.
  2. If the cargo is premium, high-visibility, or going to a customer-sensitive end recipient — request Okoume face veneer on the same MR-packing or BWR core. The finish reads cleaner and resists denting better in long sea moves.
  3. If you are buying for construction shuttering on Abu Dhabi infra and real-estate projects — that is shuttering ply, a separate SKU with a phenolic film face. It is the correct call for repeated concrete pours; using packing-grade for shuttering wastes money and breaks faster.
  4. If you need raw sawn timber or jackwood / rubberwood runners for crate cleats, dunnage, or pallet stringers — we ship sawn timber alongside the ply on the same container.

FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15-treated plywood and crates to Abu Dhabi?

Yes. Built-up crates and pallets ship with ISPM-15 heat-treatment marking from origin. Sheet plywood does not require the mark, but any solid-wood components do.

What is the minimum order quantity for Abu Dhabi shipments?

FCL is the practical floor — one 20 ft container of plywood is the smallest economic move because of fixed sea-freight and customs handling costs. For built-up crates or pallets we can mix SKUs inside one container.

Can you deliver to KIZAD or Mussafah directly, or only to the port?

Standard quotation is CIF or FOB to the named UAE port. Inland delivery to KIZAD, ICAD, or Mussafah is arranged on request through a local clearing agent — we can recommend operators we have worked with on the lane.

Request a quote

If you are sourcing packing-grade plywood, Okoume-faced ply, shuttering ply, crates, pallets, or container flooring for an Abu Dhabi operation, the fastest way to get a workable price is to send us your specification — thickness, size, monthly volume, delivery port. The Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing out of Perumbavoor since 1986, with 40+ years of group operations behind the supply chain into the GCC, and we quote with the actual loading plan in mind, not a generic per-sheet rate. Request a quote with your bill of materials, or browse our resource hub for plywood selection guides, ISPM-15 background, and packing-case specification notes before you brief us.

title: Plywood Supply to Dubai | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Containers land at Jebel Ali, get broken down and repacked for Africa, the Gulf and the CIS — and every repack needs fresh crates, pallets and dunnage. We.
html:
Why Dubai's industrial belt drives steady plywood demand

Dubai is not really a manufacturing city in the traditional sense — it is a re-export consolidation hub. Containers land at Jebel Ali, get broken down, sorted, repacked and shipped out again to Africa, the wider GCC, the Levant and the CIS. That repackaging step is where plywood enters the picture. Electronics from East Asia, textiles from the subcontinent, automotive spares from Europe and project cargo from across the Gulf all need fresh crates, pallets and dunnage before they leave the free zone. Add to that the steady tempo of construction across new districts and the result is a city that absorbs a wide spread of packing-grade plywood, ISPM-15 stamped pallets and shuttering ply month after month. For exporters in Kerala, Dubai is one of the most operationally clean export lanes in the GCC — a short sea leg, predictable port handling and a customer base that thinks in containers rather than truckloads.

The Dubai industrial picture

Most of the wood-packaging demand sits inside a handful of well-defined belts on the western and southern edge of the emirate. Understanding which belt a buyer sits in usually tells you what kind of crate they need.

Jebel Ali Port and JAFZA

Jebel Ali is the largest man-made harbour in the world and the anchor of the Jebel Ali Free Zone (JAFZA). Re-export consolidators, regional distribution centres for electronics brands and bonded warehouses that re-bundle textile shipments are concentrated here. The packing requirement is typically light to medium-duty crates and four-way pallets, used inside containers — so 12mm and 15mm packing-grade plywood with planed softwood framing tends to dominate.

Dubai Industrial City (DIC)

South of Jebel Ali, Dubai Industrial City houses heavier-engineering tenants — machinery assembly, building-materials processing, food and beverage. Here the typical job is a heavy-duty export crate for a single machine or assembled unit, often 18mm packing-grade plywood with hardwood skids, bolt-down corner cleats and silica-gel dunnage.

Dubai Aviation City and DWC

Around Al Maktoum International Airport, the demand profile shifts to aerospace MRO crates and high-value airfreight packing. These are smaller boxes but with tighter tolerance requirements — flat, void-free panels matter more than raw load rating.

Dubai Investments Park and Al Quoz

The interior industrial estates — Dubai Investments Park (DIP) and the older Al Quoz cluster — handle a mix of joinery, furniture and fit-out work for the construction sector. This is where shuttering ply and sawn timber lots tend to land alongside the packing-grade range.

What we ship into Dubai

The Cochin Wood group has been in Perumbavoor since 1986, and over forty years of group manufacturing has shaped a fairly tight, repeatable product range for GCC re-export work. We do not try to be everything for everyone — we ship what the Dubai sector actually consumes, in container quantities.

Packing-grade plywood

Our staple for Dubai is commercial / rubberwood packing-grade plywood, supplied ex-factory in 8x4 ft sheets across the 6mm-to-18mm range. For JAFZA consolidators the workhorse thicknesses are 12mm (light crate walls), 15mm (medium crates, pallet decks) and 18mm (heavy-duty bases and skids). Sheets are supplied with a flat lay, square edges, and a tolerance of "minus" on stated thickness — the same convention international packers expect.

Crate construction

Where we are quoted on finished crates rather than sheets, we build to ISPM-15 standards using heat-treated softwood framing and hardwood runners, with packing-grade plywood panels. Standard configurations include collapsible export crates, closed boarded cases for sensitive equipment, and open-top skidded bases for heavy machinery. Crate fabrication on this lane is usually buyer-spec — sizes, IPPC marking, lashing points and bolted versus nailed corners all get confirmed in the PI stage.

Pallets

Four-way ISPM-15 stamped pallets in standard 1200x1000 and 1200x800 footprints, plus custom oversize bases for project cargo. Block pallets when the buyer is feeding automated handling lines; stringer pallets for general repack work.

Container flooring

For consolidators rebuilding worn FCL boxes, we supply container-flooring grade plywood in 28mm thickness, hardwood-faced, with the standard 19-ply construction the lane expects.

Okoume and shuttering ply

Okoume veneer is offered as a face option on the MR-Packing and BWR grades when the buyer needs a brighter, smoother finish — typical for re-export crates carrying branded electronics where the outer case is visible at retail. Shuttering ply is supplied to construction and fit-out buyers in DIP and Al Quoz on standalone orders.

Kochi-to-Dubai logistics

The lane economics on this corridor are exceptionally clean, which is why GCC buyers tend to default to South Indian supply over longer routes.

LegDetail
Origin portCochin Port (Vallarpadam ICTT)
Destination portJebel Ali, Dubai
Sea distance~1,900 nautical miles
Typical transit3-5 days sailing
Trucking, factory to port2-3 hours, Perumbavoor to ICTT
Container modesFCL 20' and 40' HC; LCL via Cochin consolidators
Standard IncotermsFOB Cochin or CIF Jebel Ali

For a 40' HC load of 18mm packing-grade plywood the typical sheet count fits cleanly within payload limits, and the short sea leg keeps demurrage risk low at the Dubai end. ISPM-15 compliance is non-negotiable on this lane — every crate, pallet and dunnage piece carries an IPPC mark traceable back to the treatment facility.

Decision rules — which grade fits which Dubai job

A few practical rules we apply when scoping a Dubai order.

  • JAFZA consolidation re-pack — default to 12mm or 15mm commercial packing-grade. Volume is high, individual crates are light, the panel does not need to be exhibition-quality.
  • Heavy machinery export from DIC — 18mm packing-grade panels on hardwood-skidded bases. If the consignment is high value or going onward to a humid African destination, specify BWR packing.
  • Branded electronics or premium textile re-export — Okoume face on MR-Packing core. The brighter surface and clean grain matter to the receiving warehouse.
  • Construction fit-out in DIP or Al Quoz — shuttering ply or BWR hardwood plywood per the architect's spec; quoted as a separate line from the packing range.
  • Container floor refurbishment — 28mm hardwood-faced flooring ply only; nothing thinner survives a forklift cycle.

FAQ — common questions from Dubai buyers

Do you supply ISPM-15 stamped pallets and crates?

Yes. All wood-packaging material we ship out of Cochin Port for the GCC carries IPPC marking from a registered heat-treatment facility, with treatment certificates attached to the shipping documents.

What is the minimum order quantity for FCL to Jebel Ali?

We quote in container loads. A 40' HC is the standard unit; mixed loads (packing-grade sheets plus pre-built pallets, for example) are routine. Sub-container quantities ship LCL through Cochin consolidators on agreed lead times.

How do you handle thickness tolerance and moisture content for Gulf-climate transit?

Packing-grade is supplied minus on stated thickness per the standard packing convention. Moisture content is held to the range needed for stable transit through Gulf humidity, and we kiln-dry sawn timber lots before crate fabrication when buyer specs call for it.

Request a quote

If you are sourcing packing-grade plywood, ISPM-15 pallets, export crates or container flooring for a Dubai operation, we can usually quote on a finished container within 24-48 hours of receiving a clean spec — thickness, sheet count or crate dimensions, ISPM marking requirement, port of discharge and Incoterm. Request a quote with your packing list and we will come back with FOB Cochin and CIF Jebel Ali numbers, plus a realistic loading-plan. For background on the grades, ISPM-15 treatment and crate-construction conventions, the resource hub carries the technical reference notes we share with buyers during the spec stage.

title: Plywood Supply to Sharjah | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Sharjah is where the UAE does its welding, machining and re-exporting — cargo that needs structural packing able to survive a 1,900-mile sea leg without flex.
html:
Sharjah is the workhorse of the UAE economy. While Dubai handles the headlines, Sharjah's industrial belt does the actual welding, machining, fabricating and re-exporting. That has a direct consequence for anyone shipping plywood across the Arabian Sea: the emirate's buyers do not want decorative panels or interior-grade boards. They want structural packing material that can take a CNC-machined oil-and-gas valve assembly through Khalid Port, onto a flat-rack, and across two oceans without flex or splinter. The Cochin Wood group has been supplying exactly that profile of material from Perumbavoor since 1986, and Sharjah is one of the more natural GCC destinations for our packing line because the buying patterns there match what our Kerala operations are tuned to produce.

Why Sharjah's industrial map matters for plywood

Most plywood suppliers chase the Dubai construction story. Sharjah is a different conversation. The emirate is built around manufacturing, re-export and heavy industry, which means crates, pallets and containerised cargo move in volume every day. If you are sourcing packing material for a Sharjah-based fabricator or a Free Zone trader, the question is rarely "what looks good" — it is "what survives a 1900 nautical mile sea leg plus inland trucking at the receiving end".

The free zones and estates that drive demand

  • Hamriyah Free Zone (HFZ) — anchored around the Hamriyah Port complex on the Arabian Gulf coast. Heavy concentration of oil-and-gas service companies, steel fabrication, petrochemical packaging and maritime supply. Crates here tend to be heavy, dimensional, and built around irregular equipment shapes.
  • Sharjah Airport International Free Zone (SAIF Zone) — adjacent to Sharjah International Airport. Skews toward electronics, aerospace components, automotive parts and lighter precision manufacturing. Smaller, cleaner crates and a lot of export-ready pallets.
  • Sharjah Industrial Areas 1 through 18 — the older industrial estates around Al Sajaa and Industrial Area 13, 17 and 18 where local manufacturing, joinery, metal fabrication and SME-scale exporters operate.
  • Hamriyah Port and Khalid Port — Khalid Port handles general cargo and container traffic close to Sharjah city; Hamriyah Port handles bulk, project and oil-and-gas cargo further north. Most plywood-packed shipments in and out flow through one of these two gateways.

That mix — heavy oil-and-gas equipment, light electronics, SME manufacturing and pure re-export traders — drives a very specific demand pattern for packing-grade plywood.

What we ship into Sharjah

The Cochin Wood group's packing line is built around the realities of export crating, not interior carpentry. Below are the products that move most consistently into Sharjah.

Packing-grade plywood (commercial / rubberwood core)

This is the workhorse SKU. Hardwood-faced, rubberwood or mixed-hardwood core, glued for moisture tolerance. We supply the standard packing thicknesses — 6mm, 8mm, 10mm, 12mm, 15mm, 16mm and 18mm — in 8x4 sheets with options for cut sizes if your packing line wants pre-sized panels to reduce on-site waste. For Sharjah-bound consignments the 12mm and 15mm move most because they balance weight against rigidity for typical Free Zone crate sizes.

Okoume-faced plywood

Where the cargo is high-value — precision oil-and-gas instrumentation, aerospace components, automotive prototypes — buyers often specify Okoume face veneer on a packing-grade core. Cleaner face, lighter colour, better appearance for end-customer inspection at destination. We position Okoume as a face-veneer choice on top of the packing-grade core, not as a separate grade.

Heavy export crates and skids

We supply complete crates built to the cargo, not generic boxes. For Sharjah's oil-and-gas vertical that usually means heavy-duty bolted crates with internal bracing, ISPM-15 heat-treated dunnage, and skid bases sized for forklift entry from the long side. Wall panels are typically 12mm or 15mm packing-grade plywood depending on cargo weight.

Pallets

We build to the receiver's specification. Standard sizes that move into Sharjah include:

Pallet typeDimensions (mm)Typical use
Euro1200 x 800European-bound re-export from Sharjah Free Zones
ISO standard1200 x 1000General cargo, containerised LCL
GCC heavy1100 x 1100Intra-GCC trucking, heavy machinery
CustomAs specifiedOil-and-gas project cargo, irregular loads

Container flooring

For exporters refurbishing or fitting out containers, we supply 28mm container-grade flooring panels in standard 20ft and 40ft sizes. This is a smaller line for us than crates and pallets, but it does move into Sharjah's container repair workshops.

Kochi to Sharjah — the route economics

Cochin Port to Khalid Port (Sharjah) is roughly 1900 nautical miles. Direct container services typically deliver in 7 to 10 days port-to-port, with weekly to fortnightly sailings depending on the line. Transhipment via Jebel Ali adds 2 to 4 days. For most packing-grade plywood orders the supply chain runs Perumbavoor factory → road haulage to Cochin Port → FCL or LCL container → Khalid Port → consignee yard in Hamriyah, SAIF Zone or Industrial Area.

A 20ft FCL holds roughly 550 to 600 sheets of 18mm packing ply, or proportionally more of thinner sheets. For heavy oil-and-gas buyers we often consolidate plywood sheets together with pre-built crate kits in the same container — the kit panels nest flat against the sheet stack and the receiving site assembles on demand.

Choosing the right grade for the load

A practical decision rule for Sharjah-bound packing:

  • Standard packing-grade (commercial / rubberwood core, 12-15mm) — default for general fabricated steel, machinery sub-assemblies, automotive parts, electrical panels.
  • Okoume-faced packing-grade (12-15mm) — when the cargo is high-value or when destination buyers inspect crates at receipt. Cleaner appearance, lighter colour.
  • Shuttering plywood — relevant only if you are pouring concrete on site (Sharjah construction contractors). Not a packing product.
  • Sawn hardwood (jackwood, rubberwood runners) — for skid bases, framing members, dunnage. Often shipped alongside the plywood load.

Common questions from Sharjah buyers

Do you supply ISPM-15 compliant material?

Yes. All wooden packaging components destined for export — dunnage, pallet runners, crate framing — are heat-treated and stamped to ISPM-15. Plywood itself is exempt from ISPM-15 because it is a manufactured wood product, but we provide the documentation that compliance officers in Sharjah typically ask for.

What is the minimum order to make a Kochi-Sharjah shipment economical?

FCL economics start to make sense at around half a 20ft container — roughly 250 to 300 sheets equivalent. Below that, LCL consolidation works but transit lengthens and per-sheet cost rises. We can quote both options once you share the load profile.

Can you ship cut-to-size panels instead of full sheets?

Yes. For larger orders we run cut lists in Perumbavoor and ship pre-sized panels stacked and strapped. This reduces on-site cutting waste in your Sharjah facility and cuts down on labour at the receiving end.

Get a quote

If you are sourcing packing plywood, crates, pallets or container flooring into Hamriyah, SAIF Zone or any of the Sharjah industrial estates, share your thickness, quantity, delivery window and consignee location. We can usually return a CIF Khalid Port quotation within one working day. Request a quote with your specs, or browse the resource hub for grade comparisons, crate-build notes and packing references before you finalise the order.

title: Plywood Supply to Jebel Ali Free Zone | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, export crates and pallets that Jebel Ali Free Zone runs on, built for the repeat sea-freight cycles of a re-export.
html:
Why Jebel Ali Free Zone drives a specific kind of plywood demand

Jebel Ali Free Zone (Jafza) is not a normal industrial estate. It sits directly against the world's ninth-largest container port, hosts roughly 8,500 companies including more than 130 of the Fortune 500, and operates as a re-export hub where cargo lands from one origin, gets reworked, and leaves for a different destination. That single fact — re-export consolidation — shapes the plywood that moves into the zone. Boxes get opened, contents get repacked into multi-SKU export crates, and pallets get rebuilt for onward sea or air movement. Compared with a normal manufacturing zone where packaging is a one-shot job, Jafza consumes packing-grade plywood, crate sets and pallets in cycles, often for shipments that need to clear ISPM-15 phytosanitary rules on the outbound leg.

The Jafza industrial picture: who actually buys plywood

Jafza's footprint runs across the South, North, and Jafza One/Convention precincts, with dedicated zones for heavy industry near the port and lighter assembly and logistics sheds inland. The plywood-relevant clusters fall into a few buckets, and each consumes a slightly different specification.

Re-export and consolidation logistics

The largest plywood draw is the freight-forwarding and 3PL belt — companies running consolidation warehouses that break bulk from one container and rebuild multi-consignee export crates. They want consistent 12mm and 15mm sheets for case walls, 18mm for floors and skids, and pallets to EUR or ISO 1200x1000 footprints. ISPM-15 marking is mandatory on outbound wood, so they prefer suppliers who can provide kiln-dried, heat-treated, and stamped material with paperwork.

Heavy engineering and oil & gas packaging

Jafza hosts manufacturing and service facilities for valves, pumps, drilling equipment, transformers and modular fabrication for oilfield and downstream sectors. These shipments need heavy-duty closed crates with internal bracing — typically 18mm packing-grade plywood walls, sawn-timber skids and steel-strap reinforcement. Loads often run 800 kg to several tonnes per case and travel to refineries and project sites across the GCC and East Africa.

Automotive parts and capital-equipment re-export

Aftermarket auto parts, machinery and capital equipment flowing from East Asia to Africa frequently transit Jafza for re-labelling and split shipments. The packaging profile here is mid-weight crates and slip-sheet pallets, with a clear preference for clean-faced packing ply that takes a stencil cleanly.

FMCG, food and chemical regional distribution

The food and chemical distribution belt runs lighter packing — block pallets, four-way entry pallets, and corner protection — but at very high pallet volumes per month. Standardisation matters more than premium grade.

Project cargo and modular construction

Pre-assembled MEP modules, switchgear and structural sub-assemblies leaving Jafza for KSA and African mega-projects need bespoke crates: oversized, often 6m+ long, with shuttering-grade ply for stiffness in skids and bottoms.

What Cochin Wood Industries ships to Jafza

The Cochin Wood group has been in Perumbavoor since 1986, and the packing-plywood range is built around the kind of repeat sea-freight cycles Jafza buyers run. Typical Jafza-bound product mix:

ProductTypical specWhere it fits in Jafza
Packing-grade plywood6mm-18mm, 8x4 sheets, MR/BWR glue line, hardwood faceCrate walls, lids, internal partitions for consolidation cases
Okoume-faced plywood12mm-18mm, smooth peelable face, BWR corePremium export crates, stencil-ready surfaces, electronics/aerospace
Shuttering ply12mm and 18mm film-faced, hardwood coreHeavy skids, project-cargo bottoms, repeatable construction modules
Pre-cut crate kitsCut-to-size sheet sets with skid lumberSame-day case assembly inside Jafza warehouses
Sawn timber for skids and runnersHardwood, kiln-dried, ISPM-15 readyBottom skids, dunnage, bracing under heavy loads
Container flooring panels28mm Apitong/Keruing equivalents on requestContainer repair lines and reefer rebuilds in Jafza's logistics belt

Pallet sizes that actually move

For Jafza, three pallet footprints cover most demand: EUR 1200x800, ISO 1200x1000 and the GCC-favoured 1100x1100. Group manufacturing covers heat-treated stringer and block pallets in all three, with hardwood deckboards rated for 1000-1500 kg dynamic load.

Kochi to Jebel Ali Free Zone: the route and the economics

The sea distance from Cochin Port to Jebel Ali runs roughly 1,900 nautical miles, with mainline transit time of 6-9 days depending on the carrier and rotation. Cochin is a direct-call port for several Middle East services, which keeps freight predictable and avoids the Colombo or Nhava Sheva transhipment penalty. For Jafza buyers, the practical implications are:

  • Order-to-arrival window: roughly 14-21 days from confirmed PO, factoring in factory cut, stuffing, port handover and ocean leg.
  • Container economics: a 20-foot container takes around 22-24 tonnes of packing-grade ply or roughly 600-650 sheets of 18mm; a 40-foot takes about double in volume but is weight-limited.
  • Customs and documentation: FOB Cochin or CFR Jebel Ali both work; ISPM-15 stamps and phytosanitary certification are issued at origin so the cargo clears straight into the zone.
  • Repeat-order cadence: for consolidation hubs running monthly cycles, splitting into a fortnightly 20-foot rhythm often beats single 40-footers on warehouse turn.

Perumbavoor sits about 35 km inland from Cochin Port, which keeps the road leg short and the loading-to-vessel timeline tight.

Decision rules: packing grade vs Okoume vs shuttering ply

Use packing-grade plywood when

The shipment is industrial cargo, the case is a one-time export, and the receiver cares about structural integrity more than face cosmetics. This is the default for most Jafza consolidation work.

Use Okoume-faced plywood when

The end customer is in electronics, aerospace, premium automotive or branded capital equipment, where the crate carries a stencilled identity and will be inspected on arrival. The smoother face also helps when the case will be photographed for shipment records.

Use shuttering ply when

The load runs over 1.5 tonnes per crate, the skid takes point loading from a forklift, or the case has to survive multiple trans-shipments. Shuttering-grade stiffness reduces flex failures on the bottom panel.

FAQ

Can you supply ISPM-15 heat-treated material with documentation?

Yes. All sawn timber and pallet components used for Jafza-bound consignments can be supplied kiln-dried, heat-treated and stamped per ISPM-15. Phytosanitary certificates are arranged through Cochin Port at origin.

What's the minimum order that makes sense for a Jafza buyer?

A 20-foot container is the practical floor for sea freight. Below that, the per-sheet landed cost rises sharply because of fixed origin charges. Buyers running small monthly volumes are usually better off bundling a fortnightly 20-footer than chasing LCL.

Do you do cut-to-size kits, or only standard 8x4 sheets?

Both. Standard 8x4 sheets at 6mm-18mm are the bread-and-butter line. For consolidation hubs that want to skip the in-warehouse cutting step, the group also pre-cuts sheets and bundles them with skid lumber as crate kits keyed to the buyer's case sizes.

Request a Jafza quote

For Jafza-bound enquiries, the fastest path to a working price is a short brief covering thickness mix, monthly sheet or pallet volume, target Incoterm and any ISPM-15 paperwork needs. From there a working quote and indicative stuffing plan come back within a business day. Request a quote with your spec, or browse the resource hub for crate-design and packing-plywood reference notes before you brief us.

title: Plywood Supply to Ras Al Khaimah | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade and Okoume-faced plywood, export crates and pallets that move RAK's industrial output. Our group has manufactured in.
html:
Why Ras Al Khaimah Sits on Our Export Radar

Ras Al Khaimah is the northernmost of the seven emirates, and unlike the trading-and-services skew of Dubai or the oil-and-government skew of Abu Dhabi, RAK runs on heavy industry. Tile kilns, glass furnaces, pharmaceutical clean-rooms, cement, and aluminium fabrication all sit inside a compact industrial belt that ships finished goods out of Saqr Port and Mina Al Arab, plus by road to Jebel Ali for deep-sea legs. Every one of those export streams needs export-grade wood packaging — and most of the raw plywood, sawn timber, and crating consumables travel into the Gulf from South India. Kochi is about 2,000 nautical miles from Mina Saqr, which makes the Cochin Wood group a logical supply node for buyers who want consistent plywood specs, dependable transit, and pricing that doesn't carry a Jebel Ali warehousing markup.

The Ras Al Khaimah Industrial Picture

RAK's industrial base is concentrated across a handful of free zones and estates that are each tuned to a particular kind of cargo. Knowing where a customer sits inside this map usually tells us what their packaging actually needs to survive.

RAKEZ and the RAK Free Trade Zone

RAK Economic Zone — formed by the merger of RAK Free Trade Zone and RAK Investment Authority — clusters thousands of light-to-heavy manufacturers across Al Ghail, Al Hamra, Al Hulaila, and Al Nakheel zones. The Al Ghail Industrial Park is the heaviest of the four, with steel, building materials, and large-format equipment makers. Al Hamra leans toward food, FMCG, and lighter industrial. Al Hulaila is the deep-water-adjacent industrial city — closest to Saqr Port and home to glass and ceramic exporters.

Ceramics, Glass, and Cement Belt

RAK Ceramics, one of the world's largest tile and sanitaryware producers, operates out of Al Jazeera Al Hamra and exports into more than 150 countries — a single 40-foot container of porcelain tiles can carry 22 to 26 tonnes, which puts serious load on the pallet, the bottom slats, and the container floor. Add to that RAK Glass, Arabian Glass, and several cement and tile-adhesive plants, and you have a steady demand for heavy-duty wooden crating and certified pallets.

Pharmaceuticals and Specialty Chemicals

Julphar (Gulf Pharmaceutical Industries) — one of the largest generic-drug manufacturers in the MENA region — anchors the pharma cluster, with smaller API and packaging firms around it. Pharma exports are lighter per unit than tiles, but the packaging spec is tighter: temperature-stable, fumigation-compliant, dimensionally accurate wooden cases and pallets, often ISPM 15 stamped before they leave the gate.

What We Ship to Ras Al Khaimah

RAK buyers usually consolidate four product families with us into one container — packing-grade plywood for in-house case shops, ready-built export crates, pallets, and container-floor boards for re-flooring tired boxes.

Packing-Grade Plywood — Sheet Specs

The mainline product is our MR-Packing grade — hardwood core, phenol-bonded, two-time hot-press, calibrated thickness, and either a commercial face or an Okoume face when the customer wants a cleaner finished look. Standard supply thicknesses are 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 15, 16, and 18 mm. The 8 mm and 12 mm boards do the heaviest lifting — 8 mm for crate side panels and lids, 12 mm for bottom skids, blocking, and pallet decks. Sheets are cut to 8x4, 7x4, 6x4 ft, or to any custom dimension that loads tighter into a 20-foot box.

Wooden Crates and Cases

For RAK ceramic, glass, and engineering exporters, we build cases two ways — flat-pack (knocked-down) for buyers who reassemble at their own packing yard, and pre-assembled for buyers who load directly. Construction is plywood-skinned softwood or hardwood framing with corner gussets, with bottom skids sized for the forklift fleet on site. All export-bound timber is ISPM 15 heat-treated and stamp-marked at source, so cases clear UAE customs and onward re-export without quarantine holds.

Pallets

The standard sizes that move on the Kochi–RAK lane are 1200x1000 mm (the European/UAE generic), 1200x800 mm (Euro), and 1100x1100 mm (Asian, for buyers cross-shipping into Far East). Four-way entry with stringer or block construction, ISPM 15 treated, dynamic load rating typically 1.5 to 2 tonnes for the standard build and 3 tonnes for the heavy-duty tile variant.

Container Flooring

RAK has a healthy population of refurbished 20- and 40-foot containers used as in-yard storage and for short-haul GCC moves. We ship 28 mm container-floor plywood — high-density, 14-ply construction, hot-pressed, oil-coated, in the standard 2400 x 1160 mm board — for buyers who run their own container refurb lines.

Kochi-to-Ras Al Khaimah Logistics

The sea leg runs Cochin Port (Vallarpadam ICTT) to either Mina Saqr in RAK directly, or Jebel Ali with a feeder/road leg up the E311 to RAK — distance is roughly 2,000 nautical miles, transit 7 to 10 days depending on the rotation. Direct calls to Saqr are less frequent than Jebel Ali, so most of our consolidated containers route via Jebel Ali and then ride 110 km up the highway. A 20-foot box typically carries 6 to 7 tonnes of mixed plywood and packaging consumables; a 40-foot HC takes 12 to 14 tonnes of sheet stock or about 18 to 22 fully built export crates broken down flat. We quote FOB Cochin as standard; CFR Mina Saqr or CFR Jebel Ali on request.

Decision Rules — Which Grade for Which Load

Load typeRecommended gradeWhy
Ceramic tile export crates (heavy, abrasive)MR-Packing 12 mm bottoms, 8 mm sidesCost-controlled, takes the abrasion, ISPM 15 compliant
Glass and mirror cratesMR-Packing 12 mm with Okoume faceSmoother face reduces edge chipping; cleaner cosmetic for the buyer
Pharma export casesBWR-grade with Okoume faceTighter calibration, cleaner appearance, better dimensional stability
Standby formwork and on-site shutteringFilm-faced shuttering ply 12 or 18 mmRepeated pour cycles, water-resistant film face
Container refurb decking28 mm container-floor plywoodEngineered specifically for in-container abrasion and forklift wheel loads

Okoume is a face-veneer option on the MR-Packing and BWR grades, not a separate grade — buyers who want the lighter, cleaner look spec it when finished-appearance matters at the receiving end.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do you handle ISPM 15 certification?

Yes. All export timber and plywood-clad cases bound for the UAE leave the yard heat-treated and stamp-marked under registered ISPM 15 facilities. The stamp is visible on every skid and pallet — buyers can verify on receipt.

What is the minimum order for direct Kochi-to-RAK supply?

One 20-foot container is the practical floor for direct supply. Smaller volumes go LCL via consolidators into Jebel Ali, then truck up to RAK — slower and higher per-tonne cost, but it works for trial orders. For pallets and ready-built crates, we quote both modes.

How are payments structured for first-time UAE buyers?

The standard pattern is 30 percent advance on PI confirmation and 70 percent against scanned shipping documents before BL release. Repeat buyers move to LC at sight or DA terms once the relationship is established. Pricing is FOB Cochin in USD or INR depending on buyer preference.

Get a Quote for Your Ras Al Khaimah Shipment

If you are sourcing plywood, crates, pallets, or container flooring into RAKEZ, Al Hamra, Al Hulaila, or anywhere across the RAK industrial belt, send us the spec — thicknesses, sizes, monthly volume, and delivery point — and we will revert with a PI inside one working day. Cutting lists, drawings, and stamp samples are welcome with the enquiry. Start at our request a quote page, or browse the technical write-ups, grade comparisons, and packing case guides at the resource hub first if you want to fix the spec before pricing. The Cochin Wood group has been packing and shipping wood-based products out of Perumbavoor since 1986, and the export desk runs the GCC lane every week — drop your enquiry and we will move quickly.

title: Plywood Supply to Dammam | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade and Okoume-faced plywood, heavy-duty crates and pallets that move oil, gas and petrochem cargo through the Eastern Province.
html:
Dammam runs on heavy iron. Saudi Aramco's headquarters sits in Dhahran a few minutes inland, the Jubail petrochemical complex anchors the coast an hour north, and King Abdulaziz Port at Dammam handles the largest container throughput in the Arabian Gulf. Every valve skid, compressor module, pre-fabricated pipe spool, and spare-parts kit that moves into or out of this belt needs wooden packaging that survives sea salt, 48°C summers, and rough port handling. That is the conversation we have with packers and freight forwarders in the Eastern Province almost every week, and it is the reason the Cochin Wood group has built a steady FCL lane out of Kochi to Dammam.

The Dammam industrial picture

"Dammam" in shipping documents usually means one of four overlapping zones. Understanding which one a consignment is headed to changes the packing brief.

Dammam's industrial belts at a glance

  • Dammam 2nd & 3rd Industrial Cities (MODON) — metal fabrication, engineering workshops, MRO yards, electrical panel builders. Lots of mid-weight crates (200–2,000 kg) headed out as project cargo.
  • Jubail Industrial City (Jubail 1 & 2) — SABIC, Saudi Kayan, Petro Rabigh feed lines. Heavy petrochem equipment, catalyst drums, exchanger bundles. Crates here are big, weatherproof, and frequently ISPM-15 stamped for re-export.
  • Dhahran Techno Valley & Aramco service belt — oilfield services (drill bits, wireline tools, downhole equipment) shipped in returnable plywood cases that need to handle multiple field trips.
  • King Abdulaziz Port logistics zone — consolidators and 3PLs running container freight stations. Their daily diet is pallets, dunnage, container flooring for re-conditioning, and packing-grade ply by the bundle.

The sectors driving plywood demand are therefore upstream oil & gas services, downstream petrochem maintenance, power transformer movements between Aramco and contractor yards, and a healthy steady stream of food, beverage and white-goods crating linked to the broader Eastern Province consumer economy.

What we ship to Dammam

Most Dammam orders combine three SKUs in one container: sheet plywood for the buyer's own crate shop, ready-made crates for high-value modules, and pallets or container-flooring boards. A typical container looks like this:

ItemCommon spec to DammamUse case
Packing-grade plywood12 mm / 15 mm / 18 mm, 8×4 ft, MR or BWR glueCrate panels, case linings, void fillers
Heavy-duty crates18 mm BWR shell on 75×75 mm hardwood frame, ISPM-15 heat-treatedCompressor skids, valve modules, transformer parts
Pallets1200×1000 mm (Euro-ish) and 1100×1100 mm; 4-way entryGeneral cargo, food & beverage, spare parts kits
Container flooring boards28 mm shuttering ply, phenolic film-facedRe-flooring 20'/40' boxes used by Aramco contractors
Okoume plywood9 mm / 12 mm / 18 mm BWR, light-coloured faceHigh-end exhibition crates, instrumentation packing

Crate construction notes

For Jubail-bound petrochem cargo we default to an 18 mm BWR-glued plywood shell on a 75×75 mm or 100×100 mm hardwood frame, corner-braced, with skid runners sized for the buyer's forklift truck (usually 7 t–15 t at Eastern Province yards). Every batten and runner is ISPM-15 heat-treated and stamped at source so the crate clears Saudi customs and is re-exportable to Bahrain, UAE, or onward to Europe without a second fumigation cycle.

Pallet sizes that actually move in Dammam

Eastern Province buyers split roughly between the 1200×1000 mm footprint (used by GCC retail and most consumer-goods distributors) and the 1100×1100 mm "Aramco-friendly" size that fits oilfield kit boxes and warehouse racks at contractor yards. We build both as 4-way-entry with a notched bottom deck so a hand pallet truck can pick from any side — small detail, but it is the one buyers call out most often.

Kochi to Dammam: the logistics picture

The sea distance from Cochin Port (Kochi) to King Abdulaziz Port (Dammam) via the Arabian Sea, Gulf of Oman, Strait of Hormuz and the Persian Gulf is roughly 1,900 nautical miles. Direct services are rare; almost all FCL bookings transship at Jebel Ali (UAE), and increasingly at Khalifa Port (Abu Dhabi) or Hamad (Qatar).

  • Port-to-port transit: typically 14–21 days from Cochin to Dammam depending on the transshipment window at Jebel Ali.
  • Door-to-port lead time from our factory cluster in Perumbavoor: 3–4 days for inland trucking to Cochin Port + customs and stuffing.
  • Realistic total lead time: plan 4–5 weeks from PO to vessel arrival at Dammam, plus the buyer's customs clearance.
  • Documentation: commercial invoice, packing list, Certificate of Origin, ISPM-15 declaration for wood packaging, and any SASO / SABER paperwork the buyer needs at the Saudi end.

One operational note: for Aramco-linked contractors we keep the ISPM-15 stamp visible on at least two faces of every crate and every pallet stringer. Inspectors at King Abdulaziz Port reject loads where the heat-treatment mark is on a hidden surface, even if the paperwork is in order.

Which grade for which Dammam job?

A simple decision rule we share with first-time Dammam buyers:

  1. Standard MR packing-grade plywood — default for inland-handled cargo, short-cycle crates, dunnage and case linings. Lowest landed cost.
  2. BWR packing-grade plywood — the right call for anything moving by sea container to the humid Gulf coast, anything that may sit at a port yard, and any returnable case used by oilfield services that bounces between Aramco fields and contractor workshops.
  3. Okoume-faced BWR plywood — chosen by exhibition logistics, instrumentation packers, and downstream petrochem buyers who want a clean light-coloured face on crates that go into client-facing locations. Higher price point; the face holds up better through repeat handling.
  4. Phenolic shuttering ply (film-faced) — used in Dammam mainly for container re-flooring and for crate floor panels under very heavy point loads (skid-mounted compressors, transformers).

Note on terminology: Okoume in our catalogue refers to the face veneer applied to a BWR-glued plywood, not a separate grade. Specify the glue (MR or BWR), the face veneer (commercial, Okoume or Gurjan), and the thickness. Our team can match a sample if the buyer is replacing an existing import line.

FAQ — Dammam buyers

Do you provide ISPM-15 certification with every shipment?

Yes. All wood packaging headed to Saudi Arabia is heat-treated and stamped at source, and the ISPM-15 declaration is included in the shipping documents. Stamps are placed on visible faces of crates, pallets and dunnage.

Can you ship LCL for trial orders?

For first-time Dammam buyers we routinely ship LCL through Jebel Ali consolidation for trial loads of 5–10 cbm. Once specs are locked, most buyers move to 20'/40' FCL on a recurring lane.

What is the minimum order quantity?

For plain sheet plywood, one 20' FCL (roughly 320–360 sheets of 18 mm depending on configuration) is the practical minimum. For ready-made crates and pallets we quote either by unit count or by the container, depending on what the buyer's project needs.

Get a Dammam quote

If you handle packing or freight for a Dammam, Jubail or Dhahran consignor and want a working quote — sheet ply, crates, pallets, container flooring, or a mixed load — share thickness, glue, sizes, monthly volume and your delivery point. We will price ex-Kochi (FOB) and CFR Dammam through the standard Jebel Ali transship, both in one PI. Request a quote with your spec sheet, or browse our resource hub for ISPM-15 notes, plywood grade guides and packing-case sizing tables before you write the RFQ. The Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing and supplying packing plywood out of Perumbavoor since 1986, and Dammam is one of the GCC lanes we plan around every month.

title: Plywood Supply to Jeddah | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring that Jeddah's industrial belts ship across the GCC, East Africa and Europe.
html:
Why the Jeddah industrial belt matters for a Kochi plywood shipper

Jeddah is the trade lung of the Red Sea. Jeddah Islamic Port handles the majority of Saudi Arabia's non-oil container traffic, King Abdullah Economic City (KAEC) is pulling new light-manufacturing and logistics tenants up the coast at Rabigh, and the older industrial zones around Jeddah second, third and Phase 4 are still where most of the city's machinery, electrical and consumer-goods packing actually happens. From a plywood supplier's point of view that mix means a steady, year-round pull for export-grade packing material: crates for re-export machinery, pallets for cross-dock warehousing, and dunnage and container flooring for the long Red Sea voyages onward to East Africa, the Levant and Europe. Kochi sits in a useful position to feed that demand — roughly 2,200 nautical miles to Jeddah, on a direct east–west route, with no Suez transit and no Cape-of-Good-Hope detour. That is the practical case for shipping plywood west out of Cochin Port, and it is the lane the Cochin Wood group has been working into for several years now.

The Jeddah industrial picture

Buyers in Jeddah do not all sit in one zone. When we plan a quote we usually segment the city into four belts, because each one prefers a slightly different specification of plywood.

Jeddah Industrial City (Phases 1 through 4)

The old industrial city south-east of the airport — Phase 1, 2, 3 and the newer Phase 4 — is the dense cluster. Plastics, food processing, light electrical, building materials and a lot of contract packing units sit here. Demand is mostly 12 mm and 18 mm packing-grade plywood for crate walls, plus EUR-footprint pallets for warehouse turn.

King Abdullah Economic City (KAEC) and Rabigh

KAEC, about 100 km north of Jeddah, is the newer story. Industrial Valley tenants — automotive parts, food, pharma packaging, building products — typically want heat-treated ISPM-15 crates because their finished goods re-export across the GCC and to Europe. Spec creep here is real: buyers ask for IPPC-stamped lumber, sealed crate seams, and sometimes okoume-faced panels for higher-end consumer goods.

Jeddah Islamic Port re-export shed

Inside and immediately around the port, freight forwarders and consolidators run shed-level packing operations: stuffing, re-crating, and container-load-out of transit cargo. The pull here is fast — they want pallets and crate blanks available off-the-shelf, not built to order.

Makkah construction and project sites

Up the Haramain corridor toward Makkah, the demand is different again. Project sites pull shuttering plywood for concrete work — film-faced 18 mm sheets, multiple re-use cycles, sometimes specified by main contractors with European brand habits. We typically quote film-faced shuttering ply with a hardwood core for this segment, not packing grade.

What we ship to Jeddah

The Cochin Wood group has been operating out of Perumbavoor since 1986 — about forty years of group manufacturing in Kerala's plywood cluster — and the bulk of our Jeddah-bound volume falls into four product families. Specifications below are typical; final quote depends on container plan, load weight and ISPM-15 status.

Packing-grade plywood (the workhorse)

BWR-bonded commercial and rubberwood plywood, 6 mm to 18 mm, in 8x4 sheets. The most common Jeddah ask is 12 mm for crate walls and 18 mm for top/bottom panels and skid boards. We supply in mill-pack bundles for container stuffing and can pre-cut to crate-blank sizes if the buyer's line is set up for that. Material is moisture-tolerant for the Red Sea voyage and the Jeddah summer.

Okoume plywood

Okoume-faced panels go out to KAEC and to a handful of premium consumer-goods packers in Jeddah Industrial. The pitch is the smoother face, the lighter weight (roughly 480–520 kg/m³ versus 600+ for hardwood) and the cleaner stencil/branding surface. We quote okoume only on request — it sits at a different price point and is not interchangeable with standard packing grade.

Wooden crates, cases and pallets

Built-to-spec ISPM-15 (HT-stamped) wooden crates and pallets, sized to either standard EUR (1200x800) or ISO (1200x1000) footprints. We can ship them as flat-pack crate kits to save freight if the Jeddah buyer has a re-assembly bench. Container loading capacity for flat-pack kits is roughly 2.5x to 3x the assembled-crate equivalent.

Container flooring and dunnage

Heavy-duty 18 mm and 21 mm plywood, hardwood-core, for re-flooring reefer and dry containers, and as load-spreading dunnage under heavy machinery. Same lane, same lead time — usually bundled into a mixed container with packing-grade sheets.

The Kochi-to-Jeddah lane

The logistics for this lane are clean. From Cochin Port (ICTT Vallarpadam) to Jeddah Islamic Port is approximately 2,200 nautical miles. Direct west-bound mainline services and a handful of feeder routings via Salalah or Jebel Ali run weekly. Sailing time is typically 9 to 14 days door-to-port depending on the service and any transhipment leg. No Suez transit is involved, which keeps the cost and the risk profile lower than the Med routes.

From our side, inland trucking from Perumbavoor to Vallarpadam runs about 30 km — one of the shorter factory-to-gate hauls in India for an export lane this size. That short pre-carriage matters: it shrinks demurrage exposure and makes it practical to load on the same day the BL date is confirmed. For full-container loads we coordinate stuffing at the factory or at a Cochin Port CFS, depending on whether the cargo needs to be palletised or stuffed loose.

Which grade for which Jeddah load

A short decision rule we use when scoping a Jeddah quote.

Use caseRecommended gradeTypical thickness
Re-export crates for machinery (KAEC, Industrial City)BWR packing-grade, ISPM-15 lumber12 mm walls, 18 mm base
Consumer-goods crates needing clean branding faceOkoume-faced panels9 mm or 12 mm
Warehouse pallets, port re-stuffBWR packing-grade or hardwood block pallets15 mm to 18 mm deck
Container re-flooringHeavy-duty hardwood-core ply18 mm or 21 mm
Makkah corridor shuttering / concrete formworkFilm-faced shuttering plywood18 mm
Dunnage and load-spreadingBWR packing-grade off-cuts or 18 mm sheet12 mm to 18 mm

FAQ for Jeddah buyers

Do you ship ISPM-15 / HT-stamped material?

Yes. ISPM-15 heat-treated and IPPC-stamped crates and pallets are the standard for the KAEC and re-export segments. We supply the certification copy with the shipping documents so customs and the Saudi PIF / phytosanitary check at Jeddah Islamic Port move without flags.

What is the minimum order for a Jeddah-bound container?

The economics start to make sense at about 12 to 15 tonnes of plywood — roughly a 20-foot container of 12 mm sheets or about 700 to 800 sheets, depending on the mix. Below that, LCL via the Cochin–Jeddah groupage services is workable but loses the freight advantage.

Can you palletise and shrink-wrap before stuffing?

Yes. Standard EUR or ISO pallets, strapped and shrink-wrapped at the factory, with a cargo manifest matched to the BL. Useful for buyers who run an automated unloading line at the Jeddah end.

Request a quote

If you are sourcing packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets or container flooring into Jeddah, KAEC or the Makkah corridor, send the specifications across and we will price the full landed picture — material, ISPM-15 if needed, stuffing at Cochin Port, and the lane. You can request a quote directly, or browse the resource hub for grade comparisons, the BWP vs BWR explainer, and the ISPM-15 crate cost guide before you send the enquiry. We typically come back with a working quote within one business day for standard specs, and within two for okoume or film-faced shuttering ply, where the panel stock has to be confirmed against the order.

title: Plywood Supply to Jubail | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring that move heavy process equipment out of Jubail Industrial City. Our group.
html:
Why Jubail moves the needle for a Kochi plywood supplier

Jubail Industrial City is the largest greenfield petrochemical and primary-industry estate in the world. Thirteen of SABIC's sixteen Saudi production plants sit inside its perimeter, alongside SATORP, Advanced Petrochemical, Jubail United and an extended belt of EPC contractors building reactors, columns, valves, skids, heat exchangers and pre-assembled modules for the next phase of Vision 2030 expansion. Every one of those units leaves a factory floor packed in wood. That is the volume we exist to serve.
