The Cochin Wood group has been supplying packing-grade plywood, crate kits, pallets and container flooring out of Perumbavoor since 1986, and Jubail's heavy-engineering loads sit squarely inside what our cutting lists were originally designed for.

The Jubail industrial picture

Industrial investment in Jubail crossed SAR 525 billion in 2022, and the Royal Commission for Jubail and Yanbu has earmarked another SAR 67.5 billion to double the city's footprint by 2030. For a packing-materials supplier that translates into four predictable demand pools.

Petrochemical and refining EPC

SABIC, SATORP, SADAF, Jubail United and the surrounding contractor estates run continuous turnaround, debottlenecking and brownfield expansion cycles. Reactors, distillation internals, catalyst drums, spare exchanger bundles, instrumentation skids and process valves move in and out of these sites in heavy timber-sided crates and on engineered pallets every week.

Industrial Support Area and secondary industries

The Industrial Support Area inside Jubail-1 and the planned Jubail-3 expansion host fabricators making finished plastics, rubber goods, automotive components, home appliances and industrial machinery. These shippers send finished goods out through King Fahd Industrial Port and Jubail Commercial Port in standard crates and on four-way pallets.

Specialty chemicals, pharma and renewables

Specialty-chemicals plots, the planned pharmaceutical cluster and renewables-equipment fabricators all need export-grade packing that survives Gulf heat, sea-salt humidity and transhipment handling without warping or splitting.

Port logistics zone

The bonded logistics zone wired into both Jubail ports and the rail network is a steady consumer of one-way pallets and container flooring boards for fit-outs, container repair and stuffing operations.

What we ship to Jubail

Packing-grade plywood

Our packing-grade hardwood plywood is the workhorse for Jubail-bound crates. It is built for nailing, screwing and stapling without splitting at the edge, and it tolerates the humidity swings between Indian monsoon transit, sea voyage and Eastern Province summer storage. Standard offer below — ex-factory Perumbavoor, GST and freight extra.

ThicknessTypical use in Jubail-bound packsSheet size
6 mm and 8 mmTop covers, internal partitions, dunnage panels8 x 4 ft
10 mm and 12 mmCrate sides for valves, instrumentation, light skids8 x 4 ft
15 mm and 16 mmCrate sides and tops for process equipment, exchangers8 x 4 ft
18 mmHeavy-base bottoms, container flooring patches, reactor-spare crates8 x 4 ft

Okoume-faced plywood

Okoume is a face-veneer option on our MR-Packing and BWR boards, not a separate grade. We recommend it when the consignee specifies a clean white-cream face for show-and-tell crates, brand-visible export packs, or pharma and specialty-chemical shipments where appearance and a tight, uniform face matter. Quoted on request because veneer availability moves with the market.

Crates and ISPM-15 boxes

We build crates to drawing — closed-box, open-crate, skid-mounted and collapsible. All export crates are ISPM-15 heat-treated and stamped, which is the only treatment Saudi Customs and the Jubail port authorities accept. Crate weights are calculated against the consignee's lift plan so the rigger inside the SABIC or SATORP plot does not get a surprise at the laydown yard.

Pallets

Two-way and four-way pallets in standard 1200 x 1000 mm and 1200 x 800 mm, plus custom footprints for skid-mounted process equipment. Heat-treated and stencilled. We also build heavy-duty bearer-deck pallets for reactor internals and catalyst drums where the unit load can exceed 2 tonnes.

Container flooring

18 mm BWP plywood floorboards for container repair and re-flooring jobs inside the Jubail logistics zone. Cut to the standard 2400 x 1160 mm container plank, edge-sealed.

Kochi-to-Jubail logistics

The sea distance from Kochi to Jubail's King Fahd Industrial Port is roughly 1,900 nautical miles. The practical route is Cochin Port (Vallarpadam ICTT) to either King Fahd Industrial Port for direct petrochem-plot deliveries, or Jubail Commercial Port for general containerised cargo with onward trucking into the industrial estates. Both ports are wired into the Saudi rail spine and the logistics zone behind them.

Typical transit window on direct services is 9 to 12 days, stretched to 14 to 18 days on transhipment legs via Jebel Ali or Khalifa Port. We palletise and band loads at our packing yard so that even after a transhipment the consignment opens cleanly at the Jubail laydown. King Fahd Industrial Port runs 34 berths with about 70 million tonnes per year of capacity, so berth congestion is rarely the constraint — documentation and ISPM-15 stamping discipline are.

Decision rules — which board for which Jubail load

  • Packing-grade plywood (commercial / rubberwood): default for the vast majority of Jubail-bound crates. Petrochem valves, instrumentation, exchanger bundles, machinery spares — anything where the crate is consumed at destination.
  • Okoume face on MR-Packing or BWR core: when the consignee specifies a clean white face, when the shipment is brand-visible, or when humidity exposure on the laydown yard will run long.
  • BWR / BWP-grade plywood: for container flooring, marine-side dunnage, and any crate that will sit uncovered in monsoon transit or Gulf coastal storage.
  • Shuttering ply: not for export crates. Shuttering ply is for the civil-works side — Jubail-2 and Jubail-3 expansion concrete pours, RCJY infrastructure contracts, residential build-out. Quoted separately on civil tender.

FAQ

Do you handle ISPM-15 certification and stencilling in-house?

Yes. All export crates and pallets shipped to Jubail are heat-treated and ISPM-15 stencilled at our packing yard. The treatment certificate accompanies the shipping documents so the consignment clears Dammam and Jubail customs without a hold.

What is the minimum order size for a Jubail shipment?

One full 20-ft or 40-ft container is the comfortable minimum. We also consolidate smaller crate kits into mixed containers when a Jubail contractor needs a partial load, but the per-unit economics work better at one-container-and-up.

Can you ship straight to a SABIC or SATORP plot, or only to the port?

We ship to the port. Onward trucking from King Fahd or Jubail Commercial Port into a specific SABIC, SATORP, SADAF or Jubail United plot is handled by the consignee's nominated clearing agent and inland transporter. We coordinate paperwork, packing list and treatment certificates so the handover is clean.

Talk to us about a Jubail consignment

If you are crating petrochem spares, building export pallets for a Jubail-bound shipment, or sourcing container flooring for a logistics-zone fit-out, send us the spec — thickness, sheet count or crate drawing, monthly tonnage and destination berth. We will come back with a packing-list quote, ISPM-15 treatment scope and a realistic Cochin Port sailing window. Request a quote with your packing list, or browse the resource hub for ISPM-15 guidance, crate-construction notes and grade-selection tables before you write the RFQ.

title: Plywood Supply to Khobar | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring that Eastern Province workshops use to ship valves, instrumentation skids.
html:
Khobar sits at the centre of Saudi Arabia's Eastern Province, the country's hydrocarbon engine. Between Dhahran, Dammam Port and the Aramco operating belt, this corridor moves more oilfield service equipment, valves, instrumentation skids and heavy spares than almost any other industrial cluster in the Gulf. Every piece of that equipment leaves a workshop in a crate, sits on a pallet, or travels on a container floor. That is where the demand for engineered wood packaging quietly comes from, and that is the trade Cochin Wood Industries ships into from Kochi.

The Khobar industrial picture

To understand the plywood need in Khobar, it helps to map the industrial belt the city anchors. Khobar is one corner of the Dammam–Dhahran–Khobar triangle, the densest manufacturing and oilfield-services cluster in the Kingdom. The buyers we encounter are clustered in a few specific zones.

Dammam Second Industrial City

This is the heavyweight estate of the Eastern Province. It hosts fabrication shops, oilfield equipment assemblers, downhole tool refurbishers, wellhead and Christmas-tree builders, valve manufacturers and instrumentation houses. These workshops crate up high-value equipment for Aramco contracts, for export to other GCC operators, and for back-shipment to OEM principals in Houston, Aberdeen and Stavanger. Crate demand here is constant, and the boxes are heavy — typically 800 kg to 6 tonnes per unit, occasionally more.

Dammam Third Industrial City and the Modon estates

The newer Modon-managed parks around Dammam, including the Third Industrial City and the satellite zones running west toward Abqaiq, house lighter fabrication, electrical panel builders, HVAC skid makers, pipe-coating yards and plastic/polymer converters. Crates here are mid-weight and high-volume — long, shallow boxes for piping spools and skid-mounted units.

King Abdulaziz Port (Dammam) and King Fahd Industrial Port (Jubail)

Khobar's industrial output ships through two ports. Dammam Port handles the general cargo, project cargo and break-bulk equipment. Jubail's industrial port, a short trucking lane north, handles petrochemicals and heavy project moves. Both ports drive a steady stream of one-trip crates, plus a recurring need for IPPC-compliant pallets for stuffing 20-foot and 40-foot containers.

Jubail Industrial City

Sixty-odd miles up the coast, Jubail is the petrochemical capital. Its buyers are catalyst handlers, instrumentation OEMs and spare-parts distributors who ship temperature- and shock-sensitive equipment in cushioned plywood crates with internal bracing. This belt also pulls in shuttering-grade plywood when expansion projects break ground.

What we ship into Khobar

The Cochin Wood group has been operating from Perumbavoor since 1986 — about forty years of group manufacturing. Cochin Wood Industries Pvt Ltd carries that production capacity into the Khobar lane through three main product families.

Packing-grade plywood (sheet form)

Most Eastern Province crate builders order plywood in sheet form and convert to boxes locally. Our packing range is rubberwood / commercial hardwood, IS 303 MR-grade construction, sized 8 ft x 4 ft as standard, with 4 ft x 4 ft, 8 ft x 3 ft and 6 ft x 3 ft cuts available. Thicknesses move between 6 mm and 18 mm depending on box duty. The card we use in current quotations:

ThicknessTypical Khobar use
6 mmInternal partitions, light-duty boxes
8–10 mmBox sides for sub-tonne instrumentation crates
12 mmStandard oilfield spare-parts crate walls
15–16 mmHeavy crate sides, valve and wellhead boxes
18 mmContainer flooring, base boards, skid decks

Crates (built-to-spec, knocked down or assembled)

For buyers who prefer finished crates landed at Dammam, we build to drawing in Perumbavoor and ship either assembled or in knock-down form. Specifications are confirmed against the load: internal dimensions, gross weight, centre of gravity, lashing eyes, stencil markings and ISPM-15 heat-treatment for the softwood frame members. Standard configurations include cleated plywood boxes, framed crates with diagonal bracing, and skid-base crates for forklift handling.

Pallets and container flooring

Our pallets are built in Indian standard (1200 x 1000 mm), Euro (1200 x 800 mm) and US (1219 x 1016 mm) footprints, two-way or four-way entry, in hardwood or plywood-deck configurations. For Khobar exporters consolidating into containers, the 1200 x 1000 mm two-way pallet is the most-asked footprint. Container flooring boards — 18 mm packing-grade plywood, sized to fit the 20-foot and 40-foot floor — are shipped as flat-pack stacks.

Kochi-to-Khobar logistics

The shipping lane runs from Cochin Port (Kochi) to King Abdulaziz Port (Dammam), with Jebel Ali as the typical transhipment hub. Direct routings via Salalah are also used. The relevant numbers:

  • Sea distance: roughly 1,900 nautical miles, port-to-port
  • Transit time: 14–22 days door-to-port, depending on routing and consolidator
  • Trucking from Dammam Port to Khobar: 25–40 minutes, no border crossing
  • Container types: 20-foot dry, 40-foot dry, occasional 40-foot HC for larger crate stacks
  • Compliance: all softwood members are ISPM-15 heat-treated and stamped; documentation includes phytosanitary certificate, commercial invoice, packing list and Saudi conformity papers as relevant

For Khobar buyers we typically quote FOB Cochin or CIF Dammam — the call depends on whether the buyer is consolidating with other Indian-origin cargo. We can also quote ex-works Perumbavoor if the buyer's freight forwarder picks up at our yard.

Decision rules — which grade for which Khobar load

Not every crate needs premium veneer. A short rule-of-thumb we use when quoting:

  • Packing-grade (MR, commercial hardwood): the default. Use for oilfield service spares, valve crates, instrumentation boxes, general industrial export. Cost-efficient and tough enough for one-way sea movement.
  • Okoume face-veneer (on packing or BWR substrate): use when the buyer's spec asks for a clean export face, or when the crate goes through multiple handling legs and visual presentation matters. Common on equipment heading from Khobar onward to European OEMs.
  • BWR / shuttering-grade plywood: use when the load is humidity-exposed for long periods, when crates may sit outdoors at the port, or when the buyer is on the construction side rather than the equipment side. The Jubail expansion projects pull in shuttering ply directly.

For most Khobar oilfield service buyers, MR-packing grade with 12 mm walls and 18 mm base, on a 4-way pallet, lands the right balance of cost and protection.

FAQ

Do you handle ISPM-15 compliance for Saudi entry?

Yes. All softwood members in our crates and pallets are heat-treated and stamped to ISPM-15. The plywood faces are exempt under the standard since they are engineered panels, but the load-bearing framework carries the mark.

Can you supply in knocked-down (flat-pack) form to save container space?

Yes. For Khobar buyers shipping in 20-foot containers, knocked-down crates with pre-drilled cleats and a numbered assembly sheet are a common request. We supply the fasteners separately, packed by crate.

What is the minimum order quantity for a Khobar shipment?

The practical minimum is one 20-foot container — roughly 280 sheets of 12 mm plywood, or about 60–80 medium crates flat-packed. Smaller volumes are possible through a freight consolidator, but the unit economics are usually better at one full container.

Quote request

If you are sourcing crates, pallets, container flooring or sheet plywood into Khobar or the wider Eastern Province, send us the load details — thickness, sheet sizes, crate drawings or pallet footprints, monthly volume and the Dammam consignee. We will come back with an EX-Works Perumbavoor or CIF Dammam quotation, transit timing and the compliance paperwork list. Start at our request a quote page, or browse specifications, grade tables and shipping notes on the resource hub before you write to us.

title: Plywood Supply to Riyadh | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture export-grade packing plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring for the heavy cargo moving through Riyadh's MODON belts. Built in.
html:
Why Riyadh's industrial belt sits on our shipping desk every week

Riyadh is inland, dry, and absorbing capex at a pace most GCC capitals are not. The Vision 2030 manufacturing push has thickened the demand for export-grade wooden packaging across the Riyadh-1, Riyadh-2 and Riyadh-3 industrial cities — the kind of crates, skids and container flooring used to ship machine tools, generator sets, transformers, prefabricated MEP modules, fabricated steel and food-processing lines into and out of the capital. Because Riyadh has no port, almost every heavy item arrives via Jeddah on the Red Sea or Dammam/King Abdulaziz on the Gulf, then trucks 870-950 km inland. That double-handling — sea leg plus desert haul — punishes weak packaging. It is why Riyadh buyers tend to over-spec rather than under-spec their plywood, and it is why a Kochi-loaded container of properly graded packing ply lands competitively against regional alternatives.

The Riyadh industrial picture

The capital's manufacturing footprint is concentrated in a handful of named belts run by MODON (Saudi Authority for Industrial Cities and Technology Zones). A working buyer's map looks roughly like this:

  • Riyadh-1 (Sulay): the oldest belt, dense with general engineering, fabrication and food units. Crate volumes here skew small-and-frequent.
  • Riyadh-2 (Al-Kharj Road): heavier units — transformers, switchgear, building materials, large fabricators. Bigger one-piece crates and skids; container flooring requests show up here.
  • Riyadh-3 (Al-Khurmah / Sudair extension): newer Vision-2030 plots — auto components, EV-adjacent assembly, pharma, logistics warehousing. Pallet pools and export packaging dominate.
  • Sudair City for Industry & Business (~120 km north of Riyadh): Saudi's largest industrial zone, now anchoring electronics, EV-battery and renewables manufacturing. A major future absorber of plywood pallets and ISPM-15 crates.
  • King Salman Energy Park (SPARK) and the Modon zones at Dammam-2 / Dammam-3: not Riyadh strictly, but tied to the same trucking lane and frequently routed through Riyadh consolidators.

Across these belts, the recurring wood-packaging asks we see from sectors — not naming buyers — are: heavy machinery exporters, EPC contractors moving prefabricated skids, oilfield-service firms, food-and-beverage line builders, marble and stone fabricators (heavy, fragile, brutal on packaging), and 3PL operators standardising pallet pools.

What we ship to Riyadh

Packing-grade plywood (the workhorse)

Most Riyadh-bound containers leave Kochi loaded with our standard packing-grade rubberwood/commercial hardwood plywood — the practical export-crate material. Useful thicknesses, ex-factory:

ThicknessTypical use in Riyadh-bound crates
6 mmInner partitions, light component boxes, dunnage
8-10 mmSide panels for medium crates up to ~500 kg gross
12 mmThe default skin for export crates 500-1500 kg
15-16 mmHeavy crates, transformer and switchgear cases
18 mmBase/skids, machine bedplates, container flooring panels

Sheet sizes are cut to the buyer's bill of materials — 8x4 ft standard, with 7x4, 6x3 and 4x4 cuts available when the crate drawing calls for it. Moisture content is held tight so panels survive a Red Sea humidity spike without warping when they hit dry Riyadh air.

Crate construction

For Riyadh consignees we typically build to ISPM-15 with heat-treated and stamped pinewood/hardwood framing, 12-18 mm plywood skin, screwed-and-glued corners, internal bracing for shock loads, and labelled lift points. Edge protection and VCI lining are added when the cargo is metal or electronics sensitive to the Jeddah-to-Riyadh truck vibration profile.

Pallets

Pallet specs that travel well to Riyadh: 1200x1000 mm (the de-facto GCC export pallet) and 1200x800 mm Euro for buyers feeding into European OEM lines. Four-way entry, 18 mm top deck for pool-grade pallets, 12 mm for one-way export pallets. All ISPM-15 treated and stamped before sealing the container.

Container flooring

When buyers reefer-convert or rebuild dry containers in Riyadh's logistics parks, 18-28 mm structural plywood flooring panels are shipped flat-packed. We supply this as a cut-and-numbered kit so the rebuild crew is not sawing on-site.

Kochi-to-Riyadh logistics

The economics are straightforward once the route is set:

  • Ocean leg: Cochin Port (Vallarpadam ICTT) to Jeddah Islamic Port is roughly 2,200 nautical miles. Transit is typically 6-8 days direct, 9-12 days on transhipment services via Salalah or Jebel Ali.
  • Alternate ocean leg: Cochin to King Abdulaziz Port, Dammam — 10-14 days typical, often transhipped at Jebel Ali. Used when the consignee is east-coast Saudi or when Jeddah congestion is high.
  • Inland trucking: Jeddah to Riyadh is ~950 km on Highway 40; Dammam to Riyadh is ~400 km on Highway 80. Both are well-served by Saudi 40-ft trailer fleets; same-week last-mile is normal.
  • Documentation: ISPM-15 stamp, fumigation certificate, commercial invoice, packing list, BL, certificate of origin. CWI files the ISPM-15 paperwork as part of the order.

For most Riyadh buyers we recommend FOB Cochin and let the consignee's freight forwarder negotiate the ocean leg — this almost always lands cheaper than CIF Jeddah quoted from origin, because Saudi importers run sharp freight contracts.

Decision rules: which grade for which load?

A practical filter the procurement teams in Riyadh use:

  1. Standard packing-grade ply (rubberwood/commercial hardwood) — use when the cargo is general engineering, fabricated steel, building materials, or anything where the crate is one-trip. This is 80% of what we ship to Riyadh.
  2. Okoume-faced packing plywood — use when the buyer wants a lighter, cleaner panel for high-value or showroom-bound goods (electronics, premium machinery, exhibition gear). Okoume is a face-veneer option on our MR-packing and BWR boards, not a separate grade — it trims weight and improves cosmetic finish.
  3. Shuttering ply (film-faced) — only if the Riyadh end-use is concrete formwork on a construction site, not packaging. Vision 2030 construction sites in Diriyah Gate, NEOM-adjacent feeder projects and Riyadh Metro extensions do pull this category.

FAQ

Can you ship ISPM-15 stamped material directly?

Yes. All export crates, pallets and dunnage leave Kochi heat-treated, stamped and accompanied by the fumigation certificate accepted by Saudi customs.

What container fill should we plan for from Kochi?

A 40-ft HC typically holds 480-520 sheets of 18 mm 8x4 packing ply, or roughly 540 ISPM-15 pallets in 1200x1000 mm flat-pack. Mixed-thickness loads are routine — share the BOM and we will load-plan it.

How do you handle the humidity swing between Jeddah and Riyadh?

By controlling moisture content at the factory (typical 8-12%) and by specifying glue lines (MR or BWR) that tolerate the swing. Crates built for inland Saudi sites get tighter MC and BWR glue as standard.

Get a Riyadh-landed quote

Send a packing list, target sheet sizes, the consignee's port preference (Jeddah or Dammam) and an estimated monthly volume. We will come back with an ex-Kochi rate card, a load plan for a 40-ft HC, and an ISPM-15 documentation pack so your forwarder can quote the ocean leg cleanly. Request a quote or browse the resource hub for crate-design notes, moisture-content guides and the full packing-grade thickness chart.

title: Plywood Supply to Duqm | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, sawn timber, crates and pallets that move heavy, high-value equipment into SEZAD. Made in Perumbavoor, where our.
html:
Duqm has moved faster than most procurement teams expected. What was a fishing town on the Arabian Sea a decade ago is now SEZAD — the Special Economic Zone at Duqm — with a drydock running heavy MRO contracts, a 230,000 bpd refinery commissioned, a green-hydrogen ecosystem under build-out, and a deep-water port that has reshaped how Gulf cargo moves. For an Indian plywood supplier, that combination produces a very specific demand pattern: heavy, high-value, often awkwardly shaped equipment that needs to leave a fabrication shop, survive an ocean leg, clear customs, and arrive at a site address inside SEZAD without damage. That is a crate-and-pallet problem, and it is the workflow our packing-grade plywood, sawn timber and shuttering ply are built around.

The Duqm industrial picture — what is actually being built

If you are sourcing packaging materials into Duqm, it helps to know which clusters are generating the orders. The footprint is wider than most one-line summaries suggest.

SEZAD's core zones

  • Duqm Drydock — Oman Drydock Company runs two graving docks and is a regional hub for VLCC, FPSO and naval refits. MRO work means heavy spares, replacement valves, propeller assemblies and turbine sections moving in and out — almost all of which travel in heavy-duty wooden crates.
  • Duqm Refinery and Petrochemical Industries (OQ8) — the 230,000 bpd refinery and its downstream petchem expansion mean continuous flow of catalysts, instrumentation, spare pumps and compressor parts. Catalyst drums, instrument transmitters and rotating equipment all need crate-grade ply or pallet-mounted boxes.
  • Green hydrogen and renewables belt — Hyport Duqm, Hyduqm and other green-H2 / ammonia projects are bringing in electrolyser stacks, transformers and module skids from European and Asian fabricators. ISPM-15 compliant crating is mandatory.
  • Heavy industry, steel and minerals — Jindal Shadeed's expansion, Karwa Motors' assembly, vehicle CKD lines, and the cement / mining feedstock corridor all pull pallet volumes for component movement inside the zone.
  • Fisheries and food processing cluster — at the southern edge of SEZAD, this sector runs on hardwood pallets and lighter crates for export consignments to GCC retail.
  • Port of Duqm and logistics corridor — the dry port, container freight stations, and the Duqm–Thumrait–Salalah road link generate pallet demand for re-stuffing and warehousing operations.

Each of these clusters has a different unit economics. The drydock and refinery generate low-volume, high-value, heavy crates. Green-H2 EPC contractors generate spikes of large, awkward modules. The fisheries and logistics layers generate steady pallet pull-through. A supplier that can move across all three formats has the easier conversation.

What the Cochin Wood group ships to Duqm

The Cochin Wood group has been working in Perumbavoor since 1986, and the bulk of that 40-year run has been in packing-grade plywood and sawn timber for industrial packaging. That is the foundation under what we send to Oman.

Packing-grade plywood — the workhorse

For Duqm crate makers and in-plant packing departments, our MR-Packing range is what carries most of the volume. Indicative ex-factory rates (GST and freight extra) for the commercial / rubberwood packing grade:

ThicknessTypical crate useEx-factory (INR / sq.ft.)
6 mmTop covers, light skin panels24.00
8 mmSide panels, lid stiffeners28.00
10 mmMedium crate sides31.50
12 mmStandard heavy-crate panels33.50
14 mmReinforced sides for sea freight36.00
15 mmHeavy box, equipment crates39.00
16 mmDeck boards, cleats39.50
18 mmPallet decks, base boards, container flooring repair42.00

Okoume face plywood

Where the cargo is being re-exported into European buyers, or where the EPC tender specifies a smoother, lighter face with cleaner appearance, the Okoume option moves up. Okoume is a face-veneer option on our MR-Packing and BWR cores — it is not a separate "grade" but a face choice that changes weight, finish and price. We quote Okoume separately on request because the spec depends on what is being shipped.

Crates, pallets and container flooring

  • Heavy-duty equipment crates — built around 15 mm and 18 mm panels with hardwood frames; designed for 1–10 tonne payloads, the typical refinery spare and drydock part envelope.
  • Standard export pallets — four-way entry, 1200x1000 mm and 1100x1100 mm being the two formats most Duqm logistics terminals handle without slowing down.
  • Heat-treated (HT) and ISPM-15 marked — required for any wood entering Oman; this is a non-negotiable customs reality and a frequent rejection cause if the supplier is not set up for it.
  • Container flooring and skids — 18 mm and thicker for replacement floor sections, machinery skids, and roll-on roll-off staging.
  • Sawn timber — jackwood at INR 400/cft (¼-inch minus tolerance) and rubberwood runners for cleats, gussets and skid construction.

Kochi-to-Duqm logistics

The distance from Kochi to Duqm is roughly 1,700 nautical miles by sea. Container services routed via transhipment hubs typically land cargo in Duqm in 14 to 21 days from Cochin port, depending on the carrier and connection point. Bulk break-bulk and project cargo can also load at Kochi for direct GCC sailings. For most plywood, crate and pallet shipments we work on FOB Cochin or CFR Duqm terms; the buyer chooses the incoterm based on whether they have a freight forwarder already on the lane.

Two operational notes worth flagging:

  • ISPM-15 documentation has to travel with the shipment, not arrive separately by email. We mark and certify at packing, not at port.
  • Moisture content is a real issue on the Kochi–Duqm leg. Kerala monsoon stock that has not been kiln-dried below 12% MC can develop fungal staining in the container during transit. We dry-stack and condition material before despatch for this reason.

Decision rules — which spec for which Duqm load

Use packing-grade (MR-Packing)

When the cargo is internal-zone movement, MRO spares moving between drydock and fabrication shop, fisheries and food-cluster pallets, and standard refinery spares packing. This is the default for most SEZAD packaging tenders.

Use Okoume-faced ply

When the receiver is a European fabricator (common for green-H2 module re-export), when the crate will be photographed and audited, or when weight per panel matters and a cleaner face is specified. Okoume sits about 15–25% above commercial packing on price.

Use shuttering ply

When the customer is a SEZAD civil contractor casting foundations, jetty extensions, refinery tankage pads, or zone road formwork — not a packaging buyer. Different conversation, different spec sheet.

FAQ

Do you ship ISPM-15 certified wood to Oman?

Yes. All crates, pallets and sawn timber going to Duqm leave Kochi heat-treated and stamped per ISPM-15. Phytosanitary certification is arranged at port. Customs in Oman rejects untreated wood at inspection, so we do not ship any other way.

What is the minimum order size for a Duqm shipment?

Practical minimum is one 20-foot container — roughly 500–650 sheets of 18 mm packing ply, or a mixed load of crates and pallets. Smaller orders can ride as LCL through Cochin port but the per-unit freight cost usually does not work out in the buyer's favour below half a container.

Can you build to a SEZAD contractor's drawing?

Yes. Most refinery and drydock spares ship in custom crates against an outline drawing and a weight statement. Share the drawing, weight, lifting points and any sea-fastening requirements and we quote against the build.

Next step

If you are sourcing packing-grade plywood, ISPM-15 crates, pallets or container flooring for a project inside SEZAD — or you are an EPC contractor mobilising into the Duqm refinery, drydock or green-H2 belt — we can quote against your panel mix, crate drawings or pallet schedule. Send through the load list, the destination address inside the zone, and the timeline, and we will come back with FOB Cochin and CFR Duqm options.

Start by sharing your spec on the request a quote page, or browse specifications, grade comparisons and packing-case guidance in the Cochin Wood resource hub before you build the BOQ.

title: Plywood Supply to Muscat | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade and Okoume-faced plywood, knock-down crate kits and export pallets in Perumbavoor and ship them direct to Muscat. Our group has.
html:
Why Muscat sits on a serious plywood supply lane

Muscat is the closest GCC capital to the Kerala coast. From Cochin Port to Mina Sultan Qaboos is roughly 1,500 nautical miles, and the standard ocean transit runs 4 to 5 days on direct feeder services. For any factory or trader in Muscat that needs packing-grade plywood, crating timber, ISPM-15 pallets or container flooring, that geography is the headline: nothing else in the Gulf reaches the Sultanate with a shorter lead time than Indian South-West-coast suppliers. Muscat itself is a re-export hub for the inland Omani belt and an entry point for Saudi-bound transit cargo, which means crating volume is steadier than the population alone would suggest. The Cochin Wood group has been milling and trading in Perumbavoor since 1986, and packing-grade plywood for GCC customers has been part of the mix for most of that history.

The Muscat industrial picture

The plywood demand in Muscat does not come from one neighbourhood. It comes from a ring of industrial estates and the port-adjacent re-export zone, each with its own load profile.

Rusayl Industrial Estate

Rusayl, off the Muscat Expressway near the airport, is the oldest of the Madayn estates and the densest in Muscat governorate. Plastics, light electricals, food processing and aluminium fabrication all sit here. The crating need is steady but small-format — secondary packaging, internal pallet pools, and outbound cases for spare parts and small machinery.

Ghala and Wadi Kabir

Ghala houses workshops, auto parts, and a chunk of the marble-and-stone trade. Wadi Kabir, closer to the port, is the older industrial spine — heavy workshops, oil-and-gas service shops, and the steel fabricators who feed the construction belt. Both of these areas generate heavier crating jobs: skids and bolted cases for pumps, valves, transformers and fabricated structures.

Mina Sultan Qaboos and the re-export corridor

Mina Sultan Qaboos has largely shifted container traffic to Sohar, but it remains the cruise and re-export point for Muscat itself. Re-export crating — repacking inbound goods for onward shipment to Saudi Arabia, Yemen-bound transit, or the inland Omani trade — drives a particular kind of plywood need: thin sheets cut to box panels, light pallets, and quick-turn crate kits.

Light industry and fit-out

Hotel and retail fit-out in greater Muscat, plus the shop-fitting trade in Ruwi and Qurum, also pulls packing plywood — for site protection, temporary hoarding, and the crates that bring joinery and stone in from Kerala, Tamil Nadu and Rajasthan. This is a smaller line item than industrial crating but it is consistent.

What we ship to Muscat

The orders that move from Kochi to Muscat fall into four buckets. Each has its own spec sheet.

Packing-grade plywood

This is the workhorse. MR-grade commercial plywood, hardwood core, used by Muscat crate makers to build cases on-site. Standard sizes and thicknesses we move:

ThicknessTypical use in MuscatStandard size
6 mmLight cartons, partitions, box lids8 x 4 ft
8 mmSmall-parts cases, internal dividers8 x 4 ft
12 mmGeneral crating, light machinery cases8 x 4 ft
15-16 mmHeavy crates, marble and stone cases8 x 4 ft
18 mmSkid tops, container flooring repairs, heavy fab cases8 x 4 ft

All packing-grade sheets are quoted ex-factory in INR per square foot, with FOB Cochin or CFR Muscat options on request. GST is zero on export. Phytosanitary documentation is straightforward — Indian packing plywood is heat-pressed and accepted at Omani entry without ISPM-15 stamping (the stamp is for solid-wood dunnage, not engineered panels).

Crate construction

For buyers who would rather receive finished cases than build them, we supply knock-down crate kits: pre-cut plywood panels, sawn-timber framing, nails and corner blocks. A common Muscat order is the 1200 x 1000 x 1200 mm export case, built in 12 mm plywood on a 75 x 50 mm pine or hardwood frame, designed to nest five high in a 40-foot container on the outbound leg.

Pallets

Standard sizes shipping to Muscat are the 1200 x 1000 mm GCC pallet and the 1200 x 800 mm Euro pallet. Heat-treated hardwood deckboards over a softwood or hardwood block base. ISPM-15 stamping is available where the end-use requires onward re-export.

Container flooring

18 mm and 21 mm 28-ply Apitong-equivalent flooring panels for container repair yards and refrigerated-unit retrofitters in the port belt. This is a smaller line for us but a sticky one — once a yard standardises on a sheet spec, repeat orders follow.

Kochi-to-Muscat logistics

The route is short by Gulf standards. Direct feeders from Cochin Port (ICTT Vallarpadam) to Sohar and Mina Sultan Qaboos run weekly. Standard ocean transit is 4 to 5 days. Door-to-door lead time from our Perumbavoor yard to a Rusayl or Ghala warehouse is typically 12 to 16 days end-to-end, including inland trucking from Perumbavoor to Cochin Port (about 45 km), vessel cut-off, sailing, Omani customs clearance and last-mile delivery.

Most orders move in 20-foot containers loaded at 23 to 25 tonnes of plywood — roughly 1,800 to 2,200 sheets of 12 mm depending on density. Smaller buyers often consolidate via LCL agents in Cochin; a half-container of plywood is a reasonable starter order. FOB Cochin and CFR Mina Sultan Qaboos / Sohar are the standard Incoterms we quote.

Decision rules: which grade for which load

The most common question from a first-time Muscat buyer is which grade to specify. The short version:

  • Packing-grade MR plywood covers 80 percent of crating jobs in Muscat — industrial cases, hardware, light machinery, fit-out crates, re-export repacking. This is the default.
  • Okoume plywood is a face-veneer option built on our MR-Packing or BWR cores. Specify Okoume when the end-buyer cares about the look of the case (display crates, premium export, branded packaging) or when the destination climate punishes lower-grade faces.
  • BWR-grade plywood is the call for cases that will sit outdoors at the receiver, or for the cyclical humidity inside Mina Sultan Qaboos warehousing during monsoon transit.
  • Shuttering ply is a separate product for the Muscat construction belt — formwork for concrete pours, not packaging. We supply it but it serves a different buyer.

If the case carries marble, stone slabs, transformers or anything over 1.5 tonnes, step up to 15-18 mm packing-grade or BWR. Below that, 12 mm is the right balance of strength, weight and price.

FAQ

Do you ship FOB Cochin or CFR Muscat?

Both. Most Muscat buyers prefer CFR Mina Sultan Qaboos or CFR Sohar so the freight is locked in at order time. FOB Cochin is available if the buyer's freight forwarder gives a better rate.

What is the minimum order for Oman?

A 20-foot container is the cleanest economic unit — roughly 1,800-2,200 sheets depending on thickness. LCL consolidation is possible for smaller trials, usually pallet-quantity orders through Cochin-based forwarders.

Is ISPM-15 stamping required for Oman?

Not for engineered plywood panels. ISPM-15 applies to solid-wood pallets, dunnage and crate framing. If you order finished pallets or crate kits with timber framing, we supply with ISPM-15 stamping on request.

Get a quote for Muscat delivery

Tell us the thickness, sheet count, destination (Rusayl, Ghala, Wadi Kabir, Mina Sultan Qaboos or elsewhere) and required delivery month. We will come back with an ex-factory rate, freight estimate to Sohar or Mina Sultan Qaboos, and a sailing schedule from Cochin Port. Request a quote through our enquiry form, or browse our resource hub for grade comparisons, crate specs and packing-case design notes before you order.

title: Plywood Supply to Salalah | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, export crates, pallets and container flooring that hold up in Dhofar's monsoon humidity and 45 C heat. Our group.
html:
Salalah is the most under-rated industrial node on the Arabian Sea. The Port of Salalah handles transhipment volumes that put it inside the world's top thirty container ports, the Salalah Free Zone sits directly behind the quay with petrochemical anchor tenants, and Raysut Industrial Estate keeps adding downstream fabrication units. All of this generates one consistent material need: dense, predictable, ISPM-15 compliant wooden packaging that can hold up in monsoon humidity from June to September and bone-dry 45 C heat the rest of the year. From Kochi, the Cochin Wood group has been making and shipping exactly that material for nearly forty years, and Salalah is one of the natural destinations on the Arabian Sea lane.

The Salalah industrial picture

Three zones in Dhofar do most of the work, and each one has a distinct packaging profile.

Salalah Free Zone (SFZ)

SFZ is anchored by petrochemicals and downstream plastics. Octal — now part of OQ — runs one of the world's largest PET sheet and resin operations directly inside the zone. Salalah Methanol Company, also OQ-owned, produces methanol and ammonia at industrial scale. Both feed export streams that require heavy-duty crates for spare drums, valves, pumps, ISO tank fittings, and turnaround spares. The zone also houses minerals processing and food-grade material handling, all of which need wooden pallets and bracing for container stuffing.

Port of Salalah and the transhipment hub

Salalah is a Mediterranean and East Africa transhipment node — a high percentage of boxes landed here move out again on a different vessel within days. That makes the port a place where consolidators and freight forwarders are constantly re-packing, re-blocking and re-bracing cargo for onward voyages. Packing-grade plywood for box-build, dunnage sheets for void-fill, and floor-grade ply for damaged container repair are all routine consumables here.

Raysut Industrial Estate

Raysut, on the western side of the city, is the older industrial cluster — cement (Raysut Cement is one of Oman's largest producers), steel, building materials, and a mix of fabrication units. These sectors typically need shuttering ply for civil works, heavy pallets for cement and steel binding, and packing crates for machinery spares and dies moving in or out of refurbishment.

What we ship to Salalah from Kochi

The packing material that performs in Dhofar is not exotic. It is correctly specified, consistently made, and dispatched on time. Here is what flows out of our contract factories on the Salalah lane.

Packing-grade plywood sheets

Our standard MR-Packing range covers 6 mm through 18 mm in 8 ft x 4 ft sheets. For Salalah-bound exporters we most often supply 8 mm, 12 mm, and 18 mm — 8 mm for top and side panels on light export crates, 12 mm as the workhorse for general industrial packing, and 18 mm for base panels carrying heavy machinery or petrochem spares. Face veneer options include commercial hardwood, rubberwood, and Okoume on a BWR core when the buyer wants a tighter, denser face.

Wooden crates and packing cases

For Salalah Free Zone tenants moving valves, fittings, and turnaround spares, we build closed wooden crates with internal blocking and cleats. ISPM-15 heat-treated marking is standard on every export crate — Oman customs enforces it and rejection at Salalah port is expensive. Typical crate sizes mirror the cargo: full euro-pallet footprint (1200 x 800 mm) for stackable spares, full ISO footprint (1200 x 1000 mm) for heavier loads, and bespoke long-format crates for shafts, rotors, and pipe sections.

Wooden pallets

Standard ISO (1200 x 1000 mm) and Euro (1200 x 800 mm) pallets in two-way and four-way entry configurations. Block pallets where forklift access from all four sides is required for fast quayside handling, and stringer pallets for cost-sensitive one-way export flows. All export pallets are ISPM-15 stamped.

Container flooring and dunnage

For consolidators inside the port handling damaged 20 ft and 40 ft boxes, we cut 28 mm container floorboard panels to spec. For straight dunnage and void-fill, 12 mm and 15 mm packing-grade sheets cut to common widths (300 mm, 400 mm, 600 mm strips) ship as bundled lots.

Kochi to Salalah — the logistics picture

The sea route from Cochin Port to Salalah is roughly 1,600 nautical miles. On a direct service the transit is typically 5 to 7 days; with transhipment at Jebel Ali or Colombo, plan for 10 to 14 days door-to-door. Cochin Port is a 30-minute haul from our Perumbavoor loading yards, so cargo can be at the wharf the same day it leaves the factory.

ItemDetail
Distance (sea)~1,600 nautical miles Kochi to Salalah
Typical transit5–7 days direct; 10–14 days via transhipment
Incoterms we quoteFOB Cochin, CFR Salalah, CIF Salalah
Container load20 ft GP: ~360 sheets 12 mm; 40 ft HC: ~750 sheets 12 mm
DocumentationCommercial invoice, packing list, COO, ISPM-15 certificate, fumigation cert where applicable

For repeat buyers we hold standard SKUs in dispatch-ready bundles, so a confirmed order against an existing spec can be at Cochin Port within 48–72 hours of payment confirmation.

Which grade fits which Salalah load

Three product lines cover almost everything moving out of Dhofar. Pick by load, not by price.

  • Packing-grade plywood (MR-Packing). The default for general industrial crating, spares, valves, fittings, and consolidated transhipment cargo. Cost-efficient, dimensionally stable enough for the Salalah climate, and ISPM-15 compliant when made into crates.
  • Okoume face on BWR core. Where the buyer wants a tighter face for higher-value cargo — instrumentation, electronics destined for downstream refining, or anything that will sit on a quayside for extended periods before re-loading. Pricier; reserve for the loads that justify it.
  • Shuttering ply. For Raysut-based construction and infrastructure buyers casting concrete in the Salalah civil pipeline. Built for reuse, not for export packing.

If a buyer is unsure, the simplest rule is: if it ships, packing-grade is the starting point; if it gets poured against, shuttering is the starting point. Visit our resource hub for the full grade-selection chart and ISPM-15 explainer.

FAQ for Salalah buyers

Is your packing plywood ISPM-15 certified?

The plywood sheet itself is engineered wood and exempt from ISPM-15. The crates and pallets we build from solid-wood components are heat-treated and stamped to ISPM-15 before export. Certificate copies travel with the shipping documents.

What is the minimum order quantity for a Salalah-bound consignment?

For sea freight to make sense, most buyers consolidate to at least a 20 ft container. We can also pool smaller orders into a shared 40 ft HC if timing aligns — contact us to check the next sailing.

Can you build to a customer-supplied crate drawing?

Yes. Send the drawing or a marked-up sketch with dimensions, gross weight, and any internal blocking requirements. We will quote against the drawing and confirm whether it needs a 12 mm or 18 mm base, the cleat schedule, and the lashing points.

Get a quote on the Salalah lane

If you are sourcing packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets, or container flooring for the Salalah belt — whether you are a Free Zone tenant exporting petrochem spares, a port-side consolidator handling transhipment, or a Raysut fabricator moving machinery — we can quote in the format you need: per-sheet, per-crate, or per-container. Forty years of group manufacturing experience sits behind every load, and we ship from Cochin Port on the Salalah lane regularly. Request a quote with your spec, quantity, and target delivery date, and we will come back with rates, lead times, and the nearest sailing.

title: Plywood Supply to Sohar | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, export crates, pallets and container flooring that move heavy industrial cargo out of the Al Batinah belt. Our.
html:
Sohar sits in a strange and useful spot on the GCC map. It is not the political capital, it is not the financial capital, but for anyone who actually moves heavy industrial cargo, Sohar Industrial Port and the surrounding Al Batinah belt is where the steel coils, aluminium billets, and refinery sub-assemblies physically leave the country. That activity sets a very specific demand for plywood: not architectural sheets for showrooms, but tough, dimensionally honest boards that survive forklift loads, sea-spray, and three-week transits. The Cochin Wood group has spent forty years working on exactly this kind of plywood out of Perumbavoor, and we ship into the Sohar industrial belt from our Kochi base regularly enough that the route, the documentation, and the spec sheet are now routine.

The Sohar industrial picture

Sohar's industrial economy is anchored by three clusters, and each one drives a different plywood requirement. Knowing which cluster the buyer is in usually tells us within five minutes what board, what thickness, and what crate profile to quote.

Steel and metals at Sohar Port

Jindal Shadeed's integrated steel complex and the Vale Oman iron-ore pelletising operation sit inside the Sohar Industrial Port footprint. The downstream rebar mills, wire-rod drawers, and cold-rolling units that have grown around them ship heavy, dense, abrasive cargo. Steel coils, billets, and pipe bundles all need plywood that can carry concentrated point loads without crushing, and that will not splinter under banding tension.

Aluminium and the Sohar Aluminium cluster

Sohar Aluminium's smelter and the satellite extrusion and rolling mills inside the Sohar Free Zone push out billets, slabs, and finished extrusions to Europe and South Asia. Aluminium cargo is lighter than steel per cubic metre but far more surface-sensitive, so the plywood specification leans toward smooth-faced packing grades with tighter face quality.

Refining, petrochemicals, and capital equipment

Sohar Refinery and the Liwa Plastics complex generate a steady flow of equipment movement, both for original installation and for shutdown and turnaround windows. Pumps, heat exchangers, control panels, and skid-mounted modules ride out on custom plywood crates. These are the lower-volume but higher-spec orders, where ISPM-15 marking, moisture control, and corner protection matter more than sheer board count.

Free Zone fabricators and general industry

Sohar Free Zone (SOHAR Port and Freezone) hosts food processing, prefab, marine fabrication, and a growing cluster of light engineering tenants on the Al Batinah coastal strip. These tenants order in smaller, mixed batches: a few pallets here, a custom crate there, and occasionally a full container of sheets for their own internal packing line.

What we ship into Sohar

Our standard Sohar quotation usually covers four product families. We try to keep the spec language simple so the receiving warehouse and the freight forwarder both read the same thing.

Packing-grade plywood (MR / commercial)

This is the workhorse. Hardwood face and back, hardwood-core construction, Moisture Resistant glue line, sized for industrial packing rather than furniture. Common thicknesses we move to Sohar:

ThicknessTypical Sohar use
6 mmCrate panel cladding, light box sides
8 mmMid-weight crates, top panels, partitions
12 mmStandard crate bodies for steel and aluminium
15 mm and 18 mmCrate floors, heavy-equipment skids, container flooring

Standard size is 8 x 4 feet (2440 x 1220 mm). Tighter cut sizes are produced to the customer's bill of materials when the volume justifies it.

Okoume-faced plywood

Okoume is offered as a face veneer option on our packing-grade and BWR cores, not a separate grade. It comes into play in Sohar mainly for aluminium and finished-goods crates where face cleanliness matters, and for export buyers who simply prefer the lighter, more uniform face for their own brand crates. Light, smooth, paint-friendly, and visually consistent crate to crate.

Wooden crates and pallets

We supply both flat-pack crate kits (cheaper to freight, assembled at the Sohar end) and assembled crates and pallets where the customer wants ready-to-use units off the container. Common pallet footprints we build for the Sohar lane:

  • 1200 x 1000 mm — the dominant GCC industrial size, suits most steel and aluminium loads
  • 1200 x 800 mm — Euro footprint, for tenants whose downstream customers are in Europe
  • 1100 x 1100 mm — for buyers feeding into South-East Asian destinations

All export wood packaging from our side is ISPM-15 heat-treated and stamp-marked at the treatment yard, which is the spec Omani customs expects.

Container flooring and shuttering ply

For container repair yards and reefer / dry-box refurbishers in the Sohar Port area, we supply 28 mm container-floor grade boards on request. Shuttering ply (filmfaced and plain) is the other line that goes into the Sohar belt, mostly for construction contractors working on plant expansions inside the Free Zone.

Kochi-to-Sohar logistics

The Sohar lane is one of the more comfortable export routes for us. The voyage from Cochin Port to Sohar Port runs roughly 1,500 nautical miles across the Arabian Sea. Direct services and transhipment via Jebel Ali or Khor Fakkan are both routinely available, and our typical port-to-port transit sits in the 6 to 10 day band depending on the carrier rotation. Door-to-port from our Perumbavoor consolidation yard to the Cochin gate is a half-day trucking move, which keeps inland costs low compared to factories sitting further north in India.

On documentation we run the standard kit — commercial invoice, packing list, Certificate of Origin, ISPM-15 fumigation certificate for the wood packaging, and Bill of Lading. Buyers usually take delivery on FOB Cochin or CFR Sohar terms; we will quote either, and we are comfortable working with the buyer's nominated forwarder if there is an existing relationship.

Decision rules — which board for which load

Most Sohar enquiries collapse into one of three load profiles, and the right plywood call is usually clear once we know the cargo.

Heavy, abrasive, dense loads (steel, billets, pipe)

Packing-grade hardwood plywood at 12 mm for body panels and 15 to 18 mm for crate floors and skids. Face cleanliness is not the priority — load-bearing and impact resistance are. Standard MR commercial face is fine.

Surface-sensitive, mid-weight loads (aluminium, finished metals, machined parts)

Okoume-faced packing grade for outer panels and lid; standard hardwood ply for the structural frame. The Okoume face protects the cargo from facing scuffs and gives the buyer a cleaner crate to put their brand on.

Capital equipment and project cargo (pumps, panels, skids)

BWR-glue plywood, 18 mm minimum on the base, custom-built crate with internal bracing. For project shipments that may sit on a quayside for weeks, BWR (Boiling Water Resistant) glue is worth the small premium — it survives humidity, rain, and condensation far better than plain MR.

FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15 compliant wood packaging for Oman?

Yes. All export crates, pallets, and dunnage going from us are heat-treated at an approved IPPC facility and carry the ISPM-15 stamp. The fumigation certificate goes into the export document set so the cargo clears Sohar customs without holds.

Can you produce against a buyer's drawing or bill of materials?

Yes. Custom crate sizes, internal bracing, foam-lined inserts, and labelled panels are routine for our packing line. We ask for a clear drawing or a sample crate; turnaround on first article is usually a week.

What is the minimum order for a Sohar shipment?

Practical minimum is one 20-foot or 40-foot container, mixed product if needed. For smaller volumes we can consolidate with other GCC-bound cargo, but lead time stretches because we wait for the consolidation to fill.

Quote requests

If your operation in Sohar, Liwa, or the broader Al Batinah industrial belt needs plywood, crates, pallets, or container flooring on a recurring basis, send us your size list and monthly volume. We will come back with thickness recommendations, an ex-factory rate card, and a CFR Sohar landed cost so you can compare cleanly against your current source. Request a quote with your specifications, or browse the resource hub for plywood grade explainers, ISPM-15 notes, and crate construction guides written for industrial buyers.

title: Plywood Supply to Doha | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, export crates and pallets that Qatar's box workshops, oil-and-gas turnaround yards and Hamad Port consolidators.
html:
Why Doha's industrial belt sits on our shipping board

Doha is not a heavy-industry capital in the Mumbai or Jebel Ali sense — it is a capital city with a focused industrial footprint that punches above its weight because of Hamad Port, the post-World Cup construction legacy, and a re-export economy that funnels GCC-bound cargo through a single deep-water gateway. For a Kochi-based plywood manufacturer, that profile translates into a very specific demand pattern: ISPM-15 stamped crates for equipment going back out of the country, sturdy pallets for warehouse turnover at Ras Laffan and Mesaieed, and packing-grade plywood sheets that buyers in the Industrial Area south of the city use to fabricate their own boxes. The Cochin Wood group has been building to this kind of brief out of Perumbavoor since 1986, and the Doha lane is one of the cleaner GCC routes we handle today.

The Doha industrial picture

Most plywood-consuming activity in Qatar clusters in three zones, and it helps to name them because the freight terms, the buyer profile, and the typical order size all shift between them.

Doha Industrial Area (Street 1 through Street 50)

The Industrial Area south of the city — the grid of numbered streets that runs out toward Sailiya — is where most of the wooden-box workshops, joinery contractors, and trading companies that resell plywood sheets actually operate. Orders here tend to be mixed-thickness 8×4 ft sheets, picked up in 20-ft container quantities, and turned into crates inside the workshop. Buyers want consistent thickness tolerance and a glue line that holds when a panel saw rips it across the grain.

Ras Laffan and Mesaieed industrial cities

North at Ras Laffan and south at Mesaieed, the demand pattern shifts toward heavy-duty crating for oil-and-gas turnaround equipment, valves, pump skids, and instrumentation packages — typically returning to Europe, Houston, or East Asia after maintenance. These jobs need ISPM-15 heat-treated and stamped timber, screwed crate construction with internal bracing, and sometimes a plywood-skinned crate over a sawn-timber frame. Lead times are tighter and the spec sheets are stricter.

Hamad Port logistics yards and free zone

The Umm Al Houl free zone and the logistics yards inside Hamad Port itself drive a third stream: pallet pools for transit cargo, container flooring panels for re-purposing 20-ft and 40-ft boxes into project shipments, and packing materials for the consolidation operators who pick up the Qatar leg of a longer GCC routing.

What we ship to Doha

The product mix we send to Qatar is narrower than what we move pan-India, because the buyer is almost always packing for export and the freight cost penalises anything wasteful.

Packing-grade plywood sheets

Standard sizes are 8×4 ft and 7×4 ft, in thicknesses from 6mm through 18mm. The two workhorses on the Doha lane are 12mm for crate sides and lids and 18mm for crate bases and floor decks. We supply both commercial/rubberwood packing grade and the Okoume-faced packing grade — the Okoume face is a clean light-coloured veneer that some Qatari fabricators prefer when the end customer photographs the crate before shipping. Both are pressed on the MR-Packing or BWR base; Okoume is a veneer choice on top of those grades rather than a separate grade in its own right.

ISPM-15 crates, knockdown and assembled

For the oil-and-gas and equipment-return jobs we build crates two ways: fully assembled with internal blocking and lifting cleats for direct loading, or knocked-down flat-pack kits with pre-cut panels, marked components, and a fastener pack — the flat-pack form saves a third of the container cube on the outbound Kochi-to-Doha leg and the crate is reassembled on the customer's floor. All structural timber is heat-treated to 56°C core for 30 minutes and stamped to ISPM-15.

Pallets

Two-way and four-way entry pallets in standard 1200×800 mm (Euro), 1200×1000 mm, and the 1100×1100 mm GCC pool size. Top deck either close-boarded plywood or sawn-timber slats depending on the load class.

Container flooring

28mm marine-grade plywood floor panels for container repair and re-flooring, cut to the standard 20-ft and 40-ft box footprints, with hardwood face for screw retention.

Kochi-to-Doha logistics

The shipping lane is straightforward and that is part of why this route works cleanly. Cochin Port to Hamad Port is roughly 1,800 nautical miles direct; the mainline carriers route via either a direct GCC sailing or a Jebel Ali transshipment, with door-to-door transit running 12 to 18 days depending on which window catches the vessel rotation. We dispatch ex-Perumbavoor by road trailer to ICD Cochin or directly to the Vallarpadam terminal at Cochin Port, FCL 20-ft or 40-ft, with the bill of lading consigned per the buyer's customs broker.

For volumes that don't fill a container we consolidate with our other GCC shipments — Dammam, Jebel Ali, Sohar — and the Doha leg moves as a partial via a feeder out of Jebel Ali. INCOTERMS we work most often are FOB Cochin, CFR Hamad Port, and CIF Hamad Port; DAP into the Industrial Area is doable but the in-Qatar leg is sub-contracted to a Doha-side trucker who knows the gate clearances.

Decision rules for Doha-bound loads

The grade question comes up on almost every enquiry, so it is worth laying out the rule of thumb we apply when a Doha buyer is on the line.

Use caseRecommended productWhy
Standard equipment crates, one-trip exportCommercial packing-grade plywood, MR-Packing base, 12mm sides / 18mm baseLowest landed cost, holds spec for the trip, no over-engineering
Customer-facing crates, photographed before despatchOkoume-faced packing grade on the same MR-Packing or BWR baseClean light face accepts stencils and customer logos cleanly
Construction site formwork or repeated-pour shuttering on legacy World Cup infrastructure maintenanceShuttering ply, film-faced where the finish mattersRepeated reuse, water resistance, edge stability
Container re-flooring and project-box conversions28mm marine-grade plywood floor panelsScrew retention, abrasion, ISO container compliance
Pallet pools and warehouse rotationSawn-timber pallets with plywood top deck where load class needs itReplaceable, ISPM-15 stamped, GCC-pool dimensions available

FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15 stamped material into Qatar?

Yes. All structural timber in our crates and pallets destined for Qatar is heat-treated and stamped to the IPPC ISPM-15 standard. The stamp carries our HT mark and the registered facility code, which is what Qatar Customs and onward GCC and EU customs authorities check at gate.

What is the minimum order you ship to Doha?

Practical minimum is a 20-ft FCL — that is roughly 380 to 420 plywood sheets at 18mm depending on density, or around 24 to 28 standard equipment crates flat-packed. Below that we can consolidate with other GCC despatches, but the freight per unit climbs sharply and we will be honest about that when we quote.

Can you quote in QAR and bill against a Qatar-side LC?

We quote in USD by default for export, which is the GCC norm; QAR conversion at the prevailing rate is fine, and we work with confirmed irrevocable letters of credit through the buyer's Doha bank. Payment terms beyond LC at sight are negotiable on repeat orders.

Get a quote on your Doha shipment

If you are packing equipment for export out of the Industrial Area, running a crate workshop south of Doha, or specifying plywood for a project consolidation at Hamad Port, send us the panel sizes, thicknesses, and monthly volumes and we will come back with an ex-Cochin and a CFR Hamad Port quote within a working day. Request a quote with the spec sheet attached, or browse the technical material on our resource hub for grade explanations, packing-list templates, and the ISPM-15 documentation we issue with every Qatar-bound container.

title: Plywood Supply to Mesaieed | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade and Okoume-faced plywood, hardwood crates, pallets and container flooring for the heavy industry clustered around Mesaieed.
html:
Mesaieed Industrial City sits about 40 km south of Doha and is the heaviest piece of industrial real estate in Qatar — a concentrated belt of petrochem, fertilizer, LNG, steel and bulk liquid handling clustered around the deepwater port. Almost every line on that estate eventually exports something heavy in a wooden crate, palletises stainless-steel spares, or imports a packaged spool/skid that arrived on softwood dunnage. That is where Cochin Wood Industries comes in: we ship packing-grade plywood, ISPM-15 crates, hardwood pallets and container flooring out of Kochi into the Qatari industrial supply chain, and Mesaieed is one of the most consistent demand pockets in the GCC.

The Mesaieed industrial picture

Mesaieed is not a generic free zone — it is purpose-built around three heavy verticals that all consume engineered wood packaging in volume. Knowing which belt your loads come from changes the spec we recommend, so it is worth being specific.

Petrochemicals and downstream chemicals

QAPCO, QAFAC, Qatar Vinyl, Qatar Fertiliser Company (QAFCO) and the wider Mesaieed Petrochemical Holding cluster sit inside the estate. The recurring packaging brief is heavy: catalyst drums, reactor internals, valve skids, compressor spares, fertilizer-line refurbishment kits. Crate weights of 800 kg to 3 tonnes per box are routine, and the inner stiffening — gussets, runners and braces — is often more demanding than the skin panel itself.

LNG, gas processing and QatarEnergy operations

Mesaieed is one of the operational hubs for QatarEnergy's liquids and downstream gas business, sitting alongside the Ras Laffan complex further north. LNG project work flows through Mesaieed in the form of turbine spares, cryogenic valves, instrument racks, and long-lead E&I packages. These need crates that survive multi-modal handling, monsoon-grade humidity in the originating port, and 35–45°C heat on arrival.

Steel, port logistics and marine fabrication

Qatar Steel (in its current and successor forms), Qatar Aluminium Extrusion and the dry-bulk operations of Mesaieed Port mean a constant flow of steel coils, billets, extrusions and offshore-fabrication packages. Container flooring replacement, dunnage planking and heavy-duty pallets sit in this belt rather than crating.

What we ship to Mesaieed

The Cochin Wood group has been operating out of Perumbavoor, Kerala since 1986 — roughly forty years of group manufacturing in plywood and sawn timber — and Cochin Wood Industries Pvt Ltd is the trading and export face that consolidates this output for buyers in Mesaieed and the wider GCC. The product range that lands in Qatar is narrower than the catalogue: most Mesaieed orders fall into four buckets.

Packing-grade plywood (commercial and rubberwood)

This is the workhorse. We ship 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 15, 16 and 18 mm in 8×4 ft sheets, MR-bonded, designed to be sawn and nailed in a crate shop rather than polished. 12 mm and 18 mm move in the largest volumes — 12 mm for crate skins and lid panels, 18 mm for base sleds and load-bearing decks under petrochem and LNG spares.

Okoume-faced and BWR packing plywood

For petrochem and LNG buyers who need a longer shelf life on the crate (multiple handlings, outdoor staging in the Mesaieed sun, possible re-export onward), Okoume-faced and BWR-grade boards make sense. The Okoume face takes stencilling cleanly for project marking, and BWR resin tolerates the humidity swing between the Kochi monsoon load-out and the Qatari summer.

ISPM-15 hardwood crates and pallets

Anything wooden that enters Qatar must comply with ISPM-15 — heat-treated, stamped, and traceable. We build closed crates, open crates, skid bases, and four-way and two-way pallets to the buyer's drawing, or to standard footprints: 1200×1000, 1200×800, 1100×1100, and custom oversize for transformer and turbine work. Hardwood runners are jackwood or rubberwood depending on load class.

Container flooring and dunnage

For Mesaieed Port operators and CFS yards rebuilding reefers and dry boxes, we supply 28 mm container-flooring grade panels in standard 2400×1160 mm sizes. Loose dunnage planks in sawn hardwood — 100×100 and 150×150 sections — round out the offer for steel and aluminium shipments.

Kochi-to-Mesaieed logistics

The sea route is the only economic option for this trade lane, and it is a short one by GCC standards.

LegDetail
OriginCochin Port / Vallarpadam ICTT, Kerala
DestinationHamad Port (Doha) with road haul to Mesaieed, ~40 km south
Distance~1,800 nautical miles
Typical transit8–12 days port-to-port, direct or 1-transhipment via Jebel Ali / Khor Fakkan
Container mix20' GP for high-density plywood loads; 40' HC for crates and pallets where volume outruns weight
Inland leg in QatarShort — Hamad Port to Mesaieed Industrial City is under an hour by trailer

FOB Cochin pricing is the cleanest term for first-time Mesaieed buyers; once a steady flow is established, CFR Hamad Port or DAP Mesaieed becomes easier to plan around. We hold a working buffer of standard packing-grade thicknesses, so a confirmed PI for a full 20' container in standard sizes can usually load within 7–10 working days of advance receipt.

Decision rules: packing grade vs Okoume vs shuttering

The wrong specification is the most expensive mistake in this trade. A quick filter for Mesaieed buyers:

  • Use commercial / rubberwood packing-grade plywood when the crate is single-trip, the cargo is reasonably tolerant, and cost-per-square-foot matters. This covers the majority of fertilizer-line, general spares, and steel-mill ancillary shipments.
  • Step up to Okoume-faced or BWR packing when the crate will be staged outdoors at Mesaieed for weeks, will see multiple handlings, or carries optical, electronic or precision-machined cargo that cannot afford moisture pickup. Most LNG and instrument shipments sit here.
  • Use shuttering plywood only where the panel itself is the working surface — formwork for jetty extensions, civil works inside the estate, marine fabrication moulds — not for crating. It is overbuilt for packaging and underbuilt for some chemical exposures.
  • Choose ISPM-15 hardwood pallets over plywood pallets for steel coils, aluminium extrusions, and any load over 1.5 tonnes static. Plywood-deck pallets work well for fertilizer bags and consumer goods, not for heavy industrials.

FAQ for Mesaieed buyers

Do your crates and pallets meet ISPM-15 for Qatar customs?

Yes. All hardwood crates, pallets and dunnage destined for Qatar are heat-treated and stamped with the IPPC mark and the treating facility code. Phytosanitary certification is arranged at Kochi before loading.

What minimum order makes a Kochi-to-Mesaieed shipment economic?

A single 20' GP container is the practical floor — roughly 22–24 tonnes of mixed packing-grade plywood, or around 1,000 sq ft of finished crates depending on the build. Sub-container LCL works for sample orders but the per-unit freight gets heavy.

Can you build to a Mesaieed buyer's drawing rather than catalogue sizes?

Yes — most crate orders are drawing-to-build. Send the cargo dimensions, target gross weight, and any handling notes (forklift pockets, lifting lugs, stencilling), and we quote the panel mix, hardwood frame and ISPM-15 treatment together.

Request a quote

If you are sourcing packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets or container flooring for an operation inside Mesaieed Industrial City — whether it is a QatarEnergy-linked project, a petrochem refurbishment, fertilizer-line maintenance or steel-mill consumables — send across your specs and we will come back with FOB Cochin and CFR Hamad Port numbers, lead time and a packing list. Request a quote with thickness, monthly volume and delivery point, or browse the technical briefs in our resource hub for spec sheets, ISPM-15 notes and container-loading plans before you write the RFQ.

title: Plywood Supply to Ras Laffan | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, export crates and pallets for the case-makers and packaging contractors serving Ras Laffan Industrial City. Our group.
html:
Ras Laffan Industrial City sits roughly 80 km north of Doha and runs the world's largest single-site LNG export complex. Behind every LNG train, sulphur berth, GTL unit and helium plant is a steady flow of process equipment that has to be crated, blocked, braced and floated across an ocean. That packaging demand — quiet, recurring, unglamorous — is exactly the work the Cochin Wood group has been engineered around for four decades. This post walks through what we ship into Ras Laffan, how we ship it from Kochi, and how a buyer in Al Khor municipality should think about specifying plywood for the city's typical loads.

The Ras Laffan industrial picture

Ras Laffan is operated by QatarEnergy and built almost entirely around the North Field — the largest non-associated gas field on the planet. The city is not a generic free zone. It is a single-purpose hydrocarbon export campus, and the packaging needs flow directly from that.

Sectors that drive plywood demand here

  • LNG mega-trains and the North Field expansion — Qatargas / QatarEnergy LNG legacy trains plus the NFE and NFS expansion projects. Heat exchangers, cold boxes, compressor internals, cryogenic spools and valve skids all arrive and leave crated.
  • GTL and petrochemicals — Pearl GTL, Oryx GTL and the Ras Laffan petrochemical complex push a constant repair-and-return flow of catalyst drums, reactor internals and pump assemblies.
  • Helium plants and sulphur handling — Helium 1, 2 and 3 plus the sulphur loading berth keep specialist EPC contractors and equipment OEMs cycling spares through the port.
  • Port and marine services — the Ras Laffan Port handles LNG carriers, LPG, condensate and bulk sulphur. Marine spares, propeller assemblies and turbocharger cores all travel in custom crates.
  • Engineering, fabrication and modular yards — Qatari fabricators serving the city need export-grade crating for pre-assembled modules, skids and structural steel sub-assemblies.

The common thread: high-value, heavy, often temperature- or shock-sensitive cargo moving on tight EPC schedules. Packaging failure here is not a stationery issue — it is a project-delay issue.

What we ship to Ras Laffan

From Kochi we serve Ras Laffan buyers across four product families. Each one is matched to a specific job on the LNG and petrochemical side.

Packing-grade plywood (commercial and rubberwood)

Our workhorse for case-makers and EPC packaging contractors. Standard thicknesses in active production are 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 15, 16 and 18 mm in the 8'x4' format. The 12 mm and 18 mm sheets are the most commonly drawn for side panels and base decks on heavy equipment crates. We can hold tighter glue-line specs on request when a buyer needs a crate that survives sea fog, ambient sulphur humidity and on-site lay-down without delaminating.

Okoume-faced plywood

A premium face-veneer option on our MR-Packing and BWR grades. Okoume gives a cleaner, lighter face and dimensional stability that crating contractors prefer for high-value process equipment — cryogenic spools, instrumentation skids, calibration-sensitive analyzers — where appearance, weight saving and consistent screw retention matter. We treat Okoume as a face option on packing or BWR grade, not as a separate grade card.

ISPM-15 export crates and cases

For buyers who do not run an in-house carpentry shop, we supply finished crates and cases built to ISPM-15 compliance. Cleat-and-batten construction, heat-treated softwood framing, plywood skins, and stencilled IPPC marks. Typical formats are open crates for structural steel, closed cases for instrumentation, and tri-wall cases for vibration-sensitive process equipment.

Pallets and container flooring

  • Pallets — standard four-way 1200x1000 mm and 1200x800 mm, plus custom heavy-duty pallets up to 3,000 kg static load for compressor internals and valve assemblies.
  • Container flooring and dunnage plywood — 18 mm and above for one-way container retrofits, plus cut-to-size dunnage panels for blocking-and-bracing inside open-top and flat-rack containers.

Kochi-to-Ras Laffan logistics

The shipping economics here are very specific. Kochi (Cochin Port and Vallarpadam ICTT) to Hamad Port or directly to Ras Laffan Port sits at roughly 1,800 nautical miles across the Arabian Sea. Typical transit is 9 to 14 days on direct services, longer on transhipment via Jebel Ali or Salalah. We move material in three ways:

  1. FCL from Cochin (Vallarpadam) to Hamad Port — the standard route for full crate-set orders or bulk plywood lifts. 20' and 40' HC containers are loaded at our consolidation yard, sealed, and routed direct.
  2. FCL direct to Ras Laffan Port — where the buyer has port access and is moving against a project import licence; cuts the inland leg from Hamad to Ras Laffan (~150 km).
  3. LCL consolidation — for top-up plywood orders or smaller crate batches feeding an EPC's packing line. Slower, but useful for buyers running just-in-time crate production.

For ISPM-15 cargo, fumigation and stencilling are completed in-yard before container stuffing, so the cargo lands in Qatar audit-ready.

Decision rules — which plywood for which Ras Laffan job

A practical cheat-sheet for buyers specifying material for the city's typical loads:

Cargo profileRecommended spec
Heavy structural steel, modular skids, pipe spool setsCommercial packing ply, 12-18 mm, in cleated open crates
Process equipment, compressor internals, valvesBWR packing ply, 18 mm base + 12 mm sides, fully closed case
Cryogenic spools, analyzers, instrumentationOkoume-faced BWR, 12-15 mm, tri-wall case with desiccant
Formwork and on-site civil packagingShuttering ply, 12-18 mm film-faced
Container floor retrofits / one-way containers18 mm BWR or marine-grade flooring ply
Pallets for 1,500-3,000 kg loadsHardwood frame (jackwood/rubberwood) + 12 mm ply deck

Okoume is not a default; it is the right call when face quality, weight and stability earn back the premium. Commercial packing grade covers the bulk of the volume.

FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15 certified crates ready for Qatari customs?

Yes. Crates ship with heat-treated softwood framing, IPPC stencils, and the supporting treatment certificate so the consignment clears Hamad Port without packaging hold-ups.

Can you build to a Ras Laffan EPC's drawing pack?

Yes. Send dimensioned case drawings with weight, centre of gravity and lifting-point requirements and we will quote against the drawing. We can also reverse-engineer from a sample crate.

What is the smallest order that makes sense to ship from Kochi?

One 20' container of crates or sheets is the practical floor for FCL. Below that, LCL consolidation via Jebel Ali is workable but adds 5-7 days and a per-CBM premium. Buyers running monthly off-take usually find FCL more economical from the third shipment onward.

Request a quote

The Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing plywood out of Perumbavoor since 1986, and CWI Pvt Ltd is the export arm that takes that 40+ years of group manufacturing experience into projects across the GCC. If you are running a packaging line in Ras Laffan, Al Khor or anywhere on the Qatari LNG belt, send us your dimensions, monthly off-take and delivery point and we will build a specification and a per-CBM landed cost. Request a quote with your project details, or browse the resource hub for plywood specs, ISPM-15 notes and crate-construction guides.
