title: Plywood Supply to Manama | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, export crates and pallets to drawing and ship them from Kochi to Manama buyers through Khalifa Bin Salman Port. Our.
html:
Why Manama keeps showing up in our enquiry queue

Manama is small on a map but heavy in the kind of trade that needs timber packaging. The capital sits at the centre of a re-export economy that pulls cargo in from the Gulf, repacks it for onward shipment to Saudi Arabia via the King Fahd Causeway, and feeds finished aluminium, fasteners, and engineered parts out through Khalifa Bin Salman Port and Bahrain International Airport. Every one of those flows needs export-fit crates, pallets and container dunnage. That is where the Cochin Wood group has been quietly supplying buyers in the Bahraini industrial belt for years, shipping packing-grade plywood and built-up cases out of Kochi to Manama-bound importers, EPC contractors, and ISPM-15 packers.

The Manama industrial picture

Manama itself is not where the big-volume packing happens. The capital is the commercial face; the wood-consuming activity is clustered in nearby industrial estates that route most of their inbound cargo through Manama-area importers and clearing agents. If you sell timber packaging into Bahrain, these are the addresses that matter.

Bahrain International Investment Park (BIIP), Salman Industrial City

BIIP at Hidd / Salman Industrial City is the anchor. It hosts aluminium downstream units, electronics assembly, automotive components, food packaging, and a thick layer of logistics 3PLs. These tenants do not run wood-machining lines of their own. They buy packing-grade plywood, sawn timber and pre-built ISPM-15 crates from outside suppliers and assemble export packs in-house or through local job-work packers.

Hidd Industrial Area and Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) supply chain

Alba's billet and rolled-coil exports flow out of Hidd in containerised loads. The downstream extruders, rollers and fabricators that feed off Alba's metal need heavy-duty pallets and edge protection. This is the segment that consumes the bulk of Bahrain's 18 mm and 12 mm packing-grade plywood imports.

Mina Salman and the old industrial port belt

The older Mina Salman / Sitra industrial corridor still runs marine fabrication, oilfield service workshops, and engineering job-shops. These shops ship out skids, sub-assemblies and spares that need custom crates with bolted skids and steel-strapped lids, not just generic boxes.

Khalifa Bin Salman Port (KBSP), Hidd

KBSP is Bahrain's main container gateway, operated by APM Terminals Bahrain. Most plywood and timber cargo from Kochi lands here. The port sits about 13 km from central Manama and feeds directly into BIIP and Hidd Industrial Area by short trucking lanes, which keeps inland haulage costs reasonable for a port-side packer.

What we ship to Manama

Bahrain's buyer profile is fairly consistent: medium-volume, quality-sensitive, willing to pay a small premium for sheets that machine cleanly and crates that pass ISPM-15 first time at KBSP. The Cochin Wood group ships four main product lines into the Manama trade.

Packing-grade plywood

Our workhorse is rubberwood and mixed-hardwood packing-grade plywood, manufactured under Cochin Wood group specifications at our contract factories in Perumbavoor. Standard export pack is the 8×4 ft sheet (2440 × 1220 mm). Common Manama-bound thicknesses are 8 mm and 12 mm for case sides and tops, and 18 mm for skids, bases and heavy-load decks.

ThicknessTypical Manama useSheet size
6 mmLight box tops, internal partitions2440 × 1220 mm
8 mmStandard case sides for electronics, fasteners2440 × 1220 mm
12 mmHeavy case sides, lids, aluminium coil end-walls2440 × 1220 mm
18 mmSkids, base decks, container flooring patches2440 × 1220 mm

ISPM-15 export crates and cases

For Manama buyers who do not want to assemble in-house, we ship knock-down crates: pre-cut plywood panels with sawn-timber framing, heat-treated and ISPM-15 stamped, banded and palletised. Crate sizes are built to customer drawing — common Bahrain orders are 1200 × 1000 × 1100 mm for industrial spares and 2400 × 1200 × 1500 mm for aluminium sub-assemblies.

Wooden pallets

Four-way entry 1200 × 1000 mm and 1200 × 800 mm Euro-spec pallets, sawn from kiln-dried hardwood, ISPM-15 treated. Bahrain's aluminium and food-export sectors mostly run the 1200 × 1000.

Container flooring and dunnage

18 mm to 25 mm hardwood ply or laminated decking sheets for container floor repairs and heavy-load dunnage. Mostly bought by the 3PLs at BIIP who refurbish reefer and dry-box containers for re-export traffic.

Kochi to Manama: the logistics that actually matter

The sea distance from Cochin Port to Khalifa Bin Salman Port is roughly 1,900 nautical miles. Direct services are not common — most Kochi-Bahrain plywood cargo is consolidated into containers that move on a Kochi → Jebel Ali transhipment via mainline carriers, then a feeder to KBSP. Door-to-port transit time runs 14 to 21 days depending on the week's sailing schedule and the Jebel Ali dwell.

For an LCL pallet of plywood it is often cheaper and faster to consolidate via a Jebel Ali groupage agent than to wait for a direct slot. For full-container (FCL) orders — typically 20 ft containers loaded to about 25 to 27 tonnes of plywood — direct booking on a Kochi-origin sailing makes more sense. A 20 ft container handles roughly 550 to 620 sheets of 12 mm packing-grade plywood depending on stack pattern, which is the standard reorder unit for most Bahrain importers.

Decision rules for the Bahrain buyer

Bahrain's typical loads — aluminium downstream products, engineering spares, fasteners, food exports — fit a narrow band of plywood grades. Here is how we think about the choice.

  • Packing-grade plywood is the default for export crates and pallets. It machines cleanly, holds nails and staples, and is priced for repeat-volume crate work. This is what most BIIP packers buy.
  • Okoume plywood makes sense when the buyer needs a lighter sheet for the same panel size — useful for air-freight crates moving out of Bahrain International Airport, or for premium electronics packaging where every kilogram of pack weight is billable. Quote on request — we do not put Okoume on our standard rate card.
  • Shuttering (film-faced) plywood is the right choice when the buyer is a Manama-area construction contractor casting concrete formwork. It is not the right sheet for packing crates — the phenolic film makes it slippery for nailing and overkill on cost.

FAQ for Manama buyers

Do you ship FCL only, or can we start with an LCL trial order?

Both. First-time Manama buyers usually start with a 5-to-10 pallet LCL consolidation to test the grade, then move to 20 ft FCL once their packing line confirms the sheet machines as expected.

Is the plywood ISPM-15 compliant on arrival at KBSP?

The packing-grade plywood sheet itself is a manufactured wood product and is generally outside ISPM-15 scope. The sawn-timber framing inside our built-up crates, and our pallets, are heat-treated and ISPM-15 stamped at origin so they clear KBSP plant-quarantine first time.

What is the typical lead time from confirmed PI to vessel sailing from Kochi?

For standard packing-grade sheets in common thicknesses we hold finished stock — 5 to 7 working days from PI to stuffing. For built-up ISPM-15 crates to drawing, allow 10 to 14 working days for fabrication plus stuffing.

Request a quote for your Manama load

If you are sourcing packing-grade plywood, ISPM-15 crates, pallets or container flooring for a Manama or wider Bahrain delivery, send us the spec — thickness, sheet count or crate drawing, and target month — and we will come back with an FOB Cochin price and a sailing window for Khalifa Bin Salman Port. Request a quote for a Manama shipment, or browse our resource hub for grade specifications, ISPM-15 notes, and container-loading calculators. The Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing plywood in Perumbavoor since 1986 — forty years of group operations — and Bahrain has been part of our GCC ship-to map for a long stretch of that history.

title: Plywood Supply to Sitra | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Sitra ships heavy, irregular, high-value cargo by sea, and that cargo travels in plywood crates on heat-treated pallets. We manufacture the packing-grade.
html:
Sitra is a small island in size but a heavy one in tonnage. Bahrain's refining, petrochemical and downstream metal industries cluster on and around it, which means the export packing flow out of Sitra is not bags and cartons — it is heavy machinery, refinery spares, pump bodies, valves, instrumentation skids and aluminium semis. That kind of cargo does not ship in stretch wrap. It ships in plywood crates, sat on heat-treated pallets, sometimes inside containers floored with hardwood ply. This is the corner of the GCC packaging market where the Cochin Wood group has been quietly relevant for decades, and this post is a practical guide to what we actually move into Sitra and how the lane works.

The Sitra industrial picture

Sitra sits south of Manama, connected by causeway, and functions as Bahrain's heavy-industrial spine. The cargo profile that matters for plywood packing comes from a few specific clusters:

  • Bapco Refining (Sitra refinery): the 267,000+ bpd refinery anchors the island. Refinery turnarounds, modernisation projects and spares cycles generate a constant stream of incoming and outgoing crated equipment — heat exchanger tube bundles, valve assemblies, instrumentation, pump casings.
  • Sitra Industrial Area and Sitra Wharf: the wharf on the eastern side handles a large share of Bahrain's petroleum exports, and the surrounding industrial belt houses fabricators, EPC contractors and freight-forwarders who crate refinery and oilfield gear before it goes outbound.
  • Aluminium downstream around Alba (Askar belt): while Aluminium Bahrain itself is south of Sitra at Askar, the rolling, extrusion and downstream units that draw billet from Alba feed into the same packing supply chain. Coils and bundles travel on heavy pallets with ply caps.
  • GPIC and the Sitra petrochem cluster: Gulf Petrochemical Industries Company on the island runs ammonia, methanol and urea trains. Catalyst skids, vessels and instrumentation move in and out under crated packing.
  • Mina Salman and Khalifa Bin Salman Port (Hidd): Bahrain's container gateway at Hidd, about 15–20 km from Sitra, is where most of this crated cargo ultimately leaves on a vessel.

The common thread is heavy, irregular, high-value cargo moving by sea. That dictates what plywood spec is fit for purpose.

What we ship for Sitra-bound packing

The Cochin Wood group has been in the timber and plywood trade in Perumbavoor, Kerala since 1986 — roughly forty years of group manufacturing across packing-grade panels, sawn hardwood and downstream packing materials. The current Cochin Wood Industries (CWI) Pvt Ltd entity is more recent, but it operates inside that older group setup, which is why the packing-line product range maps so cleanly to what GCC refinery and petrochem packers actually need.

Packing-grade plywood — the core panel

Most Sitra-bound crates are built from MR-grade packing plywood, hardwood core, with Gurjan or Okoume face veneers depending on the buyer's preference. Typical specs we ship into export packing lines:

ThicknessCommon use in Sitra-bound packing
8 mmLight crate cladding, internal partitions, dunnage strips
12 mmStandard sidewall for medium-weight refinery spares, valves, pumps
15 / 16 mmHeavier sidewalls, tops and bottoms for vessel parts and skid-mounted gear
18 mmBases, structural members for very heavy lifts — exchangers, large pump bodies

Sizes are typically 8x4 ft (2440 x 1220 mm), but we cut to size for fabricators who want sheet-optimised crates and less on-site waste. For buyers handling sensitive instrumentation, we supply Okoume-faced panels with tighter surface tolerance — cleaner finish, lower fibre lift, less risk of contamination inside the crate.

Wooden crates and boxes

Most refinery and petrochem packers in Sitra prefer to build their own crates around the equipment, but they buy the panels, sawn timber framing and pallet bases as a kit. We supply both: the ply sheets and the heat-treated (ISPM-15 compliant) sawn timber needed for the framing. ISPM-15 is non-negotiable for any wooden packing entering or leaving Bahrain — every batch we ship out for export packing use carries the IPPC mark and the heat-treatment certificate.

Pallets

The pallet sizes that move easily into and out of Bahrain are the GCC-standard 1200 x 1000 mm and the ISO 1200 x 800 mm. For refinery spares we also build heavier-duty 1200 x 1200 and custom skid pallets to take exchanger tube bundles and vessel sections. All pallet timber is heat-treated and stencilled.

Container flooring plywood

This is a smaller but consistent line into the GCC. Container repair yards and reefer-conversion workshops use 28 mm and 30 mm hardwood plywood with phenolic film face for re-flooring shipping containers. We supply this on order for container fleet operators routing through Khalifa Bin Salman Port and Mina Salman.

The Kochi-to-Sitra logistics lane

Cochin Port (Vallarpadam ICTT) to Khalifa Bin Salman Port at Hidd is roughly 1900 nautical miles. The lane is not direct on most weekly services — boxes typically tranship at Jebel Ali in Dubai, sometimes at Salalah, before feeding into Bahrain. Practical transit numbers we work to:

  • Sea transit, Cochin to Hidd: typically 12 to 18 days door-to-door, depending on the tranship window at Jebel Ali.
  • Inland leg in India: Perumbavoor to Cochin ICTT is about 45 km — material loaded out of our Kerala packing line reaches the gate the same day.
  • Last mile in Bahrain: Hidd port to Sitra industrial area is around 15–20 km by causeway and trunk road, an easy trucking move for the customer's freight-forwarder.

For buyers comparing landed cost, the Kerala origin gives two practical advantages. First, the Perumbavoor cluster is the cheapest hardwood plywood manufacturing belt in India, which keeps FOB Cochin numbers competitive against Indonesian and Chinese alternatives. Second, we are physically next to a deep-water transhipment port, which removes the 1,000+ km inland leg that landlocked North-Indian plywood mills carry.

Decision rules — which grade for which Sitra load

A simple way to choose:

  • Standard packing-grade ply (MR, hardwood core): default for almost every Sitra refinery and petrochem crate. Right cost, right strength, ISPM-15 compliant framing.
  • Okoume-faced packing ply: step up when the buyer wants a cleaner internal surface — instrumentation crates, catalyst skids, anything that should not pick up fibre dust during the 12–18 day transit.
  • BWR / phenolic-bonded ply: use when the crate will sit outdoors in a yard before vessel cut-off, or when the cargo itself is moisture-sensitive and the crate may meet open weather on the Bahrain side.
  • Shuttering ply: not typically a packing product — but Sitra and the broader Bahrain construction belt do consume film-faced shuttering ply for civil works at refinery and petrochem expansion sites. We ship this on the same lane.

FAQ

Do you ship ISPM-15 compliant material for Bahrain customs?

Yes. All sawn timber and pallet stock we ship for export packing carries the IPPC heat-treatment stamp and a heat-treatment certificate. Bahrain follows the standard ISPM-15 import rule for wooden packing material.

What MOQs make sense for a Sitra buyer?

For a mixed FCL out of Cochin we typically build 20 ft and 40 ft loads combining plywood sheets, sawn timber bundles and pallet stock. Smaller buyers often consolidate through a freight-forwarder in Cochin — we can hand over to that forwarder directly from our packing line.

Can you supply pre-cut crate kits rather than full sheets?

Yes. For repeat refinery-spares packers we cut panels to crate dimensions and band them as kits — less waste at the Sitra fabrication shop and faster crate build-up around the equipment.

Quote request

If you are packing refinery equipment, petrochem spares, aluminium semis or any other heavy cargo out of Sitra and want to compare a Kochi-origin packing supply against your current source, the fastest path is to send us the equipment list and required ply specs. We will come back with FOB Cochin numbers, a packing-list breakdown and an indicative sailing window. Request a quote with your spec sheet, or browse the resource hub for the packing grade datasheets, ISPM-15 compliance notes and crate-construction guides we share with GCC buyers.

title: Plywood Supply to Kuwait City | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates and pallets that Kuwait City's port workshops and oilfield packers build with. Our group has milled packing.
html:
Why Kuwait City's industrial belt matters for a Kochi plywood supplier

Kuwait City sits at the head of the Persian Gulf, and almost everything moving in or out of the country either lands at Shuwaikh Port or transits through the workshops that ring the capital. For a Kerala-based plywood mill, that means a steady, predictable pull for one specific product class: packing-grade plywood and timber finished into export-ready crates, skids, pallets and container flooring. The Cochin Wood group has been milling and supplying packing timber out of Perumbavoor since 1986, and a meaningful share of what we ship today moves through the GCC re-export corridor that Kuwait City anchors.

This guide is for procurement and project teams in Al Asimah governorate who need a clear read on what we ship into Kuwait, how the Kochi–Shuwaikh lane actually works, and where our packing-grade plywood, Okoume face plywood, shuttering ply and sawn jackwood fit against the loads that move out of the capital's industrial estates.

The Kuwait City industrial picture

Kuwait City's plywood demand is driven less by furniture and joinery than by export-packing, oilfield service workshops, and re-export trade. The clusters worth knowing are concentrated in a tight crescent around the port.

Shuwaikh Industrial Area

Shuwaikh is the city's largest industrial zone, directly behind Shuwaikh Port. It mixes heavy workshops, automotive trade, building-material yards, packaging contractors and freight forwarders. This is where most of the packing case work for outbound shipments is actually built. Plywood here is consumed by crate-makers framing oilfield spares, electrical panels, generators, valves and palletised general cargo for re-export.

Shuaiba and Mina Abdullah

South of the capital, the Shuaiba Industrial Area and the Mina Abdullah refining belt drive much of the upstream demand. Kuwait Petroleum Corporation (KPC) and its operating arms — KOC, KNPC, KPI — run a long tail of EPC contractors, valve and rotating-equipment vendors, and inspection-service companies that need ISPM-15 compliant crates for tools, instrumentation and spares. Even where the crate is built in Shuwaikh, the spec sheet typically originates from these refining and upstream sectors.

Sabhan, Ardiya and Al Rai

Sabhan and Ardiya carry the lighter industrial work — SME manufacturing, electrical contracting, HVAC fabrication, and a large volume of small-format crating. Al Rai concentrates on building materials and wholesale trade. Plywood demand here skews toward standard pallet sizes and lighter crate-grade ply rather than heavy machinery packing.

Free Trade Zone (Shuwaikh FTZ)

The free zone behind Shuwaikh Port is a re-export hub. Cargo lands, gets repacked or consolidated, and moves out again to Iraq, the wider GCC or East Africa. Repacking work is a steady consumer of plywood sheets, runners and pre-built pallets.

What we ship into Kuwait City

Across these clusters, four product lines do most of the work.

Packing-grade plywood (MR-Packing)

Our core export to Kuwait. Hardwood-core, multi-ply, MR (moisture-resistant) bonded, built for crate sides, tops, bases and skid decking. Standard sizing for Kuwait shipments:

ThicknessTypical use in Kuwait CityStandard size
6 mmLight crate panels, partitions, dunnage8×4 ft
9 mmSpares packing, general-cargo crates8×4 ft
12 mmStandard crate sides for oilfield and engineering spares8×4 ft
15 mm – 18 mmHeavy crate bases, skid decks, container flooring repair8×4 ft

For ISPM-15 compliance, the surrounding crate framework uses heat-treated sawn timber; the plywood panels themselves are exempt under ISPM-15 because they are an engineered wood product.

Okoume face plywood

Okoume is a face-veneer option on our MR-Packing and BWR boards, not a separate grade. It gives a cleaner, lighter, more uniform face — useful in Kuwait when the crated equipment is high-value and the customer wants a presentable export package, or when the contents are sensitive to splinter and dust contamination (instrumentation, lab equipment, medical devices). It is not the right call for rough oilfield spares where cost-per-sheet matters more than finish.

Shuttering plywood

For Kuwait City's construction pipeline — commercial towers in the CBD, infrastructure work around Jaber Causeway, and the steady residential build-out toward Jahra — we ship film-faced shuttering plywood, 12 mm and 18 mm, with phenolic film on both faces. Re-use cycles are the relevant spec, not just face appearance.

Pallets and container flooring

We supply both four-way and two-way entry pallets. Standard sizes that move into Kuwait:

  • 1200 × 1000 mm — GCC/EU common pool
  • 1100 × 1100 mm — intra-Asia and container-optimised
  • 1200 × 800 mm — Euro pallet, for clients shipping onward to Europe

Container flooring is supplied as 28 mm 7-ply hardwood panels for 20 ft / 40 ft container refurbishment, a recurring requirement in Shuwaikh's freight-forwarder and container-repair workshops.

Kochi to Kuwait City — the route

The shipping lane is short by GCC standards, which is what makes Kerala mills price-competitive against South Asian and Far Eastern alternatives.

LegDetail
Origin portCochin Port (Vallarpadam ICTT) or Tuticorin, depending on mainline service
Destination portShuwaikh Port, Kuwait City
Distance~1,800 nautical miles
Sea transit10 – 14 days door-to-port, route and transhipment dependent
Typical transhipmentJebel Ali or Khor Fakkan for non-direct services
Container fit~520–560 sheets of 12 mm plywood per 20 ft FCL, depending on grade and packing

For repeat buyers, monthly FCL programmes out of Cochin Port keep the lane predictable. For smaller volumes, LCL consolidation through Jebel Ali is the usual path. Documentation is straightforward: commercial invoice, packing list, certificate of origin (with Arab-Indian Chamber attestation where required), fumigation certificate where the crate timber is involved, and bill of lading.

Decision rules — which board for which load

A practical short list for buyers in Kuwait City:

  • Oilfield spares, valves, pumps, instrumentation → 12 mm MR-Packing on a 15–18 mm base; ISPM-15 sawn timber frame.
  • Generator sets, transformers, large fabrications → 18 mm MR-Packing skid deck, 12 mm sides, jackwood runners.
  • High-value or presentable export crates → Okoume-faced MR or BWR, 12 mm sides.
  • Construction shuttering in the city's commercial pipeline → 12 mm or 18 mm phenolic-faced shuttering ply.
  • Container refurbishment in Shuwaikh → 28 mm container flooring panels, apitong/keruing equivalent hardwood core.
  • Re-export consolidation in the FTZ → standard 1200×1000 mm hardwood pallets plus 9 mm sheets for repack work.

FAQ — what Kuwait City buyers usually ask

Can you ship FCL direct from Cochin to Shuwaikh?

Yes. Direct and transhipment services both run out of Cochin Port. For mixed loads, we consolidate plywood, sawn timber, ready-built crates and pallets into the same FCL where the cube allows.

Do your boards meet IS:303 / IS:710?

Our MR-Packing grades conform to IS:303 specification; BWR boards conform to IS:710. We can quote both grades and supply mill test certification on request.

How do you handle ISPM-15 for crates and pallets?

Plywood panels are ISPM-15 exempt as an engineered product. Sawn timber components — runners, framework, skids — are heat-treated at certified facilities and stamped to ISPM-15 before shipment.

Get a Kuwait City quote

If you are buying packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets or container flooring into Al Asimah, the fastest path to a usable number is a quick brief on thickness, monthly volume, delivery point (Shuwaikh, Shuaiba, Mina Abdullah or onward) and whether you need ready-built crates or sheet stock. Request a quote with those details and we will come back with EX-Cochin and CFR Shuwaikh pricing, along with a fit assessment against the load type. For background reading on grades, sizes and packing-case construction, the resource hub has the working specs our buyers in the GCC most often ask for.

title: Plywood Supply to Shuaiba | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, heat-treated crates, pallets and container flooring that move equipment and packaged product across Kuwait's.
html:
Why Shuaiba's industrial belt matters to a Kochi plywood manufacturer

Shuaiba sits on Kuwait's southern coast, about 50 km below Kuwait City, and the cluster around Shuaiba Port plus the Shuaiba Industrial Area is one of the densest concentrations of refining, petrochemical and heavy-engineering activity in the northern Gulf. For a Kochi-based plywood manufacturer, what makes that belt interesting is not refining itself but everything bolted on to it: process equipment leaving the country for overhaul, catalyst drums, spare-part assemblies, valves and rotating equipment that need to travel cradled inside ISPM-15 wooden crates, and the constant pallet load that feeds petrochem warehouses. The Cochin Wood group has been making and trading packing-grade plywood in Perumbavoor since 1986, and the Shuaiba lane fits the kind of work we do most days — bulk packing-grade boards, crate-ready sheets and pallet stock pulled in container loads.

The Shuaiba industrial picture

Shuaiba is best understood as three overlapping things sharing one shoreline: the port, the refinery complex, and the industrial estate that grew around them.

Shuaiba Port

Shuaiba Port is Kuwait's main industrial and bulk-cargo gateway, sitting roughly 45 km south of Kuwait City. It handles general cargo, dry bulk, containers and project cargo for the refining and petrochem belt, and it is the closer port-of-call (versus Shuwaikh further north) for cargo destined to the Mina Abdullah and Mina Al-Ahmadi area.

Shuaiba Industrial Area (North and South)

The Shuaiba Industrial Area, split between a Northern and Southern zone, hosts petrochemicals, fertiliser plants, power and water desalination, cement, steel rerolling and a wide ring of supporting fabricators. The estate's tenants are heavy users of packed equipment imports and exports — anything that moves in or out of a process plant tends to travel inside plywood crates with steel banding.

Mina Al-Ahmadi and Mina Abdullah refineries

A short drive up the coast you have the Mina Al-Ahmadi and Mina Abdullah refineries, the largest in the country, plus the newer Al-Zour complex south of Shuaiba. The maintenance, turnaround and capital-project cycles at these refineries generate steady demand for export-grade wooden packing — pumps, heat-exchanger bundles, valves and instrumentation that ship out to OEMs and come back rebuilt.

The downstream petrochem cluster

Equate, the Kuwait Paraxylene Production Company and other downstream petrochemicals operate inside or adjacent to the Shuaiba belt, producing ethylene glycol, polyethylene and aromatics. The export side of that business is largely packaged — bagged on pallets, stacked, stretch-wrapped and loaded into containers — so pallet timber and pallet-grade plywood see continuous pull.

What we ship to the Shuaiba lane

The product mix we move toward Shuaiba and the wider Al Ahmadi governorate is narrower than our domestic Indian mix, because the buyer here is almost always a packer, a fabricator or an EPC sub-vendor — not a furniture or interior trade. The four lines that dominate:

Packing-grade plywood (MR-Packing)

This is the workhorse. Hardwood-core packing-grade boards in 6, 8, 9, 12, 15 and 18 mm, 8x4 sheet format, used to skin export crates and to build internal partitions, supports and cradles. The board has to take nails along the edge without splitting, take steel strapping under tension, and hold up through monsoon-season ocean transit. We supply with a commercial face that's clean enough to stencil shipping marks directly onto the board.

Okoume-faced plywood

Where the box is going through multiple handovers, or where the buyer wants a lighter, finer-grained face, we offer Okoume face veneer on the packing or BWR core. Okoume is not a grade in itself — it's a face-veneer option layered on our standard packing and BWR constructions. It's a sensible spec for premium pharma, instrumentation and specialty-chemical exports where the crate is part of the product image.

Wooden crates and ISPM-15 boxes

For Shuaiba buyers who'd rather receive a finished crate than build it themselves, we fabricate ISPM-15 heat-treated wooden boxes and skid-mounted crates to drawing — including reusable knock-down designs for repeat petrochem and refinery shipments.

Pallets and container flooring

Standard 1200x1000 and 1200x800 pallets in hardwood, plus heavier 4-way entry pallets sized for petrochem bag-loading. For 20-ft and 40-ft container conversions and tank-skid bases, we cut 18 mm and 21 mm hardwood plywood to container-floor dimensions.

Sawn hardwood and battens

Jackwood and rubberwood scantlings to runners, skids and corner-post sizes for buyers who fabricate crates in-house at their Shuaiba or Sabhan workshops.

Typical specs we quote into Shuaiba

ProductCommon specTypical use
Packing-grade plywood12 mm, 8x4, hardwood coreCrate side panels for refinery equipment
Packing-grade plywood18 mm, 8x4, hardwood coreCrate base, heavy-load skids
Okoume-faced BWR9 mm, 8x4, BWR constructionPremium pharma and specialty chemical export boxes
Pallet plywood15-18 mm, cut to sizeTop decks for petrochem bag pallets
Container flooring21 mm hardwood ply, cut to floorTank-skid bases, container refurbishment
ISPM-15 cratesBuilt to drawing, HT-stampedProcess equipment and spares

Kochi-to-Shuaiba logistics

The lane economics are friendly. Cochin Port (and the deeper-draft Vallarpadam ICTT) to Shuaiba Port is roughly 1,800 nautical miles, a typical ocean transit of 8 to 12 days direct, or 12 to 16 days on the more common feeder + transshipment routings via Jebel Ali, Khor Fakkan or Salalah. Kerala's plywood belt sits 30-40 km inland of Cochin Port, so the inland trucking leg from our Perumbavoor consolidation point to the container yard is short and cheap — a meaningful cost advantage compared to material sourced from north Indian or far-eastern origins.

What this means in practice

  • FCL bookings on the Cochin-Shuaiba lane are routine; a 20-ft container takes roughly 540-560 sheets of 12 mm 8x4 packing ply, depending on packing density.
  • FOB Cochin pricing is the cleanest starting point; we also quote CFR Shuaiba on request.
  • Documentation is straightforward — commercial invoice, packing list, certificate of origin and, where the buyer requires it, fumigation and ISPM-15 certification for crates.

Choosing the right board for a Shuaiba shipment

Three rules-of-thumb we share with first-time buyers in the belt:

  1. Default to MR-Packing for general industrial export crates — refinery spares, structural steel components, valves, pumps. The cost-to-strength ratio is the best in our range.
  2. Specify Okoume face when the box is part of brand presentation — pharma exports, instrumentation, premium chemical drums. A lighter face also reduces total crate weight on air-freight conversions.
  3. Move to shuttering ply (BWP/BWR) only if the end-use is concrete formwork at a Shuaiba construction project. For packing applications, our MR-Packing grade is already engineered for the load.

FAQ: Shuaiba buyers ask

Do you supply ISPM-15 certified crates from Kochi?

Yes. We heat-treat and stamp crates to ISPM-15 at registered facilities in the Kochi-Perumbavoor belt and ship them either flat-packed (knock-down) or pre-assembled, depending on container fit.

What's the minimum order for a Shuaiba shipment?

One 20-ft FCL is the practical floor. Below that, LCL is possible but the per-sheet landed cost climbs quickly.

Can you cut sheets to non-standard sizes before loading?

Yes. We cut to buyer drawings in Perumbavoor before container stuffing — common for container-floor refurbishment work and for crate-kit pre-cuts.

Request a quote

If you're sourcing packing-grade plywood, ISPM-15 crates, pallets or container flooring for a Shuaiba, Al Ahmadi or wider Kuwait operation, send us a line-item with thickness, sheet size, monthly volume and delivery terms (FOB Cochin or CFR Shuaiba). We'll come back with an FOB Cochin rate, a lead time and a loading plan within one working day. Request a quote or browse our resource hub for specs, packing-case guides and ISPM-15 references.

title: Plywood Supply to Mundra | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates and pallets that move cargo through India's busiest commercial port. Our group has manufactured in.
html:
Mundra is the busiest commercial port in India, and the industrial belt that has grown around it now consumes more export packing material than most state capitals. Adani Ports SEZ, the Mundra petrochemical cluster, the Kutch edible-oil refineries and the polymer warehouses that sit on the western corridor all share one daily requirement — defect-free packing plywood, crates that survive a sea voyage, and pallets that move under forklift without splintering. This is the lane the Cochin Wood group has been shipping into for years, and the page below explains exactly what we send, how the logistics work, and where each grade fits.

Why Mundra is an interesting plywood market

Mundra is not a single factory cluster. It is a port-led economy with several layers of demand stacked on top of each other, and each layer buys plywood for a different reason. Polymer and chemical importers need clean dunnage for re-bagging lines. Edible-oil refiners and pulse millers crate machinery spares that move back to OEMs across India. The SEZ-based engineering exporters build heavy crates for shipment to the GCC, East Africa and Southeast Asia. And the container yards that handle the port's 7-million-TEU throughput need a steady refurbishment supply for damaged floors — every container that comes off a ship with a cracked floorboard is out of rotation until it is re-planked.

The result is a market where a Kerala-origin supplier can compete on quality even after factoring in the 1,900 km trucking lane, because Mundra buyers value consistency and BIS-grade glue lines more than they value a 200 km radius.

The Mundra industrial picture

If you are sourcing for Mundra, the plywood demand maps to a few specific belts:

  • Adani Ports SEZ (Mundra Port) — container freight stations, custom-bonded warehouses, ICDs, and the export packing units that operate inside the bonded zone. Heavy crating for project cargo, ODC equipment, and palletisation of FMCG-for-export.
  • Mundra petrochemical and polymer cluster — bagging and re-bagging lines for HDPE, LDPE, PP and PVC granules arriving in bulk. Plywood is used as slip-sheets, dunnage, and pallet decks for forklift movement.
  • Kutch edible-oil and agri belt (Gandhidham–Anjar–Bhuj corridor) — refiners, soybean and mustard crushers, pulse millers. Machinery spares and finished-goods crates flow back through Mundra.
  • Gandhidham timber market — the historical hub for sawn timber and packing material in Kutch, used by smaller exporters and by traders who consolidate orders for the SEZ.
  • Container repair yards — concentrated around the port approaches and the Adani Logistics Park, doing both reefer and dry-box refurbishment.

Each of these sub-markets has a slightly different plywood spec, and we quote each one separately rather than pushing a single grade across the whole region.

What the Cochin Wood group ships to Mundra

Packing-grade plywood

Our core line into Mundra is MR-bonded packing-grade plywood in commercial hardwood and rubberwood face options. Standard thickness range is 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 15, 16 and 18 mm in 8x4 sheets. The grade is built for export crates and pallet decking — uniform thickness, full-area face, no telegraphing, and a glue line that passes a 72-hour cold-water soak. For the polymer and edible-oil clusters where loads are repetitive and the box pattern is fixed, 8 mm and 10 mm hardwood ply are the workhorses. Heavier machinery crates for SEZ exporters typically use 12 mm or 18 mm.

Okoume plywood

Okoume is offered as a premium face option on our MR-Packing and BWR grades, not as a separate structural grade. It is the right choice when a Mundra exporter is shipping into the GCC or European markets and the receiving end inspects the crate face — Okoume gives a clean, light, uniform appearance and accepts stencilling well. Okoume is quoted on request and is priced separately from the commercial packing card.

Export crates

We supply built and knock-down crates for project cargo, machinery, and consolidated FMCG exports. The crate construction uses our packing-grade plywood as the skin, jackwood or rubberwood runners as the framing, and is delivered ISPM-15 compliant where the destination requires heat-treated wood. Crate sizes are built to the customer's cargo dimensions; we do not stock-and-sell fixed sizes for Mundra because the SEZ flow is too varied.

Pallets

Standard pallet sizes shipped to Mundra are 1200x800 mm (Euro), 1200x1000 mm (ISO), and 1100x1100 mm (GCC/Asia). Two-way and four-way entry, with plywood top deck on hardwood runners. ISPM-15 heat-treatment marking available on the runner stock. For polymer-bagging lines we also build heavier-duty pallets with double bottom boards rated for repeated forklift cycles.

Container flooring plywood

For the container repair yards near the port, we supply 28 mm container floor plywood in the standard 2400x1160 mm and 2400x2400 mm cut sizes, hardwood face, phenolic bonded. This is a specific spec — not packing ply, not shuttering ply — and is quoted as a separate line.

Kochi to Mundra logistics

The trucking lane runs roughly 1,900 km from our Perumbavoor loading point to Mundra Port via NH-66 along the west coast through Mangaluru, Goa, Mumbai's Bhiwandi belt, Vapi, Surat, Ahmedabad, and then west through Samakhiali into Kutch. Typical transit time for a full truck is 5 to 7 days depending on the season and the Mumbai-Surat congestion. We move in 20-ft and 32-ft trailers and in containers when the volume justifies it.

For larger or recurring orders, coastal shipping from Kochi to Mundra via Mumbai/JNPT is a viable alternative — longer transit, but per-tonne economics improve once you cross full-container quantities. We discuss the route option at the quoting stage based on volume and the buyer's preference.

All rates are quoted ex-Perumbavoor; GST and transport are extra. For Mundra specifically, we are happy to quote delivered-to-site as well, since we run the route regularly enough to give a firm freight number.

Which grade fits which Mundra load

Typical Mundra loadRecommended gradeCommon thickness
Polymer / chemical re-bagging dunnageMR Packing-grade (commercial hardwood)8 mm, 10 mm
Edible-oil and agri machinery cratesMR Packing-grade (rubberwood or hardwood)12 mm, 15 mm
SEZ heavy export crates / project cargoMR Packing-grade, BWR for monsoon shipment15 mm, 18 mm
GCC / EU export with face inspectionOkoume face on MR or BWR base12 mm, 18 mm
Standard ISO / Euro pallet decksMR Packing-grade hardwood10 mm, 12 mm
Container floor refurbishmentContainer floor plywood, phenolic bonded28 mm
SEZ shuttering / civil worksShuttering ply (film-faced where needed)12 mm, 18 mm

The rule we follow when a Mundra buyer is unsure: packing-grade for domestic-bound consolidations, BWR or Okoume when the cargo will see a sea voyage and humidity exposure, and dedicated container flooring for anything going into a CHASSIS or box-floor build. We do not push a higher grade than the application needs.

FAQ

What is the typical lead time for a full truck to Mundra?

From order confirmation, 3 to 5 working days to load at Perumbavoor, plus 5 to 7 days transit. So plan on 8 to 12 days door-to-door for a standard packing-ply truckload. Pre-cut and ISPM-treated stock can extend this by 2 to 3 days.

Can the Cochin Wood group supply against an Adani SEZ purchase order with the SEZ documentation?

Yes. SEZ-bonded supply requires the buyer's SEZ unit approval, ARE-1 / GST documentation, and our regular E-way bill. We handle the paperwork side once your buyer details are in.

Do you provide ISPM-15 marking on crates and pallets shipped to Mundra?

Yes — for export-destined crates and pallets we supply with the heat-treatment mark on the runner stock. Mention the destination country at the quoting stage so we apply the correct treatment record.

Get a Mundra quote

The Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing plywood in Perumbavoor since 1986 — 40+ years of group operations behind the current product range. If you are crating for an SEZ exporter, re-bagging polymer at the port cluster, or running a container repair yard near Mundra, send us the spec and the monthly volume and we will come back with a delivered-to-site quote within a working day. Start with a short brief on our request a quote page, or browse the grade comparison and load-case studies in our resource hub first if you want to firm up the spec before reaching out.

title: Plywood Supply to Pipavav | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring that move heavy export cargo through Pipavav's deepwater terminals. Built in.
html:
Pipavav is one of the more interesting port-led industrial pockets on the west coast. APM Terminals Pipavav sits on the Saurashtra coast in Amreli district, with a hinterland that stretches up through Bhavnagar, Rajula, Sihor and the GIDC estates feeding it. The cargo mix here is not just containerised exports — it includes RoRo car shipments, heavy project cargo, agri commodities and a steady flow of engineering goods from inland Gujarat. That mix creates a very specific plywood need: export-ready packing cases, ISPM-15 compliant pallets, and container flooring that holds up under multi-modal handling. The Cochin Wood group has been supplying these grades from Kerala into Gujarat for decades, and Pipavav-bound consignments are now a regular outbound lane for our packing-case range.

The Pipavav industrial picture

Pipavav port itself is a deepwater all-weather port owned by APM Terminals, with container, dry bulk, liquid and RoRo terminals. It is the only port in India with direct rail connectivity for RoRo car exports — Maruti Suzuki, for example, runs car trains down to Pipavav from Gurugram and Hansalpur. That single fact tells you a lot about why the packing requirement around Pipavav is heavier than the average minor port.

Around the port and up the Bhavnagar belt, the sectors that buy plywood-based packaging at scale include:

  • Auto and auto-component exporters — feeding the RoRo and container lines, often using returnable wooden cases for engine assemblies, transmissions and body panels.
  • Engineering and machinery makers in the Bhavnagar, Sihor and Chitra GIDC estates, shipping heavy fabrications, valves, pumps and gearboxes.
  • Diamond and gem-tool exporters further inland (Bhavnagar is a polishing hub) needing smaller, higher-value cases.
  • Ship-breaking yard at Alang, ~70 km from Pipavav — a steady consumer of crating ply for re-rolled steel, machinery and salvaged equipment heading out as cargo.
  • Agri and bulk processors in Amreli and Junagadh districts that crate processed goods and machinery for both export and domestic dispatch.
  • Chemical and plastics manufacturers in the wider Saurashtra cluster, who often case-pack finished goods on plywood pallets for FCL export.

The common thread across these segments is that the cargo is heavy, the journey is multi-leg (factory → trucker → port → ocean), and the buyer at the far end is unforgiving about case damage. Packaging that "just looks okay" doesn't survive that chain.

What we ship to Pipavav

Packing-grade plywood

Our base grade for Pipavav-bound work is MR packing-grade plywood — hardwood core, phenolic-glue bonded, calibrated for crate manufacturing. Standard thicknesses we move regularly:

ThicknessTypical use at Pipavav
6 mmLight cases, internal partitions, dunnage
8 mmCarton-reinforced cases, smaller export cases
10 mmGeneral export crates, ~100–300 kg payload
12 mmStandard heavy crate, auto components, RoRo packing
15–16 mmMachinery cases, ship-breaking salvage crates
18 mmHeavy fabrications, container flooring panels

Sheet sizes are typically 8x4 ft, but we cut to size at the factory for high-volume crate buyers — this avoids both wastage and the labour of resawing at the customer's end.

Crate construction

For port-direct Pipavav cargo, the crate spec matters as much as the ply. The combinations we build to:

  • Plywood skin (10–18 mm) over a sawn-timber frame (pine, jackwood or rubberwood runners depending on load).
  • ISPM-15 heat-treated and marked timber for any structural member, because the destination port — not Pipavav — is what enforces it. Buyers shipping to EU, US, Australia or the GCC need this without exception.
  • Through-bolted or screwed corner joinery for cargo above ~500 kg; nailing alone is not sufficient for the road leg up to Pipavav.
  • Internal bracing and skid mounts where the cargo is fragile or top-heavy.

Pallets

The pallet sizes we supply most often into Pipavav-bound consignments are 1200x1000 mm (standard export), 1200x800 mm (Euro) and 1100x1100 mm (Asia-Pacific containerised). Two-way and four-way entry are both available. Block pallets are preferred where forklift access from all four sides matters at the destination port.

Container flooring

For high-cube and project cargo where the floor of the container itself takes damage from heavy handling, we supply 18 mm plywood flooring panels — keruing-faced where the buyer specifies it, or okoume-faced for premium export work. This is a specific ask that comes up more from Pipavav than from a typical port, because of the RoRo and heavy-cargo profile of the terminal.

Kochi to Pipavav — logistics

Pipavav is roughly 1,800 km by road from Kochi, with the route running up via Bengaluru–Hubballi–Belagavi–Pune–Vadodara–Rajkot and then west into the Saurashtra coast. Trailer transit time is typically 5–7 days door-to-door depending on the season and the number of trucks moving in convoy. We move 32-ft single-axle and multi-axle trailers on this lane, with full-truck loads of cut-to-size plywood, pre-fabricated crate panels or finished pallets stacked tight.

For buyers who prefer it, we also ship coastal — Kochi to Mundra or Hazira, and trans-shipment into Pipavav. The lane economics shift depending on diesel and trailer availability, so we quote both options. For repeat buyers we hold a small running stock at the destination and dispatch on call, which removes the lead-time question on urgent crating jobs.

Decision rules — which grade for which load

The choice of grade should follow the cargo, not the other way round. A useful rule of thumb for Pipavav-bound work:

  1. MR packing-grade plywood — default for export crates, pallets and general port packing. Phenolic-bonded, holds up across the road leg and the ocean leg under normal humidity.
  2. Okoume-faced plywood — for premium export packing, high-value engineering goods, machinery cases that need a clean face, and container flooring on long-haul deepsea routes. Lighter, more uniform face, better screw retention.
  3. BWR/shuttering ply — not normally the right grade for packing. Worth specifying only for site-cast concrete formwork on construction projects in the Bhavnagar or Pipavav port-side infrastructure builds.
  4. Sawn timber (jackwood / rubberwood runners) — for skids, runners and crate frames under the plywood skin. Sized to the crate's payload.

FAQ

Can you supply ISPM-15 compliant pallets for Pipavav exports?

Yes — we supply heat-treated and stamped pallets and crate timber for all export consignments. The treatment is done at registered facilities and the IPPC stamp is applied per the standard. We can quote with or without ISPM-15 depending on whether the cargo is domestic or export.

Do you deliver direct to the freight forwarder or only to the factory?

Either. For Pipavav-bound jobs we regularly deliver pre-fabricated crate panels and assembled pallets straight to the CFS or the forwarder's warehouse close to the port, so the cargo is packed at the last possible point. This reduces handling damage on the long road leg.

What is the minimum order quantity?

For full-truck loads from Kochi we quote from a single 32-ft trailer onwards (roughly 18–22 tonnes depending on the mix). Smaller orders are best aggregated with other buyers in the lane — talk to us before splitting an order.

Get a quote

If you crate engineering goods, auto components, ship-breaking salvage or any export cargo through Pipavav, send us the crate dimensions, payload, destination port and the monthly volume you expect. We will come back with a delivered or ex-Kochi quote, plus a recommended grade and crate spec. Request a quote through the contact form, or browse the resource hub for grade guides, sizing tables and ISPM-15 notes that may help before you confirm the spec.

title: Plywood Supply to Bhavnagar | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring board that Bhavnagar's Alang yard plots and GIDC estates run on. Built in.
html:
Bhavnagar sits at one of the most unusual material-flow crossroads in India. On one side of the district is Alang — the world's largest ship-breaking yard — where steel, machinery and salvaged equipment are cut down, sorted and trucked out for re-sale or export. On the other side are GIDC plastic, brass and engineering estates feeding containers into Pipavav and Mundra. Both streams need one thing in common: timber-based packaging that survives long road hauls, marine humidity and rough mechanical handling. That is the gap the Cochin Wood group has been filling for buyers in Saurashtra — packing-grade plywood, ISPM-15 crates, pallets and container flooring board, shipped up the West Coast from Kochi.

Why Bhavnagar's industrial belt drives unusual packaging demand

Bhavnagar is not a single-cluster city. Three distinct demand pools sit inside a 50 km radius, and each one buys plywood for very different reasons.

Alang-Sosiya ship-recycling yard

The Alang-Sosiya belt stretches roughly 10 km along the Gulf of Khambhat and processes a large share of the world's end-of-life merchant vessels. Once a ship is beached and cut, the recoverable items — marine engines, gearboxes, generators, propellers, navigation electronics, copper coils, valves, pumps, machine tools — move out of the yard by road. Most of these items are heavy, irregular, oily and headed for re-sale buyers in Mumbai, Delhi NCR, Ludhiana and the GCC. They need custom timber crates, edge-protected pallets and dunnage boards. The packaging spec is dictated by the salvaged item, not a standard SKU, which is why bulk packing-grade plywood in mixed thicknesses is the workhorse material here.

GIDC plastic, brass and engineering estates

Bhavnagar GIDC, Chitra GIDC and the nearby Sihor and Vartej clusters host a large plastic-processing base — pipes, fittings, woven sacks, household moulded goods — plus brass parts machined for export and a strong base of engineering job-shops. Container exports out of these estates run through Pipavav and Mundra ports, and exporters need pallets, crates and container flooring board that meets ISPM-15 fumigation standards. Plywood is the preferred case wall material because it does not need the same heat-treatment paperwork as solid sawn timber.

Salt, soda-ash and chemical movements

The coastal stretch up to Bhavnagar is one of India's largest salt and soda-ash producing regions. While bulk salt itself does not ride on plywood, the machinery, spare parts and process equipment moving in and out of these plants do — and they typically arrive crated.

The industrial picture: where the plywood actually lands

  • Alang-Sosiya yard plots — packing-grade plywood for ship-salvaged machinery crates, sized to the item
  • Bhavnagar GIDC and Chitra GIDC — pallets and container flooring board for plastic and brass exporters
  • Sihor and Vartej job-shop clusters — small to mid-volume engineering crates
  • Bhavnagar port and Pipavav APM Terminals — feeder volumes for general export packing
  • Mundra port (driveable in around 4 hours) — high-volume container exports that some Bhavnagar exporters route through

This mix is what makes Bhavnagar a "packing materials" town rather than a furniture or interior plywood town. The grade conversation is almost always about strength, moisture tolerance and ISPM-15, not about face polish.

What the Cochin Wood group ships to Bhavnagar

Packing-grade plywood

Our standard packing range covers commercial and rubberwood plywood from 6 mm to 18 mm. The thicknesses Bhavnagar buyers move most often are 8 mm and 10 mm for case walls on lighter exports, 12 mm as the all-purpose crate skin, and 16-18 mm for heavy machinery crates coming out of Alang. Sheets are MR-bonded for normal packing and BWR-bonded where the consignment sees rain or sea-side staging. We can also dispatch Okoume-faced sheets where the receiving buyer (typically an overseas brass or auto-component importer) specifies a uniform light-coloured face for the case.

Finished crates and packing cases

For exporters who do not run an in-house carpentry shop, we supply assembled crates and knock-down case sets. Construction is plywood walls on a sawn-timber frame, with internal cleats, corner pieces and floor runners specified to the load. Standard sizes are quoted to your container plan; non-standard sizes are built to drawing.

Pallets

The two most common pallets we ship into Saurashtra are the 1200 x 800 mm Euro pallet and the 1100 x 1100 mm standard export pallet. We supply both block and stringer constructions, with heat-treated stamps where the buyer is exporting under ISPM-15.

Container flooring board

For 20 ft and 40 ft container floor lining — common with brass scrap, plastic granule and machinery exporters — we supply 18 mm and 21 mm full-sheet plywood, BWR-bonded, sized to the container floor.

Sawn timber for crate frames

Where the buyer prefers to build crates in-house, we also ship jackwood and rubberwood runners cut to standard frame sections.

Kochi to Bhavnagar: the logistics view

Bhavnagar sits roughly 1,900 km from Kochi by road. The typical haul is via NH-66 along the West Coast through Mangalore, Karwar, Goa, the Konkan, into Maharashtra, across to Gujarat through Vapi and Surat, and then onto Bhavnagar via Vadodara or the coastal road. Transit time on dedicated trailers is normally 5 to 7 days door-to-door, depending on monsoon conditions on the Konkan stretch.

For full-truck-load orders we dispatch on 20-ft or 32-ft trailers from our Perumbavoor yard. Mixed-thickness loads are common — a single trailer might carry 6 mm packing sheets, 18 mm flooring board and a stack of pallets, all on one delivery note, which is far more efficient than booking three partial shipments. For exporters running tight cycles, container-direct dispatch via Cochin Port to a nearby West Coast port is also possible, and the comparison is worth running on bulk orders.

Choosing the right grade for the load

Load typeThicknessGradeWhy
Light brass parts, plastic moulded goods8 to 10 mmMR packing-grade plywoodCost-controlled, dry-shed staging
Engineering goods, exporting via Pipavav12 mmBWR packing gradeTolerates port-side humidity
Alang-salvaged marine engines, gearboxes16 to 18 mmBWR packing or shuttering plyMechanical load, sharp corners
20 ft and 40 ft container flooring18 to 21 mmBWR full sheetForklift point loads
Premium-face export cases10 to 12 mmOkoume-faced plywoodUniform light face, GCC buyer spec
Construction site shuttering12 mmShuttering ply (film-faced)Repeat-pour, not for packing

Shuttering ply is occasionally requested by Bhavnagar buyers, but it is engineered for concrete formwork and is not the right product for export crates — the film face is a cost without a function in packaging.

FAQ for Bhavnagar buyers

Do you supply ISPM-15 heat-treated material?

Plywood by manufacturing process is exempt from ISPM-15 fumigation requirements. For the solid sawn timber components in crates and pallets — frames, runners, cleats — we supply heat-treated and stamped material where the buyer is exporting under ISPM-15. Confirm the requirement on the enquiry so the right stamping is arranged.

Can you handle Alang yard delivery and gate-pass paperwork?

Yes. We dispatch to the yard plot or to your nominated godown in the Alang-Sosiya belt and our transporter coordinates the local gate-pass and unloading. Lead time from order confirmation is typically 7 to 10 days for stock thicknesses.

What is the minimum order to make Bhavnagar dispatch viable?

A single trailer load is the most economical unit. Below that, freight-per-sheet rises sharply because the West Coast haul is fixed cost. Mixed-thickness loads on one trailer are encouraged.

Request a quote for Bhavnagar

If you are running a crating or pallet operation in Bhavnagar GIDC, Chitra, Sihor, or any of the Alang yard plots, share your typical thickness mix, monthly volume and delivery point — request a quote and we will come back with a rate-card and a sample dispatch plan. For thickness guides, ISPM-15 background and other technical references, see our resource hub. The Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing in Perumbavoor since 1986, and packaging is the part of the catalogue we know best.

title: Plywood Supply to Jamnagar | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, export crates and pallets that Jamnagar's brass exporters, engineering shops and port-side crating yards run on.
html:
Why Jamnagar is a packing-plywood town

Jamnagar is a strange and useful city for anyone who sells packing-grade plywood. In one district you have the world's largest brass parts cluster shipping small, dense, high-value components in plywood-lined cartons; the world's largest single-location oil refinery on the coast at Motikhavdi crating spares and equipment; and Sikka Port handling project cargo that needs ISPM-15 wooden cases. Three different load profiles, three different plywood specs, one freight lane down to it from Kerala. Below is how the Cochin Wood group thinks about supply into Jamnagar — what we ship, why, and how the Kochi-to-Jamnagar leg actually works.

The Jamnagar industrial picture

The brass cluster is the loudest signal. Jamnagar accounts for the dominant share of India's brass parts exports, and most of that output moves through micro and small units concentrated in GIDC Phase I, Phase II and Phase III at Shankar Tekri, plus the older Dared, Digjam and Hapa belts. These are cable glands, sanitary fittings, automotive terminals, valve bodies, electrical accessories — small components, but each kilogram has real value, and every consignment for Europe, the Gulf or North America needs to be palletised, sometimes crated, and almost always export-compliant.

Around that you have:

  • Reliance Jamnagar refinery at Motikhavdi and the SEZ at Moti Khavdi — process equipment, instrumentation spares, catalyst drums, project cargo. Crates here tend to be heavy, often custom-built, occasionally tropicalised.
  • Sikka Port and Bedi Port — bulk and project cargo, with refinery-linked outbound and inbound steel structures, vessels and pipes that need timber dunnage and crating.
  • Bandhani textile and food processing units around Khambhalia Road — lighter crating, more pallet work.
  • Dhrol and Jamnagar GIDC engineering shops — CNC components, pump castings, valves bound for OEMs across India and the Gulf.

Each of these segments buys plywood differently. The brass exporter wants a clean 8 mm or 10 mm sheet that crates a 25 kg carton without splitting at the staple line. The refinery contractor wants 18 mm BWR for crates that may sit on a project site through a monsoon. The pallet maker wants consistent thickness and weight so loaded pallets stack predictably in a container.

What the Cochin Wood group ships to Jamnagar

Packing-grade plywood

This is the workhorse. Commercial and rubberwood-core packing-grade ply, ex-factory from our group network in Perumbavoor, in the thicknesses Jamnagar actually orders.

ThicknessTypical use in JamnagarIndicative ex-factory rate (per sq.ft)
6 mmLight brass-part carton liners, top/bottom capsRs 24
8 mmStandard brass component cases, lid panelsRs 28
10 mmMedium engineering crates, pallet deck boardsRs 31.5
12 mmHeavy crate sides, valve and pump casesRs 33.5
18 mmRefinery equipment crates, container flooring repair, runnersRs 42

Rates are ex-factory; GST and transport are extra. The base rule is purchase rate plus Rs 2.50 per sq.ft on this packing range — kept deliberately simple so a buyer can sanity-check a quote against their carpentry costing in one minute.

Crate construction

For Jamnagar's heavier outbound — refinery spares, large brass valve bodies, finished pump assemblies — we supply timber and plywood pre-cut to the customer's crate drawing, or we ship the materials and let the buyer's carpentry shop build to ISPM-15 if the consignment is bound for export through Mundra or Pipavav. Typical crate skin is 12 mm or 18 mm packing-grade ply over a hardwood framework, with jackwood or rubberwood runners.

Pallets

For Jamnagar pallet makers and for in-house refinery and SEZ pallet replacements, we supply the deck plywood (10 mm and 12 mm) plus sawn runners. Common footprints are the standard 1200 x 1000 mm export pallet, the 1100 x 1100 mm GCC pallet (heavily used for shipments out of Sikka to UAE and Saudi), and the 1200 x 800 mm euro pallet for European brass buyers.

Container flooring

For container repair shops working near Sikka and along the Mundra feeder lane, we supply 18 mm hardwood plywood as flooring replacement stock. Not the headline volume, but a steady ancillary line that often pairs with the same lorry as a packing-ply order.

Kochi to Jamnagar — the logistics reality

The road distance from Perumbavoor to Jamnagar is roughly 2000 km, typically routed Salem - Bengaluru - Hubballi - Pune - Surat - Rajkot - Jamnagar, or via the Konkan corridor depending on the operator. A full-truck 32-foot multi-axle load lands in five to seven days door-to-door once it leaves our dispatch yard, assuming no monsoon disruption on the Western Ghats.

A few practical notes from running this lane:

  • 32-foot SXL with around 9.5 tonnes of packing ply is the sweet spot. Below that, per-sheet freight gets ugly.
  • For partial loads, we consolidate with other Gujarat-bound consignments out of Kochi — adds two to three days but cuts freight cost meaningfully for first-time Jamnagar buyers.
  • Coastal break-bulk via Cochin to Pipavav is an option for very large project orders, but the road lane is usually faster and cheaper for the kind of 5 to 15 tonne consignments Jamnagar typically orders.
  • Delivery is normally to the buyer's godown in Shankar Tekri, Dared or the GIDC plots; refinery-bound material goes to the contractor's yard, not direct to the refinery gate.

Which grade for which Jamnagar load

Three decision rules that have held up across this freight lane:

  1. Packing-grade commercial or rubberwood ply — default for brass carton work, pallet decks, and any crate that ships and then gets discarded. Cost-efficient, predictable, calibrated for one-trip packaging.
  2. Okoume face plywood — quote on request. Worth specifying when the buyer wants a cleaner face for export crates that will sit on a customer's receiving dock and represent the brand, or for premium GCC-bound consignments. Priced separately from the packing card.
  3. Shuttering ply — for the construction side of Jamnagar's expansion (warehousing in GIDC, civil works at the SEZ). Different spec, different conversation, but on the same truck if the buyer is mixed-segment.

Jamnagar buyer FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15 fumigated crates direct?

We supply the plywood and timber. ISPM-15 stamping is done by the buyer's carpentry shop with a certified fumigator, which is the standard practice across Gujarat's export belt. We can recommend specs that suit ISPM-15 compliance.

What is your minimum order to Jamnagar?

For a dedicated truck, one full 32-foot load. For consolidated freight, we work down to roughly 2.5 to 3 tonnes — useful for first orders or trial batches before a buyer commits to FTL.

How long has the Cochin Wood group been doing this?

The Cochin Wood group has operated out of Perumbavoor since 1986, more than four decades of plywood and timber. CWI Pvt Ltd is the corporate vehicle through which we run the modern packing and Okoume business, including the Gujarat lane.

Getting a quote

If you are sourcing for a brass exporter in GIDC, a pallet shop near Khambhalia Road, a refinery contractor in Motikhavdi, or a port-side crating yard at Sikka, the fastest way to a usable number is to send thickness, sheet size, monthly quantity and delivery pin code. We will come back with an ex-factory rate, a freight estimate on the Kochi-Jamnagar lane, and a realistic dispatch window. Request a quote directly, or browse the resource hub for specs, grade comparisons and packing-case guides before you write the enquiry.

title: Plywood Supply to Rajkot | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates and pallets that move Rajkot's engineering exports to drawing. Our group has run its plywood operation out.
html:
Why Rajkot's industrial belt pulls steady plywood demand

Rajkot is the casting, forging and machine-tool capital of Saurashtra, and that one sentence explains nearly every plywood enquiry that reaches us from the city. When a CNC lathe bed, a ductile-iron casting, a forged crankshaft or a pump housing leaves a Rajkot shopfloor for a buyer in Germany, the United States, Vietnam or the GCC, it does not travel naked. It travels inside a wooden crate or strapped to a pallet, and the panel material that holds that crate together is packing-grade plywood. With more than 10,000 engineering MSMEs concentrated in and around the city, Rajkot generates one of the densest concentrations of export-crate demand anywhere in western India. That is the niche the Cochin Wood group has been building plywood for since 1986.

The Rajkot industrial picture

Rajkot's manufacturing footprint is spread across several well-defined industrial estates, each with its own packaging rhythm. Knowing the geography matters because it shapes truck routing, unloading windows and the kind of crate spec a buyer typically asks for.

Aji GIDC

The oldest and densest of the Rajkot estates, Aji GIDC houses a heavy mix of foundries, machine shops and auto-component units. Castings shipped out of Aji tend to be irregular and dense, which pushes buyers toward heavier panel thickness, typically 12 mm and 18 mm, with reinforced corner blocks.

Lodhika GIDC (Metoda)

Lodhika, on the Rajkot-Jamnagar highway, is the largest industrial estate by area and houses larger forging units, CNC builders and pump manufacturers. Crate orders from Lodhika frequently call for ISPM-15 stencilled material because the goods are export-bound to Europe, the United States and ASEAN.

Shapar-Veraval belt

Shapar-Veraval is the machine-tool and submersible-pump cluster on the Rajkot-Ahmedabad corridor. The work that leaves Shapar is often containerised, which is where 12 mm and 18 mm container flooring panels and full crate kits come into the picture.

Bhavnagar Road and the smaller clusters

The Bhavnagar Road belt and adjacent smaller estates handle a long tail of bearing, fastener, valve and brassware units. Volumes per unit are smaller but the cumulative pallet and small-crate demand is significant and recurring.

What we ship into Rajkot

Cochin Wood Industries supplies the Rajkot engineering belt with four overlapping product lines. All material ships to CWI specifications from contract factories operated under the Cochin Wood group, whose manufacturing operations have run out of Perumbavoor in Kerala for forty years.

Packing-grade plywood (the workhorse)

Our core SKU for Rajkot is hardwood packing-grade plywood in BWR-bonded construction. Standard offer:

ThicknessTypical use in RajkotStandard size
6 mmSide panels for light crates, separators, inner partitions8x4 ft
8 mmLids, inner ribs, light pallet decks8x4 ft
12 mmCrate walls for castings and machined parts8x4 ft
16 mm / 18 mmHeavy crate bases, forging crates, container flooring8x4 ft

Standard 8x4 ft sheets ship most commonly. We cut to size on request when a Rajkot buyer is running a repeating crate spec and wants to skip the in-house cross-cut step.

Export crates and case material

For machine-tool and large-casting exporters, we supply pre-fitted crate kits or panel sets dimensioned to the customer's part envelope. Typical construction is a hardwood frame with packing-grade ply skin, 12 mm or 18 mm depending on gross weight and stacking load. ISPM-15 heat-treated timber is available for any consignment routed through ICDs to overseas ports.

Wooden pallets

Two-way and four-way pallets in standard 1200x1000 mm and 1200x800 mm footprints are common for Rajkot's pump, valve and casting exports. Custom footprints for irregular machine bases are quoted on drawing.

Container flooring

For exporters loading 20-ft and 40-ft containers ex-Mundra or Pipavav, we supply 12 mm and 18 mm packing-grade ply as floor layout panels and load-spreaders under heavy castings and machine assemblies.

Kochi-to-Rajkot logistics

The road distance from our Kochi/Ernakulam dispatch hub to Rajkot is approximately 1,850 km. Transit by full truck load typically runs five to seven days, depending on monsoon conditions and the specific drop point inside the GIDC network. Most of our Rajkot lane volume moves by direct FTL on the Kochi - Bengaluru - Hubballi - Pune - Ahmedabad - Rajkot corridor, with the final leg routing into Aji, Lodhika, Shapar or the Bhavnagar Road belt as needed.

For coastal economics on larger orders, an alternative is rail-container or coastal-shipping movement from Kochi/Tuticorin to Mundra or Pipavav, with a short road leg of 250-350 km into Rajkot. This route gets economically interesting for orders above a couple of truck loads and where the buyer can absorb a slightly longer transit in exchange for lower per-sheet freight. We work out the trade-off at quote stage based on the consignment size and the customer's preferred port.

Decision rules: which CWI grade fits which Rajkot load

Rajkot's load mix is varied, so the right material is not always the same. A simple rule of thumb:

  • Packing grade BWR ply is the right answer for most casting, forging and machine-component crates leaving Rajkot. The bond is moisture-resistant enough to handle a monsoon sea passage, and the cost-per-crate is right for export packaging that is consumed once.
  • Okoume-faced plywood makes sense when the end-buyer is in Europe or the United States and the crate is part of the brand presentation, or when the consignment is high-value machine tools where surface finish and grain matter. Okoume is also lighter, which helps on chargeable-weight-sensitive export lanes.
  • Shuttering ply is a separate conversation. Rajkot's construction activity around Kalavad Road, the 150 Ft Ring Road and the upcoming AIIMS-Rajkot corridor drives steady film-faced shuttering demand. This is not export packaging material and should not be substituted for crate-grade.

Our practical advice to first-time Rajkot buyers: start with a small mixed trial - one or two pallets of 12 mm and 18 mm packing-grade in standard 8x4 ft - confirm the panel performs across your typical crate spec, then move to a recurring monthly indent.

FAQ for Rajkot buyers

Do you supply ISPM-15 compliant crate material?

Yes. Heat-treated, ISPM-15 stencilled timber components are available for any export crate destined for an overseas buyer. Specify on the enquiry so we route the order through the certified line.

What is the minimum order quantity for an FTL into Rajkot?

A full 20-tonne truck typically carries 12-15 m3 of plywood, depending on thickness mix. For smaller indents below FTL, we consolidate with other Gujarat-bound consignments, which adds a few days of dispatch wait but keeps freight workable.

Can you cut to size for a repeating crate spec?

Yes. Once you share the cutting list and confirm the spec is recurring, we cut at the dispatch end and ship as a bundled kit. This saves cross-cut waste and shopfloor time at the Rajkot end.

Get a Rajkot quote

If you run a casting, forging, machine-tool, pump or valve unit in Aji, Lodhika, Shapar or anywhere across the Rajkot industrial belt and you are looking for a reliable Kerala-origin plywood supply line for your export packing, send us a panel spec and a monthly volume. We will come back with a per-sheet EX-Kochi rate, an FTL freight estimate to your GIDC address and an indicative dispatch window. Request a quote or browse the resource hub for crate specs, ISPM-15 notes and packing-grade datasheets.

title: Plywood Supply to Surat | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates and pallets that Surat's mixed industrial base runs on, from heavy Hazira fabrication to the Sachin and.
html:
Surat is one of the densest industrial cities in western India, and the plywood demand that flows out of it is unusually varied. On one shift, a packer in Sachin is building a crate for a synthetic-fabric loom heading to Africa. On the next, a fabricator in Hazira is bracing a 12-tonne pressure vessel for ocean freight out of Hazira Port. A few kilometres away, a textile machinery exporter in Pandesara needs ISPM-15 pallets stacked four-high inside a 40-foot container. The Cochin Wood group has been making packing-grade plywood for exactly this kind of mixed-load industrial city since 1986, and this note explains what we ship to Surat, how we route it, and how to decide what your line actually needs.

The Surat industrial picture

Surat does not run on a single industry. Three large clusters drive packing-plywood consumption, and each one buys a slightly different specification.

Hazira industrial area

Hazira is the heavy end of the city. Reliance, L&T Heavy Engineering, Essar Steel (now ArcelorMittal Nippon Steel), ONGC and Shell run plants along the Hazira coast, and the supporting ecosystem of fabricators, valve makers, structural shops and shipyard contractors is where most of Surat's export-crate volume originates. Loads here are heavy and dimensionally awkward — reactors, heat exchangers, distillation columns, large skid packages, drilling equipment. Plywood for this segment has to take fastener pull-out, survive monsoon staging at the port, and not delaminate when the crate is repositioned three or four times before stuffing.

Sachin and Pandesara GIDC

South of the city, the Sachin and Pandesara GIDC estates are dominated by synthetic textile mills, dyeing and processing units, and the textile-machinery and chemical units that supply them. Crating here is lighter in mass but high in count — loom parts, jacquard heads, rapier assemblies, spare modules — and the buyers want consistent thickness and clean edges because crates are stacked and rolled inside the container.

The diamond belt — Mahidharpura, Varachha, Katargam, SDB

Surat polishes roughly 90% of the world's diamonds, and the cluster around Mahidharpura, Varachha, Katargam and the Surat Diamond Bourse runs a parallel demand for cleanroom-grade pallets, machinery crates for laser cutters and planning equipment, and secure containerised flooring for high-value transit. Volumes per crate are small, but specification discipline is tight.

Hazira Port and the export gateway

Adani Hazira and the Essar Hazira terminal handle a meaningful share of western India's project cargo and break-bulk. Anything moving through these gates touches a wooden crate, a pallet, or a piece of dunnage at some point. That is where most of our Surat freight ends up.

What we ship to Surat

We supply three product families to the city, plus sawn timber where the fabricator wants to build runners and skids in-house.

Packing-grade plywood

This is the workhorse for Sachin, Pandesara and the lighter Hazira loads. We make it in the standard range — 6mm, 8mm, 10mm, 12mm, 14mm, 15mm, 16mm and 18mm — on 8x4 sheets, with rubberwood and commercial hardwood cores. Density and glue line are tuned for crate building: the sheet has to hold a 50mm nail without splitting, take a screw at the edge, and survive a Mumbai monsoon transit without the face veneer lifting.

Okoume-faced plywood

Okoume is a face-veneer option on our packing and BWR grades, and it earns its place on the high-end Surat loads — pharmaceutical machinery from the chemical-zone clients, precision textile heads, and any consignment where the crate itself will be photographed for the consignee. The face is smooth enough to stencil cleanly, and it ages better in tropical port staging than commercial faces.

Crates and packing cases

For fabricators who would rather buy a finished crate than build one, we supply knock-down and assembled cases sized to the load. Typical Surat sizes start at 1200x800 for textile-machinery sub-assemblies and run to custom 6000x2400 cases for Hazira project cargo. Sawn-timber runners — jackwood for the heavier loads, rubberwood for the lighter — are dimensioned to the truck and the container floor.

Pallets and container flooring

Standard four-way entry pallets at 1200x1000 and 1100x1100 are the default for textile machinery and diamond-belt transit. For project cargo through Hazira Port, we ship 18mm packing-grade sheets cut to container-floor dimensions for one-trip dunnage — a common spec for skids that need to slide cleanly during stuffing.

Kochi-to-Surat logistics

Surat sits about 1,700 km from Kochi by road via Mumbai, and the trucking lane is one of the better-developed corridors on the west coast. A 32-foot single-axle covered truck typically clears Kochi to Surat in 4 to 5 days door-to-door, depending on the Mumbai bypass and the monsoon. A 22-foot truck moves faster on smaller orders. For larger, recurring volumes — full-container parcels destined for re-export — we also route via Cochin Port and the Mundra/Hazira coastal shipping leg, which can be more economical for 20-tonne-plus consignments and removes road handling.

RouteModeTypical transitBest for
Kochi – Surat direct32-ft road truck4 – 5 daysFull truck, single drop
Kochi – Mumbai – Surat22-ft / part-load5 – 7 daysSmaller orders, multi-drop
