Cochin Port – Hazira PortCoastal shipping7 – 10 daysHeavy recurring volumes

All rates we quote are ex-factory; GST and transport are billed separately so the buyer keeps visibility on the freight line.

Decision rules — which grade for which Surat load

The choice between packing grade, okoume-faced and shuttering ply is a question of load weight, journey length and what the crate is signalling to the consignee.

  • Packing grade, commercial face, 8 – 12mm — default for Sachin and Pandesara textile-machinery crates, diamond-belt machinery transit, and any sub-3-tonne load with a short ocean leg.
  • Packing grade, 15 – 18mm — Hazira project cargo, heavy fabrication, anything that needs a structural deck or container floor. 18mm is the workhorse for cases above 5 tonnes.
  • Okoume-faced — pharmaceutical and precision-machinery exports from the chemical zone, premium textile heads, anything where the crate face will be inspected at destination.
  • Shuttering ply — not a crating product. We supply it to Surat construction projects where the customer also runs a packing line, and want one vendor for both.

For most first-time Surat buyers, we recommend starting with a trial truck of 12mm and 18mm packing grade and adjusting from there once your line foreman sees how it nails and edges.

FAQ

What is the minimum order you ship to Surat?

We prefer full-truck loads — a 32-foot truck holds roughly 28 – 30 tonnes of mixed plywood — because the freight economics are clean. For trial orders and smaller buyers, we consolidate with other Gujarat parcels on a 22-foot truck. Single-pallet orders are not our model.

Can you supply ISPM-15 stamped pallets and crates?

Yes. Pallets and crates for export through Hazira Port can be heat-treated and ISPM-15 stamped at our packing partners. We need consignee-country and dimensions at the quotation stage so the stamp is valid for the destination.

How do you handle thickness tolerance for fabricators who CNC-cut their crates?

Our packing grade is dimensioned at face value above 12mm and the trade-standard "11+" and "17+" tolerance below. Fabricators in Pandesara who run CNC nesting lines usually ask for a fixed-thickness batch — we accommodate that on order sizes of one truck or more, and it gets noted on the PI.

Get a quote for Surat

If you are running a packing line in Hazira, Sachin, Pandesara, the diamond belt, or anywhere else in the Surat industrial area, we can quote a delivered price within a working day. Share your thickness, sheet size, monthly volume and delivery PIN code and we will come back with an ex-factory rate, the freight estimate to Surat, and a transit window. Request a quote or browse the resource hub for spec sheets, grade comparisons, and crating guides used by our Surat buyers.

title: Plywood Supply to Vadodara | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, wooden cases and pallets that ship under drums, reactor parts and pharma cartons leaving Vadodara's plants. Our.
html:
Vadodara sits at the crossroads of three industrial corridors that all generate serious packing volume: the petrochemical cluster around Koyali and Dumad, the pharma belt running north toward Halol and Savli, and the engineering and automotive footprint that has expanded along NH-48. Every drum, every reactor part, every vial-filled carton that leaves these plants needs a wooden case or pallet underneath it. That is the demand we ship into from Kochi. This page covers what we send, how we route it, and how a buyer in Gujarat should think about specifying plywood for the kind of cargo Vadodara typically moves.

The Vadodara industrial picture

Vadodara is one of the few Indian cities where you can drive twenty kilometres in three directions and hit three completely different export profiles. The packing requirements look quite different in each one.

Petrochemicals and chemicals

The Koyali refinery belt and the chemical units around Nandesari GIDC, Ranoli and Dumad generate steady demand for heavy-duty drum packing, skid bases for pumps and valves, and protective crates for catalyst and resin shipments. Drum collars, four-way pallets rated for 1,000 to 1,500 kg, and plywood-walled cases for dangerous-goods consignments are the typical asks.

Pharma and fine chemicals

The Halol GIDC corridor and the Savli industrial area host a dense pharma cluster — APIs, formulations, intermediates. Pharma export crates need to be clean, ISPM-15 stamped, and consistent in dimension because the cargo inside is high-value and temperature-sensitive. Repeat tenders for monthly export shipments through Mundra and JNPT are common from this belt.

Automotive and heavy engineering

Waghodia GIDC, Manjusar GIDC and the units along the Vadodara-Halol highway handle machined components, hydraulic assemblies and earth-moving spares. Loads run heavy and odd-shaped, so the packing usually means thicker plywood walls, internal bracing and custom skid bases rather than off-the-shelf box sizes.

Other estates worth naming

  • Makarpura GIDC — mixed engineering and ancillary units, regular flow of medium-weight crates.
  • Por-Ramangamdi — chemical and process units.
  • GIDC Vadodara (Gorwa) — older estate with mixed manufacturing.
  • Padra and Jambusar belt — petrochem expansion zone, growing pallet demand.

What we ship into Vadodara

The Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing plywood in Perumbavoor since 1986, and the packing-grade range is what we move the most of into Gujarat. Here is what typically goes onto a truck heading north.

Packing-grade plywood sheets

Our MR-Packing grade comes in thicknesses from 6 mm up to 18 mm in standard 8x4 sheets. Buyers in Vadodara usually want a working mix:

  • 6 mm and 8 mm — top and bottom panels of light pharma crates, internal partitions.
  • 10 mm to 12 mm — side walls of mid-weight export cases, drum collars.
  • 15 mm to 18 mm — base panels under heavy machinery, container flooring patches, skid construction.

The face veneer can be specified as commercial hardwood, rubberwood, or Okoume on the BWR variants where appearance and moisture tolerance matter for longer sea legs.

Finished crates and cases

For buyers who do not want to fabricate in-house, we supply ready-to-pack wooden cases built to ISPM-15 specification with the heat-treatment stamp. Standard footprints we make routinely: 1200x800 mm, 1200x1000 mm, and custom drum-cluster crates for petrochem shipments. Internal cleating and corner bracing are added on the bill of materials when the cargo asks for it.

Pallets

Four-way entry pallets in standard EUR (1200x800 mm) and ISO (1200x1000 mm) sizes, plus custom footprints for specific machine bases. Load ratings up to 1,500 kg static for the heavy-engineering work that comes out of Waghodia and Manjusar.

Container flooring

For exporters consolidating their own containers at the factory, we supply 28 mm container flooring plywood — apitong-equivalent hardwood-face panels in the standard 2400x1160 mm size. Useful for refurbishing older containers or stuffing one-trip shipments where the existing floor is suspect.

Kochi to Vadodara logistics

The road distance from our Perumbavoor dispatch yards to Vadodara is roughly 1,750 km. Two routes work, and we pick based on load size and timing.

RouteTransit timeBest for
Direct trucking via Bengaluru-Pune-Mumbai-Vadodara (NH-48 corridor)5 to 7 daysFull-truck loads of sheets, crates, or pallets going direct to a factory gate
Coastal shipping Cochin Port to Mundra, then road to Vadodara10 to 14 daysLarge repeating orders where ex-works price matters more than urgency
Rail rake to Vadodara via container terminal7 to 10 daysMixed loads consolidated with other Gujarat-bound material

A 32-foot single-axle truck out of Perumbavoor carries roughly 14 to 16 tonnes of plywood, which works out to around 350 to 400 sheets of 18 mm or proportionally more of thinner stock. For pharma buyers who run monthly shipments, a planned dispatch schedule keeps freight per sheet predictable and avoids spot-rate volatility.

Decision rules: which grade for which Vadodara load

Use MR-Packing grade when

  • The crate is going by road or short sea route to a domestic or GCC destination.
  • Cargo weight is under 2 tonnes per case.
  • The receiver does not require a hardwood-face cosmetic finish.

Use Okoume-faced BWR when

  • The shipment is going by sea to Europe, the US, or longer routes via Mundra.
  • The cargo is pharma export or high-value chemical product where the receiving auditor scrutinises packing material.
  • Multiple humidity cycles are expected in transit.

Use shuttering ply when

  • Buyer is on the construction side — civil contractors building new factory blocks in Halol or Savli often pull shuttering ply alongside packing material on the same truck.
  • The application is concrete formwork rather than packing.

FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15 heat-treated crates for export shipments out of Mundra and JNPT?

Yes. All export crates carry the ISPM-15 heat-treatment stamp from an approved facility. The compliance certificate travels with the dispatch documents so the freight forwarder can clear customs without delays.

What is the minimum order quantity for a direct dispatch to Vadodara?

For sheets, we prefer to load a full truck (14 to 16 tonnes) because part-load freight from Kerala to Gujarat eats into the unit economics. For finished crates and pallets, the minimum depends on the spec — small batches of custom crates can ride with a sheet load to the same destination.

How are prices quoted — delivered Vadodara or ex-Perumbavoor?

Both, depending on what the buyer prefers. Our standard price card is ex-factory at Perumbavoor with GST and freight extra. For repeat customers we also issue delivered Vadodara quotes that lock in the trucking lane, which makes monthly budgeting easier.

Getting a quote

If you are sourcing packing material for a Vadodara plant — pharma, petrochem, automotive, or general engineering — share the thickness, sheet size, monthly volume and delivery point. We will come back with a delivered rate, available dispatch dates, and the right grade recommendation for the cargo. Request a quote with your spec, and if you want to read more on grade selection, ISPM-15 norms, or container flooring before deciding, the resource hub has the technical guides we point most buyers at.

title: Plywood Supply to Guntur | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crate panels, runners and pallets that Guntur's export yards load every season. Made in Perumbavoor, where our.
html:
Why Guntur sits on our pan-India dispatch map

Guntur is one of those Andhra cities that quietly drives a disproportionate share of India's export tonnage. The world's largest chilli market sits here at Mirchi Yard, Lam. The tobacco auction floors at Tobacco Board complexes in Guntur and the surrounding belt move hundreds of crores of FCV leaf every season. And the granite quarries running north and west of the city, around Chimakurthi and the Prakasam-Guntur axis, feed black-galaxy and Indian-black slab orders out to the Middle East, Europe and East Asia. Every one of those three streams ends the same way at the dispatch yard: with someone asking for packing-grade plywood, crate panels, runners, or pallet boards. That is the conversation the Cochin Wood group has been having from Kochi for close to four decades.

The Guntur industrial picture

Before we talk specs, it helps to map where the demand actually sits. Guntur's industrial activity is not concentrated in one estate the way it is in some metros. It is spread across several belts that each have a different packaging fingerprint.

Granite and stone export belt

The Chimakurthi-Ongole-Martur stone cluster, technically straddling Prakasam and Guntur districts, is the heart of Andhra's black-granite trade. Slab gangsaw units, polishing lines and container-loading yards along the NH-16 corridor crate up 20-foot containers bound for Krishnapatnam, Chennai and Kattupalli ports. Each container floor needs hardwood bearers and ply lining; each slab bundle needs A-frame crates with face panels rigid enough to take strapping pressure without flexing into the polished surface.

Tobacco auction and warehousing zone

The Tobacco Board's Guntur complex, plus the Prakasam and Karepalli auction platforms, generate seasonal demand for bale crates and case-lining ply. FCV tobacco moves in standardised hessian bales that get repacked into wooden cases for marine export — humidity-resistant, ISPM-15-stamped, and dimensionally consistent so they stack predictably in the container.

Chilli, turmeric and agri-processing

Mirchi Yard at Lam village handles roughly 1.5 lakh bags of dry chilli daily at peak. Most of that moves domestically in jute, but the export portion — to Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, the Gulf — goes out in plywood-lined crates or on plywood-topped pallets. Spice processors around Patibandla and Tenali run similar workflows for turmeric and coriander.

Engineering and auto-component supply

Smaller but growing: auto-component fabricators around Vatticherukuru and the Guntur-Mangalagiri industrial corridor crate up assemblies for OEM despatch to Chennai, Pune and Pithampur. These crates are smaller and lighter than granite cases, but they are higher in volume and demand cleaner ply faces.

What the Cochin Wood group ships to Guntur

The mix we send up the lane is shaped by those four streams. Here is what tends to load onto a Guntur-bound truck.

Packing-grade plywood

Our packing-grade range — commercial hardwood and rubberwood faces on a hardwood core — is the workhorse for crate panels, case sides, and slip-sheets. Standard thicknesses we move in volume: 6mm, 8mm, 10mm, 12mm, 15mm and 18mm, in 8x4 sheets. For granite crates, the typical call is 12mm face panels with 18mm bottom boards. For tobacco cases, 8mm and 10mm dominate because the bale weight is moderate but rigidity matters. For chilli and agri-export crates, 6mm and 8mm linings are most common, often glued to a sawn-timber frame.

Okoume-faced plywood

Okoume is a face-veneer option on our packing and BWR grades, not a separate grade in itself. We position it where the buyer wants a uniform pinkish-tan show face — typically for export crates that will be opened by a customer at the destination, or for tobacco cases where the trade prefers a clean, knot-free appearance on the outside of the box. Granite exporters occasionally specify Okoume on the lid panel for the same reason.

ISPM-15 stamped crates and pallets

Anything bound for an overseas container needs heat-treated, ISPM-15 marked wood. We ship pre-cut crate kits and assembled pallets with the IPPC stamp. Common pallet footprints for the Guntur lane: 1200x1000mm (granite slab bundles), 1100x1100mm (tobacco cases), and 1200x800mm Euro for spice consignments routed via European buyers.

Container flooring boards

20-foot reefer and dry containers loading granite at Krishnapatnam need replaceable hardwood floor boards under the slab A-frames. We supply 28mm-thick BWP hardwood flooring planks in container-floor dimensions, both for new builds and for floor-repair operators in Vijayawada and Vizag who service the Guntur stone trade.

Kochi-to-Guntur logistics

The lane is roughly 1,350 km by road. Most of our Guntur dispatches take NH-544 from Ernakulam to Salem, then NH-44 north through Bangalore-Anantapur-Kurnool into Guntur. A standard 32-foot single-axle truck loaded with 9–10 tonnes of plywood and crate components clears the run in 56–72 hours including driver rest. Multi-axle 22-tonners on the same lane take 3–4 days.

Where we have flexibility on timeline, we also consolidate via Cochin Port and rail-on-flatbed up to Vijayawada Junction, then short-trail by road to the Guntur consignee — useful for granite-yard customers who already have ICD relationships and prefer a paper trail that mirrors their export documentation.

Decision rules — which grade for which Guntur load

Load typeTypical specWhy
Granite slab crates, marine exportBWP / BWR packing ply, 12mm face + 18mm bottom, ISPM-15Slab weight and sea humidity demand boiling-water-resistant glue and full heat-treatment.
Tobacco bale cases, exportMR packing ply, 8–10mm, Okoume face optional, ISPM-15Moderate weight, appearance matters for trade inspection, dry-cargo journey.
Chilli / spice export cratesMR packing ply, 6–8mm lining over sawn frameLight loads, cost-sensitive, breathability via frame gaps.
Auto-component domestic cratesCommercial packing ply, 9–12mmReusable, no marine exposure, ISPM-15 not needed for road movement.
Container floor repairBWP hardwood plank, 28mmWithstands forklift point loads and persistent moisture.
Shuttering / formwork on Guntur projectsFilm-faced shuttering ply, 12mmRepeat-pour concrete jobs around Mangalagiri-Amaravati construction zones.

Frequently asked questions

Do you quote ex-Kochi or delivered Guntur?

Either. Our standard quote is ex-factory in Perumbavoor with GST and freight extra, but for repeat Guntur lanes we maintain indicative delivered rates per truck so the buyer can compare apples-to-apples against a local supplier.

What is the minimum order for a Guntur dispatch?

One 32-foot truck is the practical floor — roughly 600–700 sheets of 12mm plywood or an equivalent mix of crate components. For smaller pilot orders we can part-load with another southbound consignment, but transit becomes less predictable.

Do your ISPM-15 crates carry the IPPC stamp visible to port inspectors?

Yes. Every heat-treated component leaves our yard with the IPPC mark, treatment code, and our facility number on a visible face. The documentation pack travels with the truck so port and customs handling at Krishnapatnam or Chennai is straightforward.

Request a quote for your Guntur dispatch

If you are crating granite slabs at Chimakurthi, casing FCV tobacco for the next auction cycle, or loading chilli containers out of Lam, the Cochin Wood group can quote a delivered Guntur rate against your spec. Send us the thickness, sheet size, sheet count, and dispatch timeline. We will come back with an ex-factory rate, the freight component to Guntur, and a loadability plan for the truck. Request a quote with the load details, or browse our resource hub for the grade-selection guides and ISPM-15 references that buyers in the Guntur belt tend to ask for before placing the first order.

title: Plywood Supply to Kakinada | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring for the loads moving through Kakinada's deep-water port and fertilizer belt. Our.
html:
Kakinada sits on the East Coast in a way few Indian ports do — a Deep Water Port that handles fertilizer, edible oils, agri-bulk and project cargo, an SEZ that has spilled industrial demand inland, and a hinterland that pushes everything from urea bags to oilfield equipment through the same gate. For a plywood supplier, that mix matters. Almost every product moving through Kakinada needs wood underneath it — pallets under fertilizer bags, crates around marine pumps and valves, container flooring for the rough road back to inland plants. This post walks through what the Cochin Wood group ships into Kakinada, the Kochi-to-Kakinada lane, and how we pick grades for the loads the city actually moves.

The Kakinada industrial picture

Kakinada's industrial belt is not one cluster, it is three overlapping ones, and the packing requirements differ across them.

The Port and SEZ corridor

Kakinada Deep Water Port (KDWP) and the adjacent Kakinada Anchorage Port together handle a heavy fertilizer and edible-oil book — DAP, MOP, urea, palm oil and breakbulk. The Kakinada SEZ at Vakalapudi-Atchutapuram and the planned extensions tied to the Visakhapatnam-Chennai Industrial Corridor (VCIC) host food processing, marine engineering and component fabrication units. Cargo here is split between bagged commodities (need pallets and dunnage) and finished engineered goods (need crates and edge protection).

The fertilizer hub

Kakinada is one of India's recognised fertilizer manufacturing and distribution clusters — large-format units along the Coringa road and Nagayalanka corridor produce, blend and bag NPK, single super phosphate and customised fertilizer mixes. Every 50 kg bag stack moving out of these gates rides on a wooden pallet, and inbound finished goods from Gulf shipments hit Kakinada in palletised form. The unit count is small but the pallet throughput per unit is high.

Engineering, marine and oilfield

Beyond fertilizer, the Kakinada-Tatipaka-Narsapur stretch supports ONGC's KG basin operations, oilfield service companies and marine-engineering workshops. Pumps, valves, control panels, drill-string accessories and survey equipment leave Kakinada in heavy crates bound for offshore platforms or coastwise rigs. These crates are not commodity items — they need ISPM-15 compliance, fumigated and HT-stamped wood, and plywood walls that can take a forklift bang without splitting.

What we ship to Kakinada

The mix that flows from our Kochi yard up the East Coast to Kakinada is concentrated in four product families. Each one maps to a different load on the ground.

Packing-grade plywood (commercial and rubberwood)

Our standard packing-grade range covers 6 mm to 18 mm thicknesses, in commercial and rubberwood face. This is the workhorse for case manufacturers across the Kakinada belt who fabricate crates locally. Thinner stock (6 mm, 8 mm) goes into case linings and partition walls; 12 mm and 14 mm are the typical wall thickness for one-trip export crates; 16 mm and 18 mm pick up the load on heavier engineered crates and reusable cases. All sheets come 8 ft x 4 ft as standard, with cut-to-size options where buyers want zero on-site wastage.

Ready-made crates and cases

For exporters who would rather not run an in-house carpentry shop, we supply finished crates built to drawing — open crates, closed cases, skid-mounted boxes, and collapsible sea-worthy cases. ISPM-15 fumigation and stamping is handled through registered units, which matters for any cargo leaving Kakinada through the Port for international destinations.

Wooden pallets

Standard sizes shipped to Kakinada include 1200 x 1000 mm (ISO), 1200 x 800 mm (Euro), and 1165 x 1165 mm (Australian) — the last one shows up often in fertilizer movement to Australian and South-East Asian markets. We also build heavy-duty pallets at 1219 x 1016 mm with twin-deck reinforcement for stacking heavy bagged loads at port godowns. Pallet wood is rubberwood or jackwood depending on the customer's reuse cycle.

Container flooring plywood

Where buyers in the marine engineering and oilfield segment need to reline reefer or dry containers, or build flatbed transport decks for project cargo coming off the Port, we supply container-flooring grade plywood — typically 28 mm WBP-bonded, hardwood face, with anti-slip film options.

The Kochi-to-Kakinada lane

The road distance from our Kochi/Perumbavoor base to Kakinada is approximately 1,450 to 1,550 km depending on routing. The two practical options:

  • NH-544 / NH-44 / NH-16 trunk route via Salem, Chittoor, Tirupati and Nellore, then onto the East Coast highway up through Ongole, Vijayawada and Eluru into Kakinada. Transit is typically 4 to 5 days door-to-door for a single-trailer FTL, depending on driver swaps and check-post delays at the Kerala-Tamil Nadu border.
  • Coastal sea route Kochi Port to Kakinada Port — economical for full container loads of finished crates or pallet stacks where the buyer has port-clearance capability. We have used this lane for larger consignments where the road trailer count would otherwise push past five trucks.

For most Kakinada buyers, road FTL is the practical default. We dispatch on flat-bed and high-side trailers from Perumbavoor, with a fixed loading plan agreed before the truck reports — typical capacity is around 600 to 700 sheets of 18 mm packing-grade ply per single-axle trailer, or 280 to 320 finished crates depending on size class. GST and transport are extra to the ex-factory price; we share a delivered-to-Kakinada landed cost on PI.

Grade decision rules for Kakinada loads

Buyers often ask which grade to specify for a given application. The short rules for the Kakinada belt:

If the cargo isUseWhy
Fertilizer bags (50 kg) on pallets, coastal road transitPacking-grade plywood pallets, rubberwood face, 12-14 mm deckCost-effective, handles the load cycle, easy to repair
Engineered crates for marine / oilfield exportOkoume-faced packing ply 12-18 mm, ISPM-15 stampedBetter surface finish, lower moisture pickup at port humidity, accepted by GCC and SE Asia consignees
Container flooring or transport decks for heavy machinery28 mm WBP container-flooring gradeDesigned for forklift point loads and water exposure
Shuttering for civil work at SEZ or fertilizer-plant extensionsShuttering ply, 12-18 mm, multi-pour gradeBWR-bonded, takes repeated casting cycles, lower per-use cost than commercial ply

The okoume face is worth specifying where the cargo will be inspected at destination — Gulf and Vietnam consignees in particular flag rough crate surfaces. For domestic moves within India, commercial face on packing-grade ply is usually fine.

FAQ for Kakinada buyers

Can you supply ISPM-15 stamped crates direct to Kakinada Port?

Yes. Finished crates are fabricated and fumigated at registered units in Perumbavoor, with the IPPC mark and treatment code applied before dispatch. We share the fumigation certificate copy with each consignment, and the original travels with the trucker for port hand-over.

What's the minimum order size that justifies a trailer to Kakinada?

A single full-trailer load (FTL) is the most economical unit. For partial loads, we consolidate with other East Coast deliveries when the schedule allows — typically a 7 to 10 day wait. For buyers needing fast turnaround at small volumes, road part-load through a third-party transporter is feasible but lands at a higher per-sheet rate.

Do you stock at Kakinada or supply only from Kochi?

We supply ex-factory from Perumbavoor near Kochi. There is no Kakinada-side stock yard at present. Buyers who need just-in-time supply usually place rolling orders with monthly schedules so we can dispatch on a planned cadence rather than emergency lots.

Requesting a quote

For buyers in Kakinada, Surasaniyanam, Tuni or the fertilizer-bagging units along the Coringa road, the fastest way to get a delivered price is to share thickness, sheet size or crate drawing, monthly quantity and the delivery point. We come back with an ex-Perumbavoor rate and a delivered-to-Kakinada landed cost on a proforma invoice. The Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing through Perumbavoor since 1986 — forty years of working with packing-case and pallet shops gives us a reasonable feel for what each East Coast destination throws up. Request a quote with your specs, or head to the resource hub for grade-selection notes, packing-case construction guides and the current price card.

title: Plywood Supply to Tirupati | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, export crates, pallets and container flooring that move finished goods out of Tirupati's EMC, auto-ancillary and.
html:
Tirupati is no longer just a temple town on the freight map. The Renigunta corridor now anchors one of South India's fastest-growing Electronics Manufacturing Clusters, a working auto-ancillary belt, and a steady pharma and food-processing pipeline feeding the Krishnapatnam and Chennai gateways. Every one of those factories ships finished goods in wood. That is where the Cochin Wood group fits in — supplying packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring to the converters, EPC contractors and export packers who actually build the boxes that leave Tirupati's gates.

The Tirupati industrial picture

If you draw a 30-km circle around Tirupati Renigunta airport, you cover the bulk of the city's manufacturing demand for industrial wood packaging. The picture breaks roughly into four belts.

Sri City and the Tirupati EMC at Renigunta

The Tirupati Electronics Manufacturing Cluster, notified at Renigunta and extended around the Sri City SEZ corridor, is the headline. Mobile-phone assembly, smart-device CKD lines and EMS contract manufacturers operate here at scale. The packaging implication is specific: lots of CKD inbound, lots of finished consumer-electronics outbound, and a constant flow of high-value reusable export crates moving between vendor parks. Crates here must be ESP-clean, dimensionally tight to the SKU, and ISPM-15 stamped for any consignment touching a foreign port.

YSR Kopparthy and the auto-ancillary corridor

The Kadapa-Tirupati stretch and the YSR Kopparthy node feed auto-component and EV-component buyers. Forged parts, machined castings, sheet-metal assemblies and battery housings travel in stackable wooden cases or on heat-treated pallets. The boxes here see rougher handling than electronics — they need heavier corner construction, thicker face-ply, and pallet bases rated for forklift use across multiple legs.

APIIC estates — Gajulamandyam, Vikruthamala, Mannavaram

The Andhra Pradesh Industrial Infrastructure Corporation estates around Tirupati host pharma APIs, food-grade processors, ceramic insulators and ancillary fabrication units. Pharma exporters in particular drive demand for moisture-resistant packing-grade plywood crates with desiccant pockets and ISPM-15 markings for European and African destinations.

Krishnapatnam Port linkage

Tirupati's export factories don't always rail out — a sizable share of containers move by road to Krishnapatnam (roughly 130 km east) or Chennai (roughly 150 km south-east) for stuffing. That means a Tirupati buyer is really specifying packaging for a sea voyage that begins at a port two-to-three hours away. Container flooring grade, crate stack ratings and fumigation compliance all need to assume an ocean leg.

What the Cochin Wood group ships into Tirupati

The Cochin Wood group has been in Perumbavoor since 1986. Group operations have built a 40-year working catalogue of packing-grade material, and the SKUs that move best on the Tirupati lane are these.

Packing-grade plywood sheets

Workhorse range, ex-factory Kochi. Commercial/Rubberwood packing grade in 8 ft × 4 ft sheets, thicknesses from 6 mm to 18 mm. Typical EMC crate spec uses 12 mm faces over a 50 × 50 mm frame, with 18 mm bottom for heavier sub-assemblies. Auto-ancillary and pharma exporters lean on 14 mm and 16 mm for export-rated cases.

ThicknessCommon Tirupati use
6–8 mmLight sleeves, internal partitions, dunnage
10–12 mmEMC crate faces, mobile-phone CKD outer boxes
14–16 mmAuto-component crates, pharma export cases
18 mmCrate bottoms, container flooring, heavy machine boxes

ISPM-15 heat-treated crates

Built to drawing — we manufacture wooden cases against the buyer's BOM, IPPC-stamped, with steel strapping, internal cleating and corner protection. Electronics buyers in the EMC typically need cases with internal foam mounts that we cut to spec. Heavier auto and machinery buyers ask for skid-mounted closed cases with 18 mm bottoms.

Wooden pallets

Two-way and four-way entry pallets, standard 1200 × 1000 mm and 1100 × 1100 mm footprints, plus made-to-order sizes. Heat-treated and stamped where the buyer ships internationally. For the YSR Kopparthy auto belt, the typical request is a forklift-rated four-way pallet capable of repeated handling between vendor and OEM.

Container flooring plywood

For exporters loading FCLs at Krishnapatnam or Chennai, we supply 18–28 mm container-flooring grade for re-skinning leased boxes and for one-time export floors under heavy machinery loads.

Sawn timber and runners

Jackwood and rubberwood runners against the buyer's specified section — used by Tirupati case makers building their own crates and needing matched framing timber alongside the face ply.

Kochi-to-Tirupati: the lane and the economics

The road distance from Kochi/Ernakulam to Tirupati is roughly 750 km. The standard lane runs Kochi — Salem — Krishnagiri — Chittoor — Tirupati. A loaded 32-foot single-axle or 22-foot truck typically transits in 36 to 48 hours from dispatch, depending on driver changeover and night-running restrictions through Tamil Nadu.

For full-truck despatches, we quote ex-factory Kochi with freight separately so the Tirupati buyer can compare against any local source. Part-truck and consolidated loads work when the buyer's offtake is between 200 and 600 sheets at a time, sharing the lane with other South-Indian deliveries. For container quantities or repeat monthly programs, we plan dedicated trips and lock the rate against a monthly schedule.

Compared to North-Indian or Punjab/Yamunanagar sources, the Kochi origin is closer to Tirupati by about 1,200–1,400 km, which usually shows up as a 1.5–2 day faster transit and a meaningfully lower freight component on each sheet.

Choosing the grade for the job

When packing-grade rubberwood ply is the right call

For the majority of Tirupati crating — EMS finished-goods boxes, auto-ancillary export cases, pharma cartons over wood frames — the Cochin Wood group's commercial packing-grade rubberwood plywood is the workhorse. It costs efficiently per square foot, takes nails and screws without splitting, and meets ISPM-15 after heat treatment. Most EMC buyers we quote land here.

When Okoume makes sense

Okoume is a premium export grade. For Tirupati it makes sense when the buyer is shipping high-value electronics or instrumentation where a cleaner face, lighter sheet weight and a longer service life justify the higher rate — for example, reusable inter-plant crates between vendor parks, or display-grade outer cases. We price Okoume on request rather than off the standard packing card.

When shuttering ply is the answer

Tirupati's industrial expansion means active EPC sites — new sheds at the EMC, vendor-park construction at Sri City, road and warehouse builds along the Krishnapatnam corridor. Civil contractors there use BWP/BWR shuttering plywood, not packing grade. We supply both, and the rule is simple: anything cast against concrete needs shuttering ply, anything boxing a product needs packing ply.

FAQ — questions Tirupati buyers usually ask

How fast can material reach a Tirupati factory after order confirmation?

For standard packing-grade thicknesses, sheets dispatch within 2–3 working days of payment terms being met, and transit on the Kochi–Tirupati lane runs 36–48 hours. For made-to-drawing crates, allow 5–7 days for manufacture before dispatch.

Do you handle ISPM-15 certification for export buyers?

Yes. Crates and pallets are heat-treated and IPPC-stamped to the standard before dispatch, so the buyer can use them directly for international consignments out of Krishnapatnam or Chennai without re-treatment at the port.

What is the minimum order for a Tirupati delivery?

Full-truck offtake (roughly 700–900 sheets depending on thickness) is the most economical. Part-truck deliveries from 200 sheets are workable when we can consolidate against an existing lane.

Get a quote for your Tirupati delivery

If you operate a packaging line at the Tirupati EMC, a contract-manufacturing unit at Sri City, an auto-component plant at YSR Kopparthy, or an export facility at any of the APIIC estates around the city, the Cochin Wood group can quote against your drawing or specification. Share the thickness, sheet size, monthly offtake and delivery point, and we will return an ex-factory rate plus the current Tirupati lane freight. To open a conversation, request a quote, or browse specifications and decision guides in the resource hub before you brief us.

title: Plywood Supply to Nellore | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing plywood, export crates and pallets that move shrimp, mineral and stone cargo through Nellore's coastal ports. Built in our.
html:
Nellore is one of those coastal Andhra districts that does not get the marketing it deserves. Sandwiched between Chennai and the Krishna delta, it quietly moves shrimp, granite, mica, silica sand and a growing list of engineering goods through Krishnapatnam Port and Chennai Port. Every one of those export lines needs wooden packing — fumigated crates for reefer cargo, ISPM-15 pallets for break-bulk, plywood-skinned boxes for mineral and machinery shipments. This post lays out what the Cochin Wood group ships into the Nellore industrial belt, how the Kochi-to-Nellore lane runs, and how to pick the right grade for the cargo you are actually packing.

The Nellore industrial picture

Nellore district sits on the Chennai-Kolkata highway (NH-16) with three port mouths within trucking distance: Krishnapatnam in the south of the district, Kakinada to the north, and Chennai about 175 km down the coast. That port density is the reason the export-oriented industries here cluster the way they do.

Krishnapatnam Port and the export hinterland

Krishnapatnam (now operated as an Adani port) handles dry bulk, containers, project cargo and a meaningful share of South India's coal, iron ore and granite movement. The container terminal pulls in shippers from across Rayalaseema and southern Andhra. For a plywood supplier the relevant cargo categories are:

  • Frozen and chilled seafood — Nellore and the broader Krishna-Godavari belt are India's shrimp heartland. Reefer container exports leave through Krishnapatnam and Chennai bound for the US, EU, Japan and the Gulf.
  • Mica, silica and mineral exports — Nellore is historically the mica capital of India. Processed mica blocks, splittings and silica sand still ship in wooden crates and on pallets to electronic-grade buyers abroad.
  • Granite blocks and slabs — quarries around Chimakurthy and Ongole feed the Krishnapatnam stone yards. Slabs travel on heavy bearers and plywood-skinned A-frames.
  • Engineering and auto-component cargo — Sri City SEZ sits just across the Tamil Nadu border (~80 km from Nellore town), but a lot of its ancillary packing work happens in Nellore's industrial estates.

Industrial estates and clusters

The APIIC estates worth knowing when you map plywood demand:

  • Naidupet Industrial Park — close to Sri City, hosts auto, electronics and FMCG units that need export-grade pallets and crates.
  • Menakur and Tada belts — feed Sri City; lot of light engineering, packaging and component makers.
  • Krishnapatnam SEZ and port-side warehousing — bulk packing, fumigation yards, CFS operators.
  • Kovur, Buchireddypalem and Nellore-Atmakur road — sawmills, rice mills, seafood processors.

What we ship into Nellore

The Cochin Wood group's range is built around bulk packaging — not retail dealer stock. For Nellore buyers, three lines do almost all the work.

Packing-grade plywood

This is our workhorse for crate skinning, base boards and side panels. The standard packing range is MR-glue commercial plywood with hardwood face/back veneers — rubberwood, jackwood or Gurjan depending on the order — supplied in 8x4 ft sheets across thicknesses from 6 mm to 18 mm. Typical use:

  • 6-8 mm — top covers and dividers for light cargo.
  • 10-12 mm — standard crate sides for engineering goods and packaged minerals.
  • 14-18 mm — base boards under heavy loads, bearers and reusable export crates.

For seafood exporters the question is usually about moisture behaviour around reefer cargo. The MR packing grade handles ambient humidity and chilled-room exposure fine for single-trip export crates. For repeated cold-chain cycling or marine-exposed cargo, we step the grade up to BWR or use Okoume-faced ply.

Crates and ISPM-15 pallets

We build closed plywood crates, open-batten wooden crates and standard four-way pallets to the buyer's drawing. ISPM-15 heat-treated and stamped material is available for any export-destined consignment — non-negotiable for shipments to the EU, US, Australia, China and most of the Gulf. Common sizes for the Nellore lane:

  • 1200 x 1000 mm and 1200 x 800 mm (Euro) pallets for containerised seafood and FMCG cargo.
  • 1100 x 1100 mm for Japan-bound mica and electronics.
  • Custom heavy-duty crates for granite slabs, mineral lumps and project cargo.

Container flooring and shuttering

Container repair yards and refurbishers around Krishnapatnam pull container-flooring plywood (28 mm, hardwood face, phenolic-bonded) on a steady cycle. For construction buyers in Nellore town and along the SH-57 corridor, our film-faced shuttering ply in 12 mm and 18 mm covers high-rise formwork and infrastructure jobs.

Kochi-to-Nellore logistics

Road distance from our Perumbavoor dispatch yard to Nellore town is roughly 1,050-1,100 km depending on route. Trucks typically run NH-544 (Salem) → NH-44 (Bengaluru-Chennai) → NH-16 (Chennai-Krishnapatnam), with transit time of 3 to 4 days for a single-driver truck and 2 to 3 days when we run a relay. For port-side delivery into Krishnapatnam, drop is normally at the consignee's CFS or fumigation yard inside the port-zone gates.

Typical lane economics

Load typeVehicleSheet capacity (8x4)Pallet capacity
Full truckload plywood32 ft single-axle~700-900 sheets (mixed thickness)—
Full truckload pallets32 ft single-axle—~280-320 standard pallets
Crate consignment22-32 ftBuilt crates loaded nested or knock-downQuoted per drawing

Quotes from Kochi are ex-factory by default; GST and freight are extra and we share the landed Nellore number per consignment based on diesel and lane availability at the time of dispatch.

Decision rules: which grade for which cargo

A quick mental checklist for Nellore buyers picking between our lines:

  1. Single-trip export crate, light to medium engineering cargo, dry container → MR packing-grade plywood, 10-12 mm sides on 18 mm base.
  2. Seafood reefer cargo, chilled or frozen → MR packing grade for single-use; BWR or Okoume-faced for premium buyers and longer voyages.
  3. Mineral and mica exports to Japan, EU, US → ISPM-15 stamped pallets in hardwood; Okoume-faced plywood crates where the buyer specifies a clean export finish.
  4. Granite, project cargo, machinery → 18 mm packing-grade base on heavy bearers, sawn-timber framing, open-batten side construction.
  5. Shuttering and concrete formwork inside Nellore → film-faced shuttering ply 12-18 mm; not the packing grade.

FAQ

Can you supply ISPM-15 stamped pallets directly to Krishnapatnam Port?

Yes. We supply heat-treated and ISPM-15 stamped pallets and crates for any export-destined cargo through Krishnapatnam, Chennai or Kakinada. Marking, treatment certificate and the IPPC stamp are all included on the supplied units.

What is the minimum order quantity for a Nellore delivery?

For a direct full-truck delivery the practical minimum is one 32 ft truckload — roughly 700+ sheets of plywood or about 300 pallets. Smaller part-loads are possible when we have a consolidated truck running the Kochi-Chennai-Nellore corridor; ask when you raise the enquiry.

Do you supply Okoume plywood for premium seafood export crates?

Okoume is part of our regular range. It is a premium export-grade ply with a clean face — used by buyers who want a presentable finished crate for FOB shipments and longer transit cycles. Pricing is quoted separately on request as the grade sits above the standard packing line.

Request a Nellore quote

If you are packing seafood, mica, granite or engineering goods out of Nellore and want a working number against your drawing or sheet schedule, send us the spec and we will come back with an ex-factory Kochi rate plus an indicative landed Nellore number. The Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing in Perumbavoor since 1986 — forty years of running the same packing-plywood and crate lines — and the Nellore lane is one we already serve regularly. Request a quote with your sheet sizes, thickness, monthly volume and delivery point, or browse the resource hub for grade comparisons, ISPM-15 notes and crate-design guides before you write the enquiry.

title: Plywood Supply to Vijayawada | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, export crates and pallets that survive the 1,300 km haul, the monsoon and the sea voyage out of coastal Andhra.
html:
Vijayawada sits at a junction most plywood suppliers underestimate. It is the trading capital of the Krishna delta, the road-and-rail crossroads of coastal Andhra, the gateway to NTPC Vijayawada and the wider thermal corridor along the Krishna river, and a quiet but persistent source of crate-and-pallet demand from agri-equipment makers, power-equipment fabricators, transformer rebuilders, and granite exporters running through Krishnapatnam, Kakinada and Machilipatnam ports. When buyers from this belt call us in Kochi, the conversation is almost never about decorative plywood. It is about boards that can survive a 1,300 km truck haul, a monsoon, a forklift, and a sea voyage to the Gulf or Southeast Asia. This is the kind of work the Cochin Wood group has been doing for the export and packing trade for roughly forty years, and what follows is a practical look at what we ship into Vijayawada and how it actually moves.

The Vijayawada industrial picture

To understand the plywood flow into Vijayawada, it helps to map the demand by sector rather than by single buyers. Andhra Pradesh's industrial promotion body, APIIC, runs several estates around the city — Auto Nagar inside Vijayawada itself, Kondapalli industrial area to the west (anchored by the Kondapalli thermal/gas cluster and BHEL-linked engineering units), Gannavaram on the airport side, and the larger Kopparthi and Jaggaiahpet belts in the Krishna and YSR Kadapa districts that feed Vijayawada's logistics nodes.

Across these belts the plywood-buying sectors tend to be:

  • Power and heavy electrical: NTPC Vijayawada and the Kondapalli gas-fired cluster generate steady downstream demand for transformer crates, switchgear packing, panel-board boxes and spare-part cases.
  • Agri-equipment and food processing: Krishna delta rice mills, cold-storage builders, cattle-feed plants and tractor-implement workshops need pallets and packing cases for machine despatch.
  • Granite and dimensional stone: Ongole and Chimakurthy quarries route export-grade granite through Vijayawada hauliers; heavy slab crates with hardwood runners are the standard.
  • Auto components and general engineering: Auto Nagar and the BHEL-adjacent vendor base build smaller component crates and export pallets for Chennai, Hyderabad and GCC routes.
  • Pharma and chemicals: Andhra's Krishnapatnam-linked chemical units use plywood boxes for drums and IBCs going outbound.

Each of these sectors has a slightly different specification preference, and a supplier who tries to sell the same board to all of them will lose money on at least one.

What we ship to Vijayawada

The Cochin Wood group is a plywood manufacturer operating through contract factories under our specifications, with the bulk of our output going to packing-grade applications. For Vijayawada buyers, three product families do most of the work.

Packing-grade plywood (MR-grade, commercial hardwood)

This is the workhorse. Built on a hardwood core (rubberwood or mixed tropical hardwood) with phenol-bonded faces, it is the right specification for crates that need to survive multi-modal handling without paying for marine-grade overkill. Common thicknesses going out to Andhra:

ThicknessTypical use in Vijayawada loads
6 mmLight component boxes, pharma cartons, internal partitions
8–10 mmAuto-component crates, smaller agri-equipment cases
12 mmStandard export crate walls, transformer covers
15–16 mmHeavier panel-board boxes, granite slab caps
18 mmPallet decks, base boards, container flooring repair

Standard sheet sizes are 8x4 ft and 7x4 ft, with custom-cut sizes available for repeat crate dimensions. For exporters who close under FOB Krishnapatnam or Chennai we can supply ISPM-15-marked heat-treated material when ordered through the right channel.

Crates and pallets

For buyers who would rather receive finished packaging than fabricate it in-house, our contract factories build:

  • Closed plywood crates with hardwood runners, IS 6662-aligned construction, and cleats sized for the specific load.
  • Skid pallets and four-way entry pallets in 1200x1000 mm, 1200x800 mm and customer-specified footprints, with rubberwood or jackwood runners depending on load class.
  • Open crates with diagonal bracing for transformer and motor despatch.

Container flooring and shuttering

Container floor repair plywood (typically 28 mm, phenol-film face, hardwood core) is a niche but recurring requirement for Vijayawada-based container leasing and refurbishing units feeding the eastern ports. Shuttering ply is a separate line — useful when an infrastructure contractor in the city is looking to consolidate plywood and packing material sourcing from a single Kerala supplier.

Kochi-to-Vijayawada logistics

Vijayawada is roughly 1,300 km from Kochi by road. The default lane is NH-544 / NH-66 up the Western Ghats, then NH-44 across the Deccan via Bengaluru and Anantapur into Andhra. With a single driver it is typically a 3-day transit; with a relay it tightens to 2 days plus loading and a half-day for unloading at the buyer's yard.

Practical points we work with:

  • A standard 32-ft single-axle truck carries roughly 16–18 tonnes of plywood, equivalent to about 850–950 sheets of 12 mm 8x4. A multi-axle 32-ft / 40-ft container truck pushes that higher.
  • Loads are quoted ex-factory Perumbavoor (Kochi). Transport and GST are extra and are billed at actuals or as a flat lane rate depending on the buyer's preference.
  • For repeat-buyer accounts a fortnightly or monthly truck slot is usually more economical than ad-hoc despatch, because we can fill the trailer to its true cubic limit rather than the buyer paying for empty headroom.
  • Coastal shipping via Cochin port to Krishnapatnam is technically available and can make sense for very large pallet orders, but for most Vijayawada-sized loads the trucking lane wins on transit time.

Decision rules: which board for which load

Buyers in Vijayawada usually ask the same underlying question: what is the minimum specification I can use without the crate failing? A working set of rules:

  • Domestic crates, single handling, under 500 kg: MR-grade commercial hardwood plywood, 8–12 mm, is normally enough.
  • Export crates, sea voyage, ISPM-15 mandatory: heat-treated packing-grade ply with hardwood runners. Okoume face is worth the premium when the consignee specifies a clean, pale, splinter-free face — common for engineered goods, electronics, and any consignment where the crate itself is inspected before opening.
  • Heavy machine bases, transformers, granite caps: 15–18 mm packing-grade ply over jackwood or hardwood runners. Pure okoume is rarely the right answer here — it is a finer face veneer and is not the load-carrying member.
  • Concrete shuttering on a local civil project: shuttering ply, separate specification, do not substitute with packing grade.

Our quoting team will normally ask three questions before sending a proforma: thickness and size, monthly quantity, and delivery point. Those three pieces let us land on a realistic ex-factory rate plus the right lane cost.

FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15 compliant crates for export from Krishnapatnam or Chennai?

Yes. Heat-treated packing-grade plywood and ISPM-15 stamped finished crates can be supplied through the appropriate channel. Lead time is slightly longer than non-treated material; planning around your shipping schedule is worth doing two to three weeks in advance.

What is the minimum order quantity for a truck to Vijayawada?

Economically, a full 32-ft truck is the sweet spot. We can ship part-loads in shared trucks, but the per-sheet landed cost rises noticeably below half-truck volumes. For first-time buyers we usually suggest aligning the first order to a full truck and then settling into a regular cadence.

Can you quote rates inclusive of delivery to Auto Nagar or Kondapalli?

Yes. Ex-factory plus a lane rate to the specific delivery point is the cleanest way to quote, because trucking rates on the Kochi–Vijayawada lane move with diesel and seasonal demand. We will share both numbers so buyers can compare against any local trucking they already use.

Request a quote

If you run a packing-case unit, an export crate workshop, a pallet line, or an engineering vendor base in Vijayawada, Kondapalli, Gannavaram or the wider Krishna district and you want a working quote on packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets, or container-floor boards, the fastest route is to request a quote with your thickness, size, monthly quantity, and delivery point. Spec sheets, indicative price cards, and the logistics calculators for the Kochi–Andhra lane are kept in our resource hub for buyers who would rather scope the requirement themselves before talking to sales.

title: Plywood Supply to Belgaum | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, export crates and pallets that ship castings and machined parts out of Belagavi. Built in Perumbavoor by a group.
html:
Why Belgaum's industrial belt matters for packing wood

Belagavi sits on a peculiar industrial spine — a foundry cluster of more than 300 units feeding automotive, hydraulic and pump castings into pan-India OEMs, layered with a fast-growing aerospace components base anchored by the HAL supplier ecosystem and the Aequs SEZ at Kanagala. Every casting that leaves a Belgaum foundry needs to ride on something, and every aerospace machined part bound for HAL Bangalore, Tata, or an export consignee needs an ISPM-15 stamped crate to clear the dock. That is the wood-and-plywood demand pattern we serve from Kochi. This page lays out what the Cochin Wood group ships into Belagavi, how the logistics work over the 750 km lane, and which grade actually fits which load — written for the procurement person who already knows what a 1200x1000 mm pallet looks like.

The Belgaum industrial picture

The packaging demand in Belagavi does not come from one estate — it comes from a belt. Knowing the belt helps us right-size the trucks and the dispatch frequency.

  • Udyambag and Machhe industrial areas — the historic foundry heartland. Grey iron, SG iron, alloy steel castings. Heavy unit weights, irregular geometry, machined faces that mark easily. This is where most of our packing-grade plywood and dense hardwood runners go.
  • KIADB Auto Park, Halaga — newer, tier-1 and tier-2 auto component manufacturers. Mix of castings, forgings, machined assemblies. Cleaner packing standard, more pallet-and-box than open crate.
  • Aequs SEZ, Kanagala — aerospace precision machining, sheet metal, surface treatment. Export-heavy. ISPM-15 heat-treated stamp is non-negotiable here, and the cosmetic standard on the crate is higher because end-consignees inspect.
  • Honaga and Kakati MIDC-adjacent units — pumps, valves, hydraulics. Mid-weight castings, recurring monthly volume — ideal for a standing pallet-and-cap programme rather than one-off crates.
  • Sambra and the airport-adjacent cluster — emerging aero MRO and defence-linked work, smaller volumes but tight quality specs.

Across these belts the dominant wood-packaging needs collapse to four things: packing-grade plywood sheets for box panels, hardwood runners and battens for skids, ready-made ISPM-15 crates for export consignments, and container flooring plywood for trailers and shipping containers being refurbished in the region.

What we ship into Belgaum

Packing-grade plywood — sheet specs

Our packing range is built around hardwood and rubberwood cores in MR (moisture-resistant) bonding, designed for box and case panels that must hold a heavy casting without splitting along the edge nail line. Standard sizes are 8x4 ft and 7x4 ft; thicknesses commonly pulled by Belagavi buyers:

ThicknessTypical use in Belagavi
6 mmLid panels on light pump-housing boxes
8 mmSide panels for medium auto-component cases
12 mmBase and side panels for foundry castings up to ~500 kg
16-18 mmHeavy crate bases, container flooring patches, base of aerospace export crates

Face veneer can be specified as commercial hardwood for cost-driven domestic packing, or Okoume for export work where the buyer wants a cleaner outer face and lighter sheet weight. Okoume is a face-veneer option on our packing and BWR grades, not a separate grade.

Crates — ISPM-15 and domestic

For export shipments out of Belagavi — which mostly route through Nhava Sheva (JNPT) or Bangalore air — the crate must be built from heat-treated wood and carry the ISPM-15 mark with the IPPC logo, country code, treatment provider code and treatment code. We supply both:

  • Closed crates — fully boarded plywood faces over a hardwood frame; used for aerospace components, precision machined parts, anything where dust and rough handling damage the load.
  • Open crates and skid-and-cap — hardwood frame with strategic plywood panels; used for foundry castings where weight is high but cosmetic protection matters less.

Cleat thickness, runner cross-section and lag-bolt pattern are sized to the actual unit weight; we ask for the load weight, dimensions and lifting method (forklift, crane sling, both) before quoting.

Pallets

The two pallet footprints we ship most into the KIADB and Aequs side of Belagavi:

  • 1200 x 1000 mm — the de-facto Asian export pallet, fits standard 20 ft and 40 ft containers efficiently for two-rows-wide loading.
  • 1200 x 800 mm — Euro pallet, asked for when the consignee is in EU automotive supply chain.

Four-way entry, double-deck, with hardwood top and bottom boards and ISPM-15 treated stringers for export. For repeated indoor use inside a KIADB plant we often supply non-treated rubberwood pallets at a lower price point.

Container flooring plywood

A smaller but recurring line into Belagavi is 28 mm container flooring plywood — heavy-duty multi-ply hardwood board used to repair shipping container floors and to line trailer beds for foundry haulage. We supply in standard 2400 x 1160 mm sheets or cut to floor pattern on request.

Kochi-to-Belgaum logistics

The lane is roughly 750 km road from our Perumbavoor / Kochi dispatch yards to Belagavi. Typical road route runs NH 66 up the Kerala-Karnataka coast to Karwar, then NH 748 / NH 4A inland through Anmod ghat into Belagavi — or the alternative inland route via Mangaluru and Hubballi when the ghats are weather-affected during monsoon. Transit time we plan against:

  • Full-truck-load (FTL), 20 ft / 32 ft trailer — 48 to 60 hours door-to-door, ex-Kochi to KIADB / Udyambag.
  • Part-load through aggregator — 3 to 5 working days; viable for small crate batches and pallet top-ups.
  • Container stuffing at Cochin Port and rail/road to ICD Belagavi — used when the buyer wants the wood landed under an export-linked GST flow.

For Belagavi exporters whose finished goods route through JNPT, it is often cleaner to manufacture the crate and pallet at our Kerala yard, ship FTL straight to the packing line in Belagavi, and let the consignment move out on the same crate to Nhava Sheva — no double-handling, single ISPM-15 stamp.

Decision rules — which grade for which Belgaum load

A practical rule-of-thumb our packing-line foremen use when a Belagavi buyer asks "what do I actually need":

  1. Domestic foundry casting, road movement within India — commercial hardwood packing-grade plywood, 12 mm sides, 18 mm base, hardwood runners. No need to pay for Okoume face or heat treatment.
  2. Aerospace or precision component, export — Okoume-faced packing-grade plywood or BWR-bonded board on an ISPM-15 heat-treated hardwood frame. Closed crate, screw-fixed not nailed, with desiccant pockets if the consignee specifies.
  3. Heavy pump or hydraulic assembly, sea export — 18 mm packing-grade plywood base, hardwood runners with chamfered ends for fork entry, lag-bolted top cap, ISPM-15 stamp.
  4. Construction-linked shuttering work at Belagavi infrastructure sites — film-faced shuttering plywood, separate product line, quoted on dimension and re-use count expectation.

If the load is borderline, we would rather over-engineer the crate base by one plywood thickness step than under-spec — a failed crate at Anmod ghat is more expensive than the extra 6 mm of board.

FAQ

Do you stamp ISPM-15 in your own yard?

Yes, the crates and pallets we supply for export work are heat-treated and carry the IPPC stamp with the registered treatment provider code. Documentation pack includes the ISPM-15 certificate, packing list dimensions and weights.

What is the minimum order for delivery to Belagavi?

For plywood sheets we typically plan in full-truck-load units to keep freight economics sensible over the 750 km lane. For crates and pallets, smaller batches can ride on a part-load or piggyback on a sheet consignment — talk to us about batch sizing, we will work out the truck plan.

Can you match a foundry's existing crate drawing?

Yes. Share the existing drawing or even photographs with dimensions, the unit weight, and any lifting points required. We can replicate to your spec or value-engineer the runner pattern if the existing design is over-built.

Talk to us about your Belgaum dispatch

The Cochin Wood group has run packing-wood operations in Perumbavoor since 1986 — four decades of supplying plywood, crates and pallets to industrial buyers across the country. CWI Pvt Ltd is the present commercial entity that handles your purchase order, GST documentation and delivery into Belagavi. If you are a foundry, aerospace component manufacturer, pump and valve maker, or any unit in the KIADB / Aequs / Udyambag belt who needs reliable packing wood, send us your part dimensions, monthly quantity and dispatch location — we will come back with a costed PI within a working day. Request a quote for your next Belagavi consignment, or browse our resource hub for technical sheets on ISPM-15, plywood grades and crate construction before you write the spec.

title: Plywood Supply to Mysuru | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade and Okoume-faced plywood, export crates and pallets that Mysuru's exporters ship on. Manufactured in Perumbavoor by a group.
html:
Mysuru sits in an unusual sweet spot for the packing-plywood trade. It is a Tier-2 city with Tier-1 export weight — IT hardware moving out of Hebbal, coffee leaving for Hamburg and Trieste via Chennai and Mangalore ports, and a steady drumbeat of light engineering shipments out of the KIADB industrial estates. Each of those flows ends in a wooden box, a crate or a pallet, and that is where the Cochin Wood group has been quietly building its Mysuru book over the last few years. This guide walks through what we ship into the city, why our Kochi-to-Mysuru lane works on cost, and how to choose the right grade for your load.

The Mysuru industrial picture

Mysuru's packing demand is concentrated across a few well-defined belts, and the plywood specification changes meaningfully depending on which one you sit in.

Hebbal Industrial Area and the IT corridor

Hebbal is the city's anchor for IT hardware, electronics manufacturing and back-office infrastructure. The sector traditionally hosts large software and services campuses, and the spillover into hardware assembly, server-rack manufacture and electronic sub-assembly means a constant pull on close-tolerance crates — boxes where the plywood face has to take printing, the corners have to survive multi-modal handling, and moisture migration during a monsoon transit is a real concern.

Belagola, Metagalli and Hootagalli

These older KIADB estates carry the city's light and medium engineering load: pumps, electrical switchgear, auto components, machine-tool sub-assemblies. Crates here are heavier, often above 500 kg gross, and the spec sheet is dominated by ISPM-15 stamps for export and heat-treatment compliance for domestic rail movement.

Coffee and agri-export cluster

Mysuru is a national centre for coffee processing and roasting, supplied by the Kodagu and Chikkamagaluru estates upstream. Bulk coffee — green bean in jute bags, instant coffee in steel drums, specialty roasts in cartons — moves out in containerised loads that need either solid wooden pallets or composite plywood-deck pallets to keep cargo off the container floor through humid sea voyages.

Mysuru Aerospace and the upcoming corridor

The aerospace SEZ and the proposed Mysuru-Bengaluru expressway expansion are pulling in precision-engineering tenants. These buyers tend to want premium veneer faces, tighter dimensional tolerance and traceability on the plywood — a different conversation from the volume packing-case market.

What we ship into Mysuru

The Cochin Wood group manufactures through contract factories under CWI specifications, with group operations running in Perumbavoor since 1986. For the Mysuru lane, four product families do most of the work.

Packing-grade plywood (the workhorse)

Our MR-Packing grade is the default for box and crate manufacturers across Karnataka. Standard panel: 8 ft x 4 ft, thicknesses from 6 mm to 18 mm. The most common pulls into Mysuru are:

  • 6 mm and 8 mm for inner partitions, lids and lightweight cartons
  • 10 mm and 12 mm for general-purpose export boxes up to ~300 kg gross
  • 16 mm and 18 mm for heavy engineering crates, machinery skids and pallet decks

Glue line is phenolic-bonded for the BWR variant where moisture or sea transit is in the picture. Face veneers are typically commercial hardwood; Okoume and Gurjan face options are available on the same MR-Packing and BWR cores when the customer needs a cleaner printing surface or improved cosmetic finish.

Assembled crates and packing cases

For exporters who would rather receive a finished crate than build one in-house, we ship knocked-down (KD) or fully-assembled cases cut to internal-dimension drawings. ISPM-15 heat-treatment marking and fumigation certification are coordinated for export-bound consignments.

Wooden pallets

Two-way and four-way entry pallets in standard footprints — 1200 x 1000 mm and 1200 x 800 mm being the most-asked — with plywood-deck or sawn-timber-deck options. Pallet thickness and deck-board specification are matched to the load: 12 mm plywood deck for ~500 kg static loads, stepping up to 18 mm or solid timber for the 1-tonne-plus engineering crates.

Container flooring plywood

For the freight-forwarding and container-refurbishment trade, 28 mm IICL-spec container flooring sheets in 2400 x 1160 mm and similar formats are available on indent.

Kochi-to-Mysuru: the logistics math

This is where the Kerala-based supplier has a real, defensible cost advantage over a North Indian source.

ParameterDetail
DistanceApproximately 480 km via NH-766 (Kochi - Walayar - Coimbatore - Mysuru)
Transit time11-13 hours for a standard truck under normal conditions
Typical truck20 ft and 22 ft open-body trucks; 32 ft multi-axle for full-loads
Loadability~800-1000 sheets of 12 mm in a 32 ft trailer, depending on packing
Cut-off for next-day arrivalDespatch by ~14:00 from Perumbavoor

Because the trucking lane runs through a single ghat (Walayar) and re-enters Karnataka at Bandipur edge, there is no interstate transhipment penalty. Most Mysuru loads dispatched on a Monday afternoon are unloading at Hebbal or Belagola by Tuesday evening. For comparison, plywood sourced from the North Indian belt typically books 4-6 days of transit and an additional handling event at a Bengaluru transshipment hub.

Which grade for which load?

The Mysuru order mix is broad enough that a single specification rarely fits. A working decision rule:

  1. MR-Packing grade — IT hardware crates, domestic-bound engineering crates, light-to-medium export boxes where the journey is overland or a short sea leg. This is 70-80 percent of our Mysuru volume.
  2. BWR (boiling-water-resistant) with Okoume or Gurjan face — long-haul sea export to Europe, GCC and ASEAN; cosmetic crates where the wood grain shows on the finished package; coffee and agri-export loads that sit in humid containers for 3-5 weeks.
  3. Shuttering ply — for the Mysuru construction sector, including the expressway projects and commercial real estate around the ring road. Film-faced shuttering plywood in 12 mm and 18 mm is the volume call.

If you are unsure, share the gross weight, the dispatch destination and the mode of transport, and we will reverse-engineer the lightest specification that still survives the journey. There is no commercial reason to over-engineer a domestic box with marine-grade plywood, and we will say so.

FAQ

Do you have a depot in Mysuru?

No — and that is a deliberate cost decision. We dispatch direct from the Perumbavoor manufacturing belt on a confirmed-order basis, which keeps overhead out of the per-sheet price. For repeat buyers we maintain a rolling production slot so lead time stays under a week.

What is the minimum order quantity?

For sheet plywood, we prefer to dispatch part-loads of 200 sheets and above to keep freight efficient; smaller orders are possible but the per-sheet landed cost climbs. For assembled crates and pallets, minimum is typically one full truckload to make the dispatch viable.

Can you handle ISPM-15 stamping and export documentation?

Yes — heat-treatment, fumigation, and the IPPC mark are coordinated through our compliance partners for export-bound consignments. Share the destination country and the consignee's wood-packaging requirements and we will build the specification accordingly.

Get a Mysuru-specific quote

If you are sourcing packing plywood, crates, pallets or container flooring into the Mysuru belt — Hebbal, Belagola, Metagalli, Hootagalli, or the coffee and aerospace clusters — the fastest route is to request a quote with your thickness, monthly volume and dispatch destination. We will come back with an ex-Perumbavoor rate, a freight estimate to your unloading point, and a realistic dispatch date. For specification reading material — grade comparisons, ISPM-15 notes, packing-case design — the resource hub has the working documents we share with our trade buyers. The Cochin Wood group has been operating in the plywood trade for over forty years; the goal of the Mysuru lane is to make that depth of supply feel like a local relationship.

title: Plywood Supply to Davangere | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring that Davangere's cotton mills, foundries and agri-equipment fabricators load.
html:
Davangere sits in the dead centre of Karnataka, on NH-48 between Bengaluru and Hubballi, and it is one of those second-tier industrial cities that quietly does a lot of physical work — cotton ginning, spinning and weaving on one side, agri-processing and farm machinery on the other. Both of those activities push finished goods out of Davangere on trucks, and a fair share of that cargo travels in wooden boxes, on wooden pallets, or inside containers laid with plywood flooring. That is where a Kochi-based packing-grade plywood supply starts to make economic sense for Davangere buyers, and it is what this guide breaks down.

The Davangere industrial picture

Davangere is historically the cotton-textile capital of Karnataka. The Bapuji Industrial Estate inside city limits, the KSSIDC industrial area at Lokikere Road, the Karur and Anagodu cluster on the Harihar side, and the larger KIADB belts at Harihar and Haralapura form the spine of the local manufacturing base. Around the edges, the Davangere–Harihar corridor on NH-48 carries a dense run of cotton mills, ginning units, oil mills, foundries and agri-equipment fabricators.

The plywood demand that flows out of this belt is fairly predictable:

  • Textile machinery and spares — ring frames, autoconers, looms and their replacement parts move in heavy wooden crates, often inter-state, and need square, dense ply that holds nails without splitting.
  • Agri-equipment and pump-set packing — irrigation pumps, sprayer assemblies, threshers and tractor spares are crated for dealer dispatch across South and Central India.
  • Cotton bales and yarn cartons on pallets — finished yarn, processed cotton and ginned lint move on wooden pallets to mills, ports and inland warehouses.
  • Foundry castings and engineered components — Davangere has a long-standing foundry cluster; iron castings need heavy-duty box bottoms and skidded crates.
  • Export-bound agri-processed goods — areca-nut, maize products and processed grains routed via Mangaluru, Krishnapatnam or Chennai ports, where ISPM-15-compliant crating becomes a factor.

The common thread: durable, predictable, fairly thick ply in 6 mm–18 mm with consistent quality across truckloads. Not retail-counter ply.

What we ship into Davangere

The Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing plywood out of Perumbavoor since 1986 — about forty years of continuous operations — and CWI Pvt Ltd today supplies pan-India out of contract factories built to group specifications. For Davangere, the typical stack is:

Packing-grade plywood (the workhorse)

Commercial / rubberwood packing-grade ply is what most Davangere crate-makers and pallet shops actually need. It is engineered for nail-holding strength, dimensional stability and a consistent thickness — not for furniture polish. We ship it in standard 8' x 4' sheets across the packing thickness range:

ThicknessCommon use in Davangere
6 mmLight cartons, partitions, top covers
8 mmSide panels of textile-spare boxes
10–12 mmPallet decks, medium crate walls
14–16 mmHeavy machinery crates, foundry boxes
18 mmContainer flooring, heavy base boards

Loads ship full truck out of Kerala with a clean GST invoice; cut-to-size is possible for repeat crate dimensions.

Wooden crates and packing cases

For buyers who would rather receive finished crates than build them in-house, the group also supplies fabricated boxes — closed, slatted, or skidded — built around the customer's product drawing. Typical Davangere builds: 1.2 m x 0.8 m x 1.0 m machinery crates with 12 mm walls and runner skids, or larger 2.0 m boxes for full ring-frame assemblies.

Pallets

Standard four-way entry pallets in 1200 x 1000 mm and 1200 x 800 mm (Euro), plus custom footprints for cotton bale stacking, are built from sawn timber decks or plywood tops with hardwood runners.

Container flooring and base boards

18 mm packing ply in 8' x 4' sheets is the common floor liner for ODC and break-bulk container loads moving from inland Davangere down to Mangaluru or across to Chennai. It protects the container metal floor and gives forklifts a clean working surface.

Okoume plywood

Okoume is a premium export-grade plywood we keep separately. For most Davangere domestic packing, it is overkill. It earns its price when the buyer is shipping high-value finished goods — engineered textile machinery for export, precision spares, electronics — where surface finish, light weight and long sea-freight life matter.

Shuttering plywood and sawn timber

Film-faced shuttering ply for the construction trade and rough sawn timber for runners and skids round out the supply.

Kochi-to-Davangere logistics

The road distance from Kochi/Ernakulam to Davangere is roughly 580–620 km, depending on whether the truck routes via Salem–Bengaluru on NH-44/NH-48 or via Mysuru on NH-766 and NH-275. The cleaner heavy-truck lane is the Salem–Bengaluru–Tumakuru–Chitradurga–Davangere run on NH-48, which is the standard six-lane corridor.

  • Transit time: a fully loaded part-load or full truck typically reaches Davangere in 24–36 hours from dispatch, weather and check-posts permitting.
  • Truck type: 32-ft single-axle or 22-ft container trucks are the practical sizes; the 32-ft handles a full pallet/crate load comfortably with room for top-stacked ply sheets.
  • Documentation: e-way bill from Kerala (origin) to Karnataka (destination) is standard; GST is charged IGST since it is an inter-state movement.
  • Port option: for the rare buyer who would rather receive at a Karnataka port, Mangaluru is the closer one (~250 km from Davangere). Most Davangere buyers find direct road from Kochi simpler and cheaper than the port detour.

Decision rules — which grade for which Davangere load

A practical shortlist that we walk every new Davangere buyer through before quoting:

  1. Domestic crate for a textile machine or agri pump → commercial packing ply, 12–16 mm walls, 18 mm base. Don't pay for Okoume.
  2. Pallet for cotton bales or yarn cartons → 12 mm packing ply deck on hardwood runners, or full sawn-timber deck. Pick by weight and re-use cycle.
  3. Container floor liner for ODC load to a port → 18 mm packing ply, full 8' x 4' sheets, laid edge-to-edge.
  4. Export crate for engineered textile machinery → Okoume face ply if the customer specifies a clean export look, ISPM-15 heat-treated softwood frame, IPPC stamp.
  5. Shuttering for a Davangere construction site → film-faced shuttering ply, separate price list, ordered by repetition count needed.

When in doubt, share the product dimensions, gross weight, dispatch mode (truck vs container vs port) and monthly off-take. We will revert with a thickness recommendation and a price per sheet or per finished crate.

FAQ

What is the minimum order to Davangere?

Practical minimum is a part-load of about 8–10 tonnes (roughly 250–300 sheets of mid-thickness packing ply, or a mixed crate-and-pallet consignment). Smaller trial orders are possible but the freight per sheet becomes uneconomical below that range.

Can you cut sheets to crate sizes before dispatch?

Yes. Repeat crate orders are usually cheaper to cut at our end and ship as a kit — the buyer saves on local cutting labour and waste. Share the BOM with grain direction and we will quote both ways.

Do you offer GST credit-invoice and e-way bill?

Every consignment ships with a proforma upfront, a tax invoice on dispatch (IGST for Karnataka), and a Kerala-to-Karnataka e-way bill. No cash, no kachcha bills.

Get a quote

If you are running a packing-case workshop, a pallet line, a textile mill packing department or an agri-equipment fabricator anywhere in the Davangere–Harihar belt, we can quote a delivered price to your gate. Share thickness, sheet size, monthly quantity and unloading location, and we will revert with a proforma the same working day. Request a quote here, or browse the resource hub for thickness charts, ISPM-15 notes and crate construction guides before you order.

title: Plywood Supply to Mangalore | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, Okoume-faced export crates, pallets and container flooring that Mangalore's port-side exporters, cashew packers and.
html:
Mangalore sits at the intersection of three things that consume a lot of plywood: a deep-water port that handles bulk and break-bulk export cargo, a cluster of heavy process industries on its outskirts, and a long-established cashew and marine economy that ships in wooden packaging every day. For a Kerala-based plywood operation, it is one of the most logical out-of-state markets to serve. The driving distance from Kochi to Mangalore on NH-66 is roughly 270 km, transit runs 7-9 hours in normal conditions, and a single 22-wheeler loaded out of our Perumbavoor yard can land at a Baikampady or Bajpe consignee's gate before the next morning's shift starts. This post lays out what kind of plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring the Mangalore industrial belt actually pulls, and how we ship it.

The Mangalore industrial picture
