Mangalore's manufacturing and export base is concentrated in a handful of well-known belts, and each one has a distinct packaging profile.

New Mangalore Port and the Baikampady - Panambur belt

New Mangalore Port (NMPT) is the anchor. It is one of India's twelve major ports and handles a heavy mix of POL products, containers, iron ore pellets, edible oils, fertilizer raw material, granite and project cargo. The Baikampady Industrial Area sits directly behind the port and is dense with packaging units, freight forwarders, CFS yards and exporters using wooden crates for break-bulk shipments. The KIADB Industrial Area at Baikampady alone has hundreds of small and medium units, most of which need either ISPM-15 stamped pallets, export-grade plywood cases, or container floor reinforcement boards.

MRPL, MCF and the heavy-engineering vertical

Mangalore Refinery and Petrochemicals (MRPL) at Katipalla and Mangalore Chemicals and Fertilizers (MCF) at Panambur are the two largest process plants in the region. Around them sits a long tail of engineering fabricators, valve and instrumentation suppliers, and pump and rotating-equipment workshops. These units routinely crate up spares, skid-mounted equipment and replacement assemblies for movement to plants across India and the Gulf. Their packing standard is unforgiving - the box needs to survive forklift handling, ship-side lifting and monsoon exposure - and that pushes them toward thicker, denser hardwood plywood.

Cashew, marine and seafood

Mangalore and the surrounding Dakshina Kannada district remain a major cashew processing belt, and the fishing harbour at Bunder is one of the largest on the west coast. The cashew industry consumes wooden cases and pallets for export consignments; the marine and seafood industry needs water-resistant plywood for thermocol-lined export cartons and for fishing-vessel deck and bulkhead work. Both of these are recurring, predictable demand streams rather than one-off project loads.

The Bajpe - airport - Mangaluru SEZ axis

The Mangaluru SEZ at Permude and the smaller estates around Bajpe and Surathkal house chemicals, electronics, and a growing cluster of light engineering units. Packaging demand here skews toward smaller, neater plywood crates and standardized pallets rather than oversized project boxes.

What we ship to Mangalore

Across these belts the consolidated material list is fairly tight, and our standard runners cover most of it.

Packing-grade commercial plywood

This is the workhorse for export crate walls, lids and bottoms. Common thicknesses pulled by Mangalore buyers:

ThicknessTypical use
6 mm and 8 mmCrate side panels for light spares, cashew cartons, electronic sub-assemblies
10 mm and 12 mmStandard export crates, lids, partitions, ISPM-15 pallet top decks
15 mm, 16 mm, 18 mmHeavy crates for MRPL/MCF spares, pump bodies, container flooring patches, skid bases

Standard sheet size is 8' x 4'. Cut-to-size from this stock list is straightforward and we can pre-cut at the yard if the buyer shares a cutting plan with their PO.

Okoume plywood for premium export crates

Okoume is a lighter, cleaner-faced hardwood plywood used where the crate has to look presentable on arrival - typically engineering exports going into the Gulf or Southeast Asia, and high-value spares for OEMs that audit their inbound packaging. It is not a replacement for our commercial packing grade; it is a separate decision (see the rules below).

Crates, cases and pallets

For buyers who want a finished crate rather than sheet stock, we supply fully assembled wooden cases and pallets built to dimension. Standard pallet footprints we run are 1200 x 1000 mm and 1200 x 800 mm; both can be supplied ISPM-15 compliant where the destination requires heat-treated, IPPC-stamped wood. Crate construction uses sawn pine or hardwood framing with packing-grade plywood skins, screwed and stapled per the load class.

Container flooring and reinforcement boards

For project-cargo and oversize loads moving out of NMPT, 18 mm packing-grade or marine-grade plywood is used as supplementary container floor protection and as load-spreading boards under heavy point loads. We ship this either as full sheets or pre-cut to the container internal dimensions.

Shuttering ply

The Mangalore region's construction belt - port infrastructure work, NH-66 widening, hospitality projects on the beachfront, and SEZ build-out - also pulls film-faced shuttering plywood in 12 mm and 18 mm. This is a separate product from packing grade and is specified by face film and reuse cycles.

Kochi-to-Mangalore logistics

The route is NH-66 the whole way: Kochi - Thrissur - Kozhikode - Kannur - Kasaragod - Mangaluru. Distance is roughly 270 km from Perumbavoor; tolls and the Kannur diversions add a little time, but a truck dispatched in the evening from our yard typically clears the Karnataka border by early morning and reaches a Baikampady gate the same day. We work with established Kerala-Karnataka trucking operators on this lane, so backhaul economics keep freight reasonable on full-truckload orders. Mixed loads with other Karnataka-bound consignees are possible for part-load buyers, though lead time stretches.

For Mangalore buyers who prefer rail-linked freight via Mangaluru Junction or coastal movement, we can quote ex-yard Perumbavoor and let the buyer's forwarder handle onward movement; in practice, road on NH-66 is the default because transit is short enough that the extra handling rarely pays off.

Decision rules: which grade for which load

A simple way to think about the grade choice for Mangalore-bound packaging.

  • Use commercial packing-grade plywood when the crate is for general export, the load is mechanical or hardware, the box will be opened and disposed of at destination, and cost matters. This is roughly 80 percent of what flows out of Baikampady.
  • Use Okoume plywood when the consignee is an OEM or premium buyer (especially in the Gulf), when the crate exterior is visible at delivery, when weight reduction matters for air freight, or when the crate may be photographed for QA records.
  • Use marine or BWR-bonded plywood for fishing-vessel work, for crates that will sit on a deck or open quay before stuffing, and for container floor reinforcement on monsoon-window shipments.
  • Use shuttering ply only for concrete formwork. It is film-faced and not meant for crate construction; mixing the two on a PO causes confusion at site.

FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15 stamped pallets and crates?

Yes. Where the destination country requires IPPC-compliant heat-treated wood, we supply pallets and crate framing built to ISPM-15 with the appropriate stamp. The buyer should confirm the destination requirement on the PO so we route the order to the correct framing line.

What is the minimum order quantity from Kochi to Mangalore?

Full-truckload economics work best - typically 400 to 600 sheets of plywood depending on thickness, or an equivalent mix of crates and pallets. We do quote part-loads for smaller buyers, but freight per sheet rises and transit time can stretch while we consolidate.

Can you cut sheets to size before dispatch?

Yes. Share the cutting plan with the PO and we will pre-cut at the yard. This saves the buyer's carpentry time at the crate-assembly stage and reduces wastage on the Mangalore end.

Get a quote

If you are running a packing line, a forwarding yard, or an engineering fabrication unit anywhere in the Mangalore - Baikampady - Bajpe belt and you want a Kochi-side plywood supplier on call, we are set up for it. The Cochin Wood group has been running out of Perumbavoor since 1986, so the trucking lanes, the trader network on both sides of the border, and the seasonal cashew-and-marine demand cycles are familiar territory. Send your thickness mix, monthly volume and delivery point and we will request a quote turnaround the same working day. For specs, FAQs and sample cutting plans, the resource hub is the right starting point.

title: Plywood Supply to Hubli-Dharwad | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, knock-down crates, pallets and container flooring for the twin-city industrial cluster of North Karnataka. Our group.
html:
Why Hubli-Dharwad Sits on Our Daily Despatch List

Hubli-Dharwad is the commercial spine of North Karnataka — a twin-city industrial cluster that quietly pushes out tile pallets, bus assemblies, food-grade exports and engineering sub-assemblies every week. For a packing-plywood supplier sitting in Kochi, that translates to a steady, predictable demand profile: heavy crates for machinery, repeatable pallet sizes for ceramic tile loads, and container flooring for agro-export consignments routed through Mangaluru and Nava Sheva. The Cochin Wood group has been working the South Indian export-packing corridor since 1986, and Hubli-Dharwad has become one of the recurring lanes where our truckloads land.

The Hubli-Dharwad Industrial Picture

The twin city's manufacturing footprint is spread across a handful of well-defined belts. Understanding which estate a buyer sits in usually tells us what plywood specification they actually need before the call even starts.

Key industrial belts and sectors

  • Tarihal Industrial Area — the largest KIADB estate on the Hubli side, housing automotive ancillary units, fabrication shops and the bus-body and rail-coach ecosystem feeding Tata Marcopolo's plant. Crates here trend large and heavy.
  • Gokul Road Industrial Area — a mix of engineering, packaged-food and FMCG units. Demand here leans toward repeatable pallet sizes and lighter export cartons with plywood reinforcement.
  • Belur Industrial Area, Dharwad — KIADB's flagship Dharwad estate, anchored by automotive, electrical and pharma units. This belt drives steady ISPM-15 crate volume because finished goods often move to GCC and South-East Asian markets.
  • Rayapur and Mummigatti — newer KIADB pockets with food-processing, cold-chain and agro units. Container flooring and slip-sheet plywood see seasonal spikes here.
  • Ceramic and granite cluster around Hubli — Murudeshwar Ceramics and the broader tile/sanitaryware ecosystem are heavy plywood consumers for tile pallets, edge-protection battens and reinforced crates for vitrified tile exports.
  • APMC Yard and agro-processing belt — chilli, cotton, jowar and oilseed processing units that need pallets and container-floor sheets for bulk export cargo.

The sectors we serve in this geography break down cleanly into four buckets: automotive and bus-body assemblies, ceramic and tile exports, agro and food processing, and general engineering exports. Each bucket has its own plywood diet.

What We Ship to Hubli-Dharwad

The truckloads heading north on NH-66 and then east via NH-748 typically carry a mix of three product families. The exact split depends on which estate the buyer sits in.

Packing-grade plywood

Our MR-Packing grade is the workhorse. Standard thicknesses moving to Hubli-Dharwad are 6mm, 8mm, 12mm and 18mm in 8x4 sheets, with 12mm being the most common for crate side panels and 18mm for bases under heavy ceramic or machinery loads. The grade carries our packing-spec glue line, hardwood core, and is graded for one-way export packaging — not furniture-finish. Selling rates ex-factory currently run from ₹24/sq.ft at 6mm to ₹42/sq.ft at 18mm, GST and freight extra. Okoume face options are available where buyers want a cleaner export-grade appearance, typically for high-value machinery crates or branded ceramic exports.

Crate construction

For Tarihal and Belur estate buyers building crates for buses, rail components, transformers or CNC machines, we ship knock-down crate kits cut to specification — base, four sides, lid, with battens and corner pieces pre-sized. ISPM-15 compliant heat-treated sawn timber for the framing is sourced through our group network and stamped at certified facilities. Loads above 2 tonnes typically take 18mm bases with 12mm sides on hardwood framing; lighter loads run 12mm bases with 8mm sides.

Pallet sizes

Ceramic and tile exporters in the Hubli belt tend to standardise on a few footprints. The two most common we ship are the 1200x1000mm Euro-style for European-bound containers and the 1100x1100mm CP standard for GCC and South-East Asia. Both are built with hardwood deckboards on rubberwood or jackwood stringers, with plywood top decks where the load profile demands a continuous surface.

Container flooring

For 20ft and 40ft containers being stuffed with granite slabs, ceramic tiles or heavy machinery sub-assemblies, we supply 18mm packing plywood floor sheets cut to container-floor dimensions. This is a small but recurring product for the Hubli-Mangaluru export movement.

Kochi to Hubli-Dharwad Logistics

The road distance from our Perumbavoor dispatch yard to Hubli is roughly 700 km. The standard route runs Perumbavoor → Thrissur → Palakkad → Coimbatore → Salem → Bangalore bypass → Tumakuru → Chitradurga → Hubli, taking the NH-44 corridor for most of the trip. Transit time for a single-driver truck is typically 36 to 48 hours; double-driver moves can land inside 30 hours when consignments are time-critical.

Trucking lane economics

The Kochi-Hubli lane is a well-priced lane because it is part of the broader South-to-North truck movement and rarely runs empty in either direction. Full-truck loads (FTL) — 20 to 22 tonnes of mixed plywood and sawn timber — are economical at this distance. Part-truck loads (PTL) are workable but the per-sheet freight bumps up noticeably, so we usually advise consolidating to FTL where the buyer's monthly draw supports it. For ISPM-15 stamped crate kits on tight export deadlines, we can also route through Mangaluru port and into Hubli inland via NH-66 → NH-67, which sometimes makes sense if the buyer's container is loading at New Mangalore Port.

Decision Rules: Which Grade for Which Load

A quick reference for buyers comparing options for typical Hubli-Dharwad use cases:

Load profileRecommended gradeWhy
One-way export crates, ceramic tiles, machinery sub-assemblies under 2 tonnesMR-Packing 12mm sides, 18mm baseBest ₹/sq.ft economics for single-use export packaging
High-value branded ceramic or machinery exports needing cleaner faceMR-Packing with Okoume faceSmooth export-grade appearance without paying for full BWR
Heavy machinery, transformer or CNC crates above 3 tonnesBWR-grade hardwood plywood with ISPM-15 framingMoisture-resistant glue line for long sea voyages and tropical destinations
Construction project boxing in Hubli's infrastructure buildsShuttering ply 12mmDesigned for repeated concrete pours, not export packing
Tile pallet decks and reusable warehouse palletsMR-Packing 12mm top deck on hardwood stringersStandard repeat-use spec at workable cost

The general rule we apply on buyer calls: if the load is going inside India, MR-Packing is almost always the right answer. If it is going on a sea voyage longer than 14 days, the conversation shifts toward BWR or Okoume-face options.

FAQ — Common Questions from Hubli-Dharwad Buyers

How quickly can a truck be despatched after the PI is settled?

For stock-grade MR-Packing in standard thicknesses, despatch happens within 48 to 72 hours of payment confirmation. Cut-to-size crate kits and Okoume-face loads typically add another 3 to 5 working days because of the cutting and pressing schedule.

Do you supply ISPM-15 stamped crates ready to load?

Yes. Crate kits ship with ISPM-15 stamped sawn-timber framing through our group network of certified facilities. Plywood panels themselves do not require ISPM-15 because they are engineered wood, but the framing timber does — and that is what carries the stamp.

What is the minimum order quantity for Hubli-Dharwad delivery?

The freight economics make full-truck loads (around 800 to 1,000 sheets depending on thickness mix) the most efficient option. Smaller volumes are possible on shared trucks but the per-sheet landed cost rises sharply.

Requesting a Quote

For Hubli-Dharwad buyers in the Tarihal, Gokul Road, Belur, Rayapur or APMC belts who need a structured quote on packing plywood, crate kits, pallets or container flooring, the fastest path is to share thickness mix, monthly draw volume, delivery estate and end-use (export vs domestic). We will return a PI ex-Perumbavoor with GST and freight calculated to your Hubli or Dharwad yard within one working day. Request a quote with your specifications, or browse our resource hub for grade comparisons, ISPM-15 documentation and crate-construction guides before you write up the requirement.

title: Plywood Supply to Karimnagar | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, export crates and pallets to drawing, dispatched from our Perumbavoor operation. Our group has run plywood.
html:
Karimnagar sits in the middle of a state that exports two things almost everyone overlooks when they think of plywood demand — silver filigree handicrafts that travel half the world, and granite blocks weighing fifteen tonnes each. Both move in wooden crates. Add the agri-processing belt around Jagtial Road and the spinning mills feeding into the broader Telangana cotton chain, and you have a city that quietly burns through several truckloads of packing-grade plywood every week. The Cochin Wood group has been supplying these kinds of upcountry industrial belts since 1986, and Karimnagar — 1,400 km from our Kochi base — is one of the lanes where the trucking economics still work cleanly.

The Karimnagar industrial picture

Karimnagar is not one cluster. It is four overlapping ones, and each has a different packing requirement.

Silver filigree and handicrafts

The Karimnagar silver filigree GI tag covers an export-oriented craft cluster that has been ramping up under the state's handicraft promotion push. The pieces themselves are light, but they are fragile, high-value, and almost always crated for air cargo or premium sea LCL. The need here is thin-section packing-grade plywood — typically 6 mm and 8 mm — used for inner trays, partitions and the outer shell of small export crates. Tolerance for splintering is low; the buyer does not want to open a crate at the destination and find a face veneer that has chipped against a delicate piece.

Granite blocks and tiles

The belt running through Karimnagar, Peddapalli and out toward Ramagundam is a granite-quarrying and processing zone. Black galaxy, tan brown and silver pearl varieties move through here. Granite blocks are loaded directly onto trucks for inland movement, but tile consignments destined for the Middle East and the UK leave in heavy crates — typically 18 mm packing-grade plywood walls with hardwood runners underneath, sized to fit a 20-ft container deck. Container flooring replacement plywood is a related ask from logistics yards that refurbish boxes.

Agri-processing and cold storage

Rice mills around Jagtial Road and turmeric processors closer to Sircilla generate a steady requirement for pallets — both EUR-pattern 1200×800 mm and the Indian 1200×1000 mm. Most are heat-treated to ISPM-15 if the consignment heads to a port. The volumes are not flashy, but they are predictable, which matters more for logistics planning than a one-time large order.

Telangana spinning and engineering

The Karimnagar–Warangal corridor pulls in spinning mill machinery, transformers and a smaller flow of pump-set and motor manufacturing into the nearby industrial estates. Heavy engineering goods need shuttering-grade or BWR plywood for the crate base where direct contact with steel skids occurs, plus 12 mm packing grade for the side walls.

What we ship to Karimnagar

Material choice should follow the load, not the brochure. The most common items in a Karimnagar dispatch are:

  • 6 mm and 8 mm packing-grade plywood — handicraft inner partitions, light export crates, dunnage sheets.
  • 12 mm packing-grade plywood — the workhorse thickness for medium engineering goods and general-purpose crates.
  • 18 mm packing-grade plywood — the heavy side panel for granite tile crates and machinery boxes.
  • Hardwood runners and sawn timber — jackwood and rubberwood, supplied in standard runner sizes for crate skids and pallet stringers.
  • ISPM-15 heat-treated pallets — assembled to EUR or Indian dimensions, stamped where the consignment is export-bound.
  • Container flooring plywood — 28 mm apitong-equivalent boards for shipping container floor refurbishment.

Where the end-customer is an export house dealing in a premium-finish product — silver, lacquered wood, certain artisanal categories — Okoume-faced plywood is worth specifying for the visible outer crate. The face veneer is cleaner, the panel weighs less per square foot, and the crate looks like it belongs to the cargo inside.

Kochi to Karimnagar logistics

The lane is roughly 1,400 km by road. We dispatch from the Perumbavoor cluster (the Cochin Wood group's home turf) and the typical route runs Kochi — Salem — Bengaluru — Anantapur — Kurnool — Hyderabad — Karimnagar. Transit time for a full truck is three to four days door-to-door, depending on the Bengaluru bypass and the Hyderabad inner-ring crossing.

One full 22-ft truck carries close to 1,000–1,200 sheets of 12 mm 8×4 packing-grade plywood, depending on density. For mixed loads — say, 600 sheets of 18 mm plus pallets — the load planning matters more, because granite-belt buyers usually want everything stacked so the heaviest material is dispatched off the truck first at site.

Buyers who can consolidate a full-truck order get the cleanest landed cost. Part-loads are workable but ride on shared transport and arrive on a slower clock — typically five to six days. For very large recurring requirements, a monthly indent against a price-protected quotation is the most economical structure.

Decision rules for Karimnagar loads

A short field guide for buyers picking material:

End useRecommended gradeTypical thickness
Silver filigree export crate (light, premium)Packing grade, optional Okoume face6–8 mm shell, 12 mm base
Granite tile export cratePacking grade with hardwood runners18 mm walls, hardwood base
Heavy machinery / transformer crateBWR or shuttering-grade base, packing-grade walls18 mm base, 12 mm walls
Rice / turmeric pallet (export)Sawn hardwood, ISPM-15 stamp1200×1000 or 1200×800 mm
Container floor refurbishmentApitong-equivalent flooring ply28 mm

Shuttering ply has a place where the crate base will see direct weather exposure during loading at the granite yard or on an open quarry dispatch — the BWR glue line stands up better to monsoon turnaround. For dry, indoor handicraft packing, paying for shuttering grade is unnecessary.

Frequently asked questions

Can you supply ISPM-15 certified pallets for direct export from Karimnagar?

Yes. Heat-treatment and stamping are arranged at dispatch, and the IPPC mark is applied to every certified piece. Share the destination country and approximate monthly count, and we will confirm the right specification.

What is the minimum order quantity?

For full economic landed cost in Karimnagar, a single-truck load (roughly 1,000 sheets equivalent or a mixed full-truck) is the cleanest structure. Smaller part-load orders are possible but ride on shared transport and a slightly higher per-sheet freight component.

Do you hold a buffer stock or do you make to order?

Both. Standard packing-grade thicknesses (6 mm, 8 mm, 12 mm, 18 mm) are held against group production. Okoume-faced panels, sized pallets and crate kits are made to the buyer's specification with a short lead time, typically one to two weeks plus dispatch.

Getting a quote

Tell us the destination yard in Karimnagar, the thickness mix, the monthly indent volume, and whether you need ISPM-15 stamping. We will come back with an ex-Kochi rate, a landed-in-Karimnagar rate, and a delivery window. The fastest route is to request a quote with those four data points in hand. For technical spec sheets, grade comparisons and the full thickness chart, the resource hub has the current reference material. Buyers in the granite belt and the handicraft cluster around Karimnagar move different volumes through the year, and we are happy to structure pricing against an annual offtake rather than a one-off enquiry.

title: Plywood Supply to Khammam | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade and Okoume-faced plywood, heavy crates, pallets and container flooring to your drawing, made in Perumbavoor by a group that has.
html:
Khammam sits in a stretch of Telangana where coal, thermal generation, granite and irrigation projects all run side by side. That mix produces a steady demand for industrial packing — heavy power-equipment crates, pallet pools for spare parts moving to Singareni and NTPC sites, and container flooring for granite blocks and processed agri loads heading toward Krishnapatnam and JNPT. The Cochin Wood group has been making plywood for packaging since 1986 out of Perumbavoor, and Khammam is a lane we serve regularly through our pan-India trucking network. This guide walks through what we ship there, why the specs matter for Telangana's load profile, and how the Kochi-to-Khammam route actually works.

The Khammam industrial picture

Khammam district is anchored by the Singareni Collieries Company Limited (SCCL) coal belt — Yellandu, Kothagudem, Manuguru and Sathupalli are all within an hour or two of Khammam town, and the coal that leaves these mines feeds thermal stations across South India. Around that coal core, several other industries are active:

  • Coal handling and washery equipment — conveyor sections, crusher housings, screen decks and electrical control panels move in and out of SCCL projects almost weekly. These are exactly the irregular, point-loaded items that need purpose-built plywood crates rather than off-the-shelf cartons.
  • Thermal power EPC supply chain — the Kothagudem Thermal Power Station and the broader NTPC/Telangana Genco footprint pull in turbine spares, transformer components, valves and instrumentation packed for cross-state transit.
  • Granite and dimensional stone — the Khammam–Paloncha–Madhira granite quarries ship blocks and cut slabs in containers, and container floors take real punishment under those loads.
  • Agri processing — chilli, cotton, mango and rice from the Khammam belt move on pallets to wholesale markets and exporters, and the pallet pool turns over fast.
  • General engineering clusters around Khammam, Sathupalli and the IDA estates — fabrication shops, motor rewinders and pump assemblers that need crates only when an order ships outside Telangana.

Each of those sectors has a different packaging brief, but they all run on the same family of inputs: BWR or MR-grade plywood at the right thickness, sawn battens for framing, and either nailed-and-strapped crates or ISPM-15 heat-treated crates when the load is leaving the country.

What we ship into Khammam

The product list below covers what actually moves on a Kochi–Khammam truck. We are happy to quote on partial truck loads when a buyer is testing a spec, but the freight economics favour full 9-tonne or 16-tonne consignments.

Packing-grade plywood

Our packing range is BWR-bonded hardwood plywood made under Cochin Wood group specifications at contract factories in Perumbavoor. Standard panel size is 8x4 ft. The thicknesses we move most into Telangana for packaging work are:

ThicknessTypical Khammam use
6 mmLightweight crate panels, electrical control box liners
9 mmInternal partitioning, spare-parts crate walls
12 mmGeneral-purpose crate walls, top and base panels for medium loads
18 mmHeavy crate bases, container flooring strips, skid panels for power equipment

For Khammam buyers handling coal-handling equipment or transformer parts, 12 mm walls with an 18 mm base on a hardwood batten frame is the workhorse construction. The plywood is moisture-resistant enough to survive monsoon-season transit between Kerala, Karnataka and Telangana without delamination at the corners.

Crates, including ISPM-15

For export crates — which matters for granite, engineering goods and any cargo moving to GCC, Vietnam or Europe — we supply ISPM-15 compliant heat-treated crates with the IPPC stamp. Domestic crates for inter-state movement inside India do not need ISPM-15, but many Khammam exporters keep both specs running because the same workshop builds them on the same jigs.

Pallets

Two-way and four-way pallets in 1200x1000 mm and 1200x800 mm (Euro size) are the most common requests. For Khammam's agri loads we also build heavier 1200x1200 mm pallets in jackwood or rubberwood runners with plywood deck boards.

Container flooring

For granite block consolidators and heavy machinery shippers, 18 mm BWR plywood used as container floor reinforcement saves the actual container floor from gouging and stops point-loads from punching through. We supply it in pre-cut strips or full sheets depending on how the loaders prefer to work.

Sawn timber and battens

Hardwood battens — jackwood at ¼-inch-minus tolerance, rubberwood at standard sizes — go out alongside the plywood so a Khammam crate workshop can finish in one purchase order instead of chasing two suppliers.

Kochi to Khammam — the route economics

Khammam is approximately 1,200 km from Kochi by road. The standard trucking lane runs Kochi → Salem → Bengaluru → Anantapur → Kurnool → Hyderabad → Khammam, on NH 44 and NH 65. Transit time for a 16-tonne full-truck-load is typically 4 to 5 days door to door, depending on weighbridge queues at the Telangana–Andhra border and monsoon conditions on the Western Ghats stretch.

The rail option (Kochi via Vijayawada to Khammam) is cheaper per tonne on paper, but break-bulk handling and the absence of a clean direct goods-shed pairing make road transport more reliable for plywood and crate panels. Most of our Telangana customers stay on road.

For very large orders we can also rail-mode through Bengaluru and then road from there, which trims about a day off transit. Container-flooring sheets that are eventually loaded into a sea container at Krishnapatnam or Visakhapatnam can sometimes ride into the same container — worth discussing case by case during the quote.

Choosing the right grade for Khammam loads

Three of our grades cover almost every Khammam packaging brief. The decision rules below are how we coach buyers when they ask:

  • BWR packing-grade hardwood plywood — default for almost every domestic and most export crates. Use it for SCCL coal-handling equipment, NTPC thermal spares, general engineering crates and pallet decks.
  • Okoume plywood — premium, lighter-weight option. Worth specifying when the crate itself adds meaningful freight cost, for high-value exports, or when the buyer wants a cleaner finished look on the panels. Common for engineering exports to GCC.
  • Shuttering / film-faced plywood — not a packaging product as such, but Khammam's irrigation, infrastructure and EPC contractors use it for concrete formwork on bridges, canals and power-plant civil works. Available in 12 mm and 18 mm, film-faced both sides.

If you are unsure which grade fits a particular consignment, the resource hub at our resource hub has plain-language guides on BWR vs BWP, ISPM-15 specifications and packing-case construction.

FAQ

How long does delivery to Khammam typically take from Kochi?

Four to five days for a full-truck-load by road, assuming normal weighbridge times and no monsoon disruption on the Western Ghats. Part-loads piggyback on consolidator trucks and may run six to seven days.

Do you supply ISPM-15 certified crates for granite and engineering exports?

Yes. Heat-treated, IPPC-stamped crates are a standard product for us. For Khammam exporters this matters when cargo is heading to GCC, the EU or any country that enforces ISPM-15 at port of entry.

Can you ship to Khammam, Kothagudem, Sathupalli and the SCCL belt directly?

Yes, we deliver door-to-site across the district. Kothagudem and Sathupalli usually add half a day of last-mile from the Khammam unloading point, which is factored into the freight quote.

Get a quote

If you are a Khammam packaging workshop, a granite shipper, an SCCL contractor or an exporter sizing up plywood, crate, pallet or container-flooring supply, send us your typical thickness, panel size, monthly quantity and delivery point — town or industrial estate is enough. We will come back with an ex-Kochi rate and a delivered-Khammam rate so you can compare. Request a quote through the form and one of our team will be in touch the same working day.

title: Plywood Supply to Warangal | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, wooden crates, pallets and container flooring in Perumbavoor, where our group has run mill operations since 1986.
html:
Warangal: a quiet, steady plywood-consuming belt

Warangal does not show up first on most plywood-buyer maps, but the city pulls a surprisingly consistent stream of packing-grade material. Two end-uses drive almost all of it: textile machinery and finished-fabric crates moving out of the Kakatiya Mega Textile Park (KMTP) and surrounding hand-loom and power-loom clusters, and granite slab packing moving south to Chennai port or west to Mundra. Both jobs need plywood that survives long road runs, weather, and rough fork-lift handling. That is the lane the Cochin Wood group has built around for forty years, and Warangal sits cleanly inside our normal pan-India dispatch grid.

The Warangal industrial picture

Warangal Tri-City (Warangal-Hanamkonda-Kazipet) anchors the manufacturing belt of north Telangana. The cluster has a specific shape that matters when sizing packing material.

Textile and apparel

  • Kakatiya Mega Textile Park (KMTP), Shayampet/Geesugonda — Telangana's flagship textile park spread across roughly 1,200 acres. End-to-end value chain from spinning to garmenting, which means heavy machinery inbound (looms, dyeing lines, finishing units) and finished-goods outbound by container.
  • Hand-loom and power-loom clusters in Warangal Rural, Bhupalpally and Chennur — Sircilla power-loom belt sits adjacent. Mostly outbound fabric in compact crates or pallet loads.
  • Warangal IDA-Madikonda and the older industrial estates around Kazipet — mixed engineering and ancillary units that crate spares and small machine sub-assemblies.

Granite and stone

  • The Karimnagar–Warangal granite belt (Tandur black, Telangana black, multi-colour) feeds processing yards around Warangal and Jangaon. Slabs and gangsaw blocks ship in heavy A-frame crates with plywood bottoms and packing-grade ply spacers.

Engineering, food and FMCG ancillaries

  • MSME estates at Madikonda and along NH-163 — light engineering, electrical panels, food-grade machinery — typical buyers of mid-size plywood boxes and four-way pallets.

What we ship to Warangal

Most Warangal orders fall into four product buckets. We quote ex-factory Kochi/Perumbavoor and arrange the truck on request.

1. Packing-grade plywood (sheets)

Our core SKU. Made on a commercial hardwood / rubberwood core, glue line tuned for crate-life rather than furniture-life. Standard thicknesses we move into Telangana:

ThicknessTypical useSheet size
6 mmLight fabric cartons, lid panels8 x 4 ft
8–10 mmMedium textile-machine crates, granite slab spacers8 x 4 ft
12 mmGeneral-purpose packing case sides and top8 x 4 ft
15–16 mmHeavy machinery sides, pallet deck boards8 x 4 ft / cut-to-size
18 mmCrate floors, pallet stringers, container flooring repair8 x 4 ft / 8 x 4 ft EU

Cut-to-size is available on full-truck orders, which usually saves the Warangal carpenter a day of saw work on heavy granite crates.

2. Built-up wooden crates and boxes

For machinery exports leaving the textile park, we supply ISPM-15 heat-treated crates built around 12–18 mm packing ply skins on hardwood framing. We can prefab and dispatch nested or knocked-down, depending on container fit. Typical inside-dimensions we build are loom-frame size (roughly 2.4 m x 1.6 m x 1.8 m) and dyeing-machine size (3 m+ long); both ride out the Kochi–Warangal road run without issue when banded correctly.

3. Pallets

Two-way and four-way pallets in 1200 x 1000 mm (Indian standard) and 1200 x 800 mm (Euro), built on hardwood blocks or stringers with packing-grade ply deck boards. For granite slab yards we also build heavy-deck pallets (18 mm deck, double bottom) rated for repeated fork-lift cycles.

4. Container flooring plywood

18 mm and 21 mm phenolic-bonded ply for retrofitting 20-ft and 40-ft container floors — useful for the granite exporters running their own captive container fleets to Chennai and Krishnapatnam. We supply in standard container-floor sheet sizes and can pre-drill on bulk orders.

Kochi to Warangal: the lane

The road math is the first thing a Warangal buyer should know, because freight is the swing variable on a plywood quote.

  • Distance: roughly 1,250–1,320 km depending on route (NH-544 Kochi–Salem–Bangalore–Hyderabad–Warangal vs Kochi–Coimbatore–Kurnool–Hyderabad–Warangal).
  • Transit time: 3–4 days door-to-door for a full truck (32-ft single-axle or 22-ft, depending on volume). Part-loads via Hyderabad transhipment add a day.
  • Truck size economics: a 22-ft truck holds roughly 350–400 sheets of 8 x 4 x 12 mm; a 32-ft single-axle takes 600–700 sheets. Granite crate orders are bulkier — we cube the load before quoting.
  • Port alternative: for very large lots (5+ containers), some buyers prefer Cochin Port → Krishnapatnam → road to Warangal. We can quote either way.

For most Warangal buyers, the right anchor question is: full truck or half? Below half a 22-ft load, part-load freight per sheet usually overtakes the savings on the plywood itself, and a local Hyderabad source can look cheaper on paper. Above half-truck, our Kochi pricing competes cleanly on a delivered basis.

Which grade fits which Warangal load

Packing grade (rubberwood/commercial hardwood core)

Default for almost everything moving out of Warangal: textile-machine crates, fabric pallets, granite spacers, engineering-spares boxes. Built for one or two one-way journeys with banding, not for re-use as furniture stock.

Okoume face / BWR construction

Step up to Okoume-faced or BWR-grade construction when (a) the crate is moving by sea container to GCC / South-East Asia from Krishnapatnam or Chennai, (b) the consignee has weight limits and Okoume's lighter density helps the airfreight or volumetric calculation, or (c) the buyer wants a cleaner face for branded engineering exports. Okoume is a face-veneer option on our packing and BWR grades, not a separate grade — we'll quote it on a per-job basis.

Shuttering ply

Not a packing product, but worth a mention. Warangal has continuous infra work (Kaleshwaram support structures, KMTP expansion phases, road widening on NH-163). When a construction buyer asks for "packing ply" for shuttering use, that is the wrong call — film-faced shuttering ply lasts 15–25 pours where packing ply will give up in 2–3. We carry both; we will steer the spec correctly before quoting.

FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15 stamped crates from Warangal?

We ship ISPM-15 heat-treated, stamped crates from our facility — the wood is treated and stamped at origin, and the crate arrives in Warangal ready to load with the consignor's goods. Most textile-machine exporters take this route to avoid local HT availability gaps.

What is your minimum order for Warangal dispatch?

Economics start to work at around half a 22-ft truck — roughly 150–200 sheets of mixed thickness, or one 20-ft worth of built-up crates. Below that, part-load is possible but the freight per sheet rises sharply.

How long from PI to delivery?

For standard packing-grade sheet stock: 3–5 days production + 3–4 days transit, so plan for around 8–10 days from confirmed PI. Built-up crates add 5–7 days for fabrication depending on the spec sheet.

Get a quote for Warangal

If you crate textile machinery out of KMTP, ship granite from the Jangaon belt, or move engineered goods through Hyderabad on the way to a port, send us a thickness mix, monthly volume, and a delivery PIN. We will come back with an ex-factory Kochi rate, a delivered Warangal rate, and a realistic dispatch date. Request a quote with your spec, or browse the resource hub for packing-case guides, ISPM-15 background, and the BWR vs MR explainer before you write the spec.

title: Plywood Supply to Aurangabad | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates and pallets that Aurangabad's Waluj, Shendra and Chikalthana MIDC estates load onto export-bound trucks.
html:
Aurangabad — officially Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar since 2023 — is one of the densest engineering-export belts in central India. Between the Waluj, Shendra and Chikalthana MIDC estates, the city pushes out auto components, two-wheelers, pharma machinery, cooking-range appliances and beer & spirits, much of it crated and shipped to JNPT (Nhava Sheva), Mundra and onward to Europe, the Middle East and ASEAN. Every CKD pack, every sea-worthy crate, every export pallet starts with one boring but unforgiving input: plywood that does not blister on the truck, does not snap on the forklift, and does not get rejected at the ISPM-15 stamp. That is the slot the Cochin Wood group has been filling out of Kerala for nearly four decades, and the lane we run into Aurangabad.

The Aurangabad industrial picture

Aurangabad is not one factory cluster — it is roughly four, stitched together by NH-52 and NH-753, and now plugged into the Delhi-Mumbai Industrial Corridor (DMIC) through the AURIC node at Shendra-Bidkin. The packing-grade plywood demand profile differs from belt to belt, so it helps to map them before talking material.

Waluj MIDC

The original 1980s estate west of the city, anchored by the Bajaj Auto two-wheeler plant and a deep tier-1/tier-2 supplier base around forgings, transmissions, sheet-metal stamping and plastic mouldings. Demand here is dominated by CKD and SKD crates for auto components moving to overseas assembly lines, plus heavy pallets for inter-plant movement.

Shendra & Bidkin (AURIC / DMIC)

The newer corridor estates east of the city. Shendra hosts large OEM-class plants — passenger vehicle, premium two-wheeler, EV cell and aerospace component lines — while Bidkin is filling up with mid-sized engineering, electronics and food-processing units. Crate sizes here run larger (full-engine and gearbox crates, machine bases), and ISPM-15 compliance is non-negotiable because most of the output is export-bound.

Chikalthana MIDC and the pharma belt

Chikalthana sits next to the airport and is heavier on pharma, packaging machinery and white-goods assembly. The plywood pull here is a mix of machinery export crates (tall, lightweight, often single-trip) and cleanroom-equipment packing where surface finish on the inner face matters.

Paithan and the upstream auto-feeder lane

South of the city on the Godavari, Paithan MIDC feeds Waluj with castings, hoses and rubber parts. Smaller crate volumes, but steady pallet demand for bulk component movement.

What we ship into Aurangabad

Three product families cover roughly 90% of what the belt actually consumes from us. Pricing is ex-Perumbavoor, GST and freight extra, and our standing rule is a flat ₹2.50/sq.ft margin over purchase rate — so quotes are short, defensible and easy for a purchase head to compare.

1. Packing-grade plywood (commercial / rubberwood core)

This is the workhorse — MR-bonded, hardwood-core packing ply for crate walls, lids, bases and pallet decks. Standard thicknesses we move on this lane:

  • 6 mm and 8 mm — light component cartons, partition inserts, lid skins
  • 10 mm and 12 mm — the dominant CKD/SKD crate-wall thickness for auto components
  • 15 mm, 16 mm, 18 mm — heavy machinery crates, gearbox/engine bases, container-lashed export packs

Sheet size is 8x4 ft standard; we cut to size at the contract factory when a buyer is running a fixed BOM (for example a recurring CKD pack for a Tier-1 transmission maker). Faces can be specified as Okoume or Gurjan veneer when the surface needs to look export-grade — Okoume and Gurjan are veneer options on the MR-Packing and BWR boards, not separate grades.

2. Sea-worthy crates and ISPM-15 pallets

Aurangabad's export volume means most plywood we ship lands inside a fabricated crate or pallet within days. We supply both as semi-knock-down kits (flat-packed boards with cleat schedule) and as finished assemblies. Pallet sizes are mostly 1200x800 (Euro) and 1200x1000 (ISO) for European auto buyers, plus 1100x1100 for Japanese OEMs. ISPM-15 heat-treatment stamping is handled on the sawn-timber components — the plywood itself is exempt under ISPM-15, which simplifies the customs story for the buyer.

3. Container flooring and heavy-duty BWR ply

For machinery exporters whose loads exceed ~3 tonnes on a single base, we move 18 mm BWR (phenolic-bonded) hardwood ply as container-floor reinforcement and base-frame planking. This is the same product family used in reefer rebuilds and dry-container patching.

Kochi to Aurangabad — the lane

It is a ~1,100 km road haul. The clean route is Kochi → Bengaluru (NH-544 / NH-44) → Hyderabad (NH-44) → Solapur (NH-65) → Aurangabad (NH-52). On a 32-ft single-axle or 22-ft container truck the realistic transit is 4 to 5 days door-to-door, depending on Ghat-section weather and how the buyer schedules unloading.

ModeCapacityTypical useIndicative transit
32-ft single-axle truck~16 t / ~9,000 sq.ft of 12 mmFull-load mixed thickness orders4-5 days
22-ft container truck~9 t / ~5,000 sq.ft of 12 mmMid-volume orders, MIDC unloading4-5 days
Part-load consolidator2-5 tTrial orders, small crate batches6-8 days

For export shipments where the buyer is consolidating multiple suppliers, an alternative is FOB Cochin → JNPT coastal feeder, with the plywood landing at the buyer's CFA in Nhava Sheva and trucking the last 350 km to Aurangabad. We rarely recommend it for inland Aurangabad delivery — direct road from Perumbavoor is cheaper and faster — but it is the right answer when the cargo is being containerised for re-export.

Decision rules — which board for which load

A quick way to think about specification, written the way a packing-engineer would brief a junior:

  1. If the crate is single-trip, sea-freight, and load is under 1.5 t per crate — 12 mm MR packing ply, commercial face. This is the bulk of Waluj's CKD pack.
  2. If the export pack must look presentable (premium two-wheeler, aerospace, medical equipment) — 12 mm packing ply with Okoume or Gurjan face veneer. Surface uplift, same core.
  3. If the load is over 3 t or the crate will be re-used (returnable bin loop with a European OEM) — step up to 15 or 18 mm BWR hardwood ply, phenolic-bonded.
  4. If the deliverable is a base or floor that takes forklift point-load — 18 mm BWR, period. Container flooring lives here.
  5. If the spec sheet asks for "shuttering ply" — that is a construction-grade board (formwork for concrete). It is not the right input for a CKD crate. We supply both, but do not let the two product families get confused in the BOM.

FAQ

Do you handle ISPM-15 compliance end-to-end?

Yes for the sawn-timber components of the crate (cleats, runners, skids) — these are heat-treated and stamped at an IPPC-registered facility. The plywood itself is exempt from ISPM-15 because it is a processed wood product, which simplifies the export documentation.

What is the minimum order you ship to Aurangabad?

Economically, a full truckload (around ₹6-8 lakh of mixed packing ply) is the cleanest unit. We do support part-loads through consolidators for trial orders and one-off crate batches, with a freight uplift.

Can you cut to size and pre-drill for our crate BOM?

Yes. On recurring orders we work off the buyer's cut-list and ship sized panels, which removes the saw-mill step at the Aurangabad end. Useful when in-house carpentry capacity is the bottleneck.

Working with us

The Cochin Wood group has been operating out of Perumbavoor since 1986 — about forty years of plywood and sawn-timber manufacturing through the same supplier network we use today. Cochin Wood Industries Pvt Ltd is the corporate vehicle through which we sell into industrial belts like Aurangabad. If you are running a CKD pack, a machinery export crate or a container-flooring spec and want a defensible quote on 10 mm, 12 mm or 18 mm packing ply delivered to Waluj, Shendra or Chikalthana, request a quote with your thickness, monthly volume and delivery PIN. Sample boards and grade comparison guides are on the resource hub, including the BWP/BWR explainer and the ISPM-15 crate-cost note that most Aurangabad buyers ask about on the first call.

title: Plywood Supply to Kolhapur | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring that Kolhapur's foundries, machine shops and textile-machinery units load.
html:
Why Kolhapur sits on our radar

Kolhapur is one of those Maharashtra districts that quietly does the heavy lifting. Between the foundry clusters around Shiroli and Gokul Shirgaon, the textile machinery shops, the sugar-engineering work that feeds the surrounding co-operative mills, and the Kolhapuri footwear and silver-jewellery trade, the district pushes a steady volume of finished goods out by road and by container. Every one of those goods needs a wooden case, a pallet, a crate, or a sheet of plywood under it before it leaves the gate. That is the part of the supply chain where the Cochin Wood group has been working for forty years, and Kolhapur is one of the up-country lanes we run regularly out of Kochi.

The Kolhapur industrial picture

Before quoting anything, it helps to know which Kolhapur the buyer is in. The district is not a single industrial estate — it is a string of MIDCs and clusters with very different packaging needs.

Shiroli MIDC — foundry country

Shiroli, on NH-48 about 15 km north of Kolhapur city, is the heart of the district's foundry and machining belt. Ductile-iron and grey-iron castings move out of here in volume — pump bodies, automotive components, valve castings, hydraulic blocks. Foundry-grade output is heavy, oily, and unforgiving on weak packing. Buyers here typically need close-boarded plywood cases on heavy-duty pallets, often ISPM-15 treated for export.

Gokul Shirgaon MIDC — machine shops and engineering

Gokul Shirgaon, south-east of the city, runs more toward machined parts, textile machinery sub-assemblies, hydraulic and pneumatic equipment, and ancillary engineering for the foundry belt. The packing here is a mix of skeleton crates for bulky machine frames and full plywood cases for delicate assemblies.

Five-Star MIDC Kagal and the auto-component belt

The Kagal–Hatkanangale Five-Star MIDC, south of Kolhapur, hosts larger units and tier-1 auto suppliers feeding the Pune-Chakan corridor. Container-bound exports out of this belt typically route through JNPT and increasingly through inland container depots, which is where container flooring plywood and export-grade cases come in.

Textile machinery and sugar engineering

Ichalkaranji, an hour east, is a textile town with a long tail of loom and processing-machinery suppliers. The sugar belt around Kolhapur, Sangli and Karad keeps a steady flow of mill-spares, evaporator parts and pump assemblies moving between factories and mills. Both segments are repeat consumers of packing plywood and ready-cut crate components.

What we ship to Kolhapur

For the typical Kolhapur buyer, three product families cover almost everything that leaves the factory gate.

Packing-grade commercial plywood

Our standard packing range runs from 6 mm to 18 mm in 8x4 sheets, hardwood core, rated for case-making and pallet decking. Thickness selection for Kolhapur loads usually settles around:

ThicknessTypical use in Kolhapur
6–8 mmSide and top panels for light machined-part cases
10–12 mmStandard foundry-casting cases up to about 500 kg
14–16 mmHeavy casting cases, pump bodies, textile machine frames
18 mmContainer flooring, pallet decks for very heavy loads

Crates and ready-built cases

For buyers who would rather receive crate kits than cut their own, we supply pre-cut plywood panels with matching reapers and battens, sized to the customer's drawing. Foundry shops in Shiroli usually want close-boarded cases; textile-machinery shops in Gokul Shirgaon often prefer skeleton or part-open crates to keep tare weight down and let the receiver inspect the load on arrival. Both can be ISPM-15 heat-treated and stencilled if the goods are container-bound.

Pallets

Standard pallet sizes that move well in the Kolhapur belt:

  • 1200 x 1000 mm — the EUR-friendly size most exporters prefer
  • 1100 x 1100 mm — common for sea-freight to Gulf and South-East Asia
  • 1200 x 800 mm — for buyers shipping into European customers
  • Custom sizes — for awkward castings and machine bases

Container flooring plywood

For the auto-component and engineering exporters running 20-ft and 40-ft containers, we supply 18 mm and 21 mm container-flooring grade, full-size sheets with hardwood face, dimensioned for direct fitment.

Kochi to Kolhapur — the lane

The road distance from Kochi to Kolhapur is about 1100 km. The lane runs north on NH-66 along the Konkan coast, climbs through Karwar and Goa, and then turns inland through Belagavi onto NH-48 into Kolhapur. A loaded 32-ft single-axle truck typically makes the run in three to four days, depending on monsoon conditions on the Konkan stretch and on driver-change schedules.

For full-truck orders we usually route directly by road. For partial loads we consolidate at our Ernakulam loading point and move onto a Maharashtra-bound trunk service. Buyers who want to pull the goods themselves can collect ex-Ernakulam and route via their own transporter; we provide the loading plan, packing list and e-way bill at dispatch.

When to pick which grade

Three quick decision rules that save Kolhapur buyers from over-specifying or under-specifying:

  1. Standard packing range — for domestic movement of castings, sub-assemblies, textile parts and engineering goods within India. This is the workhorse and covers most outbound packing from Shiroli and Gokul Shirgaon.
  2. Okoume face — when the buyer wants a clean, light, presentable face on the case, typically for export consignments where the consignee in the Gulf, Europe or South-East Asia sees the case before it is opened. Okoume is offered as a face-veneer option on both our MR-Packing and BWR grades.
  3. Shuttering plywood — not packing at all. Reserved for the infrastructure and construction buyers around Kolhapur — bridge work on the new highway upgrades, sugar-mill civil expansions, commercial construction in the city.

FAQ

Do you offer ISPM-15 heat-treated crates for export shipments out of Kolhapur?

Yes. For container-bound consignments routing through JNPT or Mundra, we can supply ISPM-15 compliant cases and pallets with the IPPC stencil. Mention this at the quote stage so we plan the treatment and documentation correctly.

What is the minimum order quantity for a Kolhapur delivery?

For full-truck dispatches we are most efficient at around 600–700 sheets or a comparable mix of cut components. For smaller quantities we can ride a partial load on a Maharashtra-bound consolidation, though transit will be slightly longer.

Can you supply against a drawing, or do you only sell standard sheets?

Both. Standard 8x4 sheets are the most common dispatch, but for crate-makers and engineering buyers we regularly cut to size, supply panels with matching reapers, and quote per piece against the buyer's drawing.

Request a quote

If you are sourcing packing plywood, crates, pallets or container flooring for a unit in the Kolhapur belt — Shiroli, Gokul Shirgaon, Kagal, Ichalkaranji or anywhere else in the district — the fastest way to get a workable price is to request a quote with the thickness, size, monthly quantity and delivery address. For background on grades, decision rules and the lane economics we work to, the resource hub has the rest of the detail. The Cochin Wood group has been doing this work since 1986, and Kolhapur is well within the corridor we serve every week.

title: Plywood Supply to Nagpur | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates and pallets that move cargo out of Nagpur's MIHAN SEZ, Butibori and Hingna estates. Our group has run.
html:
Why Nagpur sits at the centre of India's packing-plywood map

Nagpur is geographically the closest thing India has to a logistics centre of gravity — the so-called Zero Mile. Cargo here moves north to Delhi, south to Chennai, east to Kolkata and west to JNPT/Mumbai with broadly similar truck-day economics. That single fact reshapes what factories around the city ship and how they pack it. Aerospace MRO units at MIHAN need crates that pass airline cargo scrutiny. Engineering goods leaving Butibori MIDC go out on heavy trailers and need pallet decks that survive double-handling. Orange exporters out of Kalmeshwar and Katol need fumigated wooden boxes for sea legs to the Gulf. The Cochin Wood group supplies packing-grade plywood, crate kits, pallets and container-flooring boards into all four of those flows from our Perumbavoor base near Kochi.

The Nagpur industrial picture

Before we talk product, it helps to map the demand. Nagpur's plywood-packing demand is not one market — it is at least five, each with a different load profile.

MIHAN SEZ and the aerospace cluster

MIHAN (Multi-modal International Cargo Hub and Airport at Nagpur) was built around the air-cargo proposition, and the cluster around it now includes Boeing's MRO facility, Dassault Reliance Aerospace, TAL Manufacturing and a tier of avionics and component shops. Aerospace movements need crates with predictable moisture behaviour, ISPM-15 compliance for international legs, and tight tolerance on internal dunnage. Plywood thickness here is typically 12 mm or 18 mm for the case walls, with 18 mm or 25 mm bearers.

Butibori MIDC and Hingna MIDC

Butibori is one of Asia's larger industrial estates by area, and Hingna sits closer to the city. Between them you have heavy electrical equipment, transformers, auto components (Mahindra, Bajaj tier-two), steel fabrication and CNC machine builders. These are pallet-and-crate buyers in the literal sense — pallets to move work-in-progress on the shop floor, and heavy export crates for finished goods bound for JNPT or Mundra. Standard EUR-pallet (1200 x 800 mm) and ISO-pallet (1200 x 1000 mm) deck sizes dominate.

Orange and agri-export belt

The Vidarbha orange belt around Katol, Kalmeshwar, Narkhed and Warud ships fresh and processed produce out via Mumbai and Mundra to the GCC. Plywood boxes for export-grade fruit need light-weight, low-moisture panels that pass phytosanitary inspection.

Coal, mining and power equipment

Western Coalfields headquartered at Nagpur, and the surrounding thermal-power equipment trade, generates a quiet but steady stream of demand for shuttering ply (formwork during plant construction) and heavy-duty crating for rotating equipment.

Container flooring and trailer-body shops

The trailer and container-body fabricators along the Wardha Road and Kamptee Road belt buy 28 mm container-flooring panels by the lot.

What we ship into Nagpur

Our standard supply to the city falls into four product families. We do not stock-and-sell from a Nagpur yard — these move on direct trailers from our contract factories in Perumbavoor, cut and pre-bundled to the buyer's bill of materials.

Packing-grade plywood (MR / BWR)

The workhorse. Hardwood-core, phenolic-bonded panels in 8 ft x 4 ft sheets. Common thicknesses we ship to Nagpur:

ThicknessTypical Nagpur use-case
6 mmLight fruit-export boxes, internal dividers
9 mmComponent cartons, light crates
12 mmGeneral-purpose crates, MIHAN airfreight cases
18 mmHeavy export crates, transformer cases, pallet deckboards
25 mmBearers, pallet stringers, equipment skids

Okoume face-veneer plywood

Where the buyer wants a cleaner face — typically for export crates that will be inspected at the receiving port, or for fruit-export boxes where face-fibre matters — we ship Okoume-faced packing ply. Quote on request; this sits at a premium to the MR-Packing range.

Pallets and crate kits

We can ship loose plywood, or pre-cut kits, or finished pallets and crates. Standard pallet sizes built for Nagpur shops include 1200 x 800 mm (EUR), 1200 x 1000 mm (ISO), 1100 x 1100 mm (Asia) and bespoke heavy-duty decks for transformer and machine-tool loads. ISPM-15 heat-treatment is available on the wooden runners where the export leg requires it.

Container flooring

28 mm phenolic-faced container-flooring boards in standard 2440 x 1220 mm sheets for the trailer and container-body fabricators on Wardha Road.

Kochi to Nagpur — the logistics that decide the price

The Kochi-to-Nagpur leg is roughly 1,500 km by road. Realistic transit on a single-trailer movement is 4 to 5 days door-to-door, depending on whether the trailer routes via Bangalore-Hyderabad-Adilabad or via Mangalore-Pune-Aurangabad. We typically dispatch from Perumbavoor (north of Kochi, about 30 km from Kochi Port) on 32 ft or 40 ft trailers carrying 25 to 28 tonnes of plywood per load, which works out to roughly 750 to 900 sheets of 18 mm equivalent per trailer.

For Nagpur buyers, this means two things. First, full-trailer (FTL) economics dominate — a single buyer ordering one full load gets a meaningfully lower landed cost than a part-load. Second, lead time from PI to delivered material in Nagpur is usually 10 to 14 days, with 7 days for production and dispatch and 4 to 5 days in transit. We have also routed export-bound material via Kochi Port and JNPT when the final destination is the GCC, which often beats sending the finished crate back across India.

Decision rules — which grade fits which load

A short operator's heuristic for buyers in the city:

  • If the load is going by sea and will be inspected at destination — specify Okoume face on at least the visible panels. The cleaner face passes scrutiny better.
  • If the load is heavy machinery moving by road or rail within India — MR-Packing grade in 18 mm walls with 25 mm bearers is the standard call.
  • If the load is fruit going to the GCC via Mundra — 6 mm or 9 mm MR-Packing in pre-cut box kits, ISPM-15 stamp on the bearers, keeps cost-per-box defensible.
  • If the work is plant or substation construction at Butibori or Koradi — shuttering ply, not packing grade. Different specification, different price.
  • If the work is container or trailer body fabrication — 28 mm phenolic container-flooring, no substitute.

FAQ

What is the minimum order quantity for delivery to Nagpur?

For a direct trailer dispatch from Perumbavoor, the economics start to work at roughly one full trailer — 25 tonnes, or about 750 to 900 sheets of 18 mm equivalent. Smaller quantities are possible but the per-sheet freight share gets heavy. Many Nagpur buyers consolidate a quarter's requirement into one FTL.

Do you supply ISPM-15 certified packing material?

Yes — for export-bound crates and pallets, we supply with ISPM-15 heat-treatment stamps on the wooden runners, which clears most international ports including the GCC, Vietnam, US and EU receiving terminals.

How long does a quote take?

If we have the thickness, sheet size, total quantity and a Nagpur delivery pincode, a firm PI usually goes back the same working day. Bespoke crate kits or pallet designs need 24 to 48 hours for the BOM.

Talk to us about your Nagpur requirement

The Cochin Wood group has been running plywood operations out of Perumbavoor since 1986 — about 40 years of group manufacturing experience in the same wood belt. CWI Pvt Ltd is the corporate entity that handles the pan-India and export supply today, and Nagpur is one of our regular trailer lanes. If you are running a packing-case unit, a pallet shop, an aerospace MRO, or an exporter in Butibori, Hingna or MIHAN, send us your specification and we will quote landed in your yard. Request a quote with thickness, sheet size, monthly quantity and your Nagpur delivery pincode. Buyers who want to read the grade and specification background first can start at our resource hub, which covers BWR vs MR-Packing, Okoume veneer choice, ISPM-15 crate cost, and container-flooring specifications.

title: Plywood Supply to Nashik | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade and Okoume-faced plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring that Nashik's MIDC pockets move parts and bottles on. Our.
html:
Nashik sits at an unusual crossroads in Indian industry. It is the country's wine capital, home to the Currency Note Press at Jail Road, and one of the densest concentrations of auto-component manufacturing west of the Ghats — Mahindra & Mahindra, Bosch, ABB, Mahindra Sona, CEAT and a long tail of Tier-1 and Tier-2 suppliers all draw parts in and ship finished goods out of the city. Every one of those flows needs wood. Crates for engine assemblies, pallets for component bins, plywood floors for export containers, and bottle-pack cases for the vineyards in Niphad and Dindori — the volume is steady and the specs are exacting. This page explains what the Cochin Wood group ships into Nashik, how the Kochi-to-Nashik lane works, and which grade fits which load.

The Nashik industrial picture

Nashik's manufacturing belt is not a single estate — it is a ring of MIDC pockets spread across roughly 30 km, each with its own packaging profile.

  • Satpur MIDC — the oldest belt, anchored by Mahindra & Mahindra's Nashik plant (XUV, Bolero and Scorpio lines) and a dense supplier cluster. Packing here is dominated by engine, axle and chassis-component crates moving to Chakan, Pune and onward to export ports.
  • Ambad MIDC — Bosch, CEAT, Crompton, Mahindra Sona Couplings and a heavy concentration of auto-component and electrical-equipment makers. Heavy ply crates, pallet-loads of stampings, and ISPM-15 marked export packing dominate here.
  • Sinnar MIDC (STICE) — newer, larger lots, growing engineering and EV-component base, with several units running their own in-house crate shops. Bulk plywood sheets and sawn-timber runners go here in containers.
  • Dindori and Vinchur MIDCs — wine bottling, food processing and the Sula / York / Soma cluster. Wine bottle packing, divider boards and pallet collars are the recurring needs.
  • Igatpuri and Gonde — heavy engineering, refractory and a steady flow of pharma logistics from the Mumbai-Nashik corridor.

Put together, the city consumes a mix that is heavier on packing plywood and crating-grade ply than on furniture or interior boards. That mix is exactly what the Cochin Wood group is built to supply.

What we ship to Nashik

The Cochin Wood group has been in Perumbavoor since 1986, and roughly forty years of group manufacturing has been spent narrowing in on the packing-grade and Okoume range. We do not run a hardware-store catalogue — we run a packaging-supply book.

Packing-grade plywood (MR-Packing)

Our packing-grade ply is the workhorse for the Nashik auto-component lane. Standard sheet size is 8 ft x 4 ft, and we mill thicknesses to match crate function:

ThicknessTypical use
6 mm / 8 mmTop lids, internal dividers, light component sleeves
10 mm / 12 mmSide panels for medium-duty engine and pump crates
14 mm / 15 mm / 16 mmBases and walls for heavy axle, gearbox and transformer crates
18 mmDeck panels, container floors and very heavy machinery bases

Face veneers can be specified as Okoume or Gurjan depending on whether the customer wants the lighter African hardwood face (cleaner finish, lighter crate, common on export jobs) or the harder Gurjan face (more abrasion resistance under repeated handling). Okoume and Gurjan are face-veneer options on our MR-Packing and BWR grades — not separate product lines.

Finished crates and ISPM-15 export boxes

For Nashik exporters shipping to Europe, the US and the Middle East, we supply ready-built crates and cases — heat-treated, ISPM-15 stamped, and built to the dimensions the buyer specifies. Typical Nashik configurations include:

  • Engine and gearbox export crates, 12-18 mm panel, with internal cleats and steel strapping points
  • Auto-component flat crates for stampings, bumpers and plastic mouldings
  • Closed plywood boxes for electrical panels, switchgear and instrumentation
  • Wine bottle export cases — partitioned, vented, with shock-absorbing internal dividers

Wooden pallets

The most common pallet sizes drawn into Nashik MIDC are 1200 x 1000 mm (Euro-adjacent), 1200 x 800 mm (Euro standard for export) and 1100 x 1100 mm (Asian standard, common on container-direct flows). We supply both 4-way entry and 2-way entry constructions, and we will heat-treat and stamp ISPM-15 on request. Pallet decks can be packing-grade plywood (smoother, easier on the auto-component lines) or sawn-timber slats (cheaper, common in food and wine).

Container flooring

For end-users who stuff 20-ft and 40-ft containers directly at Sinnar or Ambad and need to repair or replace floors before reuse, 18-mm packing-grade or BWR ply cut to container-floor dimensions is shipped on a regular basis. We can pre-cut to the standard 20-ft and 40-ft footprints and bundle the lot on a single truck.

Kochi-to-Nashik logistics

The road distance from Kochi/Ernakulam to Nashik is approximately 1,200 km. The lane runs north through Mangaluru, up the Konkan via NH-66 and then onto NH-48 at Pune, or alternatively inland through Bengaluru and Hubballi onto NH-48. Practical transit time for a full-truck-load (FTL) is 4-5 days door-to-door once the truck is loaded, including driver rest and toll/check-post time.

For Nashik specifically, road from Kochi is almost always the right choice — there is no port economics that beats a direct truck on this route for sheet-form plywood and crating timber. A standard 32-ft single-axle takes around 14-16 metric tonnes of finished plywood; multi-axles carry more. We load and document from the Perumbavoor side, and the truck is invoiced and dispatched as a single FTL with full GST paperwork. For smaller orders we consolidate part-load with other Maharashtra-bound dispatches; lead time on a part-load is typically 7-10 days.

Choosing the right grade for a Nashik load

Across the inbound enquiries from the Nashik belt, the same decision keeps coming up: packing-grade vs Okoume-faced vs BWR vs shuttering ply. The practical rules are:

  1. Domestic auto-component crates, one-way trip: MR-Packing with Gurjan face, 12 mm sides / 15 mm base. Cheapest sensible option.
  2. Export crates (US/EU/GCC), one-way: MR-Packing or BWR with Okoume face, 12-15 mm. Lighter crate, cleaner finish, ISPM-15 heat treatment mandatory.
  3. Reusable transit racks (returnable packaging): BWR with Gurjan face, 15-18 mm. Holds up to repeated handling and humid Konkan port-side conditions.
  4. Wine bottle export cases: 6-8 mm MR-Packing for lids and dividers, 12 mm for sides; Okoume face if cosmetic.
  5. Container floor refits: 18 mm BWR, container-floor dimensions.
  6. Site-shuttering for Nashik Smart City / metro / civil projects: film-faced shuttering ply, not packing grade. Different product, ask for the shuttering catalogue.

Sawn-timber runners — typically jackwood (₹/cft) or rubberwood — go under crate bases and along pallet stringers; we quote these alongside the plywood line items.

Nashik buyer FAQ

Can you deliver to Satpur, Ambad and Sinnar in the same truck?

Yes. Multi-drop within Nashik on a single FTL is straightforward as long as drop points are confirmed before dispatch. Multi-drop into Pune-Nashik combined is also doable.

Do you supply ISPM-15 stamped crates from Kochi itself?

Yes. ISPM-15 heat treatment and stamping is done at the contract facility before the truck loads, so the crates arrive in Nashik ready for export documentation. The stamp + certificate travel with the dispatch.

What is the minimum order to make a Kochi-to-Nashik FTL worthwhile?

An FTL of plywood from Kochi to Nashik is economical from about 14 tonnes upward. Below that, part-load consolidation with other Maharashtra-bound trucks gives a better ₹/sheet — at the cost of 3-5 extra days of transit. We will quote both options on request.

Get a Nashik-specific quote

Send us the thickness, sheet size, monthly quantity, delivery MIDC and whether the application is domestic or export. We will come back with an ex-factory rate per square foot, an indicative trucking add-on for the Kochi-Nashik lane, and a lead time. For full crate or pallet builds, share the dimensions and gross weight per crate and we will quote ready-built. Request a quote for your Nashik dispatch, or browse the resource hub for spec sheets, ISPM-15 notes and grade-selection guides before you write your enquiry.

title: Plywood Supply to Solapur | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring out of Perumbavoor, Kerala, and run the Kochi-to-Solapur lane every week. Our.
html:
Why Solapur sits in our daily despatch register

Solapur is not a glamour city in the industrial press, but anyone moving plywood in western India keeps a column for it. The district runs on three engines that all need wood-based packaging: the cotton-textile cluster around the Solapur MIDC and Chincholi belt, the beedi-tobacco trade that pushes processed leaf out by the truckload, and a growing agri-equipment and oil-engine vertical sitting along NH-65 toward Pune. Add the cement, foundry and granite work around Tuljapur Road and you have a steady call for packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and the occasional container floor. This note walks through what we ship into Solapur from the Cochin Wood group's Perumbavoor base, how the lane works, and which grade fits which load.

The Solapur industrial picture

The city's manufacturing footprint is wider than the textile reputation suggests. Buyers from these clusters routinely call our desk asking for packing-grade plywood by the bundle:

  • Solapur MIDC (Akkalkot Road) and Chincholi MIDC — textile processing, terry-towel weaving, bedsheet finishing, and ancillary engineering. Bale packing, finished-goods cartons over wooden pallets, and crates for export terry-towel consignments are the recurring asks.
  • Beedi and tobacco trade in the old city and Barshi belt — processed-leaf cases, plywood-lined wooden boxes for despatch to UP, Karnataka and the GCC, and pallets for warehouse stacking.
  • Agri-equipment and oil-engine units along NH-65 toward Mohol and Pandharpur — heavy machine crates with ply lids and shooks, full-frame export boxes for diesel engines and water-pump sets, and ISPM-15 treated pallets.
  • Foundry, granite and cement ancillaries near Hotgi and Tuljapur Road — heavy-duty pallets for casting movement, occasional shuttering ply for in-plant civil work.
  • Cold storage and grape/pomegranate exporters in the wider Solapur-Pandharpur belt — crates and pallet runners for outbound containers headed to JNPT.

The common thread is bulk, repeat, and price-sensitive. Solapur buyers know their per-sheet rates to the rupee, and they expect a packing grade that holds nails, takes a screw, and survives a 1,400 km road-haul to the port without delamination.

What we ship into Solapur

Packing-grade plywood (commercial / rubberwood)

The workhorse. Most Solapur crate-makers and bale-packing units run on 8 mm, 12 mm and 18 mm sheets. Our packing range is poplar-core and rubberwood-faced where needed, calibrated for nail-holding and saw-cut clean edges. Standard size 8 ft x 4 ft, with 6 ft x 3 ft and odd cuts available on bundle orders. Indicative ex-factory selling rates (₹/sq.ft, GST and freight extra): 6 mm at 24, 8 mm at 28, 10 mm at 31.5, 12 mm at 33.5, 14 mm at 36, 15 mm at 39, 16 mm at 39.5, 18 mm at 42. These are packing-grade numbers, not furniture grade — buyers comparing against a BWR brand quote should expect the gap.

Okoume plywood

Premium grade, priced on request. We send Okoume into Solapur mainly for export crates where the consignee specifies a hardwood face — typically agri-equipment headed to Africa or the GCC, and high-value engineering goods where the outer face needs to take a stencil or screen-printed shipping mark cleanly. Lighter than gurjan-faced ply at comparable thicknesses, which matters when the crate plus cargo is fighting a road-freight weight limit.

Crates, full boxes and shooks

For Solapur loads we usually quote three formats: full assembled crates for one-shot exporters, knock-down shook sets where the buyer's own carpenter assembles on the shop floor, and ply tops/lids supplied separately when the buyer is using sawn-timber framing locally. Beedi-trade boxes tend to be small and standardised; agri-equipment crates are one-off and CAD-driven.

Pallets

Two-way and four-way entry, 1200 x 800 and 1200 x 1000 mm being the most common. ISPM-15 heat treatment with the stamp is available on the sawn-timber components for export-bound pallets — non-negotiable for anything headed to JNPT for an EU or GCC container.

Container flooring

Apitong / keruing container floorboards on request, mainly for the trailer-body fabricators in the wider Maharashtra belt rather than Solapur city itself. Quoted by the lot.

Sawn timber

Jackwood hardwood runners at ₹400/cft (¼ inch minus tolerance) and rubberwood runners to size — these go alongside ply bundles for buyers building crates in-house.

The Kochi-to-Solapur lane

Solapur sits about 900 km from Kochi by road via NH-66 up the Karnataka coast, then NH-50 / NH-65 inland through Hubballi and Vijayapura. Practical road-freight transit is three to four days door-to-door for a single full-truck consignment, longer if the buyer is consolidating a part-load. We despatch ex-Perumbavoor (Ernakulam district), which sits about 30 km inland from Cochin port and feeds directly onto NH-544 for the Karnataka leg. Full-truck economics work best at 18-20 tonnes of finished plywood, which lines up to roughly 280-340 sheets of 18 mm packing grade, or proportionally more at lower thicknesses. For Solapur buyers who cannot absorb a full truck, we consolidate with other Maharashtra-bound loads heading to Pune, Sangli or Kolhapur. Port-to-port via Cochin and JNPT is an option for export-bound crates that the buyer wants delivered already-stuffed, but the road lane wins on cost and time for raw plywood and pallet stock.

Which grade for which Solapur load

Use caseRecommended gradeTypical thickness
Terry-towel and bedsheet bale packingCommercial packing grade6-8 mm
Beedi / tobacco despatch boxesCommercial packing grade8-12 mm
Oil-engine and pump-set export cratesPacking grade body, Okoume face12-18 mm
Heavy agri-implement cratesPacking grade, jackwood runners18 mm + framing
ISPM-15 export palletsPacking grade deck, treated sawn timber12-15 mm deck
In-plant shuttering for civil workShuttering ply (film-faced)12-18 mm

FAQ

Minimum order to despatch to Solapur?

We prefer full-truck loads of around 18-20 tonnes for the best per-sheet landed cost. Part-loads are possible when we are consolidating other Maharashtra deliveries in the same week — ask the desk what is moving.

Are the sheets ISI-marked or BWP-grade?

The packing range is graded for packaging use, not BWP/IS:303 furniture grade. If a buyer needs BWP/BWR for a specific application, that is a separate quote — speak to us about Okoume or our BWR line.

How are payment and freight handled?
