Standard practice is part-advance against PI, balance against LR copy at despatch. Freight is quoted either inclusive (door-delivered Solapur) or ex-Perumbavoor, depending on whether the buyer has their own preferred transporter on the lane.

Get a quote

If you are running a crate shop in Chincholi MIDC, a textile unit on Akkalkot Road, or an export-bound engineering line anywhere in the Solapur district, send across the thickness, quantity and delivery point and we will come back with a landed rate and a despatch window. The fastest route is to request a quote with the specs in hand — sheet size, thickness, monthly off-take and delivery PIN. Buyers who want to read up on grades, ISPM-15, and packing-case economics before getting on a call can browse the resource hub. The Cochin Wood group has been making packing material out of Perumbavoor since 1986, and Solapur sits squarely on a lane we run every week.

title: Plywood Supply to Goa | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates and pallets that Goa's Verna pharma belt, Mormugao port traffic and North Goa estates run on. Manufactured.
html:
Goa is one of those markets that looks small on a map and then turns out to be punching well above its weight on the packing-materials side. Between the pharma majors in Verna, the iron-ore movement around Mormugao, and the steady flow of containerised cargo through MPT, the state burns through a meaningful volume of packing-grade plywood, wooden crates, pallets and dunnage every month. We supply that demand from Kochi, and this post is a working note on what we ship to Goa, why those specs make sense for the local load profile, and how the Kochi to Goa lane actually runs.

The Goa industrial picture

Goa's manufacturing and export base is concentrated in a handful of well-known belts. If you're sourcing packing materials for a unit in any of these, the typical board, crate and pallet conversations look fairly similar.

Verna Industrial Estate (South Goa)

Verna is the centre of gravity for Goa's pharma sector — Cipla, Glenmark, Lupin and a long tail of API and formulation units operate out of the estate. Pharma export packing has very specific requirements: ISPM-15 heat-treated crates for ocean freight, dimensional stability so cartons stack cleanly inside the crate, and clean inner faces because pharma QA inspectors look at everything. Verna also has a steady electronics and engineering component, which adds shuttering-ply demand for civil expansions and plywood crates for heavier machinery exports.

Pilerne, Tuem and Kundaim estates (North Goa)

The northern estates carry a mix of food processing, FMCG, light engineering and beverage units. Pallet demand here tends to be high frequency, lower value — standard four-way ISO pallets, EUR-size pallets for European buyers, and CP-spec pallets for chemical and pharma loads. Plywood-deck pallets become attractive when the buyer wants reusability, hygiene compliance, or pest-free shipment without a separate fumigation step.

Mormugao Port (MPT) and the iron-ore cluster

Mormugao is one of India's older major ports and historically the spine of Goa's iron-ore export. Even with mining cycles fluctuating, the port handles general cargo, container traffic and project consignments. Two demand streams flow from this: heavy-duty wooden dunnage and crating for project cargo and bulk-handled equipment, and container flooring plywood for the steady ICD and CFS traffic feeding the port.

Goa-Karnataka border belt

The Sankhali, Honda and border-adjacent industrial pockets pull in some demand from manufacturers who serve both Goa and the Belgaum-Hubli engineering belt. For these buyers, sourcing packing materials from Kochi via NH-66 is often cheaper than pulling boards from far-northern mills, simply because of the trucking economics.

What we ship to Goa

The range is built around three product families, all manufactured under Cochin Wood specifications at our contract factories near Perumbavoor. The Cochin Wood group has been operating in Perumbavoor since 1986, and the packing range is the segment where forty years of group manufacturing experience translates most directly into board behaviour you can rely on.

Packing-grade plywood

Our packing range is the workhorse for crate, case and pallet-deck manufacturing across pharma, FMCG and engineering exporters.

  • Thicknesses: 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 15, 16 and 18 mm
  • Sheet size: standard 8x4 ft, with custom cut sizes for case manufacturers
  • Core: hardwood / rubberwood combinations, MR-Packing grade
  • Face options: commercial face for standard packing; Okoume face where buyers want a cleaner, lighter export-grade look

For pharma export crates out of Verna, 12 mm and 14 mm boards are the most common conversations — strong enough for stacked cartons of formulations or API drums, light enough to keep crate weight reasonable for sea-freight chargeable weight calculations.

Wooden crates and packing cases

For buyers who don't want to run their own carpentry shop, we manufacture knocked-down or assembled crates to drawing. Typical specs for the Goa pharma lane:

  • ISPM-15 heat-treated runners and battens, stencilled with the IPPC mark
  • 12 mm or 15 mm packing-ply panels, depending on gross weight
  • Internal bracing and corner blocks for cartons that must not shift in a 40-ft container
  • Optional moisture barriers, silica gel pockets, and stencilled shipping marks on request

Pallets

Goa runs the full pallet spectrum — standard 1200x1000 mm, EUR 1200x800 mm, custom footprints for project cargo, and plywood-deck variants for hygiene-sensitive loads. We supply both sawn-timber pallets and plywood-deck pallets in the same factory cluster, which lets a buyer mix and match without juggling two vendors.

Container flooring plywood

For repair yards and container manufacturers around the MPT belt, 28 mm container flooring plywood is the standard reference. We supply matching grades and can cut to standard 20-ft and 40-ft floor sheet sizes.

Kochi to Goa logistics

The lane is straightforward and one of our most predictable routes.

ParameterDetail
OriginPerumbavoor / Kochi cluster, Kerala
DestinationVerna, Margao, Vasco / Mormugao, Panaji, North-Goa estates
Distance~600 km by road via NH-66 (coastal route)
Typical transit24-36 hours by truck, depending on stop pattern
Vehicle types22-ft, 32-ft single-axle, 32-ft multi-axle for full-truck packing-ply loads
DocumentationE-way bill, GST invoice; ISPM-15 stencils for export crates

NH-66 along the coast is a known commodity at this point — Mangaluru, Karwar, Canacona — and trucks running this lane carry mixed loads on a daily basis. That keeps freight competitive and predictable, especially for buyers who can plan in full-truck or near-full-truck quantities.

Decision rules: which board for which Goa job

A simple way to think about board selection for the typical Goa demand:

  • Pharma export crates (Verna): Packing-grade plywood, 12-15 mm. Add an Okoume face if the buyer ships into EU or Japanese clients who value cleaner aesthetics.
  • FMCG and beverage pallets (Pilerne, Tuem): Sawn-timber pallets for cost-driven internal movement, plywood-deck pallets where hygiene or reuse cycles matter.
  • Project cargo and machinery exports (port-route): Heavier 15-18 mm packing ply with reinforced runners; sawn-timber framing for the load-bearing skeleton.
  • Civil construction and infra (estate expansions, port works): Shuttering plywood, 12 mm standard, with film-faced options on request.
  • Container floor repair (MPT belt): 28 mm container flooring plywood in standard ISO sheet sizes.

FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15 compliant crates direct to Goa?

Yes. Our contract factories operate ISPM-15 heat-treatment chambers, and crates ship with the IPPC stencil applied. This matters for pharma and engineering exports moving through Mormugao or trans-shipped via Nhava Sheva.

What is the minimum order quantity for a Kochi to Goa shipment?

Economics work best at full-truck quantities (typically a 32-ft load of plywood, or an equivalent volume of crates and pallets). Smaller orders are possible on part-load basis, but per-unit freight rises sharply below half-truck volumes.

Can you supply Okoume-faced packing ply for export crates?

Yes. Okoume is offered as a face-veneer option on our MR-Packing and BWR boards. Quote on request, as Okoume sits in a different price band from the standard commercial-face packing range.

Request a quote

If you are sourcing packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets or container flooring for a unit in Verna, the Mormugao port belt or any of the North Goa estates, the fastest way to a working number is a short conversation about your specs — board thicknesses, monthly volumes, delivery location and whether ISPM-15 is in scope. Our team will come back with an EX-FACTORY rate and an indicative delivered cost for the Goa lane. Request a quote, or browse our resource hub for technical notes on board grades, crate construction and packing-ply selection.

title: Plywood Supply to Faridabad | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, heavy-duty wooden crates, pallets and container flooring for Faridabad's NIT engineering economy, the Ballabgarh.
html:
Faridabad is one of the densest concentrations of metal-working capacity in north India. Between the NIT industrial belt, the Ballabgarh foundries, and the auto-component clusters feeding Eicher, Escorts and JCB lines, the city ships out crates of castings, forgings, gearboxes and farm-equipment subassemblies almost every working hour. Each of those crates needs plywood that will not flex, fail or split when a 600 kg cylinder head is dropped onto a truck bed in 44 degree summer heat. That is the business problem the Cochin Wood group has been solving for industrial buyers for four decades, and Faridabad sits squarely in our delivery lane.

The Faridabad industrial picture

Faridabad is not one industrial estate but a stack of overlapping belts, each with its own packing rhythm. Before you order plywood here, it helps to know which belt the load is moving through, because the crate spec changes with it.

NIT and the old industrial spine

The NIT (New Industrial Township) sectors — broadly Sectors 6, 24, 25, 27, 31 and the older Sector 58/59 cluster — house the original Faridabad engineering economy: sheet-metal pressings, tool-room work, automotive electricals, switchgear, fasteners and a long tail of tier-2 and tier-3 component suppliers. Output here tends to ship in mid-sized crates of 80 kg to 400 kg, often consolidated into LCL or part-load trucking toward Mundra, Pipavav or domestic OEM plants.

Ballabgarh and the foundry belt

South of the city, Ballabgarh and the surrounding sectors are foundry-heavy: grey iron, ductile iron and aluminium castings going to commercial vehicle, tractor and construction-equipment OEMs. Castings are unforgiving cargo — sharp burrs, point loads, and weights that frequently cross 250 kg per piece. Plywood crates here have to act as both a structural frame and an abrasion barrier.

Sectors 58 to 68 and the newer estates

The newer auto-component and assembly units along the Mathura Road belt, plus the Faridabad-Noida-Ghaziabad corridor, lean toward larger consolidated export shipments — gearboxes, hydraulic assemblies, axle parts and farm-equipment subassemblies bound for the GCC, Africa and Southeast Asia. These typically need export-spec, ISPM-15 compliant wooden cases, container loading plans, and stronger pallet stacks.

What we ship to Faridabad

For industrial buyers across these belts, we move four broad product families up the Kochi-Faridabad lane. Each one has a specific job.

Packing-grade plywood (sheets)

Our core line — commercial and rubberwood-faced packing plywood, manufactured to Cochin Wood group specifications at our contract factories in Perumbavoor. Standard thickness range:

ThicknessTypical use in Faridabad
6 mmCrate lids, internal dividers, light dunnage
8 mmSide panels for medium component crates
10-12 mmSide and base panels, foundry casting crates
14-16 mmBase panels for heavy castings, gearbox cases
18 mmHeavy-duty pallet decks, container flooring sub-layers

Standard sheet size is 8 ft x 4 ft, with shorter cuts available on indent. All packing-grade is supplied EX-FACTORY; GST and transport are quoted separately so buyers can see the real freight number on the Kochi-Faridabad lane.

Wooden crates and packing cases

For buyers who would rather take a finished crate than fabricate locally, we build cases to drawing — open-top, closed, skidded base, with corner reinforcement and ISPM-15 heat-treatment stamping where the cargo is bound for export. Typical Faridabad-bound builds include foundry casting cases, gear and transmission boxes, and consolidated export crates for auto-component exporters.

Pallets

We supply two-way and four-way wooden pallets in the common Indian footprints — 1200 x 1000 mm and 1100 x 1100 mm — plus custom sizes for OEM-specified loads. For Faridabad foundries handling heavy castings, the 1200 x 1000 four-way design with hardwood runners is the workhorse.

Container flooring and dunnage

For exporters loading 20 ft and 40 ft containers out of ICDs and onward to Mundra or Pipavav, we supply thicker packing-grade sheets used as flooring overlay and bracing dunnage — typically 12 mm or 18 mm rubberwood ply, cut to container width with a small clearance for easy fit.

Kochi-to-Faridabad logistics

Faridabad sits roughly 2,350 km north of Kochi. We move material on two main modes, and the right choice depends on lot size and urgency.

Direct trucking lane

For full-truck and part-load consignments, we dispatch 32 ft single-axle and multi-axle trucks via the NH-44 corridor through Bangalore, Hyderabad, Nagpur and Agra, terminating in Faridabad sectors directly. Transit time runs 6 to 8 days door-to-door depending on season and toll-plaza congestion at Delhi-NCR entry points. This is the right mode for finished crates, pallet stacks and time-sensitive sheet orders.

Port-routed economics

For large sheet-only consignments where transit time is less critical, we can load containers FOB Cochin port and route through coastal shipping or rail to inland container depots near Delhi-NCR, with final road leg into Faridabad. This often works out cheaper per sheet on full-container volumes, particularly for buyers with their own ICD account.

We provide a clear breakup of EX-FACTORY rate, transport and GST on every quote so the buyer can compare the two routes and pick.

Decision rules — which grade for which load

Buyers in Faridabad frequently ask which grade is the right call. A short rulebook:

  • Routine domestic crates and pallets (under 500 kg): commercial or rubberwood-faced packing-grade plywood, 10-16 mm. Best cost-per-crate, designed for one-way packing duty.
  • Export crates, premium finish or higher cosmetic spec: Okoume face veneer on the packing or BWR substrate. Lighter, cleaner surface, better stencilling — useful for export buyers shipping to GCC and Vietnam who want a presentable crate at the receiving end.
  • Heavy castings, shock loads, repeated handling: 16-18 mm packing-grade base with hardwood corner reinforcement, or move up to BWR substrate where moisture exposure is likely.
  • Construction, formwork, infra projects on Faridabad sites: shuttering ply (film-faced) — a different product family from packing ply, designed for concrete contact and multiple pours.

We do not push buyers up the price ladder unnecessarily. If a 12 mm packing sheet does the job, that is what we quote.

FAQ

Can you supply ISPM-15 compliant heat-treated crates for export out of Faridabad?

Yes. Heat-treatment and IPPC stamping is standard for any export-bound case we build. The certificate is supplied with the dispatch documents so it clears phytosanitary checks at the loading port.

What is the minimum order for sheets to Faridabad?

We quote both part-load and full-truck consignments. The per-sheet rate is keenest on full-truck loads (roughly 350-400 sheets of 18 mm depending on packing), but smaller indents are workable when consolidated with other Delhi-NCR-bound material.

Do you carry stock or build to order?

Standard packing-grade sheets in popular thicknesses are run through our contract factories on continuous schedules, so lead time from order confirmation to dispatch from Perumbavoor is typically 3-7 working days. Custom crate builds, sized cases and non-standard veneers take longer — we share an exact dispatch date on PI.

Request a quote

If you are sourcing packing plywood, wooden crates, pallets or container flooring for any Faridabad sector — NIT, Ballabgarh foundries or the newer auto-component belt — share the thickness, monthly quantity and delivery point and we will come back with an EX-FACTORY rate plus a transparent Kochi-to-Faridabad freight estimate. Request a quote here, or browse specifications, thickness charts and grade explainers in our resource hub. Group operations since 1986; we know what crates north Indian foundries actually need.

title: Plywood Supply to Gurugram | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, Okoume-faced boards, crates and pallets for Gurugram's auto-component, CKD-export and data-centre packers. Our group.
html:
Why Gurugram's industrial belt keeps us on the phone

Gurugram is not one factory town — it is a stack of them. Maruti Suzuki's Gurgaon plant on Old Delhi Road, the Hero MotoCorp lines off NH-48, Honda Cars' supplier ecosystem and the IMT Manesar belt twenty kilometres south together generate one of the heaviest CKD and component-export packing demands in northern India. The IT corridor along Golf Course Road and Cyber City adds a second, lighter stream — server racks, networking gear and data-centre hardware that all need wooden crates rated for air or ocean freight. The Cochin Wood group has been packaging this kind of load out of Perumbavoor since 1986, and Gurugram's auto-component exporters and IT OEMs have become a steady lane for our packing-grade plywood, ISPM-15 crates, pallets and container flooring.

The Gurugram industrial picture

For a plywood and packing supplier, Gurugram is best understood as four overlapping zones, each with a different packing profile.

Udyog Vihar (Sectors 18, 19, 20)

The original industrial estate. Phases I to VI house garment exporters, electronics assemblers, small auto-ancillary units and a long tail of contract manufacturers. Crate volumes are moderate but frequent — typically 6mm to 12mm packing-grade plywood for export cartons and lightweight cases.

IMT Manesar (Sectors 1, 2, 3, 7, 8)

Heavy auto. This is where Maruti Suzuki's Manesar plant, Honda's two-wheeler supplier base and a Tier-1 auto-component cluster sit. CKD packing for export markets — Latin America, ASEAN, Africa — drives the bulk of our plywood movement here. Expect heavier crates, 12mm and 18mm boards, with substantial pallet runs.

Sectors 32, 33, 34 and the Pace City belt

A mixed-engineering pocket — pump makers, hydraulic equipment, electrical panel builders. These units often need fabricated crates around odd-shaped machinery, which favours sawn timber framing on a plywood skin.

Cyber City, Sector 44 and the DLF Phase III IT corridor

Data centres, server-rack OEMs and hyperscale customers' India operations. Crates here are smaller but engineered — anti-static lining is often specified, dimensional tolerance matters, and ISPM-15 marking is non-negotiable for export-bound consignments.

What we ship to Gurugram

Three product lines do most of the work on this lane.

Packing-grade plywood (commercial / rubberwood)

Our standard packing-grade range, supplied ex-factory in 8×4 sheets:

ThicknessTypical use in Gurugram
6mmLight electronics cartons, garment crates, IT accessory packs
8mm – 10mmMid-weight auto-component cases, hardware crates
12mmStandard CKD crate walls and tops, machinery skin
15mm – 18mmCrate bases, pallet decks, heavy-machinery export cases

Pricing is per square foot, ex-factory, with GST and transport added on the proforma invoice. Thickness tolerance: "11+" is sold as 12mm, "17+" as 18mm — operator-honest, no marketing thickness games.

Okoume-faced plywood

An export-grade veneer face option on our MR-packing and BWR boards. Okoume reads as a premium surface — clean, light, uniform — which matters when the crate itself is part of the buyer's brand impression (luxury automotive parts, high-value electronics, regulated medical equipment). For a Tier-1 auto-component supplier sending CKD kits to a European OEM, Okoume-faced crates often pay for themselves in customer perception. Quoted on request, not in the standard packing card.

Crates, pallets and container flooring

  • Export crates — built to ISPM-15 standard with heat-treated softwood framing and a plywood skin. Side-loading, top-loading or fully-knocked-down configurations on request.
  • Pallets — standard 1200×1000mm and 1200×800mm decks, with custom sizes for the Maruti and Hero supplier base. Two-way and four-way entry.
  • Container flooring — 28mm flooring panels for refurbishing dry containers used in the auto-component reverse-logistics loop.
  • Sawn timber — jackwood at ₹400/cft (¼-inch minus tolerance) and rubberwood runners for framing.

Kochi-to-Gurugram logistics

The lane runs roughly 2,300 km by road and 2,750 km by rail. Two options work for Gurugram buyers, and the choice usually comes down to volume and urgency.

Road — full truck loads, 6 to 8 days transit

From our Perumbavoor dispatch point, a 32-foot single-axle truck reaches the IMT Manesar belt in roughly six to eight days via the Bengaluru–Hyderabad–Nagpur–Agra–Delhi corridor on NH-44 and NH-48. This is the workhorse mode for one-time or low-frequency orders, typically a single truck of 18 to 22 tonnes of finished plywood and crate stock. Unloading at Manesar plants and Udyog Vihar yards is straightforward — we factor unloading charges into the proforma where the buyer cannot provide forklift support.

Rail / multi-modal — bulk recurring lanes, 10 to 14 days

For recurring monthly volumes, a Cochin Port containerised dispatch to ICD Tughlakabad or ICD Patparganj, then short-haul trucked into Gurugram, often works out cheaper per square foot. The trade-off is transit time and one extra handling step. We are comfortable with both modes; we will quote on whichever option lands the better delivered price for the buyer.

FOB Cochin for export buyers

For Gurugram-headquartered exporters who consolidate at JNPT or Mundra, we can quote FOB Cochin for finished export packing — useful when the buyer is comparing Kerala-origin pricing against north-India suppliers for the same EXIM destination.

Decision rules — which board for which Gurugram load

A practical operator checklist we walk buyers through over the first call:

  1. Standard auto-component CKD crate, domestic or regional export — commercial packing-grade plywood in 12mm or 15mm, jackwood framing. Best cost-per-crate.
  2. Premium-export CKD or high-value electronics for European/Japanese buyers — Okoume-faced packing or BWR plywood. Cleaner cosmetics, tighter tolerance, better customer-side perception.
  3. Construction-equipment or formwork buyers in the broader Delhi-NCR build cycle — shuttering ply, 12mm or 18mm, for concrete formwork on Gurugram's commercial real-estate and metro-corridor projects.
  4. Server-rack and data-centre crates from Cyber City — 9mm or 12mm packing-grade with anti-static or moisture-barrier lining specified by the buyer.
  5. Pallets for the Maruti / Hero / Honda supplier chain — standard pallet decks in 12mm or 15mm, four-way entry, ISPM-15 marked.

FAQ

Can you match a Yamunanagar or local NCR supplier's headline rate?

On pure sheet-pricing for thin-gauge commercial ply, a local north-India mill will usually be cheaper because there is no 2,300-km freight in the number. Where Kochi-origin makes sense is consistency of face-veneer quality (especially Okoume), thickness honesty, and bulk export-grade lanes where the freight differential gets absorbed by lower per-sheet cost. We are happy to send a delivered-Gurugram quote so the comparison is apples to apples.

Do you supply ISPM-15 marked crates for direct export?

Yes. We build to ISPM-15 with heat-treated softwood framing, the IPPC stamp applied, and full documentation for the buyer's CHA. This is the standard spec for auto-component CKD packing leaving Gurugram for Europe, Japan, ASEAN and Latin America.

What is the minimum order for a Gurugram dispatch?

Economically, a full single-axle truck — roughly 18 to 22 tonnes of plywood, or an equivalent mix of plywood and finished crates. Smaller orders are possible via part-load operators but the per-sheet landed cost rises noticeably; for a one-off trial we usually suggest a half-truck consolidated with another north-India order.

Get a delivered-Gurugram quote

If you run an auto-component plant in IMT Manesar, a packing line in Udyog Vihar, a data-centre OEM in Cyber City, or a construction contractor working the NCR build cycle, we can put a firm number against your spec — thickness, sheet size, monthly quantity, delivery address, mode of transport. Request a quote with those details and we will reply with an ex-Kochi rate, freight to your Gurugram address, and a proforma invoice within one working day. For specification reading, ISPM-15 background and grade-selection notes, the resource hub covers the technical side in more depth. Forty years of group manufacturing out of Perumbavoor stands behind every dispatch.

title: Plywood Supply to Karnal | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring that move Karnal's rice, dairy-plant and machinery exports out of the.
html:
Karnal sits in a quiet but heavyweight slice of the Indian economy. It is the centre of India's basmati rice export trade, home to IARI's regional research station, a strong dairy belt anchored by the National Dairy Research Institute, and a steady run of agri-equipment and food-processing units feeding off NH-44. Every one of those activities ends up needing the same thing at the back of the loading bay — clean, dimension-stable plywood for packing cases, pallets and container floors. That is the slice of the market the Cochin Wood group has been serving for forty years, and it is why we move material 2,350 km north out of Kochi to Haryana on a regular cycle.

The Karnal industrial picture

Karnal's industrial activity is concentrated along a few well-defined corridors rather than one single estate. Buyers we talk to in the city are usually located in or shipping out of:

  • Sector 3 and Sector 6 industrial areas — older HSIIDC plotted estates with rice mills, dal units and packaging converters.
  • HSIIDC Industrial Estate, Karnal (Sector 39 / Bastara belt) — newer plots running along NH-44 toward Panipat, with agri-machinery, cold storage and food-processing units.
  • Taraori and Nilokheri belts — the basmati paddy heartland north of the city, where mills and exporters dominate.
  • Madhuban / NDRI campus zone — dairy research, instrumentation and pilot-plant equipment that ships in crates.
  • IARI Regional Station and ICAR campuses — agricultural research, demanding ISPM-15 compliant export crating for instruments and germplasm consignments.

The common thread is that almost every outgoing shipment from Karnal is either a food-grade export carton load, a heavy and irregular piece of agri-equipment, or research material going to a partner institute abroad. All three end up specifying plywood, not solid wood, for the outer pack.

What we ship into Karnal

The material mix flowing into Karnal from our Kerala factories is fairly predictable. Below is the typical thickness and grade pattern we send up the lane, with usage notes for the city's main verticals.

Packing-grade plywood for rice and food export

Basmati exporters in Taraori, Nilokheri and Sector 3 ship in 20 ft and 40 ft containers, usually with stacked 5 kg, 10 kg and 20 kg consumer bags inside master cartons. The plywood goes into:

  • Bottom load boards and dunnage sheets — 12 mm and 14 mm commercial / rubberwood packing-grade ply, 4x8 ft, used to spread point loads on the container floor and protect the bottom layer of bags during long sea voyages to the GCC, EU and US.
  • Top covers and side liners — 8 mm and 10 mm sheets to stop carton crush during stevedoring.
  • Pallet decks for reefer or dry export — 12 mm packing-grade ply on a hardwood runner frame for 1100x1100 mm and 1200x1000 mm pallets.

Crates for agri-equipment, dairy plant parts and research instruments

This is where ISPM-15 heat-treated construction becomes non-negotiable. For Karnal's agri-machinery makers and for IARI / NDRI export consignments we typically build:

  • Closed crates in 12 mm or 15 mm packing-grade plywood on a 75x50 mm or 100x50 mm hardwood frame.
  • Open slatted crates with 18 mm sheets where the consignment is large but light (extruded parts, frames, panels).
  • Heavy-duty bolted crates with 18 mm plywood skins and internal bracing for transformers, pump assemblies and dairy plant equipment.

Pallets and container flooring

Standard four-way entry pallets in 1200x1000 mm and 1100x1100 mm are the bulk of pallet demand for the city's exporters. For specialised loads — machinery shipments out of the HSIIDC Sector 39 belt — we also ship pre-cut 18 mm container flooring blanks sized to drop into 20 ft and 40 ft container floors as a sacrificial layer protecting the original deck.

Indicative selling rates (ex-factory, GST and transport extra)

MaterialThicknessRate (Rs / sq.ft)
Commercial / rubberwood packing-grade ply8 mm28
Commercial / rubberwood packing-grade ply12 mm33.5
Commercial / rubberwood packing-grade ply15 mm39
Commercial / rubberwood packing-grade ply18 mm42
Jackwood hardwood runners1/4 inch minusRs 400 / cft
Okoume export-grade plywoodOn requestQuote per consignment

Kochi-to-Karnal logistics

Karnal is roughly 2,350 km from our Perumbavoor cluster by road. We work the lane two ways depending on volume and urgency:

  1. Full-truck road movement — single-driver and team-driver 32 ft and 40 ft multi-axles up NH-66 / NH-48 through Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Nagpur, then NH-44 via Delhi to Karnal. Transit is typically 7-9 days for team-drive, 9-11 days for single-driver, depending on monsoon and Delhi border conditions.
  2. Rail / coastal-and-road combination — for larger consolidated loads we move material into a North India hub by rail or by coastal feeder to Mundra / Pipavav and trans-ship by road into Karnal. This is slower (12-15 days) but materially cheaper on per-sheet landed cost for very large orders.

For most Karnal buyers the FTL road option wins because it delivers directly into the factory gate without a second handling. Cut sizes and pre-assembled crate kits are loaded so that the buyer can unload onto a level surface and move straight to the packing line.

Decision rules — packing grade vs Okoume vs shuttering ply

Karnal buyers ask this often, so a clear short answer:

  • Use packing-grade commercial / rubberwood plywood for rice export pallets, dunnage, food-export crates and most agri-equipment cases. It is the workhorse and the rate card above is built around it.
  • Specify Okoume face veneer when the consignee is European or Japanese and demands a clean, light, uniform face — for example precision instruments shipped from IARI / NDRI labs to overseas research partners, or premium machinery exports where the crate itself is part of the buyer experience. Okoume is a face-veneer option on the MR-Packing and BWR grades, not a separate quality tier.
  • Use shuttering ply only if the end use is concrete formwork on a Karnal construction project. Shuttering grade is a poor choice for export packing — it is heavier, oilier and not designed for one-way disposable use.

FAQ — Karnal buyers

Do you handle ISPM-15 marking for our export crates?

Yes. Crates built for export consignments out of Karnal are constructed from heat-treated timber and stencilled with the IPPC mark and our registered HT facility code, so they clear phytosanitary inspection at any port of loading without rework.

What is the minimum order you'll send to Karnal?

Because of the 2,350 km lane, the economics work best at a full truckload — roughly 18-22 tonnes of plywood and timber. We do consolidate smaller orders with other North India deliveries where the schedule allows; ask us when you enquire.

Can you cut sheets to size before despatch?

Yes. Most Karnal customers receive pre-cut blanks matched to their carton, pallet or crate drawing. Cutting at our Kerala plant reduces site waste and lets you unload directly onto the packing line.

Request a quote

If you are a rice exporter, agri-equipment manufacturer, dairy plant supplier or research institute in or around Karnal and you need packing-grade plywood, export crates, pallets or container flooring delivered to your factory gate, we would be glad to quote the consignment. Share thickness, sheet size, monthly volume and delivery sector and we will come back with rates, lead time and a proposed dispatch plan. Request a quote from our team, or browse the resource hub for specifications, ISPM-15 notes and packing-case design guides before you write to us.

title: Plywood Supply to Panipat | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Panipat runs on load-heavy verticals that all consume wood: textile bales, refinery spares and thermal-corridor heavy gear. We manufacture the packing-grade.
html:
Panipat is one of north India's busiest packaging-consumption clusters that very few south-Indian mills actively service. The city's economy runs on three load-heavy verticals — cotton and woollen textiles, an IOCL refinery on its western edge, and the NTPC thermal corridor a short haul east — and every one of them needs wood. Bales need ply caps, refinery spares need ISPM-15 crates, turbine consumables travel on hardwood pallets. This guide walks through what the Cochin Wood group ships into Panipat from Kochi, why the 2,300-km lane still works on price, and how to pick the right grade for the typical loads moving out of Haryana.

The Panipat industrial picture

Panipat's industrial footprint is unusually concentrated. Within roughly 15 km of the city centre you find four distinct buyer pockets, each with its own packing rhythm:

  • Panipat Textile Cluster & HSIIDC Sector 29 — the famous "handloom city" power-loom belt: bedsheets, blankets, furnishings, towels, durries. Bale-pressing is continuous, and the export-grade fraction is the part that actually consumes plywood (compressed bales need a top-and-bottom ply cap plus end boards to survive sea travel).
  • Old industrial area & Sector 25 HSIIDC — the woollen and shoddy-yarn mills concentrated around the GT Road, plus the recycled-textile units that ship to Africa and the Middle East. Volumes are smaller per consignment but the frequency is high.
  • IOCL Panipat Refinery & petrochemical complex on the Baholi side — a continuous flow of MRO spares, pumps, valves and exchanger tubes that need close-tolerance crates, often ISPM-15 marked for re-export of warranty returns.
  • NTPC Panipat thermal station at Asan Khurd and the surrounding ancillary belt — boiler-tube bundles, coal-handling spares, transformer accessories. Loads are heavy, point-loaded, and need hardwood-bottom pallets rather than soft-pine substitutes.

Add to that the National Highway 44 logistics corridor that puts Panipat 90 km from ICD Tughlakabad and 100 km from ICD Sonipat, and you have a city that pushes a steady volume of export-spec packaging through its gates every week.

What we ship into Panipat

Three product families cover roughly 95% of what moves on the Kochi–Panipat lane:

1. Packing-grade plywood (the workhorse)

This is the bulk line for textile-bale capping, crate side-panels and reusable internal partitions. Manufactured in the Cochin Wood group facilities to a consistent core specification:

ThicknessTypical use in PanipatStandard size
6 mmBale top/bottom caps, light separators8' x 4'
8 mmInternal crate partitions, blanket-bale tops8' x 4'
12 mmCrate sides for refinery spares, durry bale ends8' x 4'
16-18 mmCrate lids carrying loose top-stack load8' x 4'

Custom-cut sizes (4' x 3', 6' x 3', off-square panels for cylindrical equipment) are run to order in the same despatch.

2. Wooden crates and ISPM-15 export cases

For the IOCL refinery belt and any Panipat exporter pushing engineering goods out through Mundra, Pipavav or Nhava Sheva, we build closed and open-frame crates to ISPM-15 specification — heat-treated timber, HT stamp, fumigation certificate where the destination requires it. Standard build uses a hardwood skid base, plywood sheathing on the four walls and lid, and steel strapping. Internal blocking and load-spreaders are designed against the actual equipment dimensions, not a generic template.

3. Pallets and container flooring

Two-way and four-way entry pallets in standard 1200 x 1000 mm and 1200 x 800 mm footprints, plus custom sizes for textile-bale conveyors. For the NTPC corridor and the heavier refinery flows we run a hardwood-bottom pallet that handles point loads above 1.5 tonnes without crushing. Container-flooring plywood (BWP-bonded, 18-21 mm) is supplied for export box-fabricators in the Sonipat–Panipat belt who refurbish 20' and 40' containers for road use.

Kochi-to-Panipat logistics

The straight-line lane from our Perumbavoor/Kochi dispatch point to Panipat is approximately 2,300 km by road. Realistic options:

  • Direct trailer (32-ft single-axle or 40-ft multi-axle) — 6 to 8 days transit door-to-door depending on driver changes and the Maharashtra–MP corridor. Best for full-load orders of 18-22 tonnes (roughly 9,000-11,000 sq.ft of 18 mm plywood, or one full container-equivalent of crates).
  • Part-load via Delhi NCR consolidators — 8 to 10 days, useful for trial orders or 3-5 tonne shipments. Final-mile delivery from a Delhi or Sonipat hub to Panipat is short.
  • Coastal + road combination — for very large volumes, container by sea to Mundra (~5 days) plus 1,150 km trucking to Panipat. Competitive when the order touches 40+ tonnes.

Prices are quoted ex-Kochi by default; GST and freight are itemised separately so the buyer can compare against local Yamunanagar-supplied plywood on a clean apples-to-apples basis. For most textile-bale and crate-grade orders above 8,000 sq.ft, the landed Panipat cost still works in our favour because the input timber and core stock economics in Kerala are different from the Yamunanagar belt.

Decision rules — which grade for which load

A short field guide for the Panipat buyer:

  • Cotton-textile and woollen bales for domestic dispatch — packing-grade ply in 6-8 mm caps is sufficient. No need to over-spec.
  • Export bales going through Mundra/Nhava Sheva — packing-grade ply, but step up to 8-12 mm and pair with ISPM-15 hardwood end-frames if the buyer's BOL calls for it.
  • Refinery spares, valves, pump assemblies — crate build with 12-16 mm packing-grade sides and a hardwood skid; for high-value or moisture-sensitive cargo, upgrade the faces to Okoume for a tighter surface and better screw-holding.
  • NTPC heavy spares, transformer accessories — pallets must be hardwood-bottom, never soft-pine. Crate lids in 18 mm packing-grade or BWR if the load sits in an outdoor yard for weeks before despatch.
  • Shuttering ply — relevant when Panipat construction buyers (commercial real estate along GT Road, refinery shutdown civil works) need film-faced ply for repeat-use formwork. Different product line; quoted separately.

For more on the grade-selection logic, see our resource hub.

FAQ — common Panipat buyer questions

Do you supply directly to Panipat or only through a Delhi dealer?

Direct from our Kochi despatch point. The invoice is from Cochin Wood Industries to the Panipat buyer with the buyer's GSTIN. No interim layer.

Can you do ISPM-15 fumigation and HT stamping for export crates?

Yes. Crates can be supplied with HT-stamped timber and the accompanying fumigation certificate where the destination country requires it. Specify destination at the quote stage so the right treatment route is chosen.

What is the minimum order that makes the Kochi lane economical?

Roughly 4-5 tonnes for part-load economics, 18-22 tonnes for a clean full-truck rate. Smaller pilot orders are fine — we will quote them honestly, including the part-load premium, so you can compare against local sources before committing to a larger lane.

Get a quote for your Panipat consignment

Share thickness, size, monthly volume and the despatch location in Panipat — Sector 25, the HSIIDC belt, the IOCL Baholi side, or the NTPC corridor — and we will work out a landed cost with freight, GST and lead time. The Cochin Wood group has run manufacturing operations in Perumbavoor since 1986, and the Panipat lane is one we run on a recurring basis for textile and refinery-belt buyers. Request a quote with your packing list, or browse the resource hub for grade and crate specifications before you send the enquiry.

title: Plywood Supply to Sonipat | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates and pallets that move bicycles, tyres, auto-components and steel out of Sonipat. Manufactured in Perumbavoor.
html:
Sonipat sits at the northern edge of the National Capital Region, an hour up the GT Road from Delhi and the kind of district where heavy engineering, cycles, tyres and steel all share the same trucking lane. For a plywood supplier shipping out of Kochi, that mix is exactly the customer profile we are built for: factories that crate finished goods, palletise sub-assemblies, and floor export containers month after month. This page lays out what the Cochin Wood group ships into Sonipat, how the lane works from a Kerala port, and the decision rules that decide which grade of plywood ends up under your load.

Why Sonipat's industrial belt matters for packing plywood

Sonipat is not one industrial cluster but several stitched together. The Kundli-Manesar-Palwal expressway opened the western flank of the district to large-format manufacturing, and the older Rai and Kundli HSIIDC estates have hosted bicycle, auto-component, food-processing, textile and pharmaceutical units for decades. North of the city, the Barhi industrial area handles smaller fabrication and ancillary work. Add the Tata Steel BSL plant at Sahibabad-Sonipat axis and the cycle and tyre majors with operations in and around the district, and the packaging demand profile becomes clear: thousands of export-bound CKD crates, heavy steel coils that need dunnage and floor boards, and palletised cargo destined for ICDs at Tughlakabad, Garhi Harsaru and Patparganj before sea-leg.

For a factory crating bicycles in CKD form, every shipment is an export carton waiting to be skid-loaded into a 40-foot container. For a tyre plant moving stacks of casings, the load needs ply floors that survive forklift abuse. For an auto-component shop sending sub-assemblies to OEMs, returnable pallets and one-way export pallets sit on the same shop floor. Each of these flows is a separate plywood SKU conversation, and Sonipat buyers tend to mix them inside one annual contract.

The Sonipat industrial picture in detail

Kundli and Rai HSIIDC estates

Kundli's planned plots host bicycle and component manufacturers, packaging converters and pharma units. Rai is the older estate with a heavier engineering footprint. Both estates feed plywood demand from the same vendors: case-makers and pallet shops operating inside or just outside the gate, building to the OEM's drawing.

Barhi and Murthal industrial pockets

North of NH-44, Barhi and Murthal carry smaller fabrication units, food and dairy processors, and a growing cluster of warehousing and 3PL operations along the expressway. Warehousing demand tends to favour standard ISO pallets and one-way export skids rather than custom crates.

Cycle, tyre and steel sectors

Sonipat's bicycle sector ships CKD export crates with ply walls and battened skid bases. The tyre sector around the district moves casings on ply-floored skids and uses heavier-gauge plywood for export crate sides. Steel and steel-product flows need thicker shuttering or container-floor grade plywood that can carry coil weights without delamination at the edges.

What we ship into Sonipat

Three product families cover almost every load that leaves Sonipat under a CWI-stamped board.

Packing-grade plywood sheets

Our MR-Packing range runs 6 mm through 18 mm in the standard 8x4 sheet, with 9 mm, 12 mm and 18 mm doing the bulk of the case-making volume. The 6 mm and 8 mm sheets handle inner partitions and lid panels for lighter loads. The 12 mm and 18 mm sheets carry side walls and bases on heavier crates. Sheets ship strapped in mill bundles, suitable for both forklift and crane offload at a Sonipat dock.

Export crates and packing cases

When a customer prefers finished crates over sheets, we cut, batten and bolt to drawing in Kerala and ship the assembled or knock-down case. CKD bicycle crates, tyre stack crates, machinery and engineering-good cases all sit in this lane. Heat-treatment to ISPM-15 is available where the destination port demands it; the IPPC stamp goes onto the same batten.

Wooden pallets

Standard four-way entry pallets in 1200x1000 mm and 1200x800 mm sizes, plus custom skid-bases sized to the load. For warehousing customers in Murthal and along the expressway, the standard ISO sizes are the high-runner; for OEM-bound auto-component shipments, custom sizes built to the cage or rack are more common.

Container flooring and dunnage

For steel coils, machinery and heavy export, container-floor grade plywood at 18 mm or above acts as the load-bearing floor over the existing container floor, distributing point loads and protecting the box. Dunnage strips and chocks ship from the same family of timber.

Kochi-to-Sonipat: how the lane runs

Kochi to Sonipat is roughly 2,300 km by road, four to six days transit on the standard trunk route through Bengaluru, Hyderabad and Nagpur and into the NCR via the Agra-Faridabad approach. For sheet plywood and pre-cut components, the road option works because the load is dense and stacks well in a 32-foot or 40-foot truck.

For larger volumes, the rail-and-road combination via ICD Tughlakabad makes more sense: Cochin Port to JNPT or Mundra, ICD rake to Tughlakabad, last-mile truck to Sonipat. The maths depends on monthly volume and the customer's willingness to break stock into ICD-pickup batches. Where the load is mixed plywood plus finished crates, we route the crates by direct truck and palletise the sheets for the rail leg.

LegDistance / routeIndicative transit
Kochi factory to Sonipat (direct truck)~2,300 km via NH-44 / NH-484-6 days
Cochin Port to ICD Tughlakabad (sea + rail)JNPT or Mundra trans-shipment10-14 days
ICD Tughlakabad to Sonipat plant gate~50-65 kmsame day

Decision rules: which grade for which Sonipat load

  • Packing-grade MR plywood is the default for export crates carrying bicycles, tyres, auto-components and general engineering goods. It is the most cost-efficient board for one-way export packaging.
  • BWR/BWP grades earn their cost when the cargo is high-value, the sea voyage is long, or the customer ships into humid coastal destinations through the monsoon. For Sonipat buyers exporting to GCC, Africa or South-East Asia, BWR makes sense on premium SKUs.
  • Okoume-faced packing ply is a face-veneer option on the MR-Packing and BWR grades. It is the right call when the buyer specifies a light, uniform face for branded crates or when the destination customer rejects darker veneer faces.
  • Shuttering plywood is the wrong choice for export packing but the right choice for construction sites and infrastructure project buyers in the district. Different grade, different conversation.

FAQ

Can you ship directly to Kundli or Rai HSIIDC plot addresses?

Yes. Trucks deliver to the plant gate on standard HSIIDC plots; the only check is unloading access for a 32-foot or 40-foot trailer. Crane-offload is available on request for pre-assembled crates above 1.5 tonnes.

What is the minimum order for a Sonipat consignment?

Full-truck loads are the most economical, typically 18-22 tonnes per truck depending on sheet thickness. Below that, we consolidate with other northern dispatches or route via ICD Tughlakabad for buyers willing to take ICD pickup.

Can crates ship pre-stamped with ISPM-15 for export?

Yes. Heat-treatment under IPPC certification is available on the batten timber, with the IS PM-15 stamp on the finished crate. Specify on the PO whether your destination port requires the stamp.

Get a quote for your Sonipat plant

If you run a packing line, a crate shop or a warehousing operation in Kundli, Rai, Barhi, Murthal or anywhere across the Sonipat district, the fastest path to a quote is to send us your typical SKU mix, monthly volume and delivery address. The Cochin Wood group has been operating out of Perumbavoor since 1986, and that 40-year manufacturing depth is what lets us hold consistent grade and dispatch reliability over a 2,300 km lane. For specs, drawings and packing references, the resource hub has the technical sheets and guide articles; when you are ready to talk numbers, request a quote and our team will revert within one working day with a delivered-Sonipat price.

title: Plywood Supply to Ghaziabad | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, wooden cases and pallets that move finished goods out of Ghaziabad's industrial estates. Built in Perumbavoor.
html:
Ghaziabad sits at the eastern edge of the National Capital Region and behaves like a workshop for half of north India. Heavy engineering, railway component fabrication, defense electronics, and a sprawl of light-engineering MSMEs all run shifts here, and almost every one of them eventually needs a wooden case, a pallet, or a sheet of packing-grade plywood to move finished goods out of the gate. That demand is what brings us up the trunk line from Kochi — and this piece lays out what we ship into Ghaziabad, why the city's load profile suits our material, and how the 2,400-km logistics actually work in practice.

The Ghaziabad industrial picture

Ghaziabad's manufacturing footprint is concentrated across a handful of well-defined belts, and a packing-grade plywood supplier has to think in those terms rather than treating the city as a single market. The major clusters are:

  • Sahibabad Industrial Area — one of north India's older organised estates, home to heavy engineering units, electrical equipment makers, and component suppliers feeding both railway and defense supply chains.
  • Mohan Nagar industrial belt — a mix of mid-sized engineering shops, steel fabrication, and process units that crate goods for inter-state dispatch and export.
  • Site IV, Sahibabad and the South Side GT Road corridor — denser MSME activity, machined parts, fasteners, and ancillaries for the auto and railway sector.
  • Bulandshahr Road Industrial Area (BRIA) — heavier and more land-extensive units, including some sheet-metal and structural fabrication that needs flat-bed pallet packing.
  • Loni and Tronica City — newer engineering and electronics clusters where export-grade ISPM-15 crating is common.

Layered on top of that is the defense electronics ecosystem anchored by Bharat Electronics and its vendor base, plus the dense network of railway component suppliers that serve Indian Railways' Northern and North Central zones. Both of those verticals are sensitive packers: they want predictable plywood thickness, square-cut edges, and crates that survive multi-mode handling without giving way.

What we ship to Ghaziabad

Our Ghaziabad lane is dominated by three product families, all manufactured under Cochin Wood specifications at our contract factories in Perumbavoor.

Packing-grade plywood (commercial / rubberwood core)

This is the workhorse. Engineering shops in Sahibabad and Mohan Nagar buy it by the truckload to build their own crates, base frames, and case lids in-house. Standard thickness ladder we ship into NCR:

  • 6 mm and 8 mm — case linings, top covers, lightweight inner partitions
  • 10 mm and 12 mm — the most common case-wall thickness for medium-weight railway and electrical components
  • 14 mm, 15 mm and 16 mm — case walls and tops for heavier loads, transformer parts, switchgear
  • 18 mm — base boards, skid tops, and crate floors for loads above 500 kg

Sheet sizes are typically 8x4 ft, with 7x4 and 6x4 cut to order when a customer wants to reduce in-house cutting waste. All rates are quoted ex-factory; GST and transport are extra and quoted lane-specific for Ghaziabad.

Finished crates and packing cases

For defense electronics, instrumentation, and any consignment going to a port, we deliver fully built ISPM-15 compliant cases — heat-treated, stencilled with the IPPC mark, and ready to load. The construction logic for Ghaziabad's typical loads:

  • Base: 18 mm plywood skid with hardwood runners (jackwood or rubberwood) sized to the consignment weight
  • Walls and ends: 12 mm or 15 mm packing-grade plywood, framed with seasoned hardwood battens
  • Top: 10 mm or 12 mm with cleats for lift-off access
  • Inner cushioning, VCI lining, and silica sachets when the buyer specifies (common for BEL-family electronics)

Pallets

The Ghaziabad pallet mix is roughly half four-way export pallets (1200 x 1000 mm, 1200 x 800 mm) and half custom-size two-way pallets for in-plant movement. We supply both, again heat-treated where the load is heading out of India. Pallet decks are typically 12 mm to 18 mm plywood on a hardwood frame; sawn-timber-only pallets are available for buyers who prefer the conventional plank-deck design.

Container flooring

Container refurbishers and a handful of CFS operators in the wider NCR pull container-floor plywood — typically 28 mm phenolic-bonded, exterior-grade boards. We quote this on a project basis since the spec varies by buyer.

Kochi to Ghaziabad — how the logistics actually run

The road distance from our Perumbavoor manufacturing belt to Ghaziabad is roughly 2,400 km. Two routes matter:

  1. Direct road via NH 44 trunk line — Kochi to Bangalore to Hyderabad to Nagpur to Agra to Ghaziabad. A full truckload (32 ft single-axle or 22-ft container truck) typically takes 5 to 7 working days depending on driver pairing and weigh-bridge queues. This is the default for crate consignments and full-truck plywood orders.
  2. Coastal-rail-multimodal — for very large packing-plywood orders, FOB Cochin via rail rake to a north India ICD (Tughlakabad or Dadri) can work out cheaper per square foot, with final road leg to Ghaziabad. Transit is longer (10 to 14 days) but the per-sheet freight drops noticeably above ~30 tonnes.

For most Ghaziabad buyers — engineering shops ordering 200 to 800 sheets at a time, or crate buyers needing 20 to 60 finished cases — the direct truck route is the right call. We pre-load at our Perumbavoor yard, seal, and dispatch with GPS-tracked transporters who run the south-to-NCR lane regularly.

Which grade for which Ghaziabad load

A short decision rule we share with new buyers in the NCR:

Use caseRecommended material
Standard engineering crates, switchgear, motors, machined partsPacking-grade commercial / rubberwood plywood, 12–15 mm walls, 18 mm base
Defense electronics, instrumentation, export-bound consignments needing premium finishOkoume face veneer on MR-Packing or BWR grade — cleaner face, dimensionally stable, takes stencilling well
Heavy structural fabrications, transformer tanks, machinery skids18 mm packing-grade base with hardwood runners; sawn timber bracing
Reusable in-plant pallets carrying steel coils or castingsHardwood plank pallets or 18 mm ply-deck pallets
Shuttering for civil works (rare for industrial buyers but occasional)Film-faced shuttering ply, 12 mm or 18 mm

Okoume is a face veneer option, not a separate grade — it sits on top of our MR-Packing or BWR cores and is the right pick when a buyer needs a cleaner-looking, more uniform crate surface for export-grade dispatch.

FAQ — questions Ghaziabad buyers usually ask first

What is the minimum order quantity for a Ghaziabad delivery?

For loose plywood, one full truck (roughly 600 to 900 sheets depending on thickness mix) is the economic minimum on the direct road route. For built-up crates, we typically take orders from 20 cases upward. Smaller orders are possible but freight per unit rises sharply.

Are your crates ISPM-15 compliant for export consignments?

Yes. We supply heat-treated, IPPC-stencilled wooden packaging through licensed treatment facilities. The IPPC mark, treatment code, and country code are stencilled on two opposite faces as required by ISPM-15. Useful for any consignment leaving Ghaziabad via Mundra, Nhava Sheva, or air cargo from Delhi.

How are rates quoted — landed or ex-factory?

All standard rates are ex-factory, GST and transport extra. For Ghaziabad we quote freight separately because lane rates move with diesel and seasonal demand. Once thickness, monthly quantity, and exact delivery address are confirmed, we issue a proforma invoice with a landed rate the buyer can plan against.

Working with us

The Cochin Wood group has been in the Perumbavoor plywood belt since 1986 — about 40 years of group manufacturing experience feeding into how we cut, glue, and build packing cases today. CWI Pvt Ltd is the corporate vehicle we use to invoice and ship pan-India, but the workshop know-how behind every crate is older than most of the engineering plants we serve in Ghaziabad.

If you run packing, dispatch, or procurement for an engineering, railway, or defense-electronics unit anywhere in the Ghaziabad industrial belt — Sahibabad, Mohan Nagar, BRIA, Loni, Tronica City — we are set up to quote, sample, and deliver on the trunk-line route from Kochi. Share thickness, monthly volume, delivery PIN code, and the load profile, and we will come back with a thickness recommendation, an ex-factory rate, and a Ghaziabad-landed price the same day.

Request a quote with your spec sheet, or browse our resource hub for the packing-plywood thickness guide, ISPM-15 crating notes, and pallet sizing references our buyers in north India use most.

title: Plywood Supply to Meerut | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, knocked-down crate sets and pallets that Meerut's export belts stuff into containers every month. Our group has.
html:
Meerut is one of those western UP cities that quietly does more export packing than people realise. The sports goods belt around Suraj Kund and Sotiganj alone moves containers of cricket bats, balls and protective gear to the UK, Australia, the Gulf and Africa every month. Add the publishing-machinery cluster around Partapur, the scissors-and-shears makers, and the steady stream of auto-component shipments out of Modipuram, and you have a city whose packing-plywood demand is steady, year-round, and surprisingly specific. This is the kind of buyer profile the Cochin Wood group is built around — bulk packing-grade plywood, crate sets, pallets and container flooring, shipped from Kochi to wherever the export consignment is being stuffed.

The Meerut industrial picture

Meerut sits about 70 km north-east of Delhi on NH-58, which puts it squarely inside the National Capital Region's manufacturing pull but with its own distinct industrial DNA. A few belts matter for plywood demand:

  • Suraj Kund and Sotiganj sports goods cluster — the historic centre of the Indian cricket-equipment trade. Bats, balls, pads, gloves and protective wear are made here and exported through ICDs at Tughlakabad, Loni and Garhi Harsaru. Most of this freight ships in wooden crates or on plywood-decked pallets because air-freight insurance underwriters and overseas buyers ask for it.
  • Partapur Industrial Area — the larger UPSIDC estate on the Delhi road. Printing-and-publishing machinery (Meerut still has a working publishing and bookbinding equipment trade), transformer and electrical component manufacturers, and a number of food-processing units all sit here. Heavy, dense machine consignments need ISPM-15 crates and proper bracing-grade plywood.
  • Mohkampur, Udyog Puram and Modipuram — auto components, agricultural implements and metal fabrication. Pallet-heavy buyers.
  • Scissors and shears trade in the old city — smaller cartons mostly, but bulk export consolidators in this trade still buy plywood-bottom master cases.

The common thread: most of Meerut's high-value shipments leave the country, and packing failure is not an option. That puts a real floor under the quality of plywood the city's packing-case makers and exporters can use.

What we ship to Meerut

Cochin Wood Industries supplies four product families into Meerut. The mix on any given truck depends on what is being packed.

Packing-grade plywood

This is the workhorse. Hardwood-core, phenolic-bonded plywood in 8 ft x 4 ft sheets, available in 6 mm, 8 mm, 10 mm, 12 mm, 14 mm, 15 mm, 16 mm and 18 mm. For Meerut's sports goods exporters, 8 mm and 10 mm are the most common — light enough to keep the cubic-metre weight of a cricket-bat container in check, strong enough to survive port handling. For the publishing-machinery and transformer crowd, 12 mm to 18 mm is the right band because the loads are dense and the crate has to take stacking pressure in the container.

Knocked-down crate sets

For exporters who don't want to manage a captive carpentry shop, we ship pre-cut crate components — top, bottom, sides, ends, runners — fumigated and stencilled ISPM-15 where required. This shortens the loading window in Partapur from days to hours.

Pallets

Two-way and four-way entry pallets, hardwood blocks or stringers, sized to the buyer's container plan. Standard sizes for Meerut buyers tend to be 1200 x 1000 mm (Euro-style for UK/EU lanes) and 1100 x 1100 mm (Gulf and ASEAN lanes). Heat-treated and stamped where the destination requires it.

Container flooring plywood

Heavy 18 mm plywood for repair and refit of dry vans and trailer beds. The Modipuram and Mohkampur trucking fleets running between Meerut and the Mundra/Nhava Sheva ICDs are steady buyers for this.

Kochi to Meerut: the logistics

Meerut sits roughly 2,400 km by road from our Kochi loading yards. The lane we use most:

  • Route: Kochi → Salem → Bengaluru → Hyderabad → Nagpur → Gwalior → Agra → Meerut, mostly on NH-44 and NH-58.
  • Transit time: 6 to 8 days for a single-driver truck, 4 to 5 days with a two-driver relay. We plan to either.
  • Truck size: standard option is a 32-ft single-axle multi-axle carrying about 18 to 20 tonnes of mixed-thickness plywood. For full-crate orders we use 32-ft high-cube bodies that handle bulky knocked-down sets without crushing.
  • Alternative: for very large orders we will FOB Cochin to Mundra or JNPT by coastal vessel and rail the container to Tughlakabad ICD for last-mile to Meerut. This works for orders that fill a 20-ft or 40-ft container outright.

GST and transport are billed extra on top of our ex-factory price card. We share a landed-cost estimate when we send the proforma invoice so the Meerut buyer knows the full delivered number before committing.

Which grade fits which Meerut load

A quick decision rule based on what we see most often:

Typical Meerut loadRecommended plywoodWhy
Cricket bats, balls, sports kit exportsPacking-grade, 8 mm or 10 mmLight cube weight, ISPM-15 ready, low cost per crate
Publishing and bookbinding machineryPacking-grade, 15 mm to 18 mmDense load, needs stacking strength and bracing thickness
Transformers, electrical equipmentPacking-grade, 18 mm with hardwood runnersHeavy point loads, port handling shocks
Premium export crates for buyers who specify a hardwood faceOkoume plywood, 12 mm to 18 mmClean African hardwood face, takes stencilling well, accepted by quality-sensitive overseas buyers
Construction-site shuttering in the Meerut-Modipuram beltShuttering ply, 12 mmMultiple-pour reuse, film-faced options on request

For most Meerut packing-case buyers, our standard hardwood-core packing-grade is the right answer. Okoume is a specification call — quote it when the overseas buyer or the freight forwarder is asking for a premium look.

FAQ for Meerut buyers

Do you supply ISPM-15 stamped crates and pallets?

Yes. We heat-treat at registered facilities and stencil the IPPC mark, country code IN, treatment code HT and the registered facility number. This is the standard for exports to the EU, UK, Australia and most other destinations Meerut ships to.

What is the minimum order you will ship to Meerut?

One full truck — roughly 18 to 20 tonnes of plywood, or the equivalent in crate sets and pallets. Smaller part-loads are possible if we can consolidate with another Delhi-NCR delivery, but full-truck pricing is always better for the buyer.

How do payment and GST work for an out-of-state buyer?

We bill ex-factory Kochi with IGST applicable for an inter-state supply to UP. Transport is billed separately or arranged by the buyer's nominated transporter. We share the proforma invoice with the full breakup so there are no surprises at delivery.

Getting a quote

If you are packing sports goods in Suraj Kund, crating machinery in Partapur, palletising for an auto-component shipment in Mohkampur, or running a trailer-refit job out of Modipuram, we can quote for it. Share the thickness, sheet count or crate spec, the destination and the monthly volume, and we will come back with an ex-factory rate and a landed-Meerut estimate. Request a quote with your specs, or browse the technical guides in our resource hub if you are still sizing the plywood for your packing application. The Cochin Wood group has been working in Perumbavoor since 1986, and the packing-plywood side of the business exists precisely for buyers like the ones who built Meerut's export trade.

title: Plywood Supply to Kanpur | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, export crates, pallets and container flooring for Kanpur's tanneries, ordnance shops and engineering job-shops. Our.
html:
Kanpur runs on three industrial currents that all end in a wooden box: finished leather goods bound for European and US buyers, ordnance and defense components going to military depots across India, and fertilizer and chemical drums moving inland by rail. Each of those flows generates a steady demand for packing-grade plywood, ISPM-15 crates, four-way pallets and container flooring. We ship those four products into Kanpur on a regular basis from our Kochi/Ernakulam operations, and this page walks through what actually leaves our gate, how it travels the 2,300 km north, and which grade fits which load.

The Kanpur industrial picture

Kanpur is the leather capital of India. The Jajmau cluster on the south bank of the Ganga concentrates more than 350 tanneries and finishing units, and the goods that leave Jajmau — saddlery, full-grain leather garments, industrial gloves, shoe uppers — are almost all crated in plywood before they hit a 20-ft container at ICD Panki or move overland to Nhava Sheva and Mundra. Beyond leather, the city carries weight in defense and ordnance:

  • Ordnance Factory Kanpur (OFK) and Small Arms Factory (SAF), Kanpur — manufacture small arms, ammunition and field equipment that ship to depots in ISPM-15-compliant crates.
  • Field Gun Factory and Ordnance Equipment Factory, Hazratpur — large, heavy castings and assemblies needing custom-built skids and crates.
  • Panki Industrial Area — fertilizer (IFFCO Aonla is close, Krishak Bharati and Tata Chemicals operate in the region), engineering, and pharma.
  • Dada Nagar and Fazalganj industrial estates — hosiery, textile machinery, auto components, and a long bench of engineering job-shops.
  • UPSIDC Site IV and V at Panki and the Jajmau Leather Cluster (CETP zone) — concentrations of MSME exporters who order plywood by the truckload.

Add to that the Kanpur–Lucknow corridor on NH-27 and the upcoming Defence Industrial Corridor node at Sandila, and you get a city whose packaging demand is denser than its sticker would suggest.

What we ship into Kanpur

Packing-grade plywood (commercial / rubberwood)

This is the workhorse and most of what moves to Kanpur. We supply 8 ft × 4 ft sheets in the standard packing thicknesses — 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 15, 16 and 18 mm. Leather exporters and engineering crate-makers in Dada Nagar typically buy 8 mm and 10 mm for the walls and lids and step up to 18 mm for the bottom skid where a 250–500 kg load sits over a forklift slot. Rates are EX-FACTORY, GST and freight extra; we quote against your size and monthly draw rather than off a printed list.

ISPM-15 export crates

For Jajmau leather exporters and OFK shipments leaving the country, ISPM-15 heat-treated, IPPC-stamped crates are mandatory. We build to your packing list — knock-down (KD) for inland freight efficiency, or pre-assembled where the buyer takes delivery and packs in-house. Standard wall is 12 mm packing ply on a 50 × 75 mm rubberwood or hardwood frame; bottoms run 18 mm over 75 × 100 mm bearers for forklift entry. IPPC marking and treatment certificate travel with each crate.

Wooden pallets

Four-way entry, perimeter-base pallets in the standard 1200 × 1000 mm and 1200 × 800 mm Euro footprint, plus 1100 × 1100 mm for containers heading to GCC and Vietnam buyers via Kanpur consolidators. Deckboards are 18 mm packing ply or 22 mm sawn rubberwood depending on dynamic load. Heat-treated stamping available on order.

Container flooring plywood

28 mm phenolic-bonded film-faced ply in the 2400 × 1200 mm container floor size, for reefers and dry vans being refurbished at workshops around Panki and along the Lucknow–Kanpur highway. Edges are sealed, surface is anti-skid film. We ship in bundle quantities; minimum order is typically a full truck.

Kochi to Kanpur — the route

The lane runs 2,300–2,400 km depending on origin point inside the Perumbavoor cluster. Two practical options:

ModeRouteTransitWhen it makes sense
Road (32-ft SXL / 22-ft)Perumbavoor → Salem → Bangalore → Hyderabad → Nagpur → Jhansi → Kanpur6–8 daysCrates, pallets, mixed loads under 18 tonnes, or buyers without ICD access
Rail + last-mileCochin Port / Ernakulam → ICD Panki via container rake10–12 daysFull-container plywood orders to Panki, Dada Nagar; cheaper per sheet at scale

For most Kanpur MSME orders we quote FOR (freight on road) and put a 32-ft single-axle on the route with a 14–18 tonne payload. Larger draws — 4,000+ sheets a month, typical of mid-size packing-case houses — work better through Cochin Port to ICD Panki. We can also originate from FOB Cochin for buyers nominating their own forwarder.

Which grade fits which load

Quick decision rules we use when a Kanpur buyer calls in:

  • Domestic dispatch, light-to-medium loads (under 200 kg) — commercial packing-grade plywood, 8–12 mm walls, 15 mm base. Most Jajmau leather garment exports, hosiery from Fazalganj, auto components from Dada Nagar fit this bracket.
  • Export crates, ISPM-15, sea freight — same grade but heat-treated and IPPC-stamped, with the base stepped up to 18 mm. This is the default for Jajmau finished leather, saddlery, and ordnance components moving via Nhava Sheva.
  • Heavy castings, defense and ordnance equipment (over 500 kg) — BWR-grade or premium packing ply at 18 mm on a hardwood frame; for the largest pieces leaving Hazratpur or Field Gun Factory we recommend a sawn-timber skid base with plywood superstructure.
  • Okoume face-veneer (on MR-Packing or BWR grades) — when the carton aesthetics matter for branded leather exports or premium gift packing. Okoume is a face-veneer option, not a separate grade, and is quoted on request.
  • Concrete formwork on Kanpur Metro extensions and infra projects — film-faced shuttering plywood, 12 or 18 mm, sold separately from the packing range.

FAQ

What is the typical lead time from order confirmation to truck arrival in Kanpur?

For a single truck of packing-grade sheets or pallets, count 10–14 days door to door — that is 2–3 days from PI confirmation to material at our loading point, then 6–8 days transit. ISPM-15 crates built to a specific packing list take longer because of the build cycle; budget 15–18 days. Container flooring is typically available within a week if the size is standard 2400 × 1200 × 28 mm.

Do you offer ISPM-15 heat treatment and IPPC stamping?

Yes — for every export crate and pallet we ship to Jajmau or OFK that needs sea-freight clearance. The treatment certificate travels with the consignment and the IPPC mark is burned onto two opposite faces of each unit. We do not ship untreated wood as "export-ready" — that is where DG-shipping rejections start.

Can you handle a recurring monthly draw rather than one-off orders?

Yes, and that is most of what we move on the Kanpur lane. Mid-size packing-case houses and leather exporters typically commit to a monthly sheet count and a fixed price for the quarter, with deliveries staggered as call-offs. That smooths the truck-load economics and protects against rate swings in the rubberwood market.

Request a quote

If you are a Kanpur-based packing-case maker, leather exporter, ordnance contractor, fertilizer or chemical packer, or you build container flooring and need a long-run plywood partner south of the Vindhyas, we are set up for the route. The Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing in Perumbavoor since 1986 — that is 40+ years of group operations behind the specs you would receive. Send us your thickness, size, monthly quantity and delivery pin code and we will come back with a quote on the appropriate grade and a realistic transit window. Request a quote with your packing list, or browse our resource hub for crate construction notes, ISPM-15 references and the full plywood grade card.

title: Plywood Supply to Lucknow | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade and Okoume-faced plywood, crates and pallets in Perumbavoor, where our group has run plywood operations since 1986. We ship to.
html:
Lucknow sits at an interesting crossroads for anyone who supplies industrial packing material. The city is best known for chikankari handwork and a deep cultural economy, but the industrial belt around it has quietly turned into a meaningful buyer of export-grade plywood, crating timber and pallet stock. Handicraft exporters need shock-absorbing crates for fragile lacquerware, brass and chikankari consignments. The HAL Lucknow division and ancillary defense vendors need ISPM-15 compliant boxes that can stand up to sea freight. IT and electronics assemblers around Sushant Golf City and Chinhat need flat, dimensionally stable plywood for clean packing. That mix is what brings Cochin Wood Industries into the Lucknow conversation.

The Lucknow industrial picture

Lucknow's manufacturing and export activity is spread across a handful of well-defined zones, and knowing the geography helps when you are planning truck deliveries of plywood from Kerala. The main belts our trucks reach into are:

  • Chinhat Industrial Area — light engineering, food processing and packaging units along NH-28; close to the Faizabad Road corridor.
  • Sarojini Nagar Industrial Estate (UPSIDC) — long-established estate with metal fabrication, electrical goods and a steady appetite for shuttering ply during civil expansions.
  • Amausi and Nadarganj — adjacent to the airport; warehousing, third-party logistics and e-commerce fulfillment hubs that buy pallets in volume.
  • Sushant Golf City and Sultanpur Road belt — IT parks, ITeS campuses and the newer commercial construction that pulls shuttering plywood.
  • HAL Lucknow division (Faizabad Road) — accessory and avionics manufacturing for transport aircraft and helicopters; a precision sector that drives demand for export-quality wooden cases with documented heat treatment.
  • Chikankari and zardozi clusters around Chowk, Aminabad and Daliganj — small workshops feed consolidators and exporters in Gomti Nagar who do the actual crating and shipping.

Add to that the Defence Industrial Corridor nodes coming up around Lucknow and Kanpur, and the city's industrial footprint is genuinely growing. None of these sectors buy plywood the same way, which is the whole point.

What we ship to Lucknow

The Cochin Wood group has been operating in Perumbavoor since 1986, and the present-day Cochin Wood Industries supply mix into Uttar Pradesh reflects what we have seen actually move on these routes. Three categories cover almost everything Lucknow buyers ask for:

Packing-grade plywood (commercial / rubberwood)

This is the workhorse for handicraft exporters and engineering crate makers. Standard thicknesses we ship to Lucknow in container loads or part-truck consignments:

ThicknessTypical use in Lucknow
6mm and 8mmLining inside chikankari and lacquerware crates; lightweight handicraft cartons
10mm and 12mmGeneral export crates, machinery sub-assemblies, electronics packing
14mm to 16mmHeavy crates for fabricated parts and HAL-ancillary loads
18mmBase panels of heavy boxes, container side panels, shuttering reuse

All sheets are MR or BWR bonded depending on the destination port and humidity exposure. For sea-bound consignments through Mundra or JNPT we usually push buyers toward BWR.

Export crates and packing cases

We supply both knock-down crate kits (cut-to-size panels, cleats and skids) and fully assembled cases. The HAL ancillary segment and the heavier engineering exporters typically want ISPM-15 heat-treated softwood frames married to plywood skins. Common case sizes for the Lucknow handicraft trade are in the 600 x 400 x 400 mm to 1200 x 800 x 800 mm range; engineering crates run much larger and are often built around the load's center of gravity rather than to a standard size.

Pallets

Lucknow's warehousing and 3PL belt around Amausi runs mostly on two pallet footprints — the standard 1200 x 800 mm Euro and the 1200 x 1000 mm "industrial" pallet. We supply both two-way and four-way entry pallets, in commercial hardwood or rubberwood, with ISPM-15 stamping where the pallet is going onward into export.

Container flooring

For project cargo movers and the occasional containerized HAL shipment that needs reinforced flooring, we supply 18mm or 21mm BWR plywood cut to ISO container floor dimensions. This is a lower-frequency line for Lucknow but worth flagging — it travels in the same truck as the crating ply.

Kochi-to-Lucknow logistics

Lucknow is roughly 2,400 km from Kochi by road. The realistic operating window for a single-truck consignment is 5 to 7 days door-to-door, depending on whether the truck routes via Bangalore-Hyderabad-Nagpur-Jhansi or via Mumbai-Indore-Gwalior. We dispatch ex-factory Perumbavoor; long-haul trucking and unloading are quoted separately so the buyer can compare against their own freight rates if they prefer.

For larger orders — anything above roughly 25 to 30 tonnes — buyers in Lucknow sometimes find it cheaper to take FOB Cochin (port: Cochin / Vallarpadam ICTT) and rail-and-truck the container inland to ICD Kanpur or ICD Loni, then short-haul to their yard. This works well for handicraft consolidators who are already running container traffic. For smaller orders the road lane wins on simplicity. We are happy to quote either way and let the numbers decide.
