Decision rules: which grade for which Lucknow load

A practical operator view of when each grade earns its place:

  1. Use commercial / rubberwood packing plywood when the consignment is domestic, going by road to another Indian city, or being air-freighted internationally in well-controlled conditions. This is the most cost-effective option and covers the bulk of chikankari and light handicraft crates.
  2. Step up to BWR-bonded plywood with okoume face veneer when the destination is humid, the sea voyage is long (Middle East, West Africa, ASEAN), or the customer values a cleaner-looking export case. Okoume is a veneer choice on our packing and BWR grades — it is not a separate plywood grade.
  3. Choose shuttering plywood only when the buyer is a civil contractor on the Sushant Golf City or Gomti Nagar Extension projects. It is built for concrete forming, not for crating; using it for packing wastes money on the wrong feature set.

FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15 stamped boxes for HAL ancillary exports?

Yes. We supply heat-treated softwood frames with our plywood skins, stamped to ISPM-15 by an accredited treatment facility. We share the treatment certificate with the consignment so it clears phytosanitary inspection at the port.

Minimum order quantity for a Lucknow consignment?

Most of our Lucknow buyers run full-truck loads (roughly 20 to 25 tonnes of plywood, depending on thickness mix) because the per-sheet freight on a part-load over 2,400 km is uncomfortable. We will quote part-loads too — just expect a higher landed cost per square foot.

Can you cut-to-size for our standard crate?

Yes. Send the cutting list and tolerance — we cut at the factory in Perumbavoor and ship the panels bundled by crate, which speeds up assembly at your yard and reduces sawdust on your shop floor.

Request a quote

If you are sourcing packing plywood, export crates, pallets or container flooring for a unit in Lucknow, send us the thickness, monthly volume, and the delivery point in the city. We will come back with an ex-factory rate, the trucking lane we would use, and a realistic transit window. Start with our request a quote form, or browse the technical write-ups in our resource hub if you want to compare BWR versus MR grades or work out crate timber volumes before you ask for a number.

title: Plywood Supply to Greater Noida | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, built-up crates, pallets and container flooring for Greater Noida's export belts. Made in Perumbavoor, where our group.
html:
Why Greater Noida is on our supply map

Greater Noida is one of the densest export-packing belts in North India. Within a 25 km radius you have the Honda Cars India plant at Sector 40, Yamaha Motor at Surajpur, New Holland Tractors at Sector Ecotech-III, JBM and Minda component lines, hyperscale data-centre builds at Knowledge Park V, and the Yamuna Expressway corridor pushing toward Jewar Airport. Every one of those facilities pushes out cargo that has to be crated, palletised, and dunnaged in wood before it leaves the gate. That is the demand we ship plywood against, and this page explains how a Kerala-based maker delivers cost-competitively into a city 2,400 km north.

The Greater Noida industrial picture

The packing demand in Greater Noida isn't one cluster — it is at least four overlapping ones, and the plywood spec changes with each.

Auto and auto-component cluster

The Surajpur–Kasna belt (Sectors Ecotech-I through XII, Site-V, and the UPSIDC industrial area) is the heaviest user. Two-wheeler CKD kits going to Latin America and Africa, four-wheeler sub-assemblies, tractor PTO housings, and Tier-1 stamping/forging deliveries all move in plywood cases. The Yamaha CKD lane and the New Holland Tractors export lane out of Greater Noida are particularly steady.

Data centres and IT hardware

The Knowledge Park belt (II, III, V) and the Tech Zone IV cluster around Pari Chowk now host hyperscale and colocation builds. Rack delivery, UPS and chiller crating, and switchgear protection during fit-out drive a specific kind of demand — fewer crates, but heavier units, often single-piece loads above one tonne where the plywood case is structural, not just a wrap.

Film City and engineered goods

The upcoming Film City off the Yamuna Expressway plus the existing engineered-goods makers (pumps, valves, transformers in YEIDA Sectors 28 and 29) generate a steady packing case requirement that is sensitive to FOB price but not to ISPM-15 certification, since most of it moves domestic.

Jewar (Noida International Airport)

As Jewar ramps up, air-freight-grade crates — lighter, dimensionally tight, often 12mm plywood on a softwood frame — will become a growing segment. We are already quoting against that brief from forwarders setting up around Sector 32 and the Multi-Modal Cargo Hub.

What we ship into Greater Noida

Cochin Wood Industries supplies four product families against Greater Noida demand, all manufactured to CWI specifications through our contract factories in the Perumbavoor cluster.

Packing-grade plywood (sheet form)

This is the workhorse. We ship 8x4 ft sheets in 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 15, 16, and 18 mm thicknesses, hardwood face with rubberwood or commercial hardwood core, MR or BWR glue line depending on what the buyer's QC asks for. Selling rates run from ₹24/sq.ft for 6mm up to ₹42/sq.ft for 18mm, ex-factory Perumbavoor, GST and freight extra. For Greater Noida buyers ordering by the truckload, we load full 32-ft single-axle trucks (≈25 tonnes) so the per-sheet inbound freight is at its lowest.

Built-up crates and packing cases

For buyers who do not want to run their own case-making line, we supply finished cases — nailed or bolted, with skid bases, runner-and-batten sides, and lid construction matching the buyer's drawing. Jackwood runners at ₹400/cft are standard for the heavier auto and machinery crates; rubberwood runners on lighter cases.

Wooden pallets

Two-way and four-way entry pallets in 1200x800, 1200x1000, and 1100x1100 mm footprints, in standard-duty and heavy-duty load classes. For data-centre rack movement we typically build a 1200x1000 four-way pallet with a 38–50 mm top deck — strong enough for a 1,200 kg single load with the forklift entry the loading bay actually has.

Container flooring and dunnage

18 mm BWR plywood for container floor repair and one-trip container conversions, plus 12–18 mm sheets cut to dunnage sizes for in-container blocking and bracing. This is a smaller line but a useful one for the CHA and freight-forwarder cluster around the ICD at Dadri.

Kochi to Greater Noida — the logistics that matter

The honest question every North Indian buyer asks is: why source plywood from Kerala when there are mills in UP itself? Two answers.

First, raw material economics. The Perumbavoor belt is built on rubberwood and locally aggregated hardwoods — short, fast turnover, lower core cost than the gurjan-import-dependent mills further north. That gap is usually wider than the freight cost over 2,400 km.

Second, the route is well-trodden. We dispatch from Perumbavoor (≈30 km from Kochi port) by road on the standard NH-44 / Mumbai–Delhi corridor. Transit time for a single-axle 32-ft truck is 5–7 days to the Greater Noida industrial belts, and 6–8 days for a heavier 22-wheeler if the buyer prefers fewer, larger drops. For very large monthly volumes we can also offer rail-on-truck out of the Whitefield/Bangalore container terminals, which trims a day off the transit but adds handling cost.

Lane detailTypical figure
Origin (load point)Perumbavoor, Ernakulam district, Kerala
Distance to Greater Noida~2,400 km road
Transit time, 32-ft single-axle5–7 days
Truck capacity (sheet form)~25 tonnes / ~600–700 sheets of 18 mm
Delivery sectors served regularlyEcotech I–XII, Surajpur, Knowledge Park, Site-V, YEIDA
Pricing basisEx-factory; GST + freight extra

Decision rules: which grade for which Greater Noida load

A quick operator-level guide for buyers choosing a spec.

  • Packing-grade rubberwood/commercial MR ply — for domestic-moving cases, in-plant pallets, dunnage, and the bulk of the auto-component CKD that doesn't need ISPM-15 fumigation certification. Lowest landed cost per sheet.
  • Okoume face packing ply — when the buyer's export customer specifies a clean, light-coloured face (European auto OEMs and some West African importers do). Cosmetically better, marginally more expensive; we quote on request rather than off the standard card.
  • BWR plywood — for container flooring, exposed dunnage, and any case that may sit in a port stack through a monsoon. Worth the upcharge when the alternative is field rework.
  • Shuttering plywood — separate product line; we send to data-centre civil contractors and Jewar Airport civil packages. Different tooling, quoted separately.

FAQ

How do you handle ISPM-15 for export crates?

We supply the plywood and casework; ISPM-15 fumigation and stamping is typically handled at the buyer's premises or by their nominated treatment agency. Where the buyer needs it, we can coordinate ISPM-15 heat-treated softwood runners through our partner mills.

Can you deliver direct to a sector address inside Greater Noida?

Yes. We deliver to factory gates across YEIDA, GNIDA, and the UPSIDC sectors. For heavy 22-wheelers we confirm the buyer's gate height and turning radius before dispatch — narrow sector roads inside older industrial blocks need a smaller vehicle.

What is the minimum order?

For sheet supply our economic minimum is one full truck (~25 tonnes) to keep per-sheet freight rational. For finished crates and pallets we can ship part loads consolidated with sheet stock, which makes sense for buyers in the 50–200 case-per-month range.

Get a Greater Noida-landed quote

The Cochin Wood group has been making plywood in Perumbavoor since 1986 — about forty years of group manufacturing — and that gives the order desk a working sense of which spec actually performs on the lane to North India. If you are running a packing line in Surajpur, Ecotech, Knowledge Park, or anywhere along the Yamuna Expressway, share thickness, monthly sheet count, delivery sector, and whether the crates are domestic or export, and we will come back with an ex-factory rate and an indicative landed price. Request a quote directly, or browse our resource hub for the spec sheets and packing-case construction notes our existing North Indian buyers ask for first.

title: Plywood Supply to Varanasi | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and sawn-timber dunnage that Varanasi's exporters load every day. Our group has been making plywood.
html:
Varanasi is one of the more interesting industrial buyers we ship to from Kerala. The city is famous for spiritual tourism, but underneath that is a dense export economy: Banarasi silk woven in Madanpura and Lallapura, brassware and bell-metal pressed at Kazzakpura, handloom carpets coming in from the Bhadohi-Mirzapur belt 80 km south, and a growing engineering footprint around the UPSIDA estates at Chandpur and Karsara. Every one of those verticals needs the same boring, repeatable input on the outbound side of the warehouse: dimensionally honest plywood, cut into export-ready crates, pallets and container floors. That is exactly what the Cochin Wood group has been making in Perumbavoor since 1986, and what we now truck up the 2,200 km lane to Varanasi for packing-case fabricators and direct exporters.

The Varanasi industrial picture

Most outside vendors think of Varanasi as a single market. On the ground it is at least four distinct industrial pockets, each with its own packing brief.

Silk and handloom (Madanpura, Lallapura, Peeli Kothi)

The Banarasi silk and brocade trade is a GI-tagged cluster with thousands of weavers feeding consolidators who export saris, dupattas, fabric rolls and made-ups to the Gulf, the US and the UK. Cargo is light by weight but extremely high-value per cubic metre, so the packing cases need to be moisture-controlled and visually presentable. Buyers in this sector usually ask for 12 mm and 15 mm BWR-grade packing ply with smooth, sound faces, frequently in non-standard sheet sizes to match standard sari-box and fabric-roll dimensions.

Brassware and metal handicraft (Kazzakpura, Thatheri Bazaar, Bhelupur)

Brass pooja items, urlis, bells, statues and decorative metalware ship in volumes that are deceptively heavy for their cubic footprint. A single 40 ft container of brassware can pack 22-24 tonnes of net weight, and the case walls have to take the shock of stacked metal during inland trucking to JNPT or Mundra. Here we see demand pivoting toward 18 mm packing-grade ply with hardwood corner posts and ISPM-15 stamped sawn timber dunnage.

Handloom carpets (Bhadohi-Mirzapur belt, served via Varanasi)

The Bhadohi-Mirzapur carpet belt south of Varanasi is the largest hand-knotted carpet cluster in India and exports through Varanasi-based consolidators. Carpet rolls are long, awkward and prone to crushing if a pallet deck flexes mid-transit. Pallet decks here want full 12 mm or 15 mm plywood tops rather than slatted softwood, and many exporters now insist on heat-treated, ISPM-15 marked timber for the frame.

UPSIDA engineering belt (Chandpur, Karsara, Ramnagar)

The Uttar Pradesh State Industrial Development Authority estates around Chandpur and Karsara host engineering units, pump assemblers, transformer fabricators and food-grade machinery makers. Their packing problem is the classic heavy-engineering one: irregular cast iron and steel weights, point-loading on the case floor, and the need for sea-grade crates rather than transit crates.

What we ship to Varanasi

Our outbound mix to Uttar Pradesh, and specifically to Varanasi-bound buyers, breaks down roughly as follows. Numbers shift each quarter but the proportions are stable.

ProductTypical Varanasi useCommon specs
Packing-grade plywoodSilk export cases, brassware crates, carpet pallet decks8 mm, 12 mm, 15 mm, 18 mm — 8x4 sheets, MR and BWR glue lines
Okoume-faced plywoodPremium handicraft and silk presentation cases12 mm and 15 mm with Okoume face veneer on packing or BWR core
Shuttering plywoodReal-estate and infra projects along NH-19 and NH-3112 mm film-faced, 8-12 use cycles depending on grade
Sawn timberCrate frames, corner posts, ISPM-15 dunnageJackwood and rubberwood runners, kiln-dried as required
Pallet stockStandard 1200x800 and 1200x1000 export pallets12-18 mm tops, hardwood blocks, fumigation mark on request

Container flooring is a smaller but growing line — exporters consolidating their own 20 ft and 40 ft boxes from Varanasi ICDs occasionally need replacement floorboards or one-time-use sea-grade panels to line the container before stuffing.

Kochi-to-Varanasi logistics

The 2,200 km lane from Perumbavoor to Varanasi is one of the longer hauls we run, but it is a well-understood corridor with two practical routes.

  1. Road-direct (preferred for plywood and crates): Kochi to Bangalore on NH-544, then NH-44 north through Hyderabad, Nagpur and Jabalpur, joining NH-19 at Allahabad/Prayagraj and into Varanasi. Transit time runs 6-8 days for a full truckload (32 ft single-axle or 22 ft container truck), 9-10 days in monsoon. We typically dispatch 800-1,200 sheets per truck depending on thickness and packing density.
  2. Containerised via JNPT/Hazira: Useful when the consignee wants to consolidate plywood with other Kerala-origin cargo, or when monthly volumes justify a 20 ft box. Sea leg Cochin port to JNPT is 4-5 days, plus inland container haul to Varanasi (DPD or via Mughalsarai). Total door-to-door usually beats road only when the buyer is already coordinating other sea-freight.

For most Varanasi packing-case fabricators we have found road-direct is faster and cheaper unless they are taking 25+ tonnes a month. We quote freight separately, ex-Perumbavoor, with GST and transport extra, so the buyer sees the landed cost clearly.

Decision rules: which grade for which Varanasi load

Use our packing-grade ply when

  • Cargo is domestic-bound or to nearby Gulf markets with a single sea leg
  • Case will be opened within 60-90 days of fabrication
  • Buyer is cost-sensitive — silk consolidators, mid-range brassware, carpet pallet decks

Use Okoume-faced ply when

  • Cargo is premium handicraft going to EU or US end-buyers who inspect packaging condition
  • The case itself is part of the presentation (gift packaging, museum-grade brass, archival textiles)
  • Climate exposure during transit is high — Okoume's lightness reduces case weight at equivalent strength

Use shuttering ply when

  • The buyer is a builder or formwork contractor working on Varanasi's expanding hospitality and infra footprint along the Ring Road and Sarnath belt
  • Re-use cycles matter more than face finish

For the engineering crates at UPSIDA Chandpur, we usually recommend a hybrid: 18 mm packing-grade ply for the case walls, hardwood sawn timber for the skids and corner posts, and ISPM-15 marked dunnage where the consignment is sea-freight.

FAQ

Do you ship in less-than-truckload quantities to Varanasi?

Yes, but the freight economics are noticeably worse on a 2,200 km lane below about 400 sheets. For a single-pallet sample we suggest combining with another buyer in the same belt or accepting the part-load premium. A full truck of 800-1,200 sheets is typically the cleanest order size.

Is the plywood ISPM-15 compliant out of the box?

Plywood itself is exempt from ISPM-15 under the standard's processed-wood clause. Sawn timber components — case frames, skids, dunnage — need to be heat-treated and stamped, which we arrange when the buyer flags export end-use at the time of order.

What is the minimum sheet thickness for a packing case carrying brassware?

For brassware crates over 200 kg net, we generally recommend 15 mm minimum for the side walls and 18 mm for the case floor, with hardwood corner posts. Below 200 kg net, 12 mm walls with a 15 mm floor is workable and saves on packing weight.

Get a quote for your Varanasi project

If you are fabricating cases for the Banarasi silk trade, packing brassware out of Kazzakpura, building pallet decks for the Bhadohi carpet belt, or crating engineering loads from the UPSIDA estates, we can quote you a delivered Varanasi rate against your sheet schedule. Share thickness, sheet count, monthly run rate and delivery point — we will come back with a proforma and a realistic transit window. Request a quote directly, or read more technical detail in our resource hub on packing-case construction and grade selection.

title: Plywood Supply to Noida | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture commercial packing ply, hardwood runners and export crates for the Noida-Greater Noida-YEIDA corridor. Our group has manufactured in.
html:
Why Noida's industrial belt drives serious plywood demand

If you build packing cases anywhere on the Noida-Greater Noida-YEIDA corridor, your bill of materials almost certainly leans on commercial plywood, hardwood runners and the occasional Okoume face for export units. The city is India's electronics manufacturing capital — the Samsung mobile plant in Sector 81, the LG cluster at Greater Noida, ST Microelectronics, contract manufacturers feeding Apple and Xiaomi vendors, and a long tail of component suppliers across UPSIDC sectors. Every one of those units puts finished goods into wooden cases, slip-sheets onto pallets, or loads CKD kits into export containers floored with marine-grade ply. This is the kind of demand profile the Cochin Wood group has been supplying since 1986 — bulk packing material into manufacturing belts that ship hard, fast and to spec.

The Noida industrial picture, sector by sector

Plywood demand in Noida isn't one market — it's at least four overlapping ones, each with its own grade preference and crate geometry. Knowing which sector a buyer sits in tells us almost immediately what they need.

The electronics export cluster

Sector 81 (Samsung), Sector 62 (HCL, Hitachi, Vivo offices), the Surajpur Industrial Area in Greater Noida, and the YEIDA notified zones near Jewar all push out mobile phones, smart TVs, set-top boxes, washing machines and components. Mobile CKD packs leave in stackable plywood boxes built from 9mm or 12mm commercial ply with hardwood runners. Larger appliance exporters need 18mm shuttering or BWR sheets cut to international pallet footprints.

The auto-component belt at Sector 67-69

NSEZ (Noida Special Economic Zone) and the Sector 63-67 industrial blocks house tier-2 and tier-3 auto suppliers feeding Maruti, Honda and TVS plants in the wider NCR. Castings, gears, sheet-metal assemblies — every one of these ships in cleated plywood crates built to ISPM-15 for export consignments to Europe and North America.

Engineering, defence and instrumentation

Sectors 1 through 16 along with parts of Sahibabad (across the Hindon) host heavy-engineering and instrumentation units. These buyers source 18mm and 25mm packing ply for one-off transit crates and skids around lathes, transformers and DG sets.

Logistics, 3PL and warehousing

The Dadri Multi-Modal Logistics Hub on the Western Dedicated Freight Corridor, plus the IIDC bonded warehouses, run on standardised 1200x1000mm and 1100x1100mm wooden pallets. That's a recurring, predictable plywood-top-deck order for any packing-case maker who lands those contracts.

What we ship into the Noida lane

The Cochin Wood group runs a single product philosophy: bulk packing material, mill-direct, cut to the sizes your line actually consumes. The range we move into Noida breaks down as follows.

Packing-grade plywood (MR-Packing)

Commercial / rubberwood plywood in 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 15, 16 and 18mm thicknesses. Standard 8x4 ft sheets, also available in 7x4 and 6x4. This is the workhorse for inland and short-export crates — phenol-bonded enough to survive monsoon transit, priced for volume. For Noida electronics packers, 9mm and 12mm move the fastest.

Okoume-faced plywood

Where the buyer needs a cleaner export face — pharma machinery, premium electronics, defence — we offer Okoume veneer over the same packing core. This isn't a different grade; it's the same MR-Packing or BWR construction with an Okoume face instead of the standard gurjan/asian hardwood face. Useful when the consignee in Europe or the Gulf inspects crate aesthetics.

Shuttering plywood

16mm and 18mm film-faced or plain shuttering ply for the construction sites still active across YEIDA, the Noida International Airport zone and the perimeter infrastructure projects. Same Kochi origin, same mill discipline.

Hardwood runners and sawn sections

Jackwood at ₹400/cft (¼-inch minus tolerance) and rubberwood runners cut to the sizes your jig demands. Most Noida packing-case makers buy a sheet-plus-runner combo from us — it cuts their inward GST friction and consolidates freight.

Container flooring and pallet decks

For 20ft and 40ft container retrofits and for pallet manufacturers selling into the Dadri 3PL clusters, we cut 18-25mm panels to standard footprints (1200x1000, 1200x800, 1100x1100, 1219x1016 GMA).

Kochi to Noida — the logistics reality

The lane is roughly 2,400 km by road. Cochin Port to Noida via NH-44 / NH-48, the typical truck gets in at day 5-6 on a single-driver run, day 4 on a relay. We ship in 20ft and 32ft full-truck loads ex-factory Perumbavoor — GST and freight are extra of the sheet rate.

Lane elementDetail
OriginPerumbavoor, Ernakulam (Kerala)
DestinationNoida / Greater Noida / Dadri
Distance~2,400 km road
Transit5-7 days FTL
ModeRoad FTL preferred; rail rake on volume
Per-truck capacity~22-24 MT or ~600-700 sheets of 12mm
Detention buffer2 days at unloading; we plan for it

For repeat buyers placing monthly orders, we hold inward stock-rotation discipline so you can pull two trucks a month on a steady cadence rather than scrambling spot. For exporters on a tighter clock, we can pre-cut to your CAD-marked sizes at the Perumbavoor end and load directly to your strapping line.

Which grade for which Noida load — decision rules

A simple operator's heuristic for Noida buyers choosing between our three main grades:

  • Domestic transit, inland consignee, short shelf-life crate — MR-Packing commercial ply, 9mm or 12mm. Cheapest sq.ft. that holds nails.
  • Export to Gulf / SE Asia / Europe, sea freight, ISPM-15 stamped — Same MR-Packing core, but specify heat-treated runners and full ISPM-15 marking. Most Noida electronics CKDs land here.
  • Premium-face export crate (pharma, defence, instrumentation) — Okoume face on MR-Packing or BWR core. Higher cost, but the consignee sees a clean light-cream face.
  • Heavy machinery skid, multi-leg ocean+inland transit — 18mm BWR or shuttering ply for the deck, hardwood bearers underneath. Worth the upcharge when the load is one-of-one.
  • Pallet top-decks, repeat use — 12mm MR-Packing for single-use export pallets; 15-18mm BWR for returnable / 3PL fleet pallets.

FAQ from Noida buyers

Do you cover ISPM-15 stamping?

The plywood we supply is engineered and exempt from ISPM-15 — only the solid-wood runners need heat-treatment and the IPPC stamp. We can supply heat-treated jackwood and rubberwood runners pre-stamped by an authorised agency, so your finished crate ships without customs friction.

Minimum order quantity for the Noida lane?

For a viable single-trip FTL, the practical minimum is roughly one full truck — about 600-700 sheets of 12mm or equivalent mix. For smaller volumes we can consolidate with another Noida-bound load if timing aligns; ask when you enquire.

Can you cut sheets to a specific size before dispatch?

Yes. Our standard cuts are 8x4, 7x4, 6x4 ft. We also do CAD-marked cuts in-house for repeat orders, which saves you the cutting waste and the dust at your end. Send us your cut-list with the enquiry.

Get a quote for your Noida lane

If you're packing mobiles in Sector 81, crating auto components out of NSEZ, building pallets for the Dadri 3PL hubs, or fabricating skids for the YEIDA infra build, we'd like to quote your next month's plywood. Send us the thickness, sheet size, monthly volume and delivery sector — we'll come back with an ex-factory Perumbavoor rate plus a realistic landed cost to Noida. Request a quote or browse the resource hub for grade specs, crate detail drawings and the ISPM-15 quick reference.

title: Plywood Supply to Agra | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, made-to-spec crates and pallets that box, brace and palletise Agra's export freight. Manufactured in Perumbavoor by.
html:
Agra is one of those cities where the freight bill writes itself: the world's second-largest footwear cluster, a leather goods belt that exports to Europe and the GCC, and a marble inlay handicraft trade that ships fragile, high-value stone to buyers across continents. Every one of those flows ends the same way — boxed, crated, palletised, and lashed inside a container. That is the part of Agra's economy we sit inside. This post lays out what the Cochin Wood group ships to Agra, why, and how the Kochi-to-Agra lane actually works.

The Agra industrial picture

Agra district carries roughly four packaging-relevant clusters worth naming. Get the cluster right and the plywood spec almost picks itself.

Footwear belt (Hing ki Mandi, Sikandra, EPIP Shastripuram)

Agra accounts for an outsized share of India's footwear output — the city is widely cited as the second-largest footwear hub in the country after Chennai. Production runs from the dense Hing ki Mandi cluster through Sikandra industrial area and the EPIP (Export Promotion Industrial Park) at Shastripuram. Larger exporters operate in and around the Agra–Mathura belt, with finishing and packing happening in the city. The packing brief here is high-volume, low-individual-weight cartons: shoes go out in inner cartons stacked into master cartons, master cartons stacked onto pallets, pallets stuffed into 20-ft or 40-ft containers bound for JNPT/Nhava Sheva or, for GCC moves, sometimes Mundra.

Leather goods and tanneries (Nunhai, Agra trans-Yamuna)

Adjacent to the footwear belt, leather goods and small-leather manufacturing run their own packing line — wallets, belts, bags. Cartons are smaller and lighter than footwear masters, but volumes per consolidation are large and the buyer is usually the same exporter who needs the bigger footwear pack.

Marble inlay and handicraft (Tajganj, Kinari Bazaar, Fatehabad Road)

The Pietra Dura marble inlay trade is the opposite problem: low units, very high value, very fragile, often awkward shapes. Tabletops, plaques, decorative panels, occasional architectural pieces. This is custom crating work — bespoke wooden cases, internal bracing, sometimes corner protection and shock indicators. Plywood spec here is unforgiving: the case has to hold the piece through a multi-modal journey without flexing.

Engineering and auto-component spillover

Agra's industrial estates also house engineering and auto-ancillary units, and the wider Agra–Mathura–Aligarh axis pulls in foundry and lock-hardware buyers. For these we see standard heavy-engineering crating: thicker ply, framed sides, sometimes ISPM-15 stamped pallets for sea export.

What we ship to Agra

Cochin Wood Industries supplies the Agra buyer base across four product lines. We are a plywood manufacturer working through contract factories under Cochin Wood specifications, with the Cochin Wood group operating in Perumbavoor since 1986 — forty years of manufacturing inside a single tight cluster. The product list:

Packing-grade plywood (commercial / rubberwood)

Our packing range covers 6 mm to 18 mm in standard 8x4 ft sheets, with intermediate stops at 8, 10, 12, 14, 15, 16 mm. For Agra footwear and leather exporters, the typical pull is 8 mm and 10 mm — light enough to keep the carton tare down, stiff enough to survive stacking. Marble handicraft crating usually steps up to 12 mm or 18 mm for the side panels and base. We hold MR (moisture resistant) as the default for the packing line; for monsoon-window shipments or sea-export crates we move buyers to our BWR grade.

Made-to-spec wooden crates and cases

Bespoke crating is built out of our packing ply plus jackwood or rubberwood runners. Jackwood runs ₹400/cft at quarter-inch-minus tolerance; rubberwood runners price as per size. For the marble inlay trade we build framed cases with internal cleating and corner blocks; for footwear and leather we more often supply flat-pack ply sheets the exporter's in-house carpenters convert to cases on site.

Pallets, including ISPM-15 compliant

Standard four-way entry pallets in 1200x1000, 1200x800 (euro), and custom sizes. ISPM-15 heat-treatment stamping is mandatory for sea-export pallets going to most jurisdictions — we coordinate this through our packing-line suppliers in Kerala and ship pre-stamped where the buyer needs it.

Container flooring

Heavy-duty plywood flooring for container repair and reefer rebuilds — typically 18 mm BWR or marine-grade, hardwood-faced, 28 mm thickness for full container floor replacements. This is a smaller flow into Agra than the packing line but shows up periodically through CHA-linked rebuild yards.

Kochi to Agra logistics

The road distance Kochi/Ernakulam to Agra is roughly 2,300 km via NH 44 and the Mumbai–Agra corridor. Typical road transit for a full truckload is 5 to 7 days door-to-door depending on driver pairing and checkpoint delays through Maharashtra and Madhya Pradesh. Sample lane economics:

ModeTransitTypical use
Full truckload (32-ft / 22-ton)5-7 daysSteady packing-line buyer, repeat dispatch
Part-load (LTL) via Bangalore/Hyderabad hubs8-10 daysTrial orders, small handicraft consignments
Rail via Container Corporation (CONCOR) to Tughlakabad/Dadri6-8 daysLarger consolidated moves, lower freight per cube

For Agra buyers who export through JNPT/Nhava Sheva, we can also route from Kochi directly to Mumbai-area packing-line consolidators and skip the Agra leg entirely — useful for exporters whose Agra unit only does production and ships through a Bhiwandi or Panvel consolidator.

Decision rules — which plywood for which Agra load

A practical mapping for buyers comparing grades:

  • Footwear master cartons stacked on pallets, dry-warehouse to dry-container: our MR packing grade, 8 mm or 10 mm, is the right answer. Okoume is overspec.
  • Leather goods cartons going air-freight or premium-export route: the buyer often asks for Okoume-faced ply because the customer's QA wants a clean blonde face. Quote on request.
  • Marble inlay export crates, sea route, GCC or EU destination: BWR-grade packing ply 12-18 mm with jackwood framing and ISPM-15 pallets. The crate is structural, not cosmetic.
  • Engineering and auto-component crates for sea export: 18 mm BWR for base and floor, 12 mm sides, hardwood runners, ISPM-15 stamped.
  • Shuttering / formwork for an Agra construction project: different conversation entirely — film-faced shuttering ply, separate spec sheet, separate quote.

For every grade, our selling rule is purchase rate plus ₹2.50/sq.ft, ex-factory, GST and transport extra. Indicative MR-grade packing prices today: 8 mm at ₹28, 10 mm at ₹31.50, 12 mm at ₹33.50, 18 mm at ₹42 per sq.ft. Okoume and BWR are quoted separately.

FAQ for Agra buyers

Do you supply directly to Agra or through a North India distributor?

Direct from Kerala. We dispatch from our Perumbavoor cluster on full-truckload basis to the buyer's Agra address — EPIP Shastripuram, Sikandra, Nunhai, Tajganj, or any of the surrounding industrial pockets. No North India distributor layer.

Can you supply ISPM-15 stamped pallets and crates ready for sea export?

Yes. We coordinate heat-treatment and ISPM-15 stamping through our packing-line partners. Lead time adds about 2-3 working days to a standard pallet order.

What's the minimum order for a trial dispatch to Agra?

For a road dispatch, the truckload economics improve sharply above 18-20 tons. Below that we can route through part-load networks but the per-sheet freight roughly doubles. For first-time Agra buyers we usually recommend either a full-truck trial or a co-load with another North India order in the same week.

Get a quote for Agra delivery

If you run a packing-line for Agra's footwear, leather, marble handicraft or engineering exporters, send us thickness, monthly quantity, and delivery pin code — we can come back with a landed-Agra price and a dispatch window within one working day. Request a quote for a Kochi-to-Agra plywood, crate, pallet or container-flooring order, or browse our resource hub for spec sheets, BWP/BWR explainer, ISPM-15 crate cost guide, and packing-case plywood reference.

title: Plywood Supply to Ajmer | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates and pallets that move stone, textiles and engineering exports out of the Ajmer–Kishangarh belt. Built in.
html:
Ajmer sits in a peculiar industrial sweet spot. Half an hour south-west, Kishangarh runs the world's largest marble market, slabs and blocks moving by the thousands of tonnes every week to ports in Mundra, Pipavav and Nhava Sheva. Half an hour north-east, Beawar and Bhilwara feed one of north India's dense textile and processed-mineral belts. Both ends of that lane need the same thing in volume: dependable packing-grade plywood for export crates, pallets and container floors that don't fail in transit. This is a practical look at how the Cochin Wood group ships into Ajmer, what we make, and how the Kochi-to-Rajasthan logistics actually work.

The Ajmer industrial picture

Ajmer district's manufacturing footprint is split across three concentrations, each with a different appetite for wood-based packaging:

  • Kishangarh marble cluster — over a thousand processing units along the NH-448 corridor and the Kishangarh Marble Association belt. Slab and block exports run to the GCC, Europe and the United States. Each container load typically needs heavy-duty wooden crates or A-frame stillages plus container-floor protection.
  • RIICO industrial areas at Palra, Parbatpura and Kishangarh-Madanganj — mixed engineering, mineral processing, packaging units and ancillary fabrication. Mid-weight crates and ISPM-15 export pallets are the steady draw.
  • Beawar–Bhilwara textile & cement axis (just beyond the district) — synthetic yarn, denim, processed fabric and cement clinker movement that pulls pallets and shrink-stacked bundles. Light to medium-grade ply.

Add the granite and sandstone trade flowing through Makrana and the wider Rajputana belt, and you have a region whose unit economics reward plywood that is dimensionally honest and consistently glued — because rework at a Mundra warehouse is more expensive than the crate itself.

What we ship to Ajmer

Packing-grade plywood

Our MR packing-grade plywood is the workhorse for Kishangarh marble crates and Palra-area engineering exports. Sizes are 8×4 feet standard; thicknesses we ship most often into Rajasthan are 12 mm, 16 mm and 18 mm for crate walls, with 8 mm and 10 mm used for lids, partition dividers and inner blocking. The boards are hot-pressed under the Cochin Wood group's specification set — uniform density, low core gaps, glue line that holds through the dry heat of a Rajasthan summer and the cold of a winter sea leg.

Marble export crates

For Kishangarh's slab and block exporters we supply ply cut to crate-kit dimensions so local fabricators or the buyer's own carpentry shop can assemble fast. The typical brief is a heavy-duty open crate or skid base with cross-bracing, capable of carrying 1.5–4 tonnes of polished slab per unit without flex. 18 mm board is the usual call for base and bracing, 12 mm for side panels.

Wooden pallets

Standard sizes we make to order:

  • 1200 × 1000 mm (Euro-adjacent, common for marble tile cartons)
  • 1200 × 800 mm (Euro standard, for textile and processed-goods exports)
  • 1100 × 1100 mm (Asian standard, for GCC-bound containers)
  • Custom footprints for stone slab A-frames and heavy machinery skids

Pallets can be supplied ISPM-15 heat-treated and stamped for export use — non-negotiable for any container heading out of Mundra or Pipavav to Europe, the US or Australia.

Container flooring ply

For exporters loading dense, point-load cargo (marble blocks, granite tiles stacked on dunnage, heavy castings), 18 mm container-floor ply spreads the load and protects the container floor from puncture damage and rejection fines. We cut to standard 20-foot and 40-foot container interior dimensions.

Okoume plywood

Where the end buyer's spec calls for a lighter, cleaner-finish export crate — typical for high-value textile machinery, electronics or anything where customs inspection happens repeatedly and the crate has to look the part — Okoume face ply is the right call. Light weight, smooth surface, takes stencilling and shipping marks cleanly.

Kochi-to-Ajmer logistics

The route from our Perumbavoor dispatch yards to Ajmer is roughly 2,100 km by road, and we run it as a direct trucking lane rather than rail or coastal-port hop. Two practical reasons:

  1. Rajasthan's inbound rail rakes from Kerala are slow and require terminal handling at Jaipur or Ajmer goods yard — that adds 5–8 days versus a direct truck.
  2. Plywood is volume-sensitive, not weight-sensitive. A 32-foot multi-axle full-load truck moves 800–1,000 sheets economically straight from Perumbavoor to a Kishangarh or Ajmer godown.

Typical transit time is 6–8 days door-to-door depending on Maharashtra and Gujarat checkpoints and the monsoon window. The lane runs Perumbavoor → Salem → Bengaluru → Hubli → Pune bypass → Ahmedabad → Udaipur → Ajmer. For smaller orders we consolidate with other Rajasthan deliveries; for full-truck orders we dispatch direct and the customer receives one freight invoice.

Decision rules: which grade for which load

Buyer type in Ajmer regionTypical loadRecommended grade
Marble slab exporter, Kishangarh2–4 tonne export crates, sea freight18 mm MR packing-grade base + 12 mm walls; ISPM-15 pallets
Granite block exporterOpen skids, heavy point loads18 mm packing-grade + container-floor ply
Textile / yarn exporter, Beawar-BhilwaraBale or carton pallets, lighter12 mm packing-grade pallets, ISPM-15
Engineering / electronics exporterClean-finish crates for repeated inspectionOkoume-faced ply
Construction / infra contractorConcrete shuttering, repeat poursShuttering ply (BWP, film-faced where specified)

The two failure modes we see most often when buyers under-spec are: 12 mm used where 18 mm is needed (crate flexes, slab cracks in transit), and non-ISPM pallets sent to an export packer who then has to scramble for replacements at the port.

FAQ

What is the minimum order you ship to Ajmer?

Full-truck loads (32-ft multi-axle, around 800–1,000 sheets depending on thickness) make the freight economics work cleanly. Smaller orders are possible via part-load consolidation, but per-sheet freight rises. For repeat buyers in Kishangarh and Palra we hold a stocking pattern that supports faster top-ups.

Do you supply ISPM-15 stamped pallets?

Yes. Heat-treated, IPPC-stamped pallets are standard for export buyers. Specify destination country at order time so we cut to the right footprint.

Can you cut sheets to crate-kit sizes before dispatch?

Yes — we can pre-cut to a buyer-supplied cutting list. This is common for Kishangarh customers who want their carpentry crew to assemble crates rather than cut from full sheets.

Request a quote

The Cochin Wood group has been operating in Perumbavoor since 1986 — close to forty years of group manufacturing under a packing and export-oriented spec book. CWI ships to Rajasthan regularly and the Ajmer–Kishangarh corridor is one of our active lanes. Tell us the thickness, sheet count, pallet size or crate dimensions you need, and the dispatch month — we'll quote ex-Kochi rates with freight and GST broken out, and a clear transit ETA. Request a quote or browse the resource hub for grade-selection notes, ISPM-15 explainers and the rest of the packing reference set.

title: Plywood Supply to Bhilwara | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, export crates, pallets and shuttering ply for the synthetic-textile mills and carbon-graphite cluster around Bhilwara.
html:
Bhilwara is one of those Indian cities where the demand for industrial packaging is not driven by one or two factories — it is driven by an entire ecosystem. The synthetic textile mills, the suiting and shirting weavers, the polyester and viscose spinners, and a heavy carbon-graphite cluster all generate steady outbound freight, and each of those flows needs different timber and plywood. This post breaks down what the Cochin Wood group ships into the Bhilwara belt, why specific grades match specific loads, and how the Kochi-to-Bhilwara lane actually works week to week.

Why Bhilwara matters for a plywood supplier

Bhilwara is commonly called the Manchester of India for synthetic textiles. The district produces a very large share of the country's polyester-viscose suiting fabric, and the cluster has matured into a complete textile chain — spinning, weaving, processing, garmenting and machinery support. Alongside the textile economy sits HEG's carbon graphite operation at Mandpam, one of the largest graphite electrode plants in the world. The combination means a Bhilwara plywood buyer is not just a "packing case" buyer; they are buying timber for export crates, machinery skids, container floors and shuttering ply for plant expansion — sometimes within the same purchase order.

That mix is exactly the type of demand the Cochin Wood group is set up for. We are a Kerala-based plywood manufacturer with group operations in Perumbavoor since 1986 — about forty years of group manufacturing behind the spec sheet — and we ship pan-India through contract factories operating under CWI specifications. Bhilwara is a long lane from Kochi, but it is a high-value lane because the average crate from this belt is heavier, larger and more export-bound than the national average.

The Bhilwara industrial picture

Industrial areas to know

  • RIICO Industrial Area, Bhilwara — the original textile and processing hub, dense with weaving and dyeing units.
  • RIICO Growth Centre, Hamirgarh — the larger expansion belt south of the city; home to several integrated spinning and weaving plants and the segment most likely to need full container loads of plywood at a time.
  • Mandpam — HEG's graphite electrode complex; specialised heavy-duty crating and skid demand.
  • Gulabpura and Mandalgarh corridors — secondary industrial pockets along NH-79; textile machinery service units and ancillary fabrication.

Sectors we serve in this belt

  • Synthetic yarn and polyester-viscose fabric exporters needing strong, lightweight outer packing.
  • Textile machinery OEMs and service contractors shipping looms, sizing machines, draw-texturising units and spare assemblies.
  • Graphite and refractory-adjacent units requiring heavy-duty timber crates and reinforced skids.
  • Engineering ancillaries, cement and mineral processing units in the wider Bhilwara-Chittorgarh corridor that need shuttering plywood for capex builds and pallets for inbound raw material flow.

What we ship into Bhilwara

Packing-grade plywood

Our core SKU for this belt is commercial / rubberwood packing-grade plywood in the standard thickness range — 6mm, 8mm, 10mm, 12mm, 14mm, 15mm, 16mm and 18mm. For Bhilwara's typical fabric and yarn export box, 8mm and 12mm do most of the work; 12mm walls with 18mm bases is a common build for heavier textile machinery crates. All rates are ex-factory, GST and transport extra, and we maintain a fixed ₹2.50 per square foot margin over our purchase rate so the buyer sees a transparent build-up rather than a black-box price.

Crate and case construction

For full-blown export crates we usually combine packing-grade plywood panels with sawn jackwood or rubberwood runners and battens. Jackwood runs at roughly ₹400 per cubic foot (with the standard quarter-inch-minus tolerance buyers in this segment expect). Heavy textile machinery shipments to the Middle East and Vietnam typically need ISPM-15 compliant softwood framing — we coordinate fumigation stamps and heat-treatment certificates with the crate builder so the cargo is not held at port.

Pallets

The Bhilwara belt uses two pallet families:

  • Two-way and four-way export pallets for outbound yarn and fabric — generally 1200×1000 mm or 1100×1100 mm for GCC and Southeast Asia destinations.
  • Heavy-duty domestic pallets in 1200×800 mm and Indian standard sizes for inbound raw material movement inside the larger mills.

Container flooring

For exporters loading 20ft and 40ft containers directly at the plant, we supply 18mm and 28mm container-floor-grade plywood when the use case calls for a one-trip reinforced floor or a custom loading skid. This is a smaller line item by volume but a high-value one for a polished export operation.

Shuttering plywood

For capex projects — new spinning halls, dyeing additions, warehouse roofs and the periodic civil work around HEG and the textile parks — film-faced shuttering plywood in 12mm and 18mm goes in for slab and wall formwork.

Kochi-to-Bhilwara logistics

Bhilwara is roughly 2,100 km from Kochi by road. The realistic trucking lane runs Kochi (Ernakulam) → Bengaluru → Hyderabad → Nagpur → Indore → Chittorgarh → Bhilwara, or the western variant through Mumbai-Nashik-Udaipur-Bhilwara when the load originates near the port. Transit time for a full truckload (32ft single-axle or multi-axle) is typically 6 to 9 days door-to-door depending on monsoon conditions, RTO checkpoint queues and the buyer's unloading window.

Practical points we manage on this lane:

  • Truck capacity matched to thickness mix. 18mm plywood loads heavier than 8mm at the same square-footage — we plan the loading sheet so the buyer is not overpaying freight on cubed-out trucks.
  • Headroom for monsoon detours. The Konkan and Ghat sections see frequent diversions July to September; we build a 1-2 day headroom into committed delivery dates.
  • Unloading charges quoted upfront. Bhilwara mill yards usually unload by forklift or manual gang; either way the unloading charge sits in the PI so there is no surprise at the gate.
  • Single-stop or split-drop. For buyers in both Bhilwara and Chittorgarh / Bhim / Gulabpura, we can split a truck across two delivery points at a small additional charge rather than forcing a second LCL.

Decision rules: which grade fits which load

Load typeRecommended gradeWhy
Standard yarn / fabric export boxPacking-grade commercial / rubberwoodCost-efficient, strong enough for one-trip export, predictable supply.
Premium / high-finish export to GCC or EUOkoume plywoodCleaner face, lighter weight per panel, better appearance for buyers who inspect the crate exterior.
Heavy textile machinery, graphite-adjacent skids18mm packing-grade with jackwood runnersLoad-bearing combination; the hardwood runner takes the point load, the panel distributes it.
Container floor reinforcement18mm or 28mm packing-grade panelsReplaces or augments the OEM container floor for single-trip heavy export.
Capex civil work — slabs, columns, wallsFilm-faced shuttering ply, 12mm / 18mmReusable, smooth finish, predictable cycle count for the contractor.

FAQ — Bhilwara buyers

Do you supply ISPM-15 compliant crates ready for export?

We supply the plywood and timber components; ISPM-15 stamping is done by certified fumigation partners at or near the buyer's premises. We can coordinate the documentation so the crate is export-ready.

What is the minimum order quantity for a Kochi-to-Bhilwara shipment?

The lane economics start to work cleanly at one full truckload (roughly 600-700 sheets of mixed thickness, depending on the load plan). Smaller quantities are possible via part-load aggregators, but the per-sheet freight component rises sharply.

How do you price Okoume versus standard packing-grade?

Standard packing-grade is on our published EX-factory price card with the fixed ₹2.50 per square foot margin. Okoume is priced separately on request because the face veneer pricing moves with the import market — we quote it order-wise with current landed-cost backing.

Talk to us about a Bhilwara shipment

If your Bhilwara unit is sourcing packing plywood, pallets, crates or shuttering ply and you want a transparent EX-factory rate with a fully costed Kochi-to-Bhilwara delivery plan, request a quote with your thickness mix, monthly volume and delivery pincode. For specification notes on packing-grade construction, ISPM-15 framing and the Okoume vs commercial face decision, our resource hub has the operator-level detail. Group operations in Perumbavoor since 1986, pan-India delivery, GCC and Vietnam export — we will build the loading plan around your dispatch calendar, not the other way around.

title: Plywood Supply to Jaipur | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, export crates and pallets that Jaipur's gem, marble and handicraft exporters ship the world over. Our group has.
html:
Jaipur is one of the few Indian cities where three very different export industries — gemstones, marble and handicrafts — sit inside the same trucking radius and share the same packaging vendors. That overlap is what makes the Pink City interesting for a plywood supplier. A consignment of certified emeralds leaving Sitapura, a slab of Makrana white marble heading to a US showroom, and a block-printed wooden chest bound for a European fair all rely on the same core inputs: stable packing-grade plywood, properly built crates, and pallets that survive multi-modal handling. The Cochin Wood group has been working with packing-case makers and exporters in this kind of mixed-output industrial belt since 1986, and Jaipur's order profile fits the lane well.

The Jaipur industrial picture

Jaipur's export economy is concentrated in a handful of clearly defined belts. Each one generates a different style of crate demand, and that drives our material recommendations.

Sitapura Industrial Area and SEZ

Sitapura, on the southern edge of the city along the Tonk Road, is the heart of Jaipur's gem and jewellery cluster. The Sitapura SEZ and the broader industrial area host cutting and polishing units, certified jewellery exporters, and the freight forwarders that move their high-value, low-volume consignments. The packaging here is small-format but engineering-grade: precision plywood boxes lined with foam, fitted with cushioned inserts, and built to clear customs without rough handling damage.

Bagru, Sanganer and the handicraft belt

Bagru and Sanganer, west and south of the city, are the engines behind Jaipur's block-printed textiles, blue pottery and decorative wood handicraft exports. Crating here runs larger and more variable: furniture pieces, ceramic groups, framed mirrors and assorted home decor lots. Volumes are seasonal, peaking ahead of European and US trade-show cycles. Plywood specifications need to handle ocean transit and warehouse stacking without warping in transit through humid coastal ports.

Marble cluster and the Kishangarh corridor

The Kishangarh marble market sits about 100 km from Jaipur and is one of the largest marble trading hubs in Asia. While the heaviest slab packing happens closer to the quarries, finished marble tile and processed stone export work flows through Jaipur-based forwarders. Marble packing demands the heaviest grade of plywood we ship — boards rated for compression and edge-impact, on pallets sized for slab geometry rather than standard pallet footprints.

Mahindra World City and VKI

Mahindra World City near Ajmer Road and the older Vishwakarma Industrial Area (VKI) on the city's north-western edge bring in engineering goods, auto components and contract-manufacturing exporters. These units crate machine parts, sub-assemblies and tooling for shipment, which tends to use the same packing-grade plywood specifications as our pan-India engineering customers.

What we ship to Jaipur

Jaipur consignments cover most of our packing range. The mix below reflects what the city's industrial belts typically order from a packing-grade supplier.

Packing-grade plywood (MR-Packing)

Our MR-Packing range is the workhorse for crate and case manufacturers. Standard thicknesses move in regular volumes:

ThicknessTypical Jaipur use
6 mmInner partitions, jewellery box linings, light handicraft cases
8 mm / 10 mmMid-weight handicraft and ceramic crates
12 mmGeneral-purpose export crates, engineering goods
15 mm / 16 mmHeavy marble and machinery cases
18 mmContainer flooring, heavy stone crates, pallet decks

Sheet sizes follow standard 8x4 ft formats, with cut-to-size options available on bulk orders. Face options include commercial/rubberwood for the working packing range and Okoume veneer where a cleaner export aesthetic matters.

Built crates and pallets

For exporters who would rather buy a finished crate than build in-house, we supply ISPM-15 compliant wooden crates and pallets fabricated to drawing. Pallet sizes commonly ordered for Jaipur runs include the 1200x800 mm Euro footprint and the 1200x1000 mm standard for North American freight. Marble exporters often need non-standard pallet geometries — long, narrow decks with reinforced bearers — which we build to spec.

Container flooring

18 mm BWR or marine-grade plywood for container floor repair and refurbishment is a recurring item for Jaipur freight stations and reefer operators handling fragile or moisture-sensitive cargo. These ship in container-load lots on the same trucks as packing-grade orders, which keeps logistics economics workable.

Sawn timber for crate bracing

Hardwood runners and bracing timber are part of most full-crate orders. Jackwood and rubberwood sections, cut to the exporter's dimensions, move alongside the plywood on the same consignment.

Kochi to Jaipur — the logistics

Jaipur is roughly 2,000 km from our dispatch points in Kerala. The standard road route runs Kochi to Bengaluru, then up via Mumbai or via the Hyderabad-Nagpur-Indore corridor before joining NH-48 into Jaipur. Full-truckload (FTL) plywood consignments typically run 4 to 6 days door-to-door depending on weather, checkposts and the season. We work with established trucking partners on the Kerala-to-Rajasthan lane, which keeps freight predictable and avoids the surcharge volatility of spot-market trucks.

For large or recurring orders, we can also route via JNPT (Nhava Sheva) coastal shipping to Mundra, then trans-ship by road to Jaipur. This is sometimes economical for container-load volumes during peak monsoon disruptions on the inland route. Either way, our quotes are issued ex-Kochi with GST and freight stated separately, so the buyer can compare landed costs cleanly.

Decision rules — which grade for which job

Jaipur's range of cargo means a single grade rarely fits every order. The rules below match material to application.

  • Use MR-Packing (commercial/rubberwood face) for general export crates, handicraft cases, engineering sub-assembly boxes, and any dry-cargo packaging that does not need a cosmetic outer face. Best price-to-performance for high-volume crate makers.
  • Step up to Okoume veneer where the crate's outer appearance matters — premium jewellery boxes, high-end home decor exports, and buyer-facing display crates. Okoume is a face-veneer choice on the same packing-grade body, not a separate grade.
  • Use BWR or shuttering ply for marble and stone packing where compression loads, humidity exposure during port transit, and edge impact are real risks. The same grade serves container flooring and pallet decks intended for repeated use.
  • Use sawn hardwood for crate bracing, corner posts and skids — never sub-grade pine for heavy export work bound for marine transit.

FAQ

Do you handle ISPM-15 treatment?

Yes. Crates and pallets bound for export are heat-treated and stamped to ISPM-15 standards. We issue the relevant phytosanitary documentation with the consignment so the exporter can clear customs without delay.

What is the minimum order for a Jaipur dispatch?

For ex-Kochi FTL economics to work, we recommend a minimum of one full truck — typically around 800 to 1,000 sheets of standard 8x4 ft packing-grade plywood, depending on thickness. Smaller orders can ride along with consolidated loads when available; ask at the quote stage.

How are prices structured?

All rates are quoted ex-factory Kochi in rupees per square foot, with GST and transport extra. Once thickness, monthly quantity and delivery location at Jaipur are confirmed, we issue a proforma invoice with landed pricing.

Getting a quote

If you run a packing-case unit, freight-forwarding setup, marble export operation or handicraft consolidator in Jaipur or the surrounding industrial belt, we can quote your monthly requirement against a clear specification. Send through the thickness mix, sheet sizes, monthly volume and delivery point, and we will come back with an ex-factory rate, freight to Jaipur and lead time. Request a quote through the contact form, or browse the resource hub for technical specifications on packing-grade plywood, ISPM-15 crates and container flooring before you write the brief.

title: Plywood Supply to Jodhpur | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring that Jodhpur's furniture, handicraft and guar gum exporters ship the world.
html:
Jodhpur is one of the few inland cities in India where a plywood enquiry almost always points to an export shipment. Whether the carton headed for Boranada is full of Sheesham dining sets, antique-replica almirahs, guar gum powder for the food industry, or precision-engineered industrial parts, somewhere in that supply chain a packing-case maker is cutting plywood to size. The Cochin Wood group has been supplying plywood to packing and export-oriented industries since 1986, and Jodhpur sits squarely inside the kind of buyer pattern we know well: bulk, repeat, export-grade, no-surprises.

Why Jodhpur's industrial belt matters for plywood

Jodhpur's manufacturing footprint sits in a few well-defined pockets — Boranada (the largest, around 18 km south-west of the city), Sangariya, Mandore, the Heavy Industrial Area, and the newer extensions along the Pali road. The furniture cluster — solid Sheesham, mango wood, reclaimed wood, antique replica — is what put Jodhpur on the global trade map, and it is still the city's loudest export. Add the guar gum processors (Jodhpur and the surrounding belt account for a significant share of India's guar derivatives exports), the handicrafts ecosystem, and a growing engineering sub-cluster, and you have a city that ships physical goods abroad almost every working day.

Every one of those shipments needs a wooden box, a pallet, or container dunnage. That is where our packing-grade plywood, sawn timber and crate components come in.

Sectors we serve in and around Jodhpur

  • Furniture exporters — heavy solid-wood items going to the US, EU, UK and Australia. Crate panels typically 9–12 mm packing-grade, with 18 mm bases for skid-mounted heavy units.
  • Antique-replica and handicraft exporters — mixed-load LCL shipments where panel consistency and nail-holding matter more than face finish.
  • Guar gum and food-grade powder exporters — bagged product stacked on hardwood pallets, usually moved in 20-ft containers, where pallet structural ply and ISPM-15 heat-treated runners are the recurring spend.
  • Engineering and auto-component exporters — heavier crates, often with shock-absorbing internal framing on a plywood-faced base.
  • Marble and stone processors in the wider Rajasthan belt — A-frame crates, plywood-faced bracing, and dunnage planks.

What we ship to Jodhpur

The Jodhpur enquiry list is fairly predictable once you have shipped a few rounds. Below is the typical material flow.

Packing-grade commercial plywood

Our standard packing-grade ply (rubberwood / commercial hardwood core, MR-bonded) is what most Jodhpur crate makers actually want. It is built for box and case work, not for furniture finishing, which keeps the cost honest. Common Jodhpur orders:

ThicknessTypical use in Jodhpur
6 mmInner partitions, light handicraft boxes
8–10 mmSide and top panels for medium furniture crates
12 mmMain panel for export furniture crates; guar gum slip-sheets
15–16 mmHeavy crate panels, engineering goods
18 mmCrate bases, skids, container dunnage, pallet decks

Sheet size is 8×4 ft standard, but we cut to custom widths for crate makers running standing saws — useful for the antique-replica yards that work to non-standard footprints.

Okoume plywood

For exporters whose buyers specify a cleaner-looking exterior crate — display-piece furniture, premium handicraft, certain EU retailers — Okoume face veneer on the same packing core gives a uniform reddish-brown look without paying for marine-grade material. It is a finish call, not a strength call.

Crates, pallets and container flooring

  • Knock-down crate kits — pre-cut panels, ISPM-15 heat-treated framing, fasteners, shipped flat. Useful when the Jodhpur exporter does not want to run their own ply-cutting line.
  • Two-way and four-way pallets — standard 1200×1000 mm and 1200×800 mm decks for guar gum and bagged-export loads, with 18 mm plywood top decks where required.
  • Container flooring and dunnage — 18 mm boards for floor reinforcement on heavy furniture loads, and rough sawn dunnage for blocking.
  • Sawn hardwood — jackwood and rubberwood runners for crate framing, billed by cft.

Kochi-to-Jodhpur logistics

Jodhpur is about 2,100 km from Kochi by road, and almost everything we send up there moves by full-truck-load. The standard lane runs Kochi → Bengaluru → Hyderabad → Nagpur → Udaipur → Jodhpur, with transit times of roughly 6–8 days for a 32-ft single-axle truck, depending on the season and the driver's planned halts. We also use Kochi → Mumbai (Vashi/Bhiwandi) → Jaipur → Jodhpur when the load needs to consolidate with a Mumbai pickup.

For larger volumes — multi-truck monthly runs that some of the guar gum and furniture exporters consume — we plan loads at the contract factory so a 32-ft truck leaves fully cubed (around 600–700 sheets of 18 mm equivalent, give or take). That keeps the per-sheet landed cost in Jodhpur predictable. EX-factory pricing plus GST plus transport is the rule; we share the freight quote alongside the proforma so there is no last-minute surprise.

For coastal exporters working through Mundra or Pipavav, we can also bridge containerised dispatches from Cochin port — but for inland Jodhpur, road from Kochi is faster and cheaper than any sea+rail combination.

Decision rules: which grade for which Jodhpur load

Default — commercial packing-grade ply

Use this for the vast majority of Jodhpur orders: standard export furniture crates, handicraft boxes, engineering crates, pallet decks for bagged powder exports. It is what the cost-sheet wants.

Step up to Okoume face

When the buyer at the other end has specified an external finish or when the crate doubles as display packaging. Higher cost, better look. Same structural performance as the underlying packing core.

Shuttering ply

Not a packing material — but Jodhpur's construction boom (residential and commercial along the Pali road and the ring road) does generate a parallel demand. We supply film-faced shuttering ply to infrastructure and concrete-frame contractors, separately quoted.

Sawn timber

For runners, bracing, blocking and any structural element of a crate that needs to be solid wood rather than ply. Quoted by cft, separately from the ply roll-up.

FAQ — Jodhpur buyers

Do you supply ISPM-15 compliant packing?

Yes — for exporters who need heat-treated wood, we supply ISPM-15 stamped pallets and crate framing. The plywood panels themselves are exempt from ISPM-15 because they are engineered wood, but the solid-wood components (runners, skids, bracing) must be treated, and we handle that on the dispatch side.

What is the minimum order you accept for a Jodhpur delivery?

We are set up for truck-load economics. A part-load works only if we can consolidate with another Rajasthan-bound shipment in the same week. For one-off small orders we usually recommend buying through a Jaipur stockist rather than paying full freight from Kochi.

Do you handle the freight or do we arrange our own truck?

Either works. We have lane-tested transporters running Kochi-Jodhpur and can include door-delivered freight in the quote, or we can keep it ex-factory and let your Jodhpur logistics team take over at Perumbavoor. Most exporters prefer door-delivered so they can hold a single supplier accountable.

Get a quote for your Jodhpur project

If you run a packing-case yard, a furniture export workshop in Boranada, or a guar gum processing plant looking to lock in a steady pallet and ply supply, the practical next step is a short conversation about thickness, monthly volume and delivery location. Share the spec — we will come back with EX-factory rates, freight to Jodhpur, and a realistic dispatch window. Request a quote, or browse the resource hub for grade explanations, ISPM-15 details, and crate construction notes before you send the enquiry.

title: Plywood Supply to Udaipur | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates and pallets that ride Udaipur's zinc, marble and brass export lines. Our group has built export packaging in.
html:
Udaipur is not a typical plywood market. The buyers who matter here are not furniture workshops or interior contractors — they are zinc smelter packing departments, marble export yards, brass handicraft consolidators and the truck-body builders who feed all three. Each of these segments has a very specific plywood requirement, and most of them care more about edge integrity, moisture behaviour and weight per crate than they do about face cosmetics. That is the niche the Cochin Wood group has served from Kochi since 1986 — and it is exactly why a southern Kerala supplier shows up on Rajasthan packing lines roughly 1,900 km away.

The Udaipur industrial picture

The Udaipur district carries an unusually heavy industrial footprint for a city better known as a tourist destination. The plywood demand we see flowing out of it concentrates in five distinct belts:

  • Chanderiya Lead-Zinc Smelter (Hindustan Zinc) — roughly 13 km from Udaipur city, the largest single zinc smelting complex feeding the world's second-largest zinc producer. Ingot bundles, cathode plates and silver dore boxes all need export-grade wooden packaging.
  • Madri Industrial Area (RIICO) — Sector 1 through 4, packed with auxiliary engineering units, mineral processors and electrical equipment fabricators that crate finished goods for despatch across India and the Gulf.
  • Sukher Industrial Area — mixed engineering and handicraft units that export brass and metalware to the US and EU.
  • Kaladwas and Debari — the zinc-and-chemicals corridor along NH-48 east of the city, including Debari Zinc Smelter and ancillary acid/sulphuric units.
  • The marble belt — Rakhi, Morwad, Sukher and the Kankroli–Rajsamand stretch — Udaipur is the consolidation point for Rajasthan marble heading to Mumbai's JNPT, Mundra and Pipavav for export to the Middle East and East Asia. Marble blocks and slabs ride on heavy-duty wooden bases and side-protected crates.

Layered on top of these is the handicraft sector — silver jewellery, miniature paintings, brass sculptures, marble inlay work — much of it sold through Jaipur and Delhi exporters but crated locally in Udaipur. These are smaller boxes but high-value contents, so the plywood spec matters.

What we ship to Udaipur

Udaipur buyers typically pull four product lines from the Cochin Wood group's range. The fit depends on what is being packed.

Packing-grade plywood (commercial / rubberwood)

The workhorse for crate walls, lids and internal partitions. Common thicknesses we move on the Kochi–Udaipur lane:

ThicknessTypical Udaipur use
6 mmHandicraft inner boxes, marble slab face-protection sheets
8–10 mmLight engineering crates, brass-ware export boxes
12 mmZinc ingot stacking boxes, medium engineering crates
15–16 mmHeavy crate side walls, marble block crates
18 mmCrate bases, container flooring, heavy machinery skids

Sheets ship in standard 8×4 ft, with 8×6 and 7×4 cut sizes available against confirmed orders. All packing-grade is despatched ex-factory; GST and freight are extra. As a rule we work to purchase rate + ₹2.50/sq.ft on the packing range so the costing stays transparent.

Okoume plywood

Okoume is a premium-grade face veneer option for buyers who export to markets that inspect crate quality — Europe, the GCC and parts of East Asia. Where a marble exporter in Sukher or a precision-engineering unit in Madri needs a cleaner face, tighter glue line and lower variance crate-to-crate, Okoume is the right call. It is priced separately from the commercial packing card and quoted on request, typically against monthly volumes.

Pallets and ISPM-15 crates

For Hindustan Zinc despatches and any marble or handicraft container heading abroad, packaging must comply with ISPM-15 — the international heat-treatment standard for wood packaging in export. Standard pallet footprints we build to:

  • 1200 × 800 mm Euro pallet — common for engineering and electronics exports
  • 1200 × 1000 mm — the heavier base used for zinc ingot bundles
  • 1100 × 1100 mm — for ocean-container utilisation on long-haul routes
  • Custom skids — heavy-duty bearers for marble blocks, often 150–200 mm thick sawn timber tops on plywood deck

Container flooring and shuttering ply

Container floor replacement ply (typically 28 mm hardwood) and shuttering plywood for the construction projects feeding Udaipur's hospitality and housing build-out are both supplied on order. Shuttering ply is useful for Sukher and Madri civil works as well as for the steady infrastructure spend around the lakes and approach roads.

Sawn timber

Jackwood runners at ₹400/cft (¼-inch minus tolerance) and rubberwood runners as per size complete the crate bill of materials — bearers, cleats and corner posts that take the structural load while plywood handles the wall span.

Kochi-to-Udaipur logistics

Udaipur sits roughly 1,900 km by road from Kochi. The standard trucking lane runs Kochi → Bangalore → Pune → Ahmedabad → Udaipur on the NH-48 / NH-8 corridor. A full-truck movement is normally 6 to 8 days door-to-door depending on toll-gate season and Rajasthan border RTO clearance. Material despatches ex-factory from our Perumbavoor base, and we coordinate transport on request — though most repeat buyers prefer to nominate their own transporter once the lane is established.

For partial loads or fast-moving SKUs, container-based movement via Cochin Port → Mundra (sea leg) → Udaipur (road leg of about 750 km) can work out cost-competitive on certain thickness mixes, particularly where the buyer is already pulling other imports through Mundra. It is worth pricing both options on a thickness-and-volume basis before committing.

Decision rules: which grade for which Udaipur load

  • Zinc ingot crates and brass/electrical engineering exports — packing-grade commercial plywood, 12–18 mm depending on box size, ISPM-15 heat treated. The face is not the priority; edge crush resistance and consistent glue line are.
  • Marble block and slab crates heading to JNPT/Mundra — 15–18 mm packing-grade for walls, 18 mm or sawn-timber-on-ply skids for the base. Internal cushioning is the marble exporter's job; our job is the structural cage.
  • Premium handicraft, brass sculpture and silver consignments — Okoume-faced packing ply, 8–12 mm. The cosmetic uplift matters when international customs inspectors open boxes randomly.
  • Civil and infrastructure work in Udaipur city — shuttering plywood, BWR-bonded. Different product family, same Kochi origin.

FAQ

What is the minimum order quantity for Udaipur despatch?

A full truckload (roughly 200–250 sheets depending on thickness mix) keeps freight per sq.ft at a sensible level. Smaller orders are possible but the per-sheet landed cost rises sharply, so we usually recommend consolidating with another local buyer or planning a quarterly intake instead.

Do you handle the ISPM-15 fumigation and stamp?

Yes. Heat-treated, IPPC-stamped material can be supplied for any export-bound crate or pallet. Specify ISPM-15 at the quotation stage so it is built into the line item rather than added as an afterthought.

Can you build the crates in Udaipur instead of shipping flat sheets?

For most segments it is more economical to ship plywood and sawn timber to Udaipur and build crates at the packing line — the cube of empty crates is wasteful on a 1,900 km haul. We supply the boards, runners and ISPM-15-treated bearers cut to size; local fabricators in Madri and Sukher handle assembly.

Request a quote

If you are running a packing line in Madri, Sukher or Chanderiya, or consolidating marble out of the Rakhi–Morwad belt, the fastest way to a workable price is a short note covering thickness, monthly quantity and delivery point. We will come back with a rate card and a logistics option that fits the volume. Start with a request a quote and we will route it to the despatch desk the same working day. For specifications, ISPM-15 detail and crate-design references, the resource hub carries the technical sheets buyers usually ask for before the first PI.

title: Plywood Supply to Kota | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, export crates, hardwood pallets and container flooring that Kota's stone-processing, fertilizer and engineering.
html:
Kota sits on the Chambal river in south-east Rajasthan, roughly 2,000 km north of Kochi by road. To outsiders it is a coaching-class town. To anyone who actually loads trucks for a living, it is one of the densest industrial pockets between Delhi and Ahmedabad — Kota stone slabs, urea and DAP fertilizer, thermal-power equipment, precision engineering and a growing cluster of stone-processing exporters. Each of those sectors moves heavy, brittle or moisture-sensitive freight that has to survive 5-7 day pan-India transit or a long sea leg out of Mundra, Pipavav or JNPT. That is where packing-grade plywood, ISPM-15 crates, hardwood pallets and container flooring enter the picture, and where the Cochin Wood group has been quietly servicing buyers for years.

The Kota industrial picture

Kota's manufacturing footprint is spread across a handful of well-defined belts. Anyone shipping plywood into the city tends to know these by name rather than by postcode:

  • RIICO Industrial Area, Indraprastha — the largest organised estate, mixed engineering, fabrication and stone-processing units.
  • Ranpur and Sakatpura belts — older clusters where most of the stone-cutting, polishing and crate-loading yards operate.
  • RIICO Growth Centre, Rangbari — newer plots with larger engineering and ancillary sheds.
  • Gegal–Mandana stretch — the heart of the Kota stone quarrying and slab-processing trade, feeding export crates that head to JNPT and Mundra.
  • Chambal Fertilisers township, Gadepan — about 40 km out, anchors the urea and DAP demand that ripples down to bag-packaging and pallet buyers in the city.
  • Thermal power cluster — the Kota Super Thermal Power Station and the surrounding equipment workshops generate steady demand for crating spares, valves and heavy castings.

That mix — natural stone exporters, fertilizer logistics, power-plant maintenance, precision parts — is the reason Kota's packing-material demand is far less seasonal than it looks from the outside. The coaching economy gets the headlines; the industrial belt pays the freight bills.

What we ship to Kota

Most enquiries from the Hadoti region fall into four buckets. We size and quote each one differently.

Packing-grade plywood (the bulk of what moves north)

Commercial and rubberwood-core packing ply, ex-factory Kochi, is the default for box-makers, crate yards and in-house packing departments. Typical thickness picked by Kota buyers:

  • 6 mm and 8 mm — light component boxes, electronics, instrumentation crates.
  • 10–12 mm — general engineering crates and the side-walls of stone slab cases.
  • 15–18 mm — base panels for heavy crates, valves, transformer parts and the floor-grade load-spreaders under stone slab consignments.

All rates are ex-factory Kochi; GST and freight extra. We hold to a transparent purchase-plus-margin model and quote against a confirmed thickness, size and monthly quantity rather than a generic list price.

ISPM-15 export crates

Kota stone exporters and any unit shipping engineering goods out of Mundra, Pipavav, JNPT or Hazira need ISPM-15 heat-treated, marked timber for the crate frame. We supply the sawn hardwood runners (jackwood and rubberwood) and the packing-ply skin as a coordinated kit, so the local crate yard is not stitching together two different supply chains.

Hardwood pallets

Standard sizes for Kota buyers tend to cluster around 1200×1000 mm and 1100×1100 mm for fertilizer-bag stacking, and 1200×800 mm euro-style for engineering exporters. We can ship knock-down pallet kits — runners, deck boards, blocks — that are nailed locally, which keeps the freight bill sane on a 2,000 km lane.

Container flooring
