For exporters loading 20-foot or 40-foot containers with stone slabs or heavy castings, an 18 mm packing-ply floor laid over the steel deck protects both the cargo and the container's resale value. We supply cut-to-size sheets in 1220×2440 mm or made-to-order panel sizes.

Kochi to Kota logistics

The lane from our Perumbavoor / Ernakulam factories to Kota is a straight north-bound trucking move. There is no realistic coastal short-cut — Kota is landlocked, and the nearest container ports (Mundra and Pipavav in Gujarat) are themselves 900 km further west. Practical numbers:

ParameterTypical
Road distance, Ernakulam to Kota~2,000 km
Transit time, single-driver 32 ft / 22 ft truck5–7 days
Route corridorNH 544 / NH 48 via Salem, Bengaluru, Pune, Ahmedabad, Udaipur, Chittorgarh, Kota
Practical truck sizes22 ft (8–9 MT), 32 ft single-axle (15–16 MT), 32 ft multi-axle (21 MT)
Unloading pointBuyer's yard in RIICO Indraprastha, Ranpur, Rangbari or Gegal–Mandana

For volumes below a full truck we consolidate with other Rajasthan and northern-belt orders; for full truckloads we lock the freight with our trucking partners on the Kochi–Mumbai–Ahmedabad–Kota route. Either way we share the loading plan, headroom and any unloading charges up front, so there are no surprises at the gate.

Decision rules: packing grade vs Okoume vs shuttering

Three grades cover almost every Kota requirement. Picking the right one matters more than picking the cheapest.

  1. Commercial / rubberwood packing-grade plywood — the default for crates, pallets, container floors and most domestic-bound packaging. Strong enough for the loads, sensibly priced for high-volume box makers.
  2. Okoume-faced plywood — recommended where the customer's end client wants a cleaner export look (premium stone consignments, engineering exports to GCC and Europe) or where moisture exposure on the sea leg is a concern. Quoted on request; not a list-price item.
  3. Shuttering / film-faced plywood — relevant for construction and infrastructure buyers in and around Kota, including thermal-plant civil work and the steady stream of commercial real estate in the coaching-college belt. Not the right grade for packing crates.

Most Kota first-time enquiries start in bucket 1 and a fraction graduate to bucket 2 once the exporter's foreign buyer asks for a better-looking crate.

FAQ for Kota buyers

How quickly can a first order land in Kota?

From a confirmed PI and advance, dispatch from Kochi is usually within 3–5 working days and transit is 5–7 days, so plan for roughly 10–12 days door-to-yard for a fresh buyer. Repeat orders compress to about a week.

Do you supply ISPM-15 marked timber for stone exports?

Yes. Heat-treated, ISPM-15 stamped sawn hardwood runners are supplied alongside the packing-ply skin so the crate yard in Ranpur or Gegal has a single point of accountability for both materials.

What is the minimum order size that makes Kochi-to-Kota freight economical?

A 22 ft truck (around 8–9 MT of plywood and timber) is the smallest practical unit. Below that we either consolidate with other northern-belt orders or quote part-load freight, which materially changes the landed cost.

Request a quote

If you run a packing-case yard in Ranpur, a stone-export operation in Gegal, a fertilizer-bag packaging line near Gadepan, or an engineering shed in RIICO Indraprastha, we are ready to quote against a specific thickness, size and monthly volume. Share your requirement and a delivery address and we will revert with an ex-factory rate, freight to Kota, transit window and a draft PI. The fastest path is to request a quote with thickness, sheet size and monthly off-take. For grade comparisons and packing-specification background, the resource hub has the technical sheets and crate-construction notes our existing northern-belt buyers refer to. The Cochin Wood group has been operating out of Perumbavoor since 1986 — roughly forty years of plywood and packing-material manufacturing — and the Kota lane is one we work regularly.

title: Plywood Supply to Gwalior | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, Okoume-faced ply, crates and pallets for shippers across the Gwalior division. Our group has produced plywood in.
html:
Gwalior sits at an unusual junction in central India. It is a stone city, a paper city, and a quiet engineering city all at once — and each of those industries has a packaging problem that ends with the same question: where do we get reliable packing-grade plywood, crates and pallets without the lead time killing the shipment? If you ship sandstone slabs out of Bilaua, jumbo paper reels out of the ITC Mill at Bhadrauli, or fabricated parts out of Malanpur, you already know what we mean. This guide walks through what flows into the city from our Kochi dispatch yard, why operators choose us over a local sawmill on certain SKUs, and how the Kochi-Gwalior lane actually behaves.

The Gwalior industrial picture

Gwalior is the administrative and logistics anchor for a roughly 50-km industrial corridor that runs north toward Malanpur and south toward Dabra. The packaging demand here is not one block — it splits into three very different buyer profiles, each with its own crate logic.

Malanpur industrial area (AKVN / MPIDC)

Malanpur, about 40 km north of Gwalior on NH-44, is the largest organised manufacturing belt in the region. The estate hosts FMCG, food processing, dairy, glass containers, auto-ancillary fabrication and a cluster of engineering units. The plywood flow into Malanpur is mostly export crates for engineering goods, heat-treated pallets to ISPM-15 spec for outbound containers, and bulk-form packing ply for in-house carpentry shops that build their own one-off crates.

Stone belt — Bilaua, Mehgaon, Karahiya

The Bilaua-Karahiya sandstone belt south-east of Gwalior is one of India's larger dimensional-stone clusters, feeding both domestic infrastructure and export markets (GCC, EU, US). Stone exporters need very specific crate construction — heavy-duty wooden bases, vertical bracing, and packing ply skins that survive a 30-40 day sea voyage with a dense, brittle load that shifts hard if the crate flexes. Mistakes here are expensive; a cracked slab is a write-off.

ITC Bhadrachalam / paper roll converters

Paper roll packaging — both for the ITC mill ecosystem and for the secondary converter and printer cluster around Gwalior — needs wide, flat plywood sheets for end-caps on jumbo reels and for laminated roll cradles on truck beds. Specs are tighter than crate work: end-caps must sit flush against the reel face without warping, which rules out lower-density commercial plywood.

Handlooms and Chanderi adjacency

Gwalior division also acts as a forwarding hub for Chanderi and Maheshwar handloom exports. Volumes are smaller, but premium textile shippers ask for clean, sanded, non-staining packing ply for inner cases — a useful niche where our Okoume-faced packing grade earns its premium.

What we ship into Gwalior

Most loads heading to Gwalior from our Kochi yard fall into one of four product groups. We have summarised the typical specs and use-cases below.

ProductTypical specBest use in Gwalior
Packing-grade plywood (MR / commercial)6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16, 18 mm; 8x4 ft sheets; hardwood/rubberwood coreStone crate skins, in-house carpentry feed, general export crating at Malanpur
Okoume-faced packing ply9-18 mm; light, uniform face, clean grainPremium engineering exports, FMCG display crates, textile inner cases
BWR / shuttering plywood12-18 mm; phenolic film face; reusableConcrete formwork on Gwalior smart-city and Malanpur civil works
Pre-built crates, pallets, container flooringBuilt to drawing; ISPM-15 HT stamp on requestStone exporters, engineering exporters, container line repairs

Crate construction we ship

For stone exporters we build crates with full-thickness 18 mm packing ply skins on hardwood frames, with diagonal bracing inside; for engineering goods we use a lighter 12 mm or 14 mm skin with a four-way entry pallet base. Pallet sizes are typically 1200 x 800 mm (Euro), 1200 x 1000 mm (standard) or made-to-order for container optimisation. Container flooring goes out as 28 mm phenolic-bonded panels in 2440 x 1220 mm sheets for ISO container repair shops.

ISPM-15 and the export crate question

Anything leaving India in a sealed container with wood packaging needs ISPM-15 heat treatment with the IPPC stamp. We can supply pre-treated and stamped crates and pallets ex-Kochi, or supply the raw packing ply and let your local crate builder in Malanpur do the assembly and HT treatment locally — whichever maths is better for your lane.

Kochi to Gwalior logistics

The Kochi-Gwalior road lane is roughly 2,000 km. Truck transit is typically 5-7 days door-to-door depending on whether you take a single full-truck-load (FTL) or share space on a part-load. Most of our Gwalior dispatches move FTL on 32-ft single-axle or multi-axle trailers, routed Kochi → Bengaluru → Hyderabad → Nagpur → Bhopal → Gwalior on NH-44 for the northern stretch. A 32-ft trailer carries roughly 18-22 tonnes of packing plywood depending on thickness mix — enough for a meaningful month's stock for a mid-size stone exporter or a Malanpur engineering shop.

For very large planned dispatches we sometimes route via rail or via the Mundra/Nhava Sheva coastal-then-road combination, but for most Gwalior customers the direct road FTL out of Kochi is the simplest and most predictable option. We quote ex-factory Kochi and ex-Gwalior delivered, so you can compare against your local options on a like-for-like basis.

Decision rules — which product, when

A few rules of thumb we share with first-time Gwalior buyers:

  • Stone crate skins, domestic moves: 12-14 mm commercial packing ply. Cost-controlled, holds up well on short and medium hauls.
  • Stone export crates, sea freight: 16-18 mm packing ply skins, full hardwood frame, ISPM-15 stamped. Do not under-spec the skin — slab cracks cost ten times the saving.
  • Engineering export crates from Malanpur: 12 mm Okoume-faced ply if the buyer cares about appearance; 12 mm commercial packing ply if not.
  • Paper reel end-caps: 18 mm flat, low-warp packing ply or Okoume-faced for the cleanest face.
  • Civil and infra formwork (smart city, road overbridges): 12 or 18 mm phenolic-film BWR shuttering ply. Reusable 20+ pours when handled properly.
  • Textile inner cases: Okoume-faced, non-staining, sanded — small premium worth paying.

FAQ — Gwalior buyer questions

Do you sell through Gwalior dealers or only direct?

Both. We supply direct ex-Kochi to industrial buyers in the Gwalior, Malanpur and Bilaua belts, and we also work with stockists in Madhya Pradesh who carry our packing range. For a one-off crate run, direct usually makes sense; for ongoing buffer stock, a local dealer relationship is often easier.

What is the minimum order size you'll send to Gwalior?

For ex-Kochi dispatch the economics start to work above roughly 400-500 sheets (a comfortable FTL share). Below that we either consolidate with another Gwalior-bound load or quote part-load via a transport aggregator — both work, but FTL is the cleanest.

Can you supply ISPM-15 stamped crates directly?

Yes. We can supply heat-treated, IPPC-stamped crates and pallets built to your drawing, dispatched ready-to-use. Alternatively we supply the raw packing ply and your local crate builder handles assembly and the HT stamp in Gwalior. We are happy to quote both options so you can compare.

Background — who we are

The Cochin Wood group has been operating out of Perumbavoor, Kerala since 1986 — roughly 40 years of group manufacturing in plywood and allied wood products. Cochin Wood Industries Pvt Ltd is the more recent corporate entity inside that group, running packing-grade plywood, Okoume, shuttering ply, sawn timber, crates, pallets and container flooring through contract factories operating to CWI specifications. We dispatch pan-India and to GCC and Vietnam.

Request a quote

If you ship out of the Malanpur estate, the Bilaua stone belt, or anywhere in the wider Gwalior division and you'd like to compare a Kochi-dispatched packing-ply or crate quote against your current source, the fastest way is to request a quote with your thicknesses, sizes, monthly volume and delivery point. Plans, drawings or a photo of your existing crate help us quote accurately on the first pass. You can also browse our resource hub for ISPM-15 notes, packing-grade specs and crate construction guides before you reach out.

title: Plywood Supply to Indore | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, export crates, pallets and container flooring that move heavy engineering and pharma cargo out of the.
html:
Why Indore matters to a Kochi plywood mill

Indore is the commercial spine of Madhya Pradesh and the staging post for one of central India's busiest industrial corridors. The city itself handles head offices, finance and distribution; the heavy work happens 25-30 km south-west at Pithampur, where auto and pharma plants run shift patterns that demand serious volumes of export-quality packing material. For a Kochi-based plywood operation like ours, Indore is a steady, technically demanding lane: orders skew toward heavy-engineering crates, pharma export cases that have to clear ISPM-15, and pallet stock for repeat use inside large plants. This post lays out what we actually ship into the Indore-Pithampur belt, the grades and sizes that make sense, and the road economics that govern the price you see on a quote.

The Indore industrial picture

The demand profile in and around Indore breaks into a few clear segments, and each one drives slightly different plywood and crating choices.

Pithampur auto and engineering belt

Pithampur Industrial Area is split into Sector 1, Sector 2 and Sector 3, plus the newer SEZ phase. It hosts heavy-vehicle, tractor, off-highway and tier-one component plants — the kind of operations that crate transmission housings, axle assemblies, gearboxes and pressed-steel sub-assemblies for both domestic dispatch and CKD export. These loads are dense and sharp-cornered, so the crate has to be engineered around weight rather than volume: thicker bottoms, internal bracing, screwed-and-cleated panels rather than nailed corrugated.

Pharma cluster (Pithampur and Sanwer Road)

Indore's pharma footprint is large — formulations plants and API units around Pithampur and the Sanwer Road industrial belt — and almost every export carton eventually rides inside a wooden master crate or on a heat-treated pallet. ISPM-15 compliance is non-negotiable for those flows, so the wood has to come with traceable heat-treatment marks and the moisture content has to be controlled.

Food, FMCG and SEZ flows

The Crystal IT Park and the SEZ at Pithampur add a steady trickle of electronics, IT-hardware and processed-food export packing. These cartons want clean, splinter-free internal surfaces and lighter pallets — a slightly different spec sheet from the heavy-engineering side.

What we ship into Indore

The mix that goes onto trucks bound for the Indore-Pithampur belt is fairly consistent. We supply finished wooden crates and pallets built to drawing, raw packing-grade plywood for buyers who run their own carpentry shops, container flooring sheets, and sawn-timber runners and battens where the customer wants to do their own assembly.

Packing-grade plywood — sizes and thicknesses

Our packing range is built around the standard 8 ft x 4 ft sheet, with the thicknesses Indore buyers ask for most often listed below. Rates are ex-factory Perumbavoor; GST and transport extra.

ThicknessTypical use in Indore loadsEx-factory rate (₹/sq.ft)
6 mmLight cartons, internal partitions, pharma master cases24.00
8 mmFMCG and electronics export crates28.00
10 mmMedium-weight component crates31.50
12 mmAuto-component crates, pallet decks33.50
15 mmHeavy-engineering crate panels39.00
18 mmContainer flooring, heavy crate bottoms, machine bases42.00

Crate construction

For Pithampur-bound crates we typically build a hardwood frame of 50x50 mm or 75x75 mm runners, sheath the sides in 10-12 mm packing ply, and use 15-18 mm on the floor and load-bearing top. Corners are cleated and screwed. Where the cargo is export-bound, we use heat-treated timber stamped with the IPPC mark so the consignment clears phytosanitary checks at the destination port without delay.

Pallets

The two pallet footprints we run most often into the Indore belt are the Indian 1200 x 1000 mm and the Euro 1200 x 800 mm, both four-way entry. Deck boards are 12-15 mm packing ply or sawn jackwood; runners are 75 mm hardwood. Heat treatment is offered as a standard option.

Container flooring

Plants doing CKD or part-load exports out of Indore occasionally need replacement container-flooring sheets — typically 18 mm full sheets, hardwood face, sized to fit standard 20-ft and 40-ft container floor plans. We supply those in batches alongside the regular packing stock.

Kochi to Indore — the lane

The straight-line road distance from our Perumbavoor base to Indore is roughly 1,700 km. Trucks typically run via Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Nagpur and then north-west through Khandwa into Indore, or alternatively up the west coast through Pune and Dhule. Either route is a 4-5 day transit for full truck loads, depending on the season and the driver pair.

For full-truck quantities the lane is competitive on road — we move 32-ft single-axle and multi-axle trailers carrying anywhere from 20 to 28 tonnes of finished crates or raw plywood per trip. For part loads, consolidating with other northbound cargo through the Bengaluru or Hyderabad hubs usually works out cheaper than booking a dedicated vehicle. Where the consignment is genuinely small, parcel-truck operators on the Kochi-Indore route quote per quintal and run weekly.

Lead time, door to door, is usually 5-7 days from PO confirmation for stocked specs and 10-12 days where the crates have to be built to drawing.

Which grade for which Indore load

Our standard MR-Packing grade

This is the right call for the bulk of Indore's auto-component and pharma export crating. It's a phenol-bonded packing-grade plywood with a hardwood core, dimensionally stable, and priced to take the abuse that crating timber sees in a working warehouse.

Okoume face on packing or BWR

Where the buyer wants a cleaner face — pharma exports going to GCC or European customers, or high-end machinery crates where appearance matters — we offer Okoume as a face-veneer option on the packing and BWR grades. It is not its own grade; it is a face choice. Pricing is on request because the veneer market moves.

Shuttering ply

For Indore buyers in the construction-formwork space — metro extensions, real-estate towers, infrastructure projects around the AB Road and the Super Corridor — we supply 12 mm film-faced shuttering ply rated for 8-10 pours. It is a different product line from packing ply and is quoted separately.

FAQ

Do you supply heat-treated pallets with IPPC marks?

Yes. Heat treatment to ISPM-15 standard with the IPPC stamp is a standard option on both crates and pallets. The certificate accompanies the dispatch documents.

What is the minimum order quantity for Indore?

For raw packing-grade plywood, a single truckload (roughly 600-800 sheets depending on thickness) is the most economical unit. For finished crates and pallets we quote from 50 units upward, with sharper rates above 200.

How are rates quoted?

All rates are ex-factory Perumbavoor unless otherwise stated. GST and road freight to Indore are extra and shown as separate lines on the proforma so the buyer can see the breakdown clearly.

Get a quote for your Indore requirement

If you run a plant, packing line or export operation in Indore, Pithampur or anywhere along the central MP belt and you want a working quote on packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets or container flooring, send us your thickness, size, monthly volume and delivery pin code. We will turn around a proforma with the ex-factory rate, the heat-treatment option, and an honest transit estimate on the Kochi-Indore lane. Start at our request a quote page, or browse specs, grade notes and packing guides in the resource hub before you call. The Cochin Wood group has been running plywood operations out of Perumbavoor since 1986 — forty years of building the kind of crating stock that survives a 1,700 km road haul.

title: Plywood Supply to Bhopal | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, sea-worthy crates, sawn-timber framing and pallets that central India's transformer, switchgear and.
html:
Bhopal sits at one of the heaviest engineering and rolling-stock junctions in central India. A factory that builds a 200-tonne transformer body in BHEL's Piplani complex doesn't ship it in cardboard, and a railway bogie frame coming out of Nishatpura doesn't survive the trip to Mumbai port on a soft pallet. That is why plywood — specifically heavy-duty packing-grade plywood, ISPM-15 crating timber, four-way pallets and container flooring — flows into the Bhopal industrial belt in volumes most outsiders underestimate. We supply that material out of Kochi to fabricators, packing-case workshops and exporters across the Madhya Pradesh belt, and the brief below covers what we ship, how the lane works, and how to decide between grades.

The Bhopal industrial picture

The packing-plywood demand in and around Bhopal clusters in four pockets, and each one has a slightly different load profile:

  • BHEL Bhopal (Piplani / Govindpura). Heavy electrical equipment — power and traction transformers, switchgear, hydro and turbo-generator components, traction motors. Loads here run from a few hundred kilograms up to multi-tonne single pieces that go out as bolted-down sea-worthy crates with internal cradles and desiccant pockets.
  • Govindpura Industrial Estate. One of MPIDC's oldest estates, mixed engineering — auto components, capital-goods sub-assemblies, electrical panels, cable drums. Mid-weight crating, recurring pallet demand and a steady appetite for packing-grade ply at 12 mm and 18 mm.
  • Mandideep Industrial Area. Roughly 23 km south of Bhopal on NH-46, anchored by HEG (graphite electrodes), Eicher-related units, Lupin, Procter & Gamble and Crompton Greaves. Heavy export crates for graphite electrodes, white-goods cartons over wooden pallets, and a healthy run of CP-grade ply for tier-2 assemblers.
  • Modern Coach Factory (Raibareli is in UP, but coach-related sub-assemblies are made out of Nishatpura, Bhopal) and defence supply chains. Railway coach interior fit-out parts, defence vehicle sub-assemblies and ordnance support equipment all move in bolted, ISPM-15 stamped wooden cases.

The common thread: heavy point loads, long inland rail/road moves to Mundra, JNPT or Chennai, and a non-negotiable need for ISPM-15 compliance on anything going overseas. That puts plywood squarely in the bill of materials — not as a finish material but as a structural skin on engineered crates.

What we ship into the Bhopal belt

Most Bhopal-bound dispatches out of our Kochi works fall into four buckets. The Cochin Wood group has been making and supplying packing-grade material out of Perumbavoor since 1986, and that 40+ years of group manufacturing experience shows up most clearly in how the panels are pressed, edge-sealed and stacked for a 1,800 km trip.

Packing-grade plywood (MR-Packing)

Our workhorse for crate skins. Hardwood core, phenolic glue line for moisture tolerance under tarpaulin, sanded both sides, and pressed to hold nailing in the 1.5 mm-2 mm shank range without splitting along the edge. Common Bhopal-bound thicknesses:

ThicknessTypical Bhopal useSheet sizes shipped
6 mmLight crate tops, internal partitions, dunnage liners8x4, 7x4
8-10 mmMid-weight crate sides for switchgear and auto components8x4, 7x4, 6x4
12 mmThe default for Govindpura and Mandideep general engineering crates8x4, 7x4, 6x4
15-16 mmHeavy transformer side-panels, base sheathing8x4 standard, cut to spec
18 mmBolted crate floors, container internal flooring, heavy-duty pallet decks8x4, custom cuts

BWR-grade plywood with Okoume or Gurjan face

For export-grade crates that sit on a deck during monsoon transhipment or that need a cleaner finish for branded equipment, we offer the BWR grade with Okoume or Gurjan veneer faces. Okoume gives a light, uniform face that takes stencilling well; Gurjan gives a harder, denser face for repeated handling. Both are pressed on the same BWR glue line — the face veneer is the choice, not the grade.

Sawn timber for crate frames

Mostly rubberwood and jackwood scantlings in the 50x50 mm, 75x50 mm and 100x75 mm sections that crate makers buy by the cubic foot. ISPM-15 heat-treated and stamped where the buyer needs export documentation. This is the framing inside which the plywood skins sit.

Pallets and container flooring

Four-way entry pallets in the standard 1200x1000 mm and 1200x800 mm footprints, plus custom decks for non-standard equipment bases. For container flooring on flat-rack or open-top moves out of ICD Mandideep, 18 mm packing ply on hardwood bearers is the typical build.

Kochi-to-Bhopal logistics

The lane is roughly 1,750-1,850 km depending on the drop point inside the industrial belt, and we run it three ways depending on the order size and urgency:

  1. Full-truck road (32-ft SXL or 22-ft container truck). The default for orders of 18-28 tonnes. Transit is typically 5-7 days door-to-door via NH-66 to Bengaluru, NH-44/NH-46 north through Hyderabad and Nagpur, and into Bhopal from the south. Best economics for sheet plywood and pallet stacks because we can load full 8x4 sheets flat without breaking the bundle.
  2. LCL / part-load. For orders below ~10 tonnes we consolidate into a part-load through the Ernakulam-Nagpur-Bhopal corridor. Slower (8-10 days) and per-tonne freight is higher, but for sample lots and trial orders it makes sense.
  3. Containerised rail via Cochin Port / ICD Whitefield. For very large repeat orders we move through ICD Mandideep on rail rakes. Slower booking cycle but per-tonne freight drops meaningfully and the unloading happens inside the industrial estate rather than at a city godown.

Freight quotation always lists FOR-Bhopal (or FOR-Mandideep / FOR-Govindpura) inclusive, unloading separate. We pad the transit estimate with a one-day buffer for monsoon-season ghat closures on the Western Ghats stretch.

Decision rules: which grade for which Bhopal load

A quick rule-of-thumb the procurement desks at the bigger Bhopal packing units already follow:

  • Pick MR-Packing grade when the crate is going on a covered truck, transhipped within India, and used once. This is 70% of Govindpura and Mandideep volume. Lower price, perfectly adequate moisture tolerance under a tarp.
  • Pick BWR with Okoume face when the crate is going overseas (typically through JNPT or Mundra), will sit on a port apron in monsoon, and needs to look presentable when the customer opens it. Heavy electricals out of BHEL Piplani and graphite electrodes out of Mandideep usually take this spec.
  • Pick BWR with Gurjan face when the crate will be reused or repeatedly handled — captive returnable packaging, defence sub-assembly cases, railway sub-assembly cradles.
  • Pick shuttering ply if the buyer is actually a civil contractor at a new plant site, not a packing-case maker. We make this distinction up front because the price points are different and the spec is film-faced, not packing-grade.

Common questions from Bhopal buyers

Do you supply ISPM-15 stamped material direct, or only the plywood?

Both. The plywood itself doesn't need ISPM-15 (it's an engineered product, exempt). The sawn timber framing does, and we supply heat-treated and stamped scantlings on request, with the treatment certificate.

What's the minimum order to make the Kochi-Bhopal lane economical?

For full-truck road, around 18 tonnes of plywood (roughly one 32-ft truck densely loaded). Below that, part-load works but freight per sheet goes up by 15-25%.

Can you cut sheets to size at the Kochi end?

Yes. For repeat sizes we cut to spec before loading — saves on wastage at the Bhopal end and lets a packing unit nail directly without a CNC setup. Charged at a flat per-cut rate, quoted with the PI.

Get a Bhopal-delivered quote

If you're sourcing packing-grade plywood, BWR with Okoume or Gurjan faces, ISPM-15 framing timber or container flooring into Bhopal, Mandideep, Govindpura or any Madhya Pradesh node, send us the spec and we will quote FOR-destination with transit. Share thickness, sheet size, monthly volume and the drop pincode and we'll come back with a working PI and a load plan. Request a quote, or browse the resource hub for grade-selection notes, ISPM-15 detail and crate-construction guides written for buyers who size their own packaging in-house.

title: Plywood Supply to Jabalpur | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, built crates, pallets and container flooring for Jabalpur's ordnance ancillaries, marble processors and.
html:
Why Jabalpur is a serious packing-plywood market

Jabalpur is one of the few Indian cities where defense ordnance, marble processing and heavy engineering all sit inside the same municipal limits. That mix produces a steady, year-round demand for industrial packing material that has very little tolerance for failure. A defense consignment cannot arrive with a split crate. A marble slab cannot ride 1,700 km on a pallet that delaminates the first time it sees a Madhya Pradesh monsoon. The Cochin Wood group has been moving packing-grade plywood, sawn timber crates, pallets and container-grade flooring panels to buyers across this belt for decades, and the Jabalpur lane has its own specific rules. This post walks through what we ship into the city, why we ship those exact specs, and how the Kochi-Jabalpur logistics actually work.

The Jabalpur industrial picture

Most outsiders think of Jabalpur as a defense town, and they are not wrong, but the packaging demand is much wider than that. The picture splits cleanly into three blocks.

Defense and ordnance cluster

The Gun Carriage Factory (now part of the Advanced Weapons and Equipment India Ltd corporate restructuring), Vehicle Factory Jabalpur at Khamaria, Ordnance Factory Khamaria and Grey Iron Foundry are the anchor units. Around them sits a long tail of MSME ancillaries machining components, sub-assemblies and spares for the parent units. All of this output ships out by road or rail in wooden cases built to military packing standards — heavy-duty ply skins, sawn timber framing, ISPM-15 stamps when an export consignment is involved, and very tight dimensional tolerances because the cases often have to fit defined rail-wagon or container footprints.

Marble, limestone and mineral processing

Jabalpur district and the surrounding Katni-Satna belt are major marble and limestone hubs. Processors here cut, polish and crate slabs for buyers in Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Hyderabad and for export through Mundra and Nhava Sheva. Slab crates are a specific animal — they are A-frame or cradle-style, take serious point loads, and need both packing ply for the cradle deck and sawn timber for the structural frame. Marble dust, which Jabalpur produces in huge quantities, is itself shipped in bags on wooden pallets to cement, paint and chemical buyers.

General engineering and the MIDC-style estates

The Richhai, Adhartal and Umaria-Dungaria industrial estates host transformer winding shops, foundries, casting units and electrical equipment makers. Anything heavy, fragile or export-bound from these belts moves in plywood-skinned cases. Add the cable drum work that feeds power-transmission projects across central India, and the demand for 12 mm to 18 mm packing ply is constant.

What we ship into Jabalpur

Demand on this lane skews to heavy, durable packing — not pretty furniture-grade ply. The mix we move most often:

Packing-grade plywood

Commercial and rubberwood-core packing ply in 6 mm, 8 mm, 10 mm, 12 mm, 15 mm and 18 mm thicknesses, standard 8 ft x 4 ft sheets. 12 mm and 15 mm are the bread-and-butter sizes for defense and engineering cases. 18 mm goes into base panels, cradle decks for marble crates and container-floor patches. 6 mm and 8 mm are used as case lids and internal partitions. All boards are pressed for case-making, not for finishing — meaning we optimise for nail-holding, screw retention, panel rigidity and moisture tolerance rather than face cosmetics.

Built crates and cases

For buyers who want to skip the carpentry step, we supply finished crates built to drawing: plywood skins over sawn jackwood or rubberwood framing, corner blocks, base runners, lifting cleats and ISPM-15 heat-treated stamping where the crate is heading for export. Typical sizes range from compact ammunition-grade cases up to engineering-equipment cases over 2 m long.

Pallets and container flooring

Standard four-way pallets in 1200 x 1000 mm and 1100 x 1100 mm, plus custom sizes for marble slab cradles. For container-loading customers, 18 mm and 25 mm plywood floor sheets that absorb point loads and survive forklift traffic.

Sawn timber

Jackwood runners at quarter-inch minus tolerance for case framing, and rubberwood runners cut to buyer specifications. Sawn timber is sold by the cubic foot, ex-factory.

Kochi to Jabalpur — the logistics reality

The road distance from Kochi to Jabalpur runs roughly 1,650 to 1,750 km depending on route. The standard trucking lane goes Kochi - Bangalore - Hyderabad - Nagpur - Jabalpur, and a fully loaded multi-axle truck completes the run in 5 to 7 days door-to-door. For container loads heading to Mumbai or Mundra for export, the economics often work better as Kochi - Mumbai by sea + Mumbai - Jabalpur by road, but for direct factory-delivered orders into Jabalpur, road is the standard.

Three things matter on this lane:

  • Load planning. A 32 ft single-axle takes around 9 tonnes of packing ply; a 22 ft truck around 6 tonnes. We share a loading plan with truck capacity, headroom and unloading charge before despatch so the buyer is not surprised at the gate.
  • Monsoon protection. The central India leg can see heavy rain. Loads are tarped and the buyer-side unloading sequence is briefed in advance.
  • GST and e-way bill. All ex-factory rates exclude GST and transport, and we generate the e-way bill from the Kochi-side dispatch.

Decision rules — which grade fits which Jabalpur load

A quick operator's guide for the city's typical applications:

End useRecommended gradeTypical thickness
Defense ordnance cases (domestic movement)Commercial packing ply12 mm skins, 18 mm base
Defense or engineering export cases (ISPM-15)BWR packing ply + heat-treated framing12-15 mm skins, 18 mm base
Marble slab A-frame cratesCommercial packing ply + jackwood runners18 mm cradle deck
Cable drums (transformer / power projects)Commercial packing ply12-15 mm
Container floor patchingBWR or shuttering ply18-25 mm
Construction shuttering on siteFilm-faced shuttering ply12-18 mm

Okoume face-veneered packing ply is generally over-spec for domestic Jabalpur use; it earns its premium on long-leg sea export and when the buyer wants a clean, uniform face for branding. Most local defense and marble work is served better, and more economically, by the standard MR-Packing range.

FAQ

Do you ship single-truck loads to Jabalpur or only containers?

Both. A typical first order is one 22 ft or 32 ft truck of mixed thicknesses. Larger marble-export buyers and defense ancillaries often build up to a 20 ft container equivalent per dispatch. We size the dispatch to what the buyer can unload in a single shift.

Can you build to a defense or ordnance drawing?

Yes. The Cochin Wood group has been making packing cases to drawing-spec for forty years across defense, engineering and export sectors. Share the drawing, intended consignment weight, transport mode and any ISPM-15 or sealing requirement, and we quote built-crate price per piece.

What are payment terms for a first dispatch to Jabalpur?

First-order standard is advance against proforma invoice, with rates ex-factory and GST + transport extra. Repeat customers move to part-advance, balance-against-LR terms once the relationship is established.

Ready to spec a Jabalpur load?

Share thickness, quantity, the end use and the delivery address inside Jabalpur, and we will come back with a proforma invoice, a load plan and a realistic dispatch date. The Cochin Wood group has been pressing and shipping packing-grade plywood since 1986, and the Kochi-Jabalpur lane is a well-worn route for us. To start a conversation, request a quote with your specs, or browse the resource hub for grade charts, ISPM-15 notes and case-construction guides before you raise the indent.

title: Plywood Supply to Pithampur | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, ready crates, pallets and container flooring for the Pithampur–Indore industrial corridor. Our group has manufactured.
html:
Pithampur — the Detroit of Central India — runs on metal that has to travel. Commercial vehicle chassis, agricultural machinery, diesel engines, and tier-1 castings move out of this Madhya Pradesh industrial belt every day, and almost all of them ride inside a wooden case, on a wooden pallet, or over a plywood-floored container. That is the gap the Cochin Wood group fills. We are a plywood manufacturer based in Kochi, Kerala, operating through contract factories under CWI specifications, and we supply packing-grade plywood, ready crates, pallets and container flooring into the Pithampur–Indore industrial corridor.

Why Pithampur is a serious plywood-consuming belt

Pithampur sits about 30 km southwest of Indore on NH-3, and is run as a planned cluster by MPAKVN (Madhya Pradesh Audyogik Kendra Vikas Nigam, Indore). The town is laid out in four developed sectors — Sector 1, Sector 2, Sector 3 and the newer Sector 7 — plus the MPSEZ at Pithampur Special Economic Zone. The mix of tenants here is what makes the packaging demand interesting: heavy commercial vehicles, tractors, off-road equipment, engines, auto components, pharma capital equipment and food-processing machinery.

The industrial sub-belts that drive plywood demand

  • Commercial vehicle and tractor OEMs — Mahindra & Mahindra's truck and bus division, Volvo Eicher Commercial Vehicles, and John Deere all run major plants in Pithampur. CKD (Completely Knocked Down) and SKD shipments of chassis aggregates and tractor assemblies are crated for export and inter-plant movement.
  • Diesel engines and power systems — Cummins India operates in the cluster, and engines plus large genset enclosures need heavy-duty crates with rebated corners and lifting skids.
  • Tier-1 auto components — castings, forgings, transmissions, axles, sheet-metal panels and bumpers. These move on pallets between Pithampur and assembly plants in Pune, Chennai, Lucknow and overseas.
  • Pharma and food-processing capital equipment — Pithampur and the wider Indore region also house pharma machinery and food-machinery makers. Their finished equipment travels in fumigated, ISPM-15 compliant cases.
  • Container freight stations and 3PL hubs — the Dhannad and Mangliya ICDs near Indore, plus container freight stations around Pithampur, generate steady demand for 14 mm and 18 mm container flooring and side liners.

What we ship into Pithampur

The packing-grade plywood and case-makers serving Central India's auto belt typically work to a few repeatable specs. Below is the working range we run from our Perumbavoor contract factories.

Packing-grade plywood — MR Packing

ThicknessTypical use in Pithampur
6 mmLight component cases, dunnage separators, top sheets for pallet boxes.
8 mm / 10 mmSmall-engine and gearbox crates, sub-assembly boxes for tier-1 suppliers.
12 mmHeavy CKD side panels, agricultural-implement crates, axle boxes.
15 mm / 16 mmHeavy tractor and truck cab crates, large genset cases.
18 mmContainer flooring, base boards on heavy machinery skids, ICD repair work.

Standard sheet size is 8' x 4'. We also cut to size where the case-shop wants pre-sized blanks to reduce wastage. Face veneer options on this grade are commercial hardwood and Gurjan; Okoume face is available on request for export-grade aesthetics.

Wooden cases and CKD crates

For the heavy commercial vehicle and tractor lines in Sector 1 and Sector 3, the typical case is a plywood-clad pinewood or hardwood frame, with 12 mm or 15 mm packing ply skin, runners on the base, and ISPM-15 stamped softwood for any solid-wood members. We build to drawing — load weight, lashing points, fork-pocket spacing, internal cradles and foam profiling. Where the OEM has a packaging engineer's spec sheet, we work directly off that.

Pallets

Common pallet sizes for the Pithampur auto belt:

  • 1200 x 1000 mm — the ISO/euro-adjacent standard for component movement.
  • 1200 x 800 mm — Euro pallet, where the consignee is in Europe.
  • 1100 x 1100 mm — common for Japanese and South-East Asian OEM lines.
  • Custom oversized skids — for engine blocks, transmissions and chassis sub-assemblies.

Container flooring and CFS work

18 mm BWR plywood with phenolic glue is the working spec for container re-flooring jobs at the ICDs around Indore. We supply sheet stock or pre-cut planks to the CFS contractors.

Kochi to Pithampur — the logistics picture

Pithampur is roughly 1,650–1,700 km by road from our Perumbavoor base near Kochi. Trucks run via NH-544 / NH-44 through Salem, Bengaluru bypass, Hubballi, Pune, then NH-3 / NH-52 through Dhule and Indore to Pithampur. Single-driver transit is typically 4–5 days, double-driver 3 days for time-critical loads.

For most packing-grade plywood orders the economics favour direct road from Perumbavoor — a 32-foot multi-axle SXL carries roughly 22–24 tonnes of plywood, which is around 950–1,050 sheets of 12 mm depending on density. Where the customer wants a smaller drop and the freight per sheet is sensitive, we consolidate loads to Indore CFS and arrange last-mile drops to Sector 1, 2, 3 or 7. For Okoume export-grade lots, we can also ship FOB Cochin if the consignee is moving the finished crate overseas from a different port — that decision is usually driven by who controls the export leg.

Which grade for which Pithampur load — decision rules

Use MR Packing grade when

  • The case is moving domestic — Pune, Chennai, Lucknow, Jamshedpur.
  • The voyage, if any, is short (UAE, Sri Lanka, Bangladesh).
  • Cost per sheet matters and the case will be opened once and discarded.

Use BWR / Phenolic-bonded plywood when

  • The crate is going on a long sea voyage — Europe, US, West Africa, Latin America.
  • The case will sit on a port apron or open yard for extended periods.
  • Container re-flooring or repeated-use cradles.

Use Okoume face when

  • The end customer is overseas and case appearance is part of brand presentation — premium agricultural machinery, defence equipment, high-end engines.
  • Light-weight case construction is preferred for air freight or weight-sensitive sea freight.

Use shuttering ply when

  • The buyer is a civil contractor working on a Pithampur factory shed, MPAKVN infrastructure or warehouse expansion — not a packaging use, but a recurring Pithampur demand we serve from the same stock.

FAQ

How much plywood fits in one truck from Kochi to Pithampur?

A 32-foot SXL carries around 22–24 tonnes. For 12 mm packing-grade plywood that translates to roughly 950–1,050 sheets of 8' x 4'. For 18 mm container flooring the count drops to around 640–700 sheets at the same weight.

Can you supply ISPM-15 stamped wooden cases ready to ship?

Yes. The plywood skin is BIS-grade; the solid-wood frame and runners are heat-treated and ISPM-15 stamped before despatch. We co-ordinate the HT treatment through approved facilities and send the case to the OEM's stuffing yard in Pithampur ready to load.

Do you take partial loads or only full trucks?

Both. Full truckload (FTL) from Perumbavoor direct to Pithampur is the cheapest per sheet. For smaller orders we consolidate at the Indore CFS hub and last-mile to the consignee, or piggyback on an existing Pithampur load — ask when you request the quote.

Request a quote

If you operate a packaging shop, a case-making unit, a tier-1 export packer or a captive packaging line inside the Pithampur cluster, send us your monthly thickness mix, sheet sizes, face-veneer preference and the destination plant. We will return a landed-Pithampur price with transit time and a payment-term proposal. The Cochin Wood group has been in the Perumbavoor plywood belt since 1986 — 40+ years of group manufacturing — and we have the contract-factory capacity to commit to repeat monthly volume rather than one-off spot orders. Request a quote or browse our resource hub for grade datasheets, ISPM-15 reference notes and packing-case construction guidelines.

title: Plywood Supply to Amritsar | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, made-up crates and heat-treated pallets that Amritsar's exporters load every month. Built in Perumbavoor by a group.
html:
Amritsar runs on three export engines that all eat plywood: the denim and ready-made garment cluster in the East and West industrial focal points, the centuries-old brassware and utensil belt around Jandiala Guru, and the dry-fruit and spice trade flowing through the Integrated Check Post at Attari. Add the food-processing units in Verka and Mehta Road, the auto-component vendors feeding the Punjab cluster, and the ICP-Attari overland trade with Afghanistan and Central Asia, and the city becomes one of North India's denser pockets of bulk packing demand. That is why we ship trailer-loads of packing-grade plywood, made-up wooden crates, heat-treated pallets and 28 mm container flooring up the western trunk route from Kochi to Amritsar every month.

The Amritsar industrial picture

Buyers in Amritsar tend to sit in a few well-defined belts. Understanding which one a consignment is bound for usually tells us the right plywood spec before the buyer has even opened a sample.

  • East and West Focal Point (Verka, Mehta Road) — the largest organised industrial estates in the district, anchored by textile processing, ready-made garments, denim, food and beverage units, and auto components. This is where most export crates for apparel and finished goods originate.
  • Jandiala Guru brassware cluster — a 200-year-old hand-beaten brass and bell-metal hub. Heavy, awkward, scratch-sensitive utensils that ship to GCC, the UK and the US in cushioned wooden cases.
  • Chheharta and G.T. Road belt — engineering, hand-tools, agro-machinery and smaller fabricators. Mixed packing requirements — anything from a 6 mm sheet for a light carton liner up to a 25 mm shuttering board for a foundation pour.
  • ICP Attari trade corridor — dry fruits (almonds, pistachios, raisins, figs), spices and cement crossing into and out of Afghanistan. Wooden cases here see rough handling across two borders and Punjab winters.
  • Rajasansi airport cargo and the Amritsar–Delhi freight lane — feeders for air-freight crates and high-value consolidations heading to IGI Cargo Terminal.

The common thread is bulk, cost-sensitive packaging that still has to survive a 1,500 km road run, a sea container or an air-cargo handover. That is the brief our packing-grade plywood was built for.

What we ship to Amritsar

Packing-grade plywood — the workhorse

The bulk of Amritsar's order book is commercial / rubberwood plywood in standard 8 ft x 4 ft sheets across the packing-grade thickness range — 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 15, 16 and 18 mm. The 12 mm and 18 mm sheets do most of the heavy lifting (case walls, lids, base boards). 6 mm and 8 mm go into liners, dividers and internal cushioning for brassware and dry-fruit cartons. Thicker 18 mm and 25 mm boards build the skids and base frames that take the forklift load.

Made-up crates and packing cases

For the textile and apparel exporters in the Focal Point, and for the brassware shippers in Jandiala Guru, we send finished crates cut and pre-assembled to drawing — open-top, closed, slotted, or two-piece with screwed lids. Typical specs that move:

  • Garment export cases — 18 mm walls, 12 mm internal partitions, 25 mm skid base, three-runner bottom.
  • Brassware crates — 18 mm panels with 8 mm internal lining for scratch protection, corner-block reinforcement.
  • Dry-fruit and spice cases — heat-treated to ISPM-15, lower thickness (12–15 mm) where load allows, stencilled with the IPPC mark.

Pallets — ISPM-15 stamped

The standard four-way entry 1200 x 1000 mm and 1200 x 800 mm Euro footprint move regularly, alongside custom sizes for awkward brassware and machinery loads. All export pallets are heat-treated and IPPC-stamped at a registered facility so Attari and JNPT clear them without query.

Container flooring and shuttering ply

For the auto-component and machinery shippers loading 20 ft and 40 ft containers out of ICD Dhandari Kalan and ICD Ludhiana, we supply 28 mm container flooring plywood. The agro and infra builders along G.T. Road buy our BWR shuttering plywood for slab and column work, where re-use cycles matter more than finish.

Kochi to Amritsar — the logistics

The lane runs roughly 2,500 km by road, Kochi up through Bengaluru, Pune, Mumbai, Ahmedabad, Udaipur and Delhi into Amritsar. A standard 32 ft single-axle or multi-axle trailer takes 6–8 days door-to-door depending on the season and the driver's run. We aggregate sheet orders into full-truck loads of roughly 18–22 tonnes (around 800–1,000 sheets of 18 mm equivalent) — that is the cost-efficient lot size for the lane. Smaller orders go part-load through scheduled freight movers running the Kochi–Delhi–Amritsar leg, with 10–14 day transit. Both options land at the buyer's gate with sealed truck delivery and a single LR for reconciliation.

For larger or recurring buyers, we can quote ex-Cochin port (FOB) and route via coastal feeder to Mundra, then road into Amritsar — the maths sometimes favours this for 40 ft container loads of finished crates and pallets.

Decision rules — which spec for which job

Amritsar use caseRecommended specWhy
Garment / denim export cratesPacking-grade commercial ply, 12 mm walls, 18 mm baseCost-efficient, dimensionally stable for stacked sea-freight loads.
Brassware export casesPacking-grade 18 mm + 8 mm liner, ISPM-15 treatedScratch protection on soft brass surfaces; border-compliant.
Dry-fruit cases via ICP Attari12–15 mm packing-grade, IPPC stampedLighter case weight where load is moderate; treated for overland border movement.
Auto-component / machinery export18 mm packing ply + Okoume face for premium finishOkoume face panels give cleaner aesthetics for end-customer-facing crates without the cost of full Okoume construction.
Container flooring28 mm container flooring plywoodLoad rating for forklift traffic inside the box.
Site shuttering — infra and real-estateBWR shuttering plywood, 12 mmMultiple-pour re-use; resists Punjab monsoon damp.

Where a buyer is unsure, our default position is: order packing-grade for case walls and bases, upgrade to Okoume face only where the crate is end-customer visible or the buyer's importer specifies a premium finish.

FAQ

Do you supply heat-treated, IPPC-stamped pallets for Attari and JNPT clearance?

Yes. All export pallets and crates can be heat-treated at a registered facility and stencilled with the IPPC mark. We send the treatment certificate with the LR so the export documentation is closed at dispatch.

What is the minimum order size you can ship to Amritsar?

Full truck loads (around 800–1,000 sheets of 18 mm equivalent, or the equivalent in made-up crates and pallets) are the most cost-efficient. Smaller part-load consignments are possible through scheduled freight movers — transit stretches to 10–14 days and per-sheet freight goes up, but it is workable for trial orders.

Can you cut crates to drawing, or do you only ship sheets?

Both. We ship raw sheets to Amritsar packers who build cases in-house, and we ship pre-cut and pre-assembled crates and pallets to specification for buyers who would rather receive finished packing units at the gate.

Talk to us about your Amritsar shipment

The Cochin Wood group has been operating in Perumbavoor since 1986 — forty years of group manufacturing experience built around bulk packing plywood and finished crates. CWI Pvt Ltd carries that operating playbook into pan-India supply, with regular trailer movements up the western corridor into Punjab. If you are running an export programme out of the Focal Point, Jandiala Guru, the ICP Attari corridor or an industrial vendor base anywhere in Amritsar district, send us your thickness mix, drawing or pallet footprint and monthly volume. We will come back with a per-sheet or per-crate rate, freight to your gate, and a realistic transit window. Start with our request a quote form, or browse the resource hub for spec sheets, ISPM-15 guidance, FOB Cochin notes and the rest of our reference library.

title: Plywood Supply to Jalandhar | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates and pallets that Jalandhar's export packers nail and band every day. Our group has manufactured plywood in.
html:
Why Jalandhar sits on our daily despatch board

Jalandhar is one of the few Indian cities where a packing-grade plywood truck is almost always met with an export carton or crate waiting to be built. The Doaba belt is unusual in that three serious export verticals share the same set of carpenters and packing yards: sports goods, hand-tools and surgical instruments. Each ships in different box sizes, but they all need the same things from a plywood supplier 2,400 km away in Kerala — dimensional stability, a reliable transit time, and a sheet that the local crate-makers can nail and band without splitting. This is a quick operator's view of what the Cochin Wood group ships to Jalandhar, why the lane works, and how to specify the right grade for the load.

The Jalandhar industrial picture

The city's export economy is concentrated in a handful of well-known belts. Anyone running a packing line in Jalandhar will recognise these names — and the plywood flowing into them is broadly the same story across the lot.

Sports goods cluster (alongside Meerut)

Focal Point Phase I to VIII, Basti Nau, Industrial Area-A and the units around Mithapur Road carry most of the sports goods manufacturing. Cricket bats, hockey sticks, footballs, boxing equipment, gym hardware and inflatables move out from here to the UK, Australia, the Gulf and Africa. The packing job is largely export cartons and lightweight crates — flat sheets cut to size, sometimes a die-cut insert.

Hand-tools — the world's largest exporter to Western markets

Jalandhar is the single largest export cluster for hand tools sold into Europe and North America. Wrenches, pliers, hammers, garden tools, agricultural implements — the volumes are heavy and the crates need to hold up under stacked sea transit. Focal Point and the Leather Complex Road belt run a lot of this work, plus the smaller yards around Kapurthala Road and Nakodar Road.

Surgical instruments

Forceps, scalpels, orthopaedic tools and dental instruments — small unit weight but high value, often shipped in nested plywood inner boxes with foam, then crated for the outer. This is precision packing work and the buyers are unforgiving on moisture, splinters and odour.

Adjacent flows

Auto parts and tractor components from the Phagwara–Jalandhar corridor, leather goods from the Leather Complex, and a growing line of agri-machinery exports all add to the same packing demand. Anything export-bound that needs ISPM-15 compliant crating eventually sits on a plywood pallet or inside a plywood box.

What we ship to Jalandhar

The orders from Punjab tend to cluster around four product calls. Pricing below is the indicative selling band — ex-factory, GST and freight extra, quoted on confirmation.

Packing-grade plywood (commercial / rubberwood)

ThicknessTypical useIndicative rate (per sq.ft, ex-factory)
6 mmLight sports goods cartons, dividersRs 24
8 mmInner boxes, surgical inner packsRs 28
12 mmStandard hand-tools export crate sides and topsRs 33.5
15-16 mmHeavy hand-tools, stacked crate loadsRs 39 - 39.5
18 mmHeavy-duty bases, container flooring repairsRs 42

Okoume plywood

For the export crates that get visually inspected on arrival — premium surgical kits, branded sports equipment, anything that goes into a Western retail supply chain — Okoume face plies give a cleaner external finish and lower weight per crate. We quote Okoume separately because the pricing tracks African log markets, not the Indian commercial board.

Pallets and crate kits

Standard despatches into Punjab include 1200 x 1000 mm and 1200 x 800 mm pallets with hardwood runners (jackwood at Rs 400/cft or rubberwood as per size), and pre-cut crate sets where the buyer wants to assemble locally. Container flooring (typically 28 mm marine-pattern board for 20-ft and 40-ft container repairs and one-way export crates) is a recurring line.

Sawn timber

Jackwood and rubberwood runners go up with the plywood when the buyer's own carpentry is building bespoke crates around heavier machinery or auto components.

Kochi-to-Jalandhar logistics

The lane is roughly 2,400 km by road. Loaded trucks out of Perumbavoor and the Kochi belt typically run via NH 544 to Salem, NH 44 through Bangalore-Hyderabad-Nagpur, then up via Jhansi-Agra-Delhi-Ambala-Jalandhar. Realistic transit is 6 to 8 days for a single-driver truck, 4 to 5 days with a relay driver, depending on Punjab entry checkpoint timings.

For larger orders we run full-truck despatches direct to the buyer's yard in Focal Point or wherever the packing line sits. For smaller mixed loads we consolidate via established Kochi-to-North-India transporters and offload at the Jalandhar transport nagar. Coastal-shipping to JNPT with onward road to Jalandhar is an option for very large container-equivalent volumes, but on most lanes road-direct from Kochi wins on cost and headache.

One operator note: Punjab's entry and weighment formalities are smoother for full-load consignments with clean GST paperwork than for partials, so we usually advise customers to time their indents to fit a full truck rather than three small ones in a month.

Decision rules — packing vs Okoume vs shuttering

  • Standard hand-tools and sports goods export crates: commercial / rubberwood packing grade in 12 mm for sides and 15-16 mm for the base. This is the workhorse for Jalandhar.
  • Surgical instruments and premium retail-bound sports goods: Okoume face on the outer crate when the brand cares about presentation; commercial packing grade for the inner boxes.
  • Heavy machinery, auto-component and agri-equipment crates: 18 mm packing grade base with jackwood runners; 15 mm packing grade sides.
  • Container flooring repairs / one-way export container builds: 28 mm board, BWR glue line, often paired with hardwood bearers.
  • Shuttering ply: reserved for Jalandhar's construction and infra buyers, not the packing belt. We do despatch it on the same lane but it is a separate conversation.

FAQ for Jalandhar buyers

What is the minimum order you ship to Punjab?

A single full truck (roughly 8 to 10 tonnes of plywood depending on thickness mix). Smaller orders are possible via consolidated transport but the per-sheet landed cost goes up noticeably, so most regular buyers indent in full-truck lots.

How firm are delivery dates from Kochi?

For a confirmed indent against a proforma invoice, the truck is loaded within 3 to 5 working days from our factory dispatch yard, then 6 to 8 days in transit. We share the lorry receipt and driver number on loading; buyers in Focal Point usually plan their packing-line schedule around an 11 to 14 day total window from PI to gate.

Do you supply ISPM-15 heat-treated crates?

Yes — we supply the plywood and the runners; the heat-treatment stamp is applied by accredited fumigation units at the buyer's location, which is the more practical arrangement for export crates being built and stuffed in Jalandhar itself.

Get a quote for your Jalandhar requirement

If you are running a packing line in Focal Point, Basti Nau, the Leather Complex Road belt or any of the surrounding industrial estates and want a quote against your specific thickness and quantity, share the indent and we will come back with a delivered cost and the next available loading slot. Send the specs through our request a quote form, or browse our resource hub for grade selection guides, crate-building references and container-loading notes before you specify. The Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing plywood out of Perumbavoor since 1986 — Jalandhar is one of the lanes we know well.

title: Plywood Supply to Ludhiana | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, export crates and pallets that move woollens, bicycle CKD kits and forgings out of Ludhiana. Built in Perumbavoor.
html:
Why Ludhiana matters for a Kochi plywood operation

Ludhiana is one of the most export-dense industrial cities in northern India. The Focal Point belt alone packs out hosiery and woollens that ship into Europe, North America and the Gulf every season; the bicycle cluster around Gill Road and Industrial Area A sends CKD kits into Africa, Latin America and Southeast Asia; and the auto-components ring on the GT Road side feeds Tier-1 suppliers across Pune, Chennai and Manesar. Each of those flows ends the same way at the warehouse door — goods need to leave Ludhiana in a wooden crate, on a wooden pallet, or inside a container with a plywood floor that can take a forklift. That is where the Cochin Wood group fits in, even at a 2,400 km distance. Plywood is light per cubic foot, the truck lane from Kerala into Punjab is mature, and the volumes Ludhiana exporters move justify a planned-supply relationship rather than spot buys from the local mandi.

The Ludhiana industrial picture

Before getting into specs, it is worth being concrete about who actually buys plywood-based packaging in this city. The buyer profile is not one homogeneous market — it splits across at least four belts, each with its own packing logic.

Focal Point (Phases I–VIII) — hosiery and woollens

Focal Point is the workhorse industrial estate of Ludhiana, running across multiple phases east and south-east of the city. The dominant tenants are hosiery manufacturers, woollen and knitwear units, and the dye-houses and finishing units that support them. Their export packing requirement is fairly specific: light-weight crates and cartons that have to survive container stuffing at ICD Dhandari Kalan or Sahnewal and then a long sea leg through Mundra or JNPT. The wood content is usually a plywood lid + base with sawn timber cleats, or full plywood crates for higher-value woollen garments and made-ups.

Industrial Area A, B and Gill Road — bicycle and bicycle parts

Ludhiana's bicycle cluster is internationally known. CKD (Completely Knocked Down) bicycle exports are the high-volume use case here — frames, forks, rims, hubs and components packed flat into multi-layer crates designed to fit a 20' or 40' container's internal dimensions exactly. This is one of the most plywood-intensive packaging segments in India: a single export order can pull hundreds of crates a week, and consistency of thickness and core quality matters because the crates get re-stacked five and six high during ocean transit.

GT Road and Doraha belt — auto components and forgings

Auto-component, forging and fastener units, especially toward Doraha and Khanna, ship dense, heavy goods. Their crates need thicker plywood walls, heavier sawn-timber framing and properly nailed bases. They also consume a meaningful volume of pallets — both standard 1200×800 mm Euro and 1200×1000 mm "industrial" sizes — to handle inbound steel and outbound finished parts on forklifts.

Sewing-machine, hand-tools and small machinery

Ludhiana still has a strong sewing-machine and hand-tools base. These units typically run smaller export crates with mid-range packing-grade plywood lids and bases, and consume sawn timber for cleats and edge protection.

What we ship to Ludhiana

The Cochin Wood group's core line for a city like Ludhiana is packing-grade plywood — not the decorative product you would see in a furniture showroom, but a hardwood-core ply built specifically for crates, cases and pallets. The everyday SKUs we move into Punjab look like this:

ProductTypical thicknessTypical sheetWhere it gets used in Ludhiana
Commercial / Rubberwood packing-grade plywood6, 8, 10, 12, 15, 18 mm8'×4'Hosiery & woollen export crates, bicycle CKD crate walls, light auto-component cases
Okoume-faced plywood (premium packing)9, 12, 15, 18 mm8'×4'High-value woollen made-ups, branded bicycle exports where lid appearance matters
BWR/shuttering ply12, 18 mm8'×4'Heavy auto-forging crates, reusable returnable crates, container flooring repair
Sawn timber (hardwood)Cut-to-size cleats, runnersBy cftCrate framing, pallet stringers, edge protection

Crate construction patterns

Three patterns cover most of what Ludhiana actually builds: open crates with a ply lid + ply base + sawn-timber sides (cheapest, used for non-fragile woollens and knitwear); closed plywood cases with 6–9 mm ply on all six faces over a hardwood frame (the bicycle CKD standard); and heavy nailed crates with 12–15 mm ply walls and 18 mm bases for forgings and machinery. We supply the plywood and sawn timber as raw inputs; the assembly happens at the packer or in-house at the exporter.

Pallets and container flooring

Pallet demand from Ludhiana is split between the auto-components belt (industrial sizes for forklift handling) and the hosiery belt (lighter pallets for warehouse stacking and stuffing). For container flooring — replacement plywood when an existing ISO container's floor has rotted or the operator wants to convert to a flush plywood floor — 18 mm BWR is the standard call.

Kochi-to-Ludhiana logistics

The lane is mature and we plan around it rather than against it.

  • Distance: roughly 2,400 km by road from Kochi/Ernakulam to Ludhiana.
  • Mode: primarily 32-foot multi-axle trucks, full-truck-load. A standard 32-ft truck takes a useful 8'×4' plywood payload of meaningful volume depending on thickness mix — we plan the loading sheet thickness-by-thickness to maximise it.
  • Transit: 6–8 days door-to-door is the realistic working window, longer in monsoon and during Punjab's harvest-season trucker shortage.
  • Route: Kochi → Bengaluru/Salem belt → Maharashtra → MP → Rajasthan → Punjab. Toll, diesel and detention build the freight component.
  • Order economics: for Ludhiana, full-truck loads make sense; LTL/part-loads from Kerala are slow and unreliable. We typically advise customers to consolidate a month's requirement into one or two trucks.

All rates are quoted ex-factory; GST and transport extra — once destination, exact size mix and monthly quantity are confirmed, we build the proforma against an actual lane rate rather than a thumb-rule.

Decision rules — which grade for which load

Use commercial / rubberwood packing-grade when…

The crate is one-way export, the goods are moderately fragile but not delicate (knitwear, woollens, hardware, bicycle CKD without premium branding), and you are buying in volume. This is the workhorse SKU for Ludhiana and covers the majority of Focal Point demand.

Use Okoume-faced when…

The lid or visible face matters — branded woollens, premium bicycle exports, anything where the buyer at the other end opens the crate in a showroom or distributor warehouse and surface appearance reflects on the brand. Okoume is also lighter for the same thickness, which helps when ocean freight is charged by weight.

Use BWR / shuttering ply when…

Crates will be reused, are exposed to outdoor staging in monsoon, carry forging or heavy-machinery loads, or you are doing container floor replacement. BWR is the moisture-and-load answer; it is not the right call for light hosiery cases where you are paying for performance you do not need.

FAQ

What is the minimum order for shipping from Kochi to Ludhiana?

Full-truck-load is the economical unit on this lane. Below FTL, freight per sheet rises sharply and transit times stretch unpredictably through transhipment hubs. Most Ludhiana buyers consolidate a 3–4 week requirement into one truck.

How are payment and delivery handled at this distance?

Typical sequence: WhatsApp or phone qualification (thickness, size, monthly volume, delivery point), proforma invoice from us with ex-factory + transport + GST broken out, advance against PI, dispatch, balance against POD/lorry receipt. Detention at Ludhiana drop-off is on the buyer's account.

Do you supply finished crates, or only raw plywood and timber?

We are a plywood and sawn-timber supplier. The crate assembly itself is best handled at a local Ludhiana fabricator close to the packing line — both for cost and for handling rework. We supply the raw inputs in the exact thickness, size and grade mix the fabricator needs.

Talk to us about your Ludhiana requirement

If your unit sits anywhere in the Focal Point, Industrial Area, GT Road or Doraha belts and you are buying plywood, sawn timber or container flooring material every month, it is worth a 10-minute conversation about lane economics and a planned-supply rate from Kochi. We will ask three things: thickness and size mix, monthly volume, and the exact drop-off point. From there we build a proforma you can compare line-for-line against your current source. Request a quote with those three inputs, or browse the resource hub for grade-selection notes, crate specs and packing-case literature before you reach out.

title: Plywood Supply to Mohali | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, ready crates, pallets and container flooring and ship them from Kochi into Mohali's PSIEC estates and IT campuses. Our.
html:
Mohali sits at an unusual intersection — IT campuses in Sectors 67 to 91, a deep pharma cluster spilling over from Baddi and Derabassi, and an auto belt anchored by Hero MotoCorp and Sonalika Tractors. Each of those verticals ships finished goods in wooden packaging, and each has different tolerances for moisture, drop-shock and ISPM-15 compliance. We supply packing-grade plywood, ready crates, heat-treated pallets and 28 mm container flooring into all three from our Kochi base, and this post is a practical breakdown of what we ship north, how it moves the 2,400-odd kilometres up, and how to pick the right grade for the load.

The Mohali industrial picture

Mohali district's manufacturing footprint is concentrated across a handful of well-defined estates, and each has a distinct packaging profile.

  • Industrial Area Phase 1, 2, 7, 8, 8B and 9 — the original PSIEC estates running along the Chandigarh-Ludhiana corridor. Heavy on auto-component machining, sheet-metal fabrication and electrical switchgear. Crates here are often heavy, irregular and built to ride the road network down to Mundra or JNPT.
  • Sectors 82, 83, 91 (IT City and Quark City) — IT campuses, data-centre kit, networking hardware. Lighter loads, but exporters here are strict on look-and-feel of the packaging and on ISPM-15 stamping for international shipments.
  • Sector 75, 80, 81 and 82 industrial belt — mixed engineering, food processing and a growing pharma packaging cluster feeding Baddi-Barotiwala-Nalagarh (BBN) just across the Himachal border.
  • Derabassi-Lalru belt — pharma APIs, agro-chemicals and tractor-component vendors feeding Sonalika at Hoshiarpur. Crate volumes here are recurring and the spec list is short — boxes, pallets, container liners.

Add the Hero MotoCorp plant at the Industrial Area Phase 1 entry, the tractor CKD packing demand pulled in by Sonalika and Mahindra-Swaraj's vendors, and you get a fairly steady monthly draw on packing plywood across thicknesses from 8 mm up to 18 mm.

What we ship to Mohali

Three product families cover almost every Mohali requirement we see.

1. Packing-grade plywood — sheets

This is our core product for crate builders and in-house packing departments. Built on a hardwood core (rubberwood or jackwood), MR-grade phenolic-bonded, finished in standard 8x4 ft sheets. Most Mohali buyers pull from a narrow band:

ThicknessTypical use in Mohali loads
8 mmLight IT hardware boxes, switchgear lids, internal partitions
10-12 mmStandard crate sides and tops for auto components, pharma export cases
15-18 mmCrate bases, heavy machinery boxes, tractor CKD bottoms, container floors

We can run an Okoume face-veneer option on the same MR-Packing core when the export customer wants a cleaner look (common for European-bound pharma and IT shipments). Okoume is a face choice on our packing or BWR grade — it's not a separate grade. Ask if your buyer is specifying it by name.

2. Ready crates and boxes

For factories that don't want to run their own carpentry shed, we build complete crates to drawing — pinewood frame with packing-ply panels, optionally heat-treated to ISPM-15 with the IPPC mark for export. Typical Mohali jobs: tractor CKD crates (1.2 to 1.5 cubic metres each), engine-and-gearbox boxes for two-wheeler exports, server-rack crates with internal foam dunnage. We can ship knocked-down (KD) bundles for the buyer to assemble on site if road volume is the bottleneck.

3. Pallets and container flooring

Standard Indian export pallets — 1200 x 1000 mm and 1200 x 800 mm Euro — in heat-treated pinewood with a 12 mm packing-ply top deck where the load needs a continuous surface (sacks, drums, IBC bottoms). For container-stuffing operations at the ICDs around Mohali (ICD Sahnewal, ICD Dronagiri-equivalent rail nodes), 28 mm shuttering-grade ply works as a single-use container floor liner under wheeled cargo or sharp-edged machinery — cheaper than rebuilding the steel floor after each tractor or generator shipment.

Kochi-to-Mohali logistics

The route is roughly 2,400 km by road. Two practical options:

  1. Direct trucking — multi-axle 32-footer or container truck from Ernakulam via Bengaluru-Hyderabad-Nagpur-Delhi-Mohali. Transit usually 6 to 8 days. Best for full-truck orders (one container of plywood is roughly 500 to 600 sheets of 18 mm or 900+ sheets of 8 mm depending on density). FOR (freight-on-receiver) and FOB Cochin terms both available; we typically advise FOR for first-time Mohali buyers so the rate lands clean at their gate.
  2. Containerised rail via Cochin Port (Vallarpadam ICD) to Ludhiana / Dadri ICD — works for export-bound crates and pallets that are already going into a sealed box, and for buyers in the BBN belt where rail to Ludhiana is the natural unload point. Slower (10 to 14 days door-to-door) but takes the diesel exposure off the table.

For ready crates and pallets, road wins almost every time — the volumetric weight of empty crates makes rail uneconomic unless you're consolidating with sheet stock.

Which grade for which Mohali load

A quick decision rule we run when scoping a Mohali enquiry:

  • Domestic crates, road transit, dry storage — MR-grade packing plywood, 12 mm sides, 15 mm base. Lowest landed cost per crate.
  • Export crates, sea transit, monsoon-season stuffing — same construction but BWR-grade (boiling-water resistant) panels and full ISPM-15 heat-treated frames. The premium over MR is small versus the cost of a rejection at the destination port.
  • Visible-face exports (pharma, IT, premium auto) — packing or BWR core with an Okoume face veneer. The finish is cleaner and the buyer's QC team stops asking questions about the timber.
  • Container flooring, ramp boards, jigs — 28 mm film-faced shuttering ply. Single shipment use, then either resold as scrap or re-used in the factory as work-table tops.

FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15 heat-treated crates with IPPC stamp?

Yes. Our pinewood frames are heat-treated at registered facilities and stamped with the IPPC mark and the treatment provider code. The packing plywood panels themselves don't require treatment under ISPM-15 (processed wood is exempt), but the framing timber does, and we handle that end-to-end so the export documentation is clean.

Can you hold buffer stock in or near Mohali?

For monthly off-takes above roughly 20,000 sq ft of sheet equivalent, yes — we work with stocking partners in the Ludhiana-Mohali belt to keep 30 to 45 days of cover so urgent draws don't wait on a Kochi truck. Below that volume, scheduled monthly trucks usually beat the warehousing cost.

What's the minimum order for a first trial?

For sheets, one truck (roughly 18 to 22 tonnes depending on thickness mix) is the practical minimum to keep per-sqft freight reasonable to Mohali. For ready crates, we'll quote from 20 crates upwards. Smaller trial volumes are possible by consolidating with another north-India lane, but lead time stretches.

Getting a quote

Send us the thickness and quantity for sheets, or the drawing and monthly run-rate for crates and pallets. We'll come back with a delivered-Mohali rate, transit estimate and grade recommendation in one quote. The Cochin Wood group has been in Perumbavoor since 1986 — about forty years of packing-plywood manufacturing experience sits behind every truck that leaves for Mohali. Request a quote with your spec list, or browse the resource hub for grade-selection guides, ISPM-15 notes and crate construction references before you brief us.

title: Plywood Supply to Bhilai | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, export crates and pallets that move steel, castings and machinery out of the Bhilai-Durg corridor. Our group has.
html:
Bhilai runs on steel, and steel does not move without wood. Every coil, billet, rail section, forging and casting that leaves the Bhilai-Durg industrial belt for a port, a project site or an overseas buyer rides on plywood cases, hardwood skids and treated pallets. That single fact shapes what plywood buyers here ask for: heavy-section bearings, predictable thickness, BWR/BWP glue lines that survive monsoon road moves, and ISPM-15 compliance on anything destined for an export container. This is what the Cochin Wood group ships into Chhattisgarh from Kerala, and the notes below explain what we send, how we send it, and how to scope a quote.

The Bhilai industrial picture

The packaging-timber demand in this region is not one market — it is at least four overlapping ones, and each pulls a different specification.

Bhilai Steel Plant and the SAIL ecosystem

Bhilai Steel Plant (BSP) under SAIL is the single largest rail producer in India and the country's only manufacturer of wide steel plates and 260-metre-long rails. The plant alone consumes a steady volume of heavy-duty plywood for export rail crates, plate crates and machinery covers. Around BSP sits a dense web of ancillary forging shops, fabricators, roll-makers and refractory units in the Bhilai-Durg-Rajnandgaon corridor that ship semi-finished goods to other SAIL plants and to private end-users.

The Bhilai-Borai and Industrial Growth Centres

The CSIDC industrial areas at Borai (~25 km from Bhilai), Sector 6 and Hathkhoj, plus the Urla and Sirgitti estates in adjoining Raipur, host hundreds of medium engineering units — pumps, valves, structural steel fabrication, agri-machinery, food processing and pharma packaging.
