These units crate heavy, lumpy and odd-shaped loads. Plywood case manufacturers in and around Bhilai serve them all, and that is where most of our trade flows land.

Foundry and casting cluster

The Chhattisgarh foundry belt — concentrated around Rajnandgaon, Raipur and Bhilai — produces grey iron, ductile iron and steel castings for railways, defence, power and automotive. Castings are dense per cubic foot, which means crates must carry concentrated point loads on the floor and the side walls.

Mineral and refractory exports

Bauxite, dolomite, iron ore and refractory products from the Chhattisgarh-Odisha belt move through Visakhapatnam and Paradip. The crates that line trial shipments and value-added refractory bricks need clean ISPM-15 stamps and dimensional tolerance that does not drift across a 1,200 km road leg.

What we ship into Bhilai

Three product groups move regularly on this lane.

Packing-grade plywood (MR-Packing)

Our standard packing grade is built for export crates and one-way pallets. Glue line is MR (moisture resistant) with a BWR option on request. Common thicknesses for this region:

  • 6 mm and 8 mm — top and side panels on light machinery cases, lining boards for steel-plate crates.
  • 10 mm and 12 mm — workhorse for general engineering crates and box-pallets.
  • 14 mm to 18 mm — base panels, heavy machinery cases, foundry casting crates, container floor patches.

Standard sheet size 8 ft × 4 ft. Custom sizes (7 ft, 9 ft and odd widths) are cut to order when the crate bill of materials justifies it — usually for repeat case-makers.

Crates and ISPM-15 cases

For buyers who do not run their own carpentry shed, we supply finished crates to drawing — open crates, closed cases, collapsible crates, and skid-bases. Crates destined for export carry the ISPM-15 heat-treatment stamp on the dunnage and hardwood members. Typical crate furniture for the Bhilai mix: rubberwood or jackwood runners, 12 mm or 15 mm ply skins, and reinforced corner blocks for forklift and crane handling.

Pallets

Two-way and four-way pallets in standard 1200 × 1000 mm and 1200 × 800 mm footprints, plus custom sizes for in-house racking. For heavy castings and steel sections we recommend a hardwood-runner pallet with 18 mm ply deck rather than a sawn-board deck — the load distributes better across the lower stringer and the corners do not split on the first forklift bite.

Container flooring patches

Marine plywood patches (BWP) for repair of 20 ft and 40 ft container floors, and full apitong-style replacement boards where the original floor has cratered. Useful for fleet operators on the Vizag and Paradip lanes who move steel and refractories.

Kochi to Bhilai — how the logistics actually work

The road distance from our Perumbavoor despatch base to Bhilai is roughly 1,500 km. The standard truck routing is Kochi → Salem → Bengaluru → Hyderabad → Nagpur → Bhilai, or the eastern variant through Vijayawada and Vizag when buyers prefer mixed road-rail. Transit times for a full truckload:

ModeTypical transitWhen it makes sense
Single-axle 19/22 ft FTL5–6 daysSmall case-maker orders, 600–900 sheets
Multi-axle 32 ft FTL5–7 daysStandard full container of 1,400–1,800 sheets
Part-load (LTL)8–10 daysSample orders, trial crates, top-up consignments

Rates are quoted Ex-Factory Perumbavoor, with GST and freight extra, so the buyer keeps clean visibility on the freight component. For repeat buyers in the Bhilai-Durg belt, we also quote landed (FOR Bhilai) once the route and offload point are locked. Monsoon months (June to September) add a day on the western ghats leg — we plan despatches accordingly and shrink-wrap pallets that will sit in open yards.

Which grade for which Bhilai load

The same buyer often needs different sheets for different lines. A quick decision rule:

  • MR-Packing grade — domestic crates, one-way export crates that stay dry in transit, internal pallets, dunnage. This is the default and the cheapest credible option.
  • BWR / Okoume-faced packing — premium export crates where the buyer wants a cleaner face, lower core gaps, and tighter dimensional tolerance. Common on European and GCC-bound rail and forging shipments.
  • Shuttering ply (film-faced) — not a packing material. Specify only when the Bhilai buyer is a contractor working on plant expansion, township construction or infra projects.
  • Marine plywood (BWP) — container floor repair, marine equipment crates, and any case with a known wet-leg exposure.

If you are unsure, send a load profile — gross weight, dimensions, leg of journey, destination port — and we will recommend the grade and thickness with no obligation. More background on the underlying material differences is in the resource hub.

FAQ — Bhilai buyers

Do you supply ISPM-15 certified crates direct from Kerala?

Yes. Heat-treated members carry the IPPC stamp and the treatment certificate travels with the despatch. Most Bhilai exporters specify the stamp on hardwood frames, runners and skids — the plywood skins themselves do not require treatment.

What is the smallest viable order on the Kochi-Bhilai lane?

Economically, a 32 ft full truckload (typically 1,400 to 1,800 sheets, mixed thicknesses). Smaller orders go as part-loads with longer transit and proportionally higher freight per sheet. For first-time buyers we usually suggest a trial FTL with a thickness mix so the in-house crate shop can validate against their bill of materials.

Can you cut to size or supply pre-cut crate kits?

Cut-to-size is standard. Pre-cut crate kits are supplied where drawings are shared in advance — useful for case-makers who want to skip the sizing step on the shop floor and for foundries that crate a stable SKU range.

Request a quote

If you ship steel, castings, refractories, machinery or any export-grade product out of the Bhilai-Durg-Raipur belt, we can quote packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring on a delivered or ex-factory basis. Share the thicknesses you use, monthly quantity, and the destination offload point. We will revert with a Proforma Invoice the same working day. Request a quote or browse specifications and packing-case guides in the resource hub. The Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing in Perumbavoor since 1986 — forty years of plywood, packing-case and sawn-timber work behind every truck we put on the road.

title: Plywood Supply to Raipur | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring that Raipur's steel, engineering and rice exporters load every working day.
html:
Raipur sits at the centre of one of India's heaviest industrial corridors. The city is the administrative capital of Chhattisgarh, but its real economic identity is steel, iron, sponge iron, and rice — three sectors that move enormous tonnages out by road and rail every working day. Every steel coil, every sponge iron billet bound for a foundry, and every premium basmati or non-basmati consignment leaving Raipur needs packaging that can survive multi-modal handling, monsoon humidity, and in many cases an ISPM-15 export leg. That is where packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets, and container flooring become a routine purchase item rather than an occasional spend. This guide explains what the Cochin Wood group ships into Raipur, why Kochi-to-Raipur is a workable lane despite the distance, and how to match grade to load.

The Raipur industrial picture

Raipur's manufacturing footprint is concentrated in a handful of well-defined belts. Understanding which belt a buyer sits in usually tells us what packing they need.

Siltara industrial area

Siltara, on the northern edge of Raipur, is dominated by steel — integrated steel plants, rolling mills, sponge iron units, and ferro alloy producers. Loads here are dense, sharp-edged, and frequently bound for domestic dispatch by truck or rail rake. Crates and pallets need to handle point loads from billets, plates, and coils without splitting at the corners.

Urla industrial area

Urla is the older, more diversified estate — steel re-rolling, structural fabrication, engineering, and a long tail of ancillary units. Smaller engineering exporters here ship machined components, castings, and fabricated assemblies, often into the Middle East. Wooden boxes with ISPM-15 stamped pallets and ply linings are routine.

Atal Nagar (Naya Raipur) and the Sirgitti / Bhilai overflow

Atal Nagar is the planned capital city, attracting newer manufacturing and logistics parks. Bhilai, an hour west, is the Steel Authority of India anchor and pulls a long shadow of ancillary packaging demand across the corridor. Buyers from Bhilai and Sirgitti regularly source from Raipur consolidators.

The rice milling cluster

Chhattisgarh is one of India's largest paddy producers, and Raipur, Tilda, and Dhamtari host hundreds of rice mills. Export-grade rice — particularly to the GCC and African markets — moves in wooden crates or on pallets inside containers. The packaging spec is lighter than steel, but the volume is steady year-round.

What we ship to Raipur

The Cochin Wood group has been in Perumbavoor since 1986, and 40+ years of group manufacturing experience sits behind the specifications we supply today. Three product families move into Raipur most often.

Packing-grade plywood

This is the workhorse for the steel and rice belts. Standard thicknesses sold into Raipur:

  • 8mm / 10mm — light crates, internal partitions, rice export box panels.
  • 12mm — the most common general-purpose packing thickness; tops and sides of engineering crates.
  • 15mm / 16mm — heavier engineering and steel component crates, pallet decks for medium loads.
  • 18mm — heavy steel crates, pallet stringers, container flooring patches, and load-bearing tops.

Sizes are made to packing-case standards rather than furniture standards. Sheets are pressed for moisture tolerance suitable for monsoon-season storage at the consignee end. All quoted ex-factory; GST and transport extra.

Crate construction inputs

Most Raipur crate makers buy plywood sheets plus sawn timber runners. We supply hardwood and rubberwood runners cut to the typical sections used in box construction — heavier sections for steel crates, lighter sections for rice and engineering boxes. Jackwood runners at ¼-inch-minus tolerance are available for buyers who specify them.

Pallet sizes

Standard pallet decks supplied to the Raipur belt include 1200×1000 mm (the most common export footprint into the GCC), 1100×1100 mm (Asian standard), and custom sizes for steel coil cradles. Deck thickness usually 15mm or 18mm depending on the load class.

Container flooring

For exporters loading 20-ft or 40-ft containers with heavy steel and engineering goods, container flooring patches and load-spreading sheets are quoted on request. This typically uses 18mm packing-grade ply and is sized to the container floor and load points.

Kochi to Raipur — logistics

Raipur is roughly 1,500 km from Kochi by road. Two routings work in practice:

  • Direct trucking lane — Kochi / Ernakulam → Bengaluru → Hyderabad → Nagpur → Raipur. Multi-axle trailers run this route in 4 to 6 days depending on season, check-post conditions, and driver changeovers.
  • Coastal port + inland — for very large consignments, Cochin port to Visakhapatnam by coastal shipping and then inland by rake or truck. Slower but cheaper per cubic foot once volumes cross full container lots.

The freight rate is the single largest variable in landed cost. Direct truck FTL is usually the right answer for crate makers ordering 1 to 2 truckloads per cycle. We quote ex-factory and let the buyer choose transport, or arrange transport on request.

Decision rules — which grade for which Raipur load

A short framework we use when a Raipur buyer enquires:

Load typeSuggested gradeWhy
Rice export boxes for GCC / AfricaPacking-grade ply 8-10mmLight load, low cost per box, moisture-tolerant for monsoon storage.
Engineering exporter crates from UrlaPacking-grade ply 12mm + hardwood runnersStandard ISPM-15 box construction with good strength-to-weight.
Sponge iron / steel billet crates from SiltaraPacking-grade ply 15-18mm + heavy runnersPoint-load resistance; survives stacking and forklift handling.
Container flooring / load spreadingPacking-grade ply 18mmSpreads point loads across container floor; structural integrity under shifting cargo.
Shuttering for infrastructure projects in Atal NagarShuttering ply (separate spec)Re-use cycles and concrete-grade face quality; different product line.
Premium export packaging where appearance mattersOkoume plywood (quoted separately)Lighter, cleaner face; specified by some exporters of higher-value engineering goods.

Okoume is a premium grade quoted on request. For most steel and rice loads out of Raipur, the packing-grade range is the right starting point.

FAQ

How long does delivery to Raipur take from Kochi?

4 to 6 days by direct trucking lane (Kochi → Bengaluru → Hyderabad → Nagpur → Raipur) for an FTL load. Coastal port plus inland is slower but can be cheaper at scale. Confirmed once thickness, quantity, and delivery point in Raipur are agreed.

Do you supply ISPM-15 compliant material for export crates?

Plywood as a manufactured board is exempt from ISPM-15 marking under ISPM-15 itself. The runners, blocks, and any solid wood components in the crate must be ISPM-15 heat-treated and stamped — we can supply runners specified accordingly. Crate fabrication is done at the buyer's end or through their nominated box maker.

What is the minimum order size for a Raipur delivery?

The economics work best at full truckload (roughly 800-1,000 sheets of 12mm equivalent). Smaller orders can be combined with other consignments on the south-to-central lane but quote depends on combinability at the time of booking.

Request a quote for your Raipur facility

If you operate a packing-case unit, a steel ancillary, a rice mill, or an engineering exporter in Siltara, Urla, Atal Nagar, Bhilai, or Sirgitti — and you need a reliable plywood and sawn timber supply line from a Kerala source — request a quote with thickness, monthly quantity, and delivery point. We will revert with an ex-factory rate, lead time, and a transport indication for the Kochi-to-Raipur lane. For grade comparisons, packing-case construction guides, and ISPM-15 notes, see the resource hub. Pricing is ex-factory; GST and transport are extra; rates are valid as confirmed on each Proforma Invoice.

title: Plywood Supply to Dhanbad | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring that Dhanbad's mining-equipment packers and heavy-fabrication shops ship.
html:
Why Dhanbad's industrial belt needs plywood from down south

Dhanbad is shorthand for coal in India. Bharat Coking Coal Limited (BCCL) is headquartered here, Eastern Coalfields Limited (ECL) operates immediately adjacent in the Asansol-Raniganj belt, and the city anchors a deep cluster of coal washeries, mining-equipment OEMs, refractory makers and heavy fabrication shops feeding the steel corridors at Bokaro, Jamshedpur and Durgapur. Every one of those activities throws off a downstream packing requirement — long-tail mining spares for export, drawcut conveyor parts going to a project site in Odisha, refractory shapes on pallets for a Bhilai despatch, transformer accessories crated for a port load-out. That is the plywood demand the Cochin Wood group ships into Dhanbad from Kerala.

The Dhanbad industrial picture

The buying universe around Dhanbad sits in a few recognisable clusters. We size our packing-grade plywood, crates and pallets against what these clusters actually move.

Coal and mining majors

BCCL (a Coal India subsidiary) runs washeries, workshops and central stores across the Jharia coalfield. ECL extends the same demand pattern westwards into Asansol-Raniganj. Both buy packing material indirectly through their vendor base — mining-equipment dealers and spares packers who crate dragline parts, conveyor idlers, gear assemblies and electrical panels for despatch to project sites and overseas refurbishment lines.

Mining machinery and heavy fabrication

Dhanbad and the contiguous Govindpur / Nirsa belt host fabrication shops supplying coal-handling plants, crushers, hydraulic roof supports and conveyor systems. The Indian School of Mines (IIT-ISM) ecosystem has also pulled in a steady mining-tech vendor base. Export crates for this segment are the highest-spec work we do for the region — ISPM-15 heat-treated timber framing, plywood skinning that holds shape on a 30-day sea leg, and pallets sized to the equipment's actual centre of gravity rather than a standard footprint.

Refractories, steel-corridor feeders and ancillaries

Dhanbad's proximity to the Bokaro Steel Plant (~50 km) and onward to Jamshedpur and Durgapur means a steady stream of refractory bricks, alloy castings and rolled product moves through pallet-and-strap operations. These are typically 8-12mm plywood-topped pallets rather than full crates.

What we ship into Dhanbad

We supply the Dhanbad belt across four product families, each tuned to a specific job on the packing floor.

Packing-grade plywood (commercial / rubberwood)

Ex-factory, GST and freight extra. These are the thicknesses that move most into the eastern industrial belt:

ThicknessTypical use in a Dhanbad packing line
6 mmLight spares cartons, internal partitions inside larger crates
8 mmMid-weight machinery boxes, top sheet on pallets
12 mmStandard wall ply for mining-spares export crates
16-18 mmHeavy-duty crate walls and pallet decks for sub-assemblies and castings

Okoume plywood for export-grade crates

For mining machinery going overseas — especially the refurbished dragline parts and crusher assemblies that ship to Africa and Southeast Asia — Okoume is a step up in face cleanliness, dimensional stability and humidity tolerance. We quote Okoume separately from the commercial card; it is the right call when the crate has to survive a coastal warehouse stint and a buyer inspection at the other end.

Crates, pallets and container flooring

Standard pallet sizes we run for the region are 1200x1000 mm and 1200x800 mm, with custom decks for odd-footprint equipment. Container flooring — the plywood sheet that goes down inside a 20'/40' container to spread load and protect machinery during handling — is typically 18 mm packing-grade ply cut to container ID. Crate framing uses jackwood or rubberwood runners; we quote jackwood at ₹400/cft and rubberwood by size.

Shuttering ply

For infra and EPC contractors working coal-handling plant civils or substation builds in the wider belt, shuttering ply is supplied film-faced for repeat pours.

Kochi to Dhanbad logistics

Dhanbad sits roughly 1,950 km from Kochi by road via the NH-44/NH-19 corridor through Bengaluru-Hyderabad-Nagpur-Raipur-Ranchi. Practical lane economics:

  • Mode: Direct truck (32-ft single-axle or 22-ft taurus) is the default for plywood loads. A full-truck consignment of 6-18 mm sheets moves cleanly on a single lane.
  • Transit time: 6-8 days door-to-door, weather and seasonal road closures permitting. The Ranchi-Dhanbad final leg adds half a day.
  • Sea + rail option: For very large repeat orders, FOB Cochin to Haldia/Kolkata by coastal vessel, then road into Dhanbad (approximately 280 km), can come out cheaper per CBM. We size this against the truck quote on each enquiry.
  • Documentation: E-way bills, GST invoicing and inter-state movement papers are issued from Kochi. ISPM-15 stamping is done at the packing-case stage where required.

Decision rules — which grade for which Dhanbad load

  1. Domestic mining-spares crates (BCCL/ECL vendor jobs, regional fabricators): Commercial packing-grade plywood, 12-16 mm walls, 18 mm deck. The packing card rates apply.
  2. Export crates for refurbished mining machinery: Okoume face, 12-18 mm depending on weight class. Quote separately; the face quality and dimensional stability are what the overseas buyer is paying for.
  3. Refractory and steel-ancillary pallets: 8-12 mm packing-grade top sheet, hardwood runners. High-cycle, low-spec.
  4. Coal-handling plant shuttering and substation civils: Film-faced shuttering ply, not packing grade.
  5. Container flooring for outbound machinery containers: 18 mm packing-grade ply, cut to container ID, supplied loose.

FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15 heat-treated crates for mining exports from Dhanbad?

Yes. ISPM-15 stamping is standard on export crates we build. Heat-treated timber framing with plywood skinning is the spec for mining-machinery and electrical-assembly exports going through Kolkata, Haldia, Nhava Sheva or Chennai.

Can you handle repeat monthly volumes for a Dhanbad packer?

Yes. We run monthly programmes for packing-case houses across India. Pricing is locked against a forecast volume; despatch is scheduled against the customer's truck plan. The right starting point is a thickness-and-quantity sheet by SKU.

Which is more economical for Dhanbad — truck from Kochi or coastal sea via Haldia?

Truck wins on speed and small-to-medium volumes (one to two trucks per consignment). Coastal sea + Haldia road can win on large repeat lots, typically four-plus truck-equivalents per shipment. We benchmark both on each enquiry.

Get a Dhanbad-specific quote

For a packing-floor manager or sourcing lead working out of Dhanbad, the fastest way to a usable number is to send us a thickness-wise quantity break, the destination pin code, and whether the load is domestic or export. We come back with ex-Kochi rates, a delivered-Dhanbad landed estimate, and an ISPM-15 note where it applies. Request a quote or browse the resource hub for grade selection, crate construction notes and packing-ply specifications before you send the enquiry.

The Cochin Wood group has been operating out of Perumbavoor since 1986 — forty years of group manufacturing in plywood and packing material. That is the bench we ship from when a Dhanbad crate has to land on a buyer's floor in spec.

title: Plywood Supply to Jamshedpur | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, hardwood pallets and export crates for the Adityapur estate and the wider East Singhbhum engineering belt. Our group.
html:
Why Jamshedpur is a serious plywood town

Jamshedpur is a planned steel city, and almost everything that moves out of it moves on wood. Tata Steel's flat and long products, Tata Motors' commercial vehicle aggregates, TRF's bulk material-handling equipment, Tata Cummins engines, and a deep ecosystem of tier-1 and tier-2 suppliers in the Adityapur belt all share a common downstream problem: how to box, brace and skid heavy industrial goods for road, rail and ocean transit without movement or damage. That problem is solved with packing-grade plywood, hardwood pallets, ISPM-15 crates and container flooring. The Cochin Wood group has been making plywood and sawn-timber products in Perumbavoor since 1986, and Jamshedpur is one of the eastern-belt destinations our packing range routinely heads to.

The Jamshedpur industrial picture

To size up packing demand from this city, it helps to look at the geography. Jamshedpur sits in the East Singhbhum district of Jharkhand, with industrial activity clustered in a few clearly identifiable zones.

The Tata works core

The original Tata Steel plant inside the city, plus the cold-rolling and tube-making units nearby, are anchors. Steel pipes, tubes, plates, coils and structurals leaving these mills need either heavy-duty open-top crates with wooden bolster bases or skid pallets sized to the coil cradle. Plywood here is mostly for end caps, dunnage and skid tops rather than full-box construction.

Adityapur Industrial Area (AIA)

Across the Kharkai river, AIA is one of the larger industrial estates in eastern India and the densest source of packing-plywood demand in the region. Auto-component makers, casting and forging units, machine shops, hydraulic and pneumatic suppliers and dozens of Tata Motors vendors operate from AIA's Phase I through Phase VIII. Their typical packing brief: small-to-medium wooden boxes built on a plywood base and cap, sized for 4 to 8 units per crate, sometimes returnable, often export-marked for Africa, the Gulf and Southeast Asia.

Gamharia, Jugsalai and the rail belt

Gamharia hosts heavier engineering — rolling-stock components, wagon parts, and bulk material handling gear. Crates here are larger and structural; the plywood spec moves from 6mm liners to 12mm and 18mm side panels capable of taking lashing point loads. Jugsalai and the inland container depot at Tatanagar handle the rail-leg outbound; container-flooring grade plywood shows up here for reefer and dry-box refurbishment.

What we ship to Jamshedpur

Packing-grade plywood — the workhorse

Our commercial / rubberwood packing plywood is the most-quoted line into Jharkhand. Standard thicknesses cover the full crate stack: 6mm and 8mm for top liners and division panels, 10mm and 12mm for side walls of medium crates, 15mm and 18mm for base panels and heavy-duty side walls. Sheets are made to a 1220 x 2440 mm (4' x 8') base, with custom-cut sizes available for repeat-order box makers who want zero offcuts.

Okoume face plywood — when the box has to look the part

For export crates that go through customs handling and sit on a buyer's loading dock under their brand mark, an Okoume face gives a cleaner finish, fewer surface defects and accepts stencil markings well. We supply Okoume-faced packing plywood as a parallel SKU; the cost premium is real, so it is usually specified only on top-tier export consignments.

Hardwood pallets and runners

For the AIA auto-component flow, jackwood and rubberwood pallet runners are the more common ask than full plywood pallets. Standard footprints we ship: 1200 x 800 mm (Euro), 1200 x 1000 mm (industrial), and 1100 x 1100 mm for the Southeast-Asia container fit. Block-style and stringer-style both available. Jackwood runners are quoted at Rs 400 / cft (with the standard quarter-inch-minus tolerance).

Container flooring grade

For container builders and refurbishment shops near the Tatanagar ICD, we supply 28mm container-floor plywood with the keruing/apitong-style hardwood face, hot-pressed and phenolic-bonded. This is a narrower spec — quote on request rather than off-the-shelf.

Shuttering plywood — the secondary line

Jamshedpur's infrastructure projects (steel-plant expansions, road overbridges around Mango and Bistupur, residential builds on the Adityapur side) consume film-faced shuttering plywood. We carry the standard 12mm and 18mm BWP-bonded shuttering ply for site contractors.

Kochi-to-Jamshedpur logistics

The road distance from our Perumbavoor (Ernakulam) loading point to Jamshedpur is approximately 1,900 to 2,000 km depending on which national-highway combination is chosen. The realistic transit lane runs Kochi to Bangalore on NH-544 / NH-44, then NH-44 north through Hyderabad and Nagpur, before turning east onto NH-49 and NH-33 through Raipur and Ranchi into Jamshedpur. Transit time for a full-truck load is typically 6 to 8 days by road, depending on driver shifts and seasonal road conditions in the central belt.

For sea-leg economics on heavier volumes, the alternative is Cochin Port to Haldia or Paradip, then a short inland leg to Jamshedpur. This is rarely cheaper for plywood — coastal shipping windows are long and the inland transfer eats the saving — so road remains the default lane. We size loads to 32-feet single-axle or multi-axle trailers; a typical packing-plywood truck carries around 18 to 22 tonnes depending on thickness mix.

Decision rules — which grade for which load

Load typeRecommended gradeTypical thickness
Auto-component export box (AIA)Commercial packing ply8 / 12 mm
Steel coil dunnage and skid topsCommercial packing ply12 / 18 mm
Heavy machinery crate, export-markedOkoume-faced packing ply12 / 18 mm
Container floor refurbishmentContainer-flooring ply28 mm
Site formwork, infra projectsBWP shuttering ply12 / 18 mm
Standard pallet constructionJackwood / rubberwood runnersAs per pallet drawing

Rule of thumb: if the box is going through a port and a buyer's audit, Okoume earns its premium. If it is staying inside a domestic supply chain or going on a road consignment, commercial packing grade is the right call.

FAQ

What is your typical lead time to Jamshedpur on a full-truck order?

For stocked thicknesses in commercial packing grade, around 7 to 10 days from PI confirmation — that covers production scheduling at the contract factory, loading at Perumbavoor, and the 6 to 8 day road transit to Adityapur or central Jamshedpur. Okoume-faced and custom-cut sizes add 3 to 5 days.

Do you supply ISPM-15 stamped pallets and crates?

We supply ISPM-15 compliant packing-grade plywood and hardwood pallet components. The actual ISPM-15 heat-treatment stamp is applied by registered packers at the box-assembly stage. For exporters who need the full assembled, stamped crate, we coordinate with treatment partners on request.

How are prices quoted — and what is included?

All rates are quoted ex-factory Perumbavoor. GST and transport are extra. Once the thickness mix, monthly quantity and delivery address are confirmed, a proforma invoice with the all-in landed Jamshedpur price is issued. For repeat consignees we set up monthly rate cards.

Get a Jamshedpur quote

If you are running a packing operation in Adityapur, a tier-1 vendor line for Tata Motors, an engineering exporter shipping out of the Tatanagar ICD, or a contractor needing shuttering ply on a Jharkhand project, send us your thickness mix, monthly quantity and delivery PIN code and we will respond with a landed price and a realistic dispatch window. Request a quote with your spec sheet, or browse our resource hub for grade comparisons, crate-construction notes and FOB Cochin reference material.

title: Plywood Supply to Ranchi | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, export crates, pallets and container flooring that Ranchi's mining, transformer and structural-fabrication packers.
html:
Ranchi sits in a strange and useful place on the Indian industrial map. It is the capital of Jharkhand, the state that holds the country's deepest reserves of coal, iron ore, copper and bauxite, and it is also home to one of India's oldest heavy-engineering footprints. When a city ships transformers, mining shovels, hydraulic presses and forged components out by rail and road, the packaging bill of materials looks very different from the average consumer-goods packer. It is built around heavy crates, hardwood pallets and load-bearing plywood — and that is precisely the order book the Cochin Wood group has been built around since 1986.

The Ranchi industrial picture

Three clusters drive most of the heavy-packaging demand in and around the city. The first is the HEC (Heavy Engineering Corporation) complex at Dhurwa — a 7,000-acre PSU campus that turned out blast-furnace equipment, mining shovels, walking draglines and steel-plant gear for half a century, and still does. The second is the Tatisilwai industrial area on the Ranchi-Purulia road, a state-promoted estate that hosts auto-components, structural fabrication, electrical equipment and a long tail of MSME engineering units. The third is the Tata footprint south-east of the city, anchored by Tata Steel's Noamundi and Jamshedpur operations and supported by the BIT-Mesra engineering ecosystem that feeds technical talent into the belt.

The packaging takeaway from this mix is consistent. Ranchi ships heavy, dense, low-density-per-cubic-metre goods — castings, structural assemblies, mining wear parts, transformers, machined components. Almost all of it travels in wooden export crates built to ISPM-15 standards, sits on hardwood or plywood pallets, and rides containers that need fresh plywood flooring when they arrive used. Corrugated and honeycomb packaging is a non-starter at this weight class.

What we ship into the Ranchi belt

The Cochin Wood group supplies four overlapping product lines that together cover most of the city's packaging-engineering needs. Each is built for a different load type, and the right choice usually comes down to the weight of the goods, the destination port and whether the consignment is one-way or returnable.

Packing-grade commercial / rubberwood plywood

This is the backbone product for crate manufacturers across Jharkhand. We ship 6 mm through 18 mm sheets in standard 8x4 and 7x4 sizes, with rubberwood and mixed-hardwood cores glued on phenolic resin for moisture resistance under monsoon transit. Typical Ranchi-bound thicknesses:

  • 6–8 mm — light-machinery cartons, internal partitions, spare-parts kits.
  • 10–12 mm — general-purpose export crates for engineering goods, transformer accessory boxes.
  • 15–18 mm — heavy-duty crates for mining equipment, castings, structural fabrications, and pallet top boards designed to take fork-lift point loads.

Okoume-faced plywood (premium export grade)

For exporters in the Ranchi belt who are crating goods bound for European or Gulf buyers with stricter cosmetic and uniformity expectations, Okoume veneer-faced plywood is the upgrade. It is lighter than rubberwood for the same panel size, takes a cleaner stencil and stamp, and is less prone to surface checking on long sea legs. We typically recommend it for high-value transformer enclosures, instrument crates and any consignment where the outer packaging itself is part of the brand impression.

Pallets and crate kits

Beyond raw sheets, we build finished pallets and pre-cut crate kits to drawing. Standard sizes shipped into eastern India are 1200x1000 mm and 1200x800 mm two-way and four-way entry pallets, with bottom-board options to suit slab and casting loads up to roughly 2.5 tonnes. Crate kits — pre-machined panels, runners and cleats — let Ranchi packers cut on-site assembly time and reduce wastage when they are running short of yard space.

Container flooring plywood

Used 20-ft and 40-ft containers that come off lease through Kolkata or Vizag almost always need a new floor before they are repurposed for static storage or short-haul movement. We supply 28 mm container-floor-grade plywood, hardwood-cored and phenolic-bonded, in the standard 2400x1160 mm panel that drops directly into a container chassis without re-cutting.

Kochi-to-Ranchi: how the consignment actually moves

The road distance from our Kochi/Perumbavoor dispatch point to Ranchi is roughly 1,900 km. We move two ways depending on order size and urgency.

ModeTypical laneTransit timeBest for
Full-truck roadKochi → Bengaluru → Hyderabad → Nagpur → Raipur → Ranchi6–8 days door-to-doorSingle-buyer 20–24 tonne consignments, time-sensitive crate kits
Coastal container + last-mile roadCochin Port → Haldia / Kolkata → road haul to Ranchi (~430 km)12–16 daysMulti-buyer pooled loads, container-floor plywood, lower per-sheet freight
Rail + roadErnakulam → Hatia (Ranchi) rake, road to estate8–10 daysLarger repeat orders, bulk packing-grade in mixed thicknesses

For most one-off Tatisilwai or HEC-area buyers in the 400–800 sheet range, full-truck road is the cleanest answer — single hand-off, single LR, no port handling damage risk. For repeating monthly orders above one container, the coastal route through Haldia is materially cheaper per square foot and is what we lean on for our larger eastern-India accounts.

Choosing the right grade for typical Ranchi loads

A simple decision rule works for most of the city's order patterns.

  • Use packing-grade rubberwood ply when the consignment is engineering goods bound for domestic destinations, the crate is one-trip, and the buyer cares about cost per square foot more than cosmetics. This is the right answer for most Tatisilwai and HEC-supplier cartons.
  • Use Okoume-faced ply when the consignment is export-bound, the end buyer is European or Gulf-based, and the crate carries high-value or branded equipment. Common for transformer exports, precision-machined components and instrument boxes.
  • Use shuttering ply when the requirement is actually civil/construction — formwork for HEC's expansion sheds, MSME factory shells in Tatisilwai, or BIT-Mesra infrastructure. Shuttering ply is film-faced and built for repeat concrete pours, not transit.
  • Use 28 mm container-flooring ply only for container repurposing or trailer-bed work — not for crates. The thickness is overkill for packaging and the cost does not pencil.

FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15 compliant material to Ranchi exporters?

Our packing-grade and Okoume plywood are bonded sheets — ISPM-15 fumigation/heat-treatment certification applies to the solid-wood components (runners, cleats, pallet blocks) that crate fabricators in Ranchi typically procure locally or from us bundled with the sheets. We can quote either way; let us know if you need the runners and pallet blocks included with the consignment.

What is the minimum order size you ship to Jharkhand?

Practical minimum for a direct full-truck dispatch is roughly 350–400 sheets of mixed thicknesses. Below that, we either pool with other eastern-India consignments or quote on a per-container basis through Haldia. Either way, a single sheet-count and thickness mix gets a clean number back inside 24 hours.

How do you handle quality and dimensional consistency at this distance?

Every dispatch is inspected at our Perumbavoor yard for thickness tolerance, core gaps and edge straightness before it leaves. For repeat Ranchi buyers we share batch-level dispatch records — sheet count by thickness, truck number, LR copy — so the receiving QC team can match consignment to PO without surprises.

Talk to us about your Ranchi requirement

If you are a crate fabricator in Tatisilwai, an export packer feeding the HEC supplier ecosystem, or an MSME engineering unit anywhere across the Ranchi-Jamshedpur corridor, we can quote a delivered-to-yard number against your thickness mix and monthly volume. Share the sheet sizes, thicknesses and approximate cadence and we will turn it around with freight built in. Request a quote for a Ranchi delivery, or browse our resource hub for grade comparisons, ISPM-15 notes and crate-design references.

title: Plywood Supply to Bhubaneswar | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring for the IT hardware corridor, the Kalinga steel belt and Odisha's port.
html:
Why Bhubaneswar Matters for a Kochi Plywood Supplier

Bhubaneswar sits at an unusual crossroads in India's industrial map. On one side, you have a maturing IT and electronics services hub that ships finished hardware, racks and lab equipment to clients across India and overseas. On the other, you have the Kalinga industrial belt feeding ITER (Kalinganagar) steel ancillaries, foundries and heavy-engineering exporters that crate machined components for sea freight. Both flows generate steady demand for packing-grade plywood, ISPM-15 crates, four-way pallets and the occasional container flooring repair. From our base in Kochi, the Cochin Wood group has been routing material to Odisha for several years on the same trucking lanes that feed Kolkata and Vizag — and Bhubaneswar's industrial estates are reachable through both rail-fed and road-fed channels.

The Bhubaneswar Industrial Picture

Buyers in Bhubaneswar split into a few distinct clusters, and each cluster wants slightly different plywood:

Infovalley and the IT/ITES corridor

Infocity-I, Infocity-II and the Infovalley corridor along NH-16 host data-handling, electronics R&D and IT hardware assembly units. The packing problem here is not weight — it is shock, humidity and theft resistance during multi-modal transit. Server racks, networking gear, lab-grade equipment and prototype hardware typically ship in 12 mm to 18 mm plywood crates with internal foam cradles. Edge protection and corner reinforcement matter more than raw thickness.

Kalinga belt and ITER steel ancillaries

The Kalinganagar industrial complex (about 100 km north of Bhubaneswar) and the auto-component clusters around Khurda anchor demand for heavy crate-and-pallet packing. Machined steel parts, forgings, valves and pumps from these belts go to Paradip and Visakhapatnam ports for export. Crate skids see real point-loads here, and that drives plywood-thickness decisions toward 18 mm hardwood-core grades with through-bolted bottom rails.

Bhubaneswar Industrial Estate, Mancheswar and Jagatpur

The older Mancheswar Industrial Estate and the adjacent Bhubaneswar Industrial Estate host a mix of light engineering, food processing, pharma packaging and ancillary fabrication. Demand from this belt is more fragmented — smaller crates, knockdown pallets, and bundles of plywood sheets sold to local packing-case makers who consolidate orders for downstream exporters.

Khurda and the export-back-haul

Khurda Road junction is a key freight aggregation point. Several export-focused units in textiles, leather goods and engineered ceramics use the Khurda-to-Paradip and Khurda-to-Vizag corridors. These exporters typically need ISPM-15 heat-treated crating that meets the destination country's phytosanitary norms.

What We Ship to Bhubaneswar

Packing-grade plywood (the workhorse SKU)

Our commercial / rubberwood packing-grade plywood is what most Bhubaneswar buyers actually order. Standard sizes are 8 ft x 4 ft (2440 x 1220 mm) in thicknesses from 6 mm to 18 mm. The most common pulls for Odisha:

  • 6 mm and 8 mm — top and bottom panels for lightweight electronics and IT hardware crates.
  • 12 mm and 15 mm — the everyday workhorse for general industrial export crating.
  • 18 mm — bottom skids, base panels and crates carrying heavy machined steel from the Kalinga belt.

Material is rubberwood-core, hot-pressed, glue line tuned for packing-case service. Tolerance on what the purchase market calls "11+" is a true 12 mm; "17+" is a true 18 mm. We quote ex-factory Kochi; GST and transport are extra and depend on the lane.

Crate construction

For finished crates, the practical kit is plywood skin over a sawn-timber frame (jackwood or seasoned hardwood runners at ₹400/cft, ¼-inch-minus tolerance), screwed and stapled, with corner cleats. ISPM-15-stamped heat-treated timber is sourced for any crate going to a sea-freight export buyer in the Kalinga belt or out of Paradip / Vizag.

Pallets

Standard four-way entry pallets in 1200 x 800 mm (Euro) and 1200 x 1000 mm (industrial) sizes are the bulk of pallet demand. Heavy-duty stringer pallets with 18 mm plywood top decks are specified for steel-component movement. Lighter two-way pallets ship for IT hardware staging.

Container flooring

Less frequent but real: container flooring plywood for the repair and refit shops that service the truck-trailer and reefer fleets running between Bhubaneswar, Paradip and the Vizag-Kolkata corridor. We supply 28 mm container flooring sheets on order.

Kochi to Bhubaneswar Logistics

The road distance from Kochi to Bhubaneswar is roughly 1,700 km. The practical trucking lane runs through Bangalore — Hyderabad — Vijayawada — Visakhapatnam — Bhubaneswar, broadly tracking NH-16 for the last 1,000 km. Single-driver transit is 4 to 5 days; team-driven trucks can run it in 3 days during favourable seasons.

Route segmentDistance (approx.)Mode
Kochi to Bangalore550 kmRoad (NH-544 / NH-44)
Bangalore to Vijayawada700 kmRoad (NH-44 / NH-65)
Vijayawada to Bhubaneswar~830 kmRoad (NH-16)

For full-truck loads (FTL) of 20 to 25 tonnes, FOR-Bhubaneswar pricing makes sense and we route through partner transporters. For smaller pulls, part-load consolidation through Vijayawada or Hyderabad transit hubs is cheaper but adds 2 to 3 days. Coastal sea-rail movement via Cochin Port to Paradip is technically possible but rarely beats road on door-to-door cost for plywood SKUs at typical Bhubaneswar order sizes.

Decision Rules: Packing vs Okoume vs Shuttering Ply

Bhubaneswar buyers occasionally ask which grade to specify. The practical answer depends on what is going inside the crate and where it lands:

  • Use our commercial packing-grade plywood for domestic crates and most export crates where the receiver is in South or Southeast Asia. It carries the load, it's competitively priced, and it ships fast.
  • Consider Okoume face-veneered packing plywood when the crate is going to a European or US receiver, when surface finish matters (e.g. branded electronics, premium machinery), or when humidity exposure on a long sea route is a concern. Okoume sits on the MR-Packing and BWR substrates as a premium face option — we quote it on request.
  • Specify shuttering ply only when the buyer is in construction or infrastructure. It's not a substitute for packing ply in crating — different glue line, different surface, different price point.

FAQ for Bhubaneswar Buyers

What is the minimum order quantity for a Bhubaneswar delivery?

For full-truck-load pricing, we recommend a minimum of roughly 18 to 22 tonnes of plywood per order, which translates to one full container or one full 20-foot truck. Smaller orders are quotable as part-loads, but freight per sheet is higher.

Do you supply ISPM-15 stamped crates from Kochi or do we get them stamped locally?

Either model works. We can supply ISPM-15 heat-treated timber and plywood-clad knock-down crates from Kochi for assembly at the buyer's site, or supply just the plywood sheets and rough sawn timber, leaving the buyer to use a Bhubaneswar-area ISPM-15 certified treater. The right choice depends on the buyer's existing crate-shop setup.

How do you handle quality complaints if a load reaches Bhubaneswar with damage?

Every dispatch carries a packing slip with batch numbers and thickness verification. If sheets arrive with transit damage or thickness variance outside our stated tolerance, we work the claim against photographic evidence taken at unloading. Practical, no-fuss.

Request a Quote

If you are sourcing packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets or container flooring for the Bhubaneswar industrial belt — whether you're a packing-case maker in Mancheswar, an exporter in the Kalinga corridor, or an IT hardware unit in Infovalley — we'd rather quote a specific load than send a generic price list. Send us your size mix, monthly volume, target thickness and delivery point, and we'll work back from FOR-Bhubaneswar pricing. Request a quote through our enquiry form, or browse the resource hub for technical notes on grades, glue lines and crate construction. The Cochin Wood group has been working out of Perumbavoor since 1986 — forty years of group manufacturing experience sits behind every load we dispatch.

title: Plywood Supply to Cuttack | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade and Okoume-faced plywood, export crates and pallets in Perumbavoor, where our group has run plywood operations since 1986. We.
html:
Cuttack sits at one of the more under-discussed industrial intersections in eastern India. The Mahanadi-Kathajodi twin-river city is the historic capital of Odisha, the home of filigree silver (Tarakasi), a serious handloom and textile cluster, and — less romantically but more relevant to anyone shipping plywood — the gateway to the Choudwar sponge iron and ferro-alloy belt. Every one of those sectors generates outbound cargo that has to be packed into wooden crates, stacked on pallets or sat on a container floor before it leaves the state. That is where the Cochin Wood group comes in.

The Cuttack industrial picture

To plan a plywood supply lane into a city, you have to understand what the city actually makes and ships. Cuttack does four things at industrial scale, and each one buys plywood differently.

Choudwar — sponge iron, ferro-alloys, steel

Choudwar Industrial Estate, about 12 km north of Cuttack city across the Mahanadi, is one of the original IDCO estates and the heart of the local metallurgical belt. Sponge iron (DRI) plants, induction furnaces, ferro-chrome units and re-rolling mills cluster here, extending up toward the Jagatpur and Athagarh nodes. The cargo is heavy, high-density and abrasive: billets, ingots, ferro-alloy lumps, machined castings, spares. For these loads the packaging question is rarely "does plywood look nice" — it is "will the box and pallet hold 800 kg without flexing".

Jagatpur, Madhupatna and the SME belt

Jagatpur Industrial Estate on the city's western fringe, plus Madhupatna and the older Khapuria cluster, host a long tail of engineering job-shops, auto-component units, transformer and switchgear assemblers, plastic moulders and food processors. This is classic export-crate territory — mixed SKUs, moderate weights, frequent shipments via Paradip or Kolkata.

Textiles and handloom — Bayanika, Sambalpuri, Cuttack handloom

Odisha's handloom and textile export footprint runs from Bargarh and Sambalpur through Cuttack, where weaver co-operatives, Odisha State Handloom (Boyanika) hubs and private exporters consolidate stock. Textile cargo is lighter but volumetric; it travels in marine-grade plywood crates with internal partitions and moisture protection.

Tarakasi filigree and high-value handicrafts

Cuttack's filigree silver, brassware and stone-craft exporters move low-volume, very high-value consignments. Air freight is common, and the crates have to be precision-fit with foam inserts. The plywood spec here is about face quality and dimensional accuracy, not raw strength.

What we ship to Cuttack

The Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing plywood in Perumbavoor since 1986, and the bulk of our pan-India dispatch today is built around the packing-case and export-crate sector. For the Cuttack mix above, the line-up looks like this.

Packing-grade plywood — the workhorse

Our commercial rubberwood packing-grade plywood is the default for sponge iron, ferro-alloy and engineering crates. Standard sheet sizes are 8x4 ft and 7x4 ft, with thicknesses from 6 mm through 18 mm. Indicative selling rates (ex-factory, GST and transport extra):

ThicknessRate (Rs/sq.ft)Typical Cuttack use
6 mm24Crate panels for light handicrafts, textile cartons
8 mm28Side panels for medium engineering crates
10 mm31.5General-purpose export boxes
12 mm33.5Sponge iron sample crates, auto-component boxes
15 mm39Heavy crate sides, transformer panels
18 mm42Crate bases, pallet decks, container flooring

Okoume face veneer — for handicraft and high-spec exports

Okoume is a face-veneer option on our packing and BWR grades — not a separate "grade" on its own. It gives a clean, light, tight-pored surface that handicraft and silver exporters in Cuttack like for presentation crates and ISPM-15 stencilled boxes that have to look as good as they perform. Okoume-faced sheets are quoted on request because the price moves with veneer availability.

Pallets and container flooring

For Choudwar's metallurgical loads and Paradip-bound textile consignments, the standard pallet decks we cut are 1200x1000 mm and 1100x1100 mm, generally 18 mm deck on a hardwood block-and-runner frame. Where customers run their own pallet shop, we supply the deck sheets in bulk and let them assemble. For 20-ft and 40-ft container floor repairs and heavy-cargo stuffing, 18 mm BWR packing ply in 8x4 ft is the workhorse.

Sawn timber for crate frames

We also dispatch jackwood and rubberwood runners alongside the plywood. Jackwood at Rs 400/cft (quarter-inch minus tolerance) is the usual frame for heavier crates; rubberwood runners are quoted to size.

Kochi to Cuttack — the logistics

Cuttack is roughly 1,700 km from our Perumbavoor dispatch yard by road. Two practical lanes exist:

  1. Road-direct via NH-16 (Chennai-Vijayawada-Visakhapatnam-Bhubaneswar): a 32-foot single-axle full truck takes 5-7 days door-to-door depending on weighbridge and check-post conditions. This is the right answer for full truckloads of 4-5 containers' worth of sheets.
  2. Coastal feeder via Cochin Port / Paradip: for large recurring requirements, sheet cargo can move as a coastal container from Cochin to Paradip, then road-trail the 75 km into Cuttack and Choudwar. Slower in transit but cheaper per sheet at scale, and avoids the inland fuel and toll burn.

For most Cuttack buyers placing their first order with us, the road-direct option wins on simplicity. Once monthly volume crosses a couple of truckloads, the coastal route starts paying back.

Decision rules — which grade for which Cuttack load

  • Sponge iron, ferro-alloy, billet crates (Choudwar): 12 mm or 15 mm commercial packing ply on a jackwood runner frame. Skip okoume — the load doesn't see it.
  • Engineering, auto-component, transformer exports (Jagatpur): 12 mm packing ply panels, 18 mm base. BWR upgrade if the route crosses monsoon transit.
  • Textile and handloom exports (Bayanika, private weavers): 6 mm or 8 mm packing ply with internal moisture-barrier liner. Cost-sensitive segment — don't over-spec.
  • Filigree, brass, stone handicrafts: okoume-faced packing ply, 8-10 mm, with precision foam inserts. Presentation matters as much as strength.
  • Pallet decks and container flooring: 18 mm BWR packing ply, 8x4 ft.
  • Shuttering ply is the right call only if the buyer is on a Cuttack construction site (Mahanadi bridge work, township builds, IDCO infrastructure). It is not a packing material — please don't use it as one.

FAQ

Do you have a depot or dealer in Cuttack?

Not at the moment. We dispatch direct from Perumbavoor to the buyer's yard in Choudwar, Jagatpur or wherever the crate workshop sits. For repeat buyers we hold a quote-locked rate so you don't have to re-negotiate every truck.

What's the minimum order to make Kochi-to-Cuttack worthwhile?

Practically, a full single-axle truck (about 600-700 sheets of 12 mm in 8x4 ft, or equivalent mix). Smaller orders work if they piggyback on an existing eastern-India dispatch — worth asking us when a lane is open.

Can you supply ISPM-15 compliant material?

The ISPM-15 stamp belongs on the finished crate from a registered fumigator, not on the raw plywood. We supply the panels and runners to spec; the heat-treatment certification is done at the crate workshop. Our hardwood ply already meets the moisture and species norms most Cuttack exporters need.

Request a quote

If you are building crates, pallets or container packs in Cuttack, Choudwar, Jagatpur or anywhere across coastal Odisha and want a Kochi-dispatched, group-manufactured plywood supply, the fastest path is a one-page enquiry with thickness, monthly volume and delivery address. Request a quote and a CWI sales engineer will respond with an ex-factory PI within a working day. For background reading on grades, ISPM-15, crate spec and pallet sizing, our resource hub collects the operator-level guides we use internally.

title: Plywood Supply to Paradip | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Paradip is a working port belt where almost every plywood need traces back to a refinery turnaround, a fertilizer campaign or project cargo crossing the.
html:
Paradip is a working port town first and a city second — almost every plywood need in the area traces back to a cargo flowing through the docks or a chemical line feeding them. Refinery shutdowns, fertilizer bagging campaigns, project-cargo movements out of the eastern coast — they all generate a steady, technical demand for packing-grade ply, sturdy crates, pallets and container flooring. This post explains what the Cochin Wood group ships into the Paradip industrial belt, how the Kochi-to-Paradip lane runs, and how we match grade to load for the cargo types Paradip exports and consumes.

The Paradip industrial picture

Paradip Port Trust handles iron ore, coking coal, thermal coal, POL products, fertilizer raw materials and a growing volume of containerised general cargo. The port is the anchor, but the demand for industrial packaging fans out across a tight cluster of plants and estates sitting within 25 km of the berths.

Refinery and petrochemical belt

The Indian Oil Corporation Paradip Refinery — a 15-MMTPA grassroots facility commissioned in 2016 — sits on the southern flank of the port. Around it has grown a downstream cluster: polypropylene units, MEG, paraxylene and an expanding petrochemical complex. Shutdown seasons, catalyst movements, spare-parts inflows and finished-goods despatch generate continuous demand for engineering-grade crates and heat-treated pallets.

Fertilizer and chemicals

IFFCO's Paradip phosphatic fertilizer complex (DAP, NPK) and the broader fertilizer-handling infrastructure at the port mean a constant flow of bagging machinery spares, instrumentation, valves and imported sub-assemblies — all of which arrive or leave in wooden cases. Paradip Phosphates Ltd. and the cluster of acid and ammonia handlers add to the load.

Port-side and project cargo

The Paradip Port industrial area, the IDCO industrial estate at Paradip and the Kalinganagar / Duburi industrial belt about 100 km inland (TATA Steel Kalinganagar, JSL, Neelachal Ispat) draw heavy project cargo through Paradip. Steel mills, power-plant equipment, transformers and heavy fabrications either land here or move out for export through this port — each consignment a multi-crate, multi-pallet job.

What we ship to Paradip

Almost every consignment we send eastbound is built around three product families: packing-grade plywood for case-makers, finished crates and pallets, and container flooring stock for trailer and reefer rebuilds. The mix shifts by season — fertilizer campaigns in pre-monsoon months, project cargo in Q3, refinery shutdowns scheduled around the turnaround calendar.

Packing-grade plywood — the workhorse

Our MR packing-grade plywood is the default specification for box-makers serving Paradip's fertilizer and petrochem buyers. Standard offerings:

  • Thickness range: 6 mm, 8 mm, 10 mm, 12 mm, 14 mm, 15 mm, 16 mm and 18 mm
  • Standard sheet size: 8 ft x 4 ft (2440 x 1220 mm) — custom-cut to box dimensions on request
  • Core: rubberwood / commercial hardwood, hot-pressed
  • Face veneer options: commercial, Gurjan or Okoume on the MR packing grade and on our BWR grade
  • Moisture: 8-12% kiln-conditioned, suited to coastal climate

For petrochem spares and refinery turnaround crates that travel under tarpaulin or hit monsoon transhipment, we recommend the BWR grade with an Okoume face — phenolic-bonded, marginally better against humidity ingress, and noticeably stiffer at the same thickness.

Crate construction

Finished crates leave our contract-factory floor cut, nailed and ISPM-15 heat-treated when the consignment is bound for export through Paradip. Typical specifications for the cargo profiles moving through the port:

Cargo typeRecommended cratePlywood spec
Refinery spares / valvesClosed box, batten-reinforced12 mm BWR ply, hardwood frame
Fertilizer machineryClosed box, ISPM-15 stamped15-18 mm packing-grade ply
Transformers / heavy electricalsSkid-mounted closed crate18 mm ply, hardwood skid runners
Steel fabrications (Kalinganagar)Open crate or pallet-mount12 mm ply decking, sawn runners

Pallets

Standard four-way entry pallets in 1200 x 1000 mm and 1200 x 800 mm (Euro), with custom sizes for fertilizer bagging lines and refinery part runs. Block pallets or stringer pallets, depending on whether the loading bay uses pallet jacks or forklifts. All export pallets are ISPM-15 treated and stamped on request.

Container flooring

Apitong-substitute container flooring stock — 28 mm phenolic-bonded ply, 2400 x 1160 mm planks — for trailer rebuilds and reefer / dry-van refurbishment work coming through the port-side transport workshops. Less common as a line item than crates, but a steady niche.

Kochi-to-Paradip logistics

The lane is long but well-trodden. Distance Kochi (Perumbavoor manufacturing belt) to Paradip Port is roughly 1,800 km by road via NH-66 / NH-16 — the east-coast highway through Bengaluru bypass, Vijayawada, Visakhapatnam and Bhubaneswar. Typical transit:

  • FTL truck (20 ft / 32 ft body): 4-5 days door-to-door, weather permitting
  • Container (FCL via Cochin Port to Paradip): coastal feeder routing, 7-10 days door-to-door — slower but useful for high-volume single-buyer despatches
  • Part-load (LCL trucking): 5-7 days, consolidation in Bengaluru or Hyderabad

For most plywood-only consignments under 12 tonnes we recommend FTL trucking — pricing is predictable, handling damage is lower, and ISPM-15 stamped crates clear pre-shipment inspection without container demurrage risk. For project-cargo lots where the buyer wants to stage stock at Paradip for a multi-month shutdown, FCL coastal containers from Cochin Port make sense.

Decision rules — which grade for which Paradip load

Three rough buckets cover most of what we send east:

Packing-grade ply (commercial face)

The default for fertilizer industry buyers, general-engineering crates and pallet manufacturers. Cost-effective, kiln-conditioned, glues and nails well, holds a 1.5-tonne unit load over a 1200 x 1000 mm pallet without bowing. This is what 70-80% of Paradip-bound orders ship as.

Okoume face on MR or BWR

For petrochem, refinery spares, instrumentation and export-bound consignments. Smoother face takes branding stencils cleanly, slightly lower weight per sheet, and the BWR variant tolerates the Paradip-monsoon transhipment window better than MR. Specify Okoume when the buyer asks for "exportable presentation" or when the cargo will sit at the port for more than two weeks.

Shuttering ply

For Paradip's infrastructure side — jetty extensions, refinery civil works, fertilizer plant expansions, township construction — film-faced shuttering plywood (12 mm, 18 mm) is the standard. Reusable for 8-15 pours depending on care. Order in 20-ft container loads to make the trucking lane economical.

Frequently asked questions

Do you supply ISPM-15 stamped crates for Paradip export consignments?

Yes. All export-bound crates are heat-treated at registered facilities and carry the IPPC stamp. Documentation is shared with the cargo invoice. Useful for petrochem spares re-exports, fertilizer machinery returns and steel-product shipments out of Paradip.

Can you cut plywood to my crate sizes before despatch?

Yes — sheet ripping and panel cutting to buyer-specified dimensions are part of the standard offer. Most fertilizer and petrochem case-makers in the Paradip belt prefer pre-cut panels because it removes on-site cutting labour and waste. Send the cut-list with the PO.

What's the minimum order quantity for a Kochi-to-Paradip truck?

An FTL load is roughly 8-10 tonnes (around 600-800 sheets of 18 mm packing ply, or a mixed load of crates and pallets). We do consolidate smaller orders into LCL movements, but FTL pricing per sq.ft. lands cheaper once you cross the half-truck mark — worth aggregating quarterly orders.

Asking for a quote

The Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing plywood at Perumbavoor since 1986 — about 40 years of group operations on this lane, with CWI Pvt Ltd as the more recent corporate entity that handles pan-India industrial buyers. We service Paradip's fertilizer, petrochem, project-cargo and port-side packaging sectors with packing-grade plywood, Okoume-faced BWR, shuttering ply, finished crates, ISPM-15 pallets and container flooring stock. To get a worked-up quote with the right grade, sizes and the trucking-lane economics for your monthly volume, request a quote with your cut-list and despatch frequency. For grade reference, packing-case specifications and ISPM-15 detail, our resource hub carries the technical guides.

title: Plywood Supply to Rourkela | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, Okoume-faced ply, crates and pallets in Perumbavoor, where our group has run plywood operations since 1986. From our.
html:
Rourkela sits in an unusual spot on the industrial map. A steel city by birth, an aerospace MRO city by recent ambition, and a hub for downstream fabrication that stretches across the Sundargarh belt. For anyone packing finished goods out of Rourkela, the question is rarely do we need plywood. The question is which thickness, which face, and how fast it can land on the factory floor. This post lays out how the Cochin Wood group supplies packing-grade plywood, export crates, pallets and container flooring into Rourkela from our Kochi/Ernakulam base, and what the Kochi-to-Odisha lane actually looks like.

The Rourkela industrial picture

Rourkela is anchored by the Rourkela Steel Plant (SAIL), one of the original integrated steel mills set up under the first wave of public-sector industrialisation. The plant feeds plate, coil, structurals and pig iron into downstream users across eastern India, and an enormous share of the city's logistics activity grows out of that single complex. Around SAIL, an entire ecosystem of fabricators, gas suppliers, refractory makers and engineering job-shops occupies the Kalunga Industrial Estate and the adjacent IDCO sheds on the Rourkela-Sundargarh corridor.

The second axis is aerospace. HAL's Sukhoi MRO facility at Rourkela handles overhaul work for fighter airframes and brings with it a defence-grade packaging requirement that is materially different from steel mill packing. Sub-assemblies, line-replaceable units, instrumentation and engine components need crates that survive multi-modal handling and meet stricter timber treatment specifications.

Around these two anchors sit the smaller but persistent sectors: ferro-alloy units in the broader Sundargarh-Sambalpur belt, downstream sponge iron plants, transformer and switchgear assemblers, and a growing set of engineering exporters who ship through Paradip and Visakhapatnam ports. Every one of those flows generates demand for plywood — either as raw sheet, as crate kits, as pallets, or as container flooring repair stock.

What this means for plywood demand

Steel packing tends to be heavy, low-aesthetic, repetitive. Aerospace and defence packing is lower volume, higher specification, with audit trails. Export-bound engineering goods sit in between. The Cochin Wood group's range is built to serve all three, but the grade decision matters — which we get to below.

What we ship into Rourkela

Packing-grade plywood (commercial / rubberwood core)

This is the volume product for the Rourkela belt. Hardwood-faced plywood, MR-bonded, manufactured to packing-case specifications rather than furniture cosmetics. Used by case-makers building crates for steel plates, machined components, transformer accessories and general engineering exports. Standard thicknesses in regular flow: 6 mm, 8 mm, 10 mm, 12 mm, 14 mm, 15 mm, 16 mm and 18 mm. Sheet size 8 ft x 4 ft is the workhorse; bespoke cuts are arranged where the crate bill-of-materials needs it.

Okoume-faced packing plywood

Where the crate is going to a European or Japanese consignee, or where the goods inside are high-value — aerospace spares, calibrated instruments, machined dies — Okoume face veneer is the option to ask about. It is a face-veneer specification on the MR-Packing or BWR core, not a separate grade family. The reason buyers pay the premium: a cleaner face that takes stencils and ISPM-15 marks legibly, tighter glue lines, fewer overlaps.

Shuttering plywood

Rourkela's construction load — new shed expansions inside the steel township, IDCO industrial sheds, HAL infrastructure work, and the routine civil contracts around SAIL — keeps shuttering ply in demand. 12 mm and 18 mm film-faced ply are the common pulls, sold by the sheet for formwork contractors.

Sawn timber, runners and pallet stock

Jackwood hardwood runners (¼ inch minus tolerance) ship out of Kochi by the cubic foot for case-makers who build their own pallets and crate skids. Rubberwood runners are offered to size. Standard pallet footprints for the Rourkela lane are 1200 x 1000 mm and 1200 x 800 mm (Euro), but most steel-plant pallet orders are bespoke to the load.

Container flooring stock

Container repair workshops along the eastern corridor pull 28 mm container flooring grade from time to time. We supply it on indent where the volume and specification justify the lead time; for one-off repairs, a packing-grade 18 mm sheet often does the job at lower cost.

Kochi to Rourkela: the lane

The straight road distance from Kochi to Rourkela is roughly 1,900 km. The practical trucking route runs Kochi → Salem → Bengaluru → Hyderabad → Vijayawada → Visakhapatnam → Bhubaneswar → Rourkela, or alternatively up the central spine through Hubli, Sholapur, Nagpur and Raipur. Transit time for a full truckload is typically 5–7 days depending on the season, the operator and the number of axles.

ModeTypical transitWhen it makes sense
Full truckload (FTL), 32 ft single axle5–7 daysSingle buyer, 600–800 sheets, predictable delivery slot
FTL, 32 ft multi-axle / 40 ft5–7 daysMixed loads of plywood + runners + pallet stock
Part-load (LTL) via Hyderabad hub7–10 days50–200 sheets, smaller fabricators
Coastal via Vizag port (rare)10–14 daysVery large indent loads where road economics break down

All prices are quoted ex-factory Kochi. Transport and GST are billed extra. For Rourkela, we routinely combine packing ply, runners and pallet stock into a single FTL so the buyer absorbs the freight across more material — bringing the landed cost per sq.ft down meaningfully.

Which grade for which Rourkela load

Use packing-grade plywood when

  • The crate is for steel mill output, fabricated components or general engineering exports inside India or to the GCC.
  • Cost-per-sheet is the dominant decision.
  • The consignee does not specify a face veneer.

Use Okoume-faced packing plywood when

  • The crate is going to Europe, Japan, the US or any consignee with stencil-legibility expectations.
  • The goods inside are high-value or audit-tracked — aerospace LRUs, calibration equipment, dies and moulds.
  • You need cleaner ISPM-15 mark visibility on the crate face.

Use shuttering plywood when

  • The use is formwork — civil contracts inside the steel township, IDCO sheds, or HAL infrastructure work.
  • Multi-pour reuse is expected and a film-faced surface is needed.

Use sawn timber / runners when

  • You are building the crate skid or pallet base yourself.
  • The load is heavy and a hardwood runner is required for fork-truck handling.

FAQ

How much lead time do you need for a Rourkela order?

For regular packing-grade thicknesses, 3–5 days to dispatch from Kochi once the proforma invoice is settled. Add 5–7 days transit. For Okoume-faced or non-standard thicknesses, plan on 7–10 days to dispatch.

Do you handle ISPM-15 heat treatment?

The plywood we ship is engineered wood and generally exempt from ISPM-15. Where the consignee requires HT-stamped solid wood components (runners, skids), we coordinate with treatment facilities so the final crate is compliant.

Can you supply cut-to-size for crate kits?

Yes, on indent. For Rourkela case-makers running standardised crate sizes, we cut sheets to the crate bill-of-materials before dispatch, which reduces on-site waste and saves labour at the fabrication shed.

Request a quote

If you are sourcing for a Rourkela operation — steel plant packing, aerospace MRO crates, IDCO-estate fabrication, or formwork for site expansion — send the thickness, sheet size, monthly quantity and delivery location, and we will revert with an ex-factory rate and an indicative landed cost to Rourkela. The Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing out of Perumbavoor since 1986, and the Kochi-to-Odisha lane is one we run regularly. Request a quote directly, or browse the resource hub for grade comparison sheets, packing case construction notes and ISPM-15 reference material.

title: Plywood Supply to Durgapur | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, hardwood runners and built-to-drawing crates that protect coils, plates and heavy machinery leaving Durgapur.
html:
Durgapur runs on heavy metal. The Durgapur Steel Plant rolls coils and plates that need to leave the city in protective wooden cases. Mining and Allied Machinery Corporation (MAMC) and the cluster of foundries, forging units and alloy steel makers around the DSP-MAMC belt all ship finished goods that have to survive a 1,500 to 2,500 km road or rail journey before they reach the end customer. Every one of those movements creates a plywood requirement — packing-grade sheets for case panels, hardwood runners for skids, and container flooring for export consolidation. This is the bread-and-butter of what we ship into West Bengal from Kochi, and Durgapur is one of the more interesting addresses on the lane because the load profile is so heavy and so specific.

The Durgapur industrial picture

Durgapur is a planned steel township in Paschim Bardhaman district, about 175 km north-west of Kolkata along NH-19. It is one of the older Tier-2 industrial cities in eastern India and the second-largest urban-industrial centre in West Bengal after Kolkata. For a plywood supplier, the city is interesting because the bulk demand is concentrated, not scattered — a handful of estates absorb most of the wooden packaging tonnage.

Estates and belts to know

  • Durgapur Steel Plant (SAIL) township and approach roads — coil packing, plate banding skids, and ancillary structural shipments.
  • MAMC industrial belt — heavy mining machinery, draglines, conveyor parts. These ship in custom-built crates with thick base boards.
  • Durgapur Projects Ltd (DPL) area — fabrication and power-equipment ancillaries.
  • WBIIDC industrial estates: Bidhannagar (Durgapur), Bamunara, Rajbandh, Muchipara, Gopalpur — secondary engineering, alloy steel re-rollers, refractories, and a growing cluster of light engineering units that crate finished goods for dispatch.
  • Sponge iron and ferro-alloy clusters around Raniganj–Andal–Pandaveswar — adjacent to Durgapur and pulling from the same packing supply chain.

The common thread is heavy goods that need wooden packaging built to handle weight and abrasion, not consumer-grade boxes. That is exactly the slot packing-grade plywood is designed for.

What we ship to Durgapur

The Cochin Wood group has been operating in Perumbavoor since 1986 — close to forty years of group manufacturing in plywood and allied wood products. Cochin Wood Industries Pvt Ltd, the corporate entity behind cochinwood.in, produces through contract factories under our own specifications, which is how we keep grade and thickness tolerance consistent across pan-India dispatches. For Durgapur, three product lines pull most of the volume.

1. Packing-grade plywood (commercial / rubberwood face)

Used for case sides, lids, base panels and partition boards inside steel-coil and machinery crates. Standard sheet size is 8 ft x 4 ft (2440 x 1220 mm). Indicative thickness range and ex-factory rates (GST and freight extra):

ThicknessTypical useEx-factory rate (Rs/sq.ft)
6 mmLight partitions, separators24
8 mmInner liners28
10 mmSide panels, light cases31.5
12 mmStandard case sides and lids33.5
15-16 mmHeavier crate panels39 - 39.5
18 mmBase boards, machinery crates42

For DSP coil packing the typical pull is 12 mm and 15 mm in volume, with 18 mm reserved for base boards under heavy coils.

2. Sawn timber runners and skids

Plywood panels are only half the crate. The structural skeleton — runners, headers, base skids — is sawn hardwood. We supply jackwood at Rs 400 per cft (quarter-inch minus tolerance) and rubberwood runners as per the sizes the customer's design calls for. Most Durgapur crate makers cut their own runners from sized stock, so we ship in standard scantling.

3. Container flooring and export crates

Exporters routing through Kolkata/Haldia ports for shipments out of eastern India often need 18 mm BWP-equivalent panels for container flooring or for crates that will see a sea voyage. Where the box has to last six weeks in marine humidity, we recommend stepping up to the boiling-water-resistant grade rather than running standard packing ply.

4. Okoume-faced premium packing

Okoume is a face-veneer option layered onto the packing or BWR core. It is the right choice when the consignee is overseas, when crate aesthetics matter (display goods, precision instruments) or when the carton needs a smoother, brand-printable surface. Quoted separately on request.

Kochi to Durgapur — the logistics reality

The road distance from Kochi to Durgapur is approximately 2,050 km. The practical route runs Kochi → Bengaluru → Hyderabad → Vijayawada → Vizag → Bhubaneswar → Kharagpur → Durgapur, mostly on NH-44 / NH-16 / NH-19. We dispatch in full-truck loads — a single 32-ft multi-axle trailer carries roughly 24-26 tonnes of sheet stock, and a container load works out cheaper per sq.ft than LCL once you cross about 800 sheets of 12 mm.

  • Transit time: typically 6-8 days door-to-door by road, depending on the season and Odisha-coast weather.
  • Rail option: for very large standing orders, parcel-rake or container-rake economics start to make sense, but most Durgapur buyers stay on road for flexibility.
  • Port routing: for export-bound buyers, we can also ship FOB Cochin or DDU Haldia/Kolkata when the end customer is overseas — useful for the MAMC-style heavy-machinery exporter.

Decision rules — which grade for which load?

Use packing-grade (commercial / rubberwood-faced) when:

  • The crate will be opened at the destination within 4-6 weeks.
  • The freight is road or short-haul rail within India.
  • Cost-per-sheet matters and the load is well under the panel's structural limit.

Use BWR / boiling-water-resistant when:

  • The crate sees humid storage, monsoon transit or a sea leg.
  • The end use is container flooring or marine-export packing.

Use shuttering ply when:

  • The need is concrete formwork for plant expansion, not packing — common for projects inside the DSP and DPL complexes.

Add an Okoume face when:

  • The crate carries premium or branded goods.
  • The buyer specifies a smoother surface or better screw-holding for repeated assembly.

FAQ

What is the minimum order quantity you ship to Durgapur?

Most Durgapur dispatches are full-truck loads. Mixed thickness on one truck is fine. For trial orders we can co-load with another West Bengal destination, but the freight per sq.ft is meaningfully better at full-truck volumes.

How long from order confirmation to delivery in Durgapur?

For standard packing-grade thicknesses we ship within 3-5 working days from advance confirmation, plus 6-8 days transit. Custom-cut sizes or Okoume-faced runs add a few days.

Are your rates inclusive of GST and freight?

The ex-factory rates published above are exclusive. GST (12% on plywood) and freight to Durgapur are quoted line-wise on the proforma invoice. Freight depends on diesel and return-load availability on the lane.

Request a quote

If you are crating steel coils, mining machinery, alloy castings or export consignments out of the Durgapur belt and want a quote from a Kerala-based supplier with forty years of group manufacturing history, the fastest route is to send us thickness, monthly quantity and delivery PIN. We will come back with an ex-factory rate, a landed-in-Durgapur estimate and a realistic dispatch schedule. Request a quote directly, or browse the resource hub for grade guides, packing-case construction notes and logistics references.

title: Plywood Supply to Asansol | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, crates and pallets that move heavy machinery, mining gear and finished steel out of the Asansol-Durgapur corridor.
html:
