Asansol sits at the heart of one of the densest heavy-industry corridors in eastern India — the coal pits of Raniganj, the integrated steel works at Burnpur, the Eastern Coalfields Limited (ECL) headquarters at Sanctoria, and the long backbone of NH-19 and NH-2 carrying out everything from coking coal and HR coils to mining electricals and pump skids. Every one of those movements needs wood: packing-grade plywood for export crates, hardwood pallets for forklift handling, runners and gluts for heavy castings, and container-floor-grade ply for stuffed export consignments leaving via Kolkata, Haldia and Paradip. This is the kind of industrial footprint that drives steady, repeating demand for the products the Cochin Wood group has been manufacturing in Perumbavoor since 1986.

The Asansol industrial picture

Asansol-Durgapur is officially treated as a single metropolitan industrial region under the Asansol Durgapur Development Authority (ADDA), and the wood-packaging demand is best understood belt by belt rather than as one block.

Coal and mining: Raniganj coalfield and ECL

Eastern Coalfields Limited, headquartered at Sanctoria in Dishergarh just outside Asansol, runs the Raniganj coalfield — one of the oldest mined coal basins in the country. The downstream packaging demand here is rarely about coal itself; it is about capital equipment going into the mines: longwall shearers, conveyor belt drums, switchgear, transformer panels, hydraulic roof supports, pumps, motors and spare-part shipments inbound from OEMs. All of these move in heavy plywood crates with ISPM-15 certified pallets underneath.

Steel: IISCO Burnpur and the auxiliary belt

The IISCO Steel Plant at Burnpur — now under SAIL — is the anchor steel facility, modernised to roughly 2.5 MTPA of crude steel. Around it sits a dense ring of rolling mills, foundries, refractory plants and steel-fabrication units across the Burnpur-Kulti-Hirapur axis. Demand here is for heavy-load crates around mill rolls, gear boxes, refractory blocks, and finished steel parcels being skidded for road movement. Pallets and runners tend to be 19 mm and thicker, with sawn hardwood gluts to take the point loads off the ply.

Engineering, cement and chemicals

The Durgapur side of the corridor adds Alloy Steels Plant, Mining and Allied Machinery, Bharat Heavy Electricals' Durgapur unit and several private engineering shops; the cement clusters around Chittaranjan and Salanpur add bagged-cargo and machinery packing demand. ADDA's Mangalpur and Bamunara industrial estates round out the SME base — typical buyers here are engineering job shops crating sub-assemblies for OEMs further down the line.

Railways and exports

Chittaranjan Locomotive Works (CLW) — one of the largest locomotive builders in the world — is a short drive from Asansol and ships spares and sub-assemblies in heavy ply crates. Export-oriented consignments out of the region typically route through Kolkata (Haldia or Kolkata Dock System) for break-bulk, or stuff into containers for sea export, which is where container-floor-grade and BWR ply come in.

What the Cochin Wood group ships into the Asansol belt

The product mix that lands well in this corridor is narrower than our full range. These are the SKUs that move:

Packing-grade plywood (commercial / rubberwood core)

The bread-and-butter SKU for crate makers. Standard 8 ft x 4 ft sheets, IS 303 MR-bonded, in the thicknesses listed below. Selling rates are ex-Perumbavoor factory; GST and transport are extra and quoted on the truck size.

ThicknessTypical useIndicative ₹/sq.ft (ex-factory)
6 mmLight export cartons, panel separators24.0
8 mmLight crates, side panels28.0
10 mmMedium crates, internal partitions31.5
12 mmGeneral packing-case standard33.5
15 mm / 16 mmHeavy crates, machinery bases39.0 / 39.5
18 mmHeavy export, container flooring, base boards42.0

Okoume-faced packing plywood

Where the consignment is high-value or the receiver is overseas — typically OEM machinery, export-grade castings or anything that has to look the part inside an air-freight crate — we offer Okoume face veneer on the same MR-Packing or BWR core. It machines cleanly, nails without splitting and presents better at customs inspection. Quoted on request.

Hardwood pallets and runners

Standard sizes we build to: 1200 x 1000 mm and 1200 x 800 mm two-way and four-way entry. Heavier 1219 x 1016 mm (48 in x 40 in) GMA-style pallets for North American export consignments. Jackwood and rubberwood runners are supplied at ₹400/cft (jackwood) and as-per-size (rubberwood); the standard tolerance reads as ¼ inch minus on jackwood.

Container flooring and shuttering ply

For stuffed export containers we supply 18 mm BWR-grade ply suited for floor lining. For the construction side — slab work at the cement, power and infra projects in the corridor — film-faced shuttering plywood is available in standard sheet sizes.

Kochi to Asansol: the logistics economics

Asansol is roughly 2,000 km by road from Kochi. We do not pretend that is a short trip — but for repeating bulk orders the trucking lane is well-established and predictable.

  • Road, full-truck: 32-ft single-axle or multi-axle from Perumbavoor / Kochi, routed via Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Nagpur, Raipur and into West Bengal — transit typically 7 to 9 days door-to-door, depending on the trailer type and the season.
  • Coastal + road: for very large volumes we can break the trip — sea leg from Cochin Port (Vallarpadam/ICTT) up to Haldia or Kolkata, then short-haul road into Asansol (around 230 km from Kolkata). This usually only pencils out above 2-3 container-equivalents per consignment.
  • Rail container: CONCOR / private container trains from south India terminals to ICDs in the east are available; lane economics shift quarterly so we price each load individually.

Volume-wise, a single 32-ft trailer carries roughly 1,000-1,200 sheets of 12 mm packing ply depending on the stacking plan. Truck-load economics are the single biggest variable in the landed price into Asansol, which is why we quote ex-factory and transport separately rather than bury it in the sheet rate.

Decision rules: which grade for which load

  1. Inland packing, single-trip crates inside India: MR-bonded packing-grade ply, 12 mm sides, 18 mm base. Lowest landed cost per crate.
  2. Export crates, OEM machinery, sea voyage: step up to BWR core, Okoume face on the visible surfaces, 18 mm base with hardwood gluts under heavy point loads.
  3. Container floor lining and dunnage: 18 mm BWR. Single sheet across the container width where possible to avoid joints under wheel loads.
  4. Mining / heavy electrical equipment crates: 15 to 18 mm sides, hardwood corner posts, base sized for the lifting-tackle profile rather than the cargo footprint.
  5. Construction slab work in the Asansol-Durgapur corridor: film-faced shuttering ply, not packing-grade. The two are not interchangeable.

FAQ for Asansol buyers

Is it worth buying plywood from Kerala when sources in Yamunanagar or Kolkata are closer?

For one-off small orders, no. For repeating bulk packing-grade demand — say a full truck a month or more — Kerala mill rates plus our trucking lane are competitive on landed cost, and the consistency of the core construction tends to be tighter because of the contract-factory specification model we operate.

Do you supply ISPM-15 heat-treated pallets for export?

Yes. ISPM-15 compliant heat-treated and stamped pallets and crates are available; we quote the stamp and the certificate together with the order.

What is the minimum order size you ship into West Bengal?

The economics work cleanly from a full 32-ft trailer upward. We can consolidate smaller part-loads where another consignment is already routed in the same direction, but the per-sheet rate moves up accordingly.

Asking for a quote

If you are running a packing operation, an export crate workshop, a pallet line, or an EPC project in the Asansol-Durgapur belt, the cleanest way to get a workable number is to send us the thickness, sheet size, monthly quantity and delivery pin code. We will come back with an ex-factory rate, an indicative trucking cost on the Kochi-Asansol lane, and a sample of the relevant grade if you need to test it before placing the first order. Request a quote, or if you want to read the underlying specs and certifications before talking commercials, the resource hub has the grade sheets and FAQs in one place. Forty years of group manufacturing experience sit behind every sheet that leaves Perumbavoor — that is the operating context, and we are happy to be measured on the load that actually arrives in Asansol.

title: Plywood Supply to Howrah | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, wooden crates, pallets and container flooring that Howrah's casting and machinery shipments ride on. Our group has.
html:
Howrah is one of the few Indian cities where the smell of casting sand still hangs in the air alongside the clatter of jobbing machine shops. If you walk through Belur, Liluah, Salkia or the Andul Road belt, you will see foundries pouring grey iron, fabrication units rolling boiler shells, and engineering exporters preparing crates for shipments that often weigh more than a small car. Every one of those shipments needs wood underneath it, wood around it, and wood between it and the container floor. That is the demand we ship into from Kochi, and this guide explains how a packing-grade plywood, crate, pallet and container-flooring programme works for buyers in the Howrah industrial belt.

The Howrah industrial picture

Howrah district sits on the west bank of the Hooghly and is, in practical terms, the foundry and engineering capital of eastern India. The buyer profile here is concentrated in a small number of well-known belts:

  • Liluah and Bamangachi — historically a railway workshop town, now dense with structural fabrication, forging shops and ancillary engineering units feeding the railways and the steel sector.
  • Belur and Bally — heavy fabrication, jute machinery, marine equipment and a long-standing cluster of grey-iron and SG-iron foundries.
  • Salkia and Ghusuri — older mixed-use industrial pockets with sheet-metal, hardware and packaging conversion units.
  • Dasnagar, Domjur and Andul Road — newer engineering, casting and export-oriented sheds, including some of the larger pump, valve and switchgear units that ship to the Gulf and South-East Asia.
  • Howrah Dock and the Kolkata Port system — Netaji Subhas Dock, Khidirpur and the Haldia outport handle the export side; a lot of crated cargo from Howrah moves through these gates.

The thread that runs through all of these is heavy, dense, point-loaded cargo: castings, machined components, pump bodies, valve assemblies, transformer parts, switchgear cabinets and structural sub-assemblies. That cargo needs packaging engineered for weight and shock, not for shelf appeal — which is why the demand pattern here leans firmly toward packing-grade hardwood plywood rather than decorative material.

What we ship to Howrah

The Cochin Wood group has been making packing-grade plywood out of Perumbavoor since 1986, and the current Cochin Wood Industries programme is built around the sectors above. A typical Howrah order falls into one of four buckets:

1. Packing-grade plywood sheets

This is our core line — hardwood-core, MR-bonded plywood specifically engineered for one-trip industrial packaging rather than furniture. Standard sizes are 8x4 ft, with thicknesses pulled from this card:

ThicknessTypical use in Howrah crates
6 mmLightweight lids, dunnage separators, void fillers
8 mmSide panels for medium crates up to ~500 kg
10–12 mmSides and tops for general engineering crates 500–2,000 kg
15–16 mmHeavy crate sides, base sheathing for foundry castings
18 mmCrate bases, skid sheathing, container flooring patches

For Howrah's casting and machinery loads, the 12 mm, 15 mm and 18 mm sheets do most of the work. We rate prices ex-factory; GST and transport are extra, and we quote against a confirmed thickness-size-quantity-destination set rather than a generic list.

2. Wooden crates and packing cases

For buyers who would rather receive finished crates than convert sheets in-house, we supply crates built to drawing — typically softwood or hardwood battened frames sheathed with our packing-grade plywood, screwed or nailed to engineering specification. ISPM-15 heat-treated and IPPC-marked timber is available for any export crate, which matters for almost every shipment heading out through Kolkata or Haldia to the Gulf, South-East Asia, Europe or North America.

3. Wooden pallets

Common pallet sizes for the Howrah belt are 1200 x 1000 mm (general engineering), 1200 x 800 mm (Euro footprint for European exporters) and 1100 x 1100 mm (common in container stuffing for Far East trade). Pallets are built from hardwood runners with plywood or sawn-timber decks, again ISPM-15 stamped where the consignee requires it.

4. Container flooring and dunnage

For exporters who stuff their own containers — especially foundries shipping castings and machine builders shipping skidded equipment — 18 mm packing-grade plywood is widely used as container flooring overlay, deck patching and heavy dunnage between rows. We ship full bundles for this purpose alongside the main crate order.

Kochi-to-Howrah logistics

Howrah sits roughly 2,000 km by road from Kochi. The standard movement is a full-truck load on the NH-66 → NH-44 → NH-16 corridor via Bengaluru, Vijayawada and Bhubaneswar, with transit typically running 5–7 days door-to-door depending on season, driver-change pattern and the gate timings at the receiving estate. For very large repeat programmes, coastal sailings from Cochin Port to Kolkata or Haldia are sometimes a cleaner economic option, particularly for full 20-ft or 40-ft consignments of finished crates and pallets where road damage risk is higher.

In either case, freight and GST are quoted separately from the ex-factory rate, and we plan dispatch around the buyer's stuffing or production calendar rather than around our own. Loading plans, truck capacity, headroom and unloading-charge expectations are documented up front on the proforma invoice.

Decision rules — packing grade vs Okoume vs shuttering ply

Buyers in Howrah occasionally ask whether they should be specifying Okoume face or even shuttering ply for their crates. A simple rule of thumb:

  • Hardwood packing grade — default choice for foundry castings, structural fabrication, pump and valve crates, and any one-trip export packaging. Strong, fairly priced, and engineered for the job.
  • Okoume face on packing/BWR grade — worth the premium when the consignee is sensitive to face appearance (premium equipment, GCC and European projects), or where the crate doubles as display packaging on site.
  • Shuttering plywood — only when the customer is actually a construction or infrastructure buyer, not a crate buyer. Shuttering grades are formulated for repeat concrete pours, not for one-trip packaging.

For most Howrah enquiries — a foundry sending castings to a Gulf project, or an engineering exporter crating valves for Vietnam — the right answer is hardwood packing grade in 12, 15 and 18 mm, with ISPM-15 timber framing where the destination demands it.

FAQ for Howrah buyers

What is a realistic lead time from Kochi to Howrah?

Plan on 5–7 days road transit after dispatch for a full truck. For first-time orders, build in an extra 3–5 working days for sheet production, crate fabrication and quality check before the truck moves.

Do you supply ISPM-15 certified crates and pallets?

Yes. Any crate or pallet destined for export can be built from heat-treated, IPPC-stamped timber. We note this requirement at PI stage so it flows through to the dispatch paperwork.

Can you handle blanket orders for monthly drawdown?

Yes. For Howrah buyers with steady monthly volumes — particularly foundries and engineering exporters with predictable shipment calendars — we run blanket arrangements with a fixed price for a defined period and call-off dispatches against the agreed volume.

Request a quote

If you are sourcing packing-grade plywood, wooden crates, pallets or container flooring for a unit in Liluah, Belur, Salkia, Domjur or anywhere else in the Howrah belt, the fastest path is to request a quote with your thickness, size, monthly quantity and delivery point. The team will come back with an ex-factory rate, an indicative freight figure on the Kochi-Howrah lane, and a build plan for any crate or pallet specifications you share. Specs, grade comparisons and case studies are on the resource hub for buyers who want to dig into the technical side first.

title: Plywood Supply to Kolkata | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, crate kits and pallets in Perumbavoor and ship them into the Kolkata-Howrah belt every month. Our group has run plywood.
html:
Kolkata sits at a junction that very few Indian cities can claim: a working deep-water river port, a century-old industrial belt across the Hooghly in Howrah, and an export base built on jute, tea, leather, and engineering goods that all have to leave the country inside a wooden box. That last detail is where Cochin Wood Industries comes in. We are a Kerala-based plywood supplier shipping packing-grade ply, crate kits, ISPM-15 pallets and container flooring into the Kolkata-Howrah belt every month, and this page lays out what we send, how we send it, and how to decide what fits a Kolkata buyer's load.

The Kolkata industrial picture in one read

Greater Kolkata is not a single market — it is at least four overlapping ones, and each has its own packing logic.

  • Kolkata Dock System and Haldia Dock Complex (Syama Prasad Mookerjee Port Authority): break-bulk and container traffic for engineering goods, tea chests, jute bales, and project cargo. Crate-heavy.
  • Howrah industrial belt — Liluah, Belur, Bally, Dasnagar, Salkia: foundries, light engineering, wire and bar units, fan and pump makers. Heavy, dense loads that need stiff plywood cases and pallets.
  • Kasba, Topsia and Tangra: leather goods, tannery output and small engineering — lighter export crates, often on jute-and-ply combination packing.
  • Falta SEZ, Bantala Leather Complex, Kalyani and Dankuni industrial zones: export-oriented units with steady ISPM-15 pallet and case demand.

Add the tea auction trade routed through Kolkata from North Bengal and Assam, the jute mills along the Hooghly from Titagarh down to Budge Budge, and the project cargo that moves through Khidderpore for power, metro and refinery work in the East and North-East, and the picture is consistent: Kolkata is a packing-and-shipping city, and packing-grade plywood is one of its working consumables.

What we ship to Kolkata

Cochin Wood Industries is a plywood manufacturer running through contract factories under our own specifications. The Cochin Wood group has operated in Perumbavoor since 1986, and forty years of group manufacturing sits behind every sheet that leaves for the eastern lane. To Kolkata buyers we typically dispatch the following lines.

Packing-grade plywood (MR / commercial)

The workhorse for crate panels, case lids, internal partitions and engineered packing. Standard sheet is 8 ft × 4 ft, with thickness commonly stocked from 6 mm to 18 mm. The thicknesses Kolkata foundry packers ask for most often are 8 mm, 12 mm and 18 mm — 8 mm for case sides and tops on light engineering loads, 12 mm for medium-duty machinery crates, 18 mm for heavy base panels and pallet decks. Face veneer options on MR-Packing and BWR grades include Okoume and Gurjan; Okoume is the lighter, cleaner face preferred by exporters who pay container freight by weight.

Crate kits and case-ready cuts

For repeat exporters in Howrah and Falta we ship pre-cut panel sets keyed to a drawing: base, four sides, top, and corner runners. The kit lands at the customer's nail-gun line and assembles directly. This saves the buyer cutting wastage and avoids dust on the shop floor — important for tea and food-grade exporters in particular.

Pallets — ISPM-15 stamped

Standard footprints we ship east are 1200 × 1000 mm (the European pallet most engineering exporters use), 1200 × 800 mm (Euro pallet), and 1100 × 1100 mm (used in some South-East Asia and Far-East lanes that Kolkata exporters serve). Heat-treatment and the IPPC stamp are mandatory for all wood packaging leaving Indian ports under ISPM-15; every pallet we dispatch carries the stamp and the lot record.

Container flooring and sawn timber

For the heavier project loads that move out of Khidderpore — transformers, paper-mill rolls, heavy castings — we supply 28 mm container flooring grade plywood and dressed sawn timber runners for skids and dunnage. Jackwood runner stock and rubberwood runners are available in the sizes most lashing crews specify.

Kochi to Kolkata: the logistics

The distance from Perumbavoor / Kochi to Kolkata is roughly 2,000 km by road. Practical lane behaviour:

ModeTransit timeTypical use
Full-truck (32-ft / 20-ft multi-axle)6–8 days door-to-doorFull-load sheet stock, bundled crate kits, pallet decks
Part-load (LTL via Bengaluru / Hyderabad hubs)9–12 daysSmaller repeat orders, sample crates
Coastal container (Cochin → Kolkata / Haldia)10–14 days port-to-portLarger consolidated loads where coastal economics work out

The road route runs Kochi — Bengaluru — Hyderabad — Vijayawada — Visakhapatnam — Bhubaneswar — Kharagpur — Kolkata, mostly on NH-16 once east of Hyderabad. Coastal shipping via Cochin Port to Kolkata or Haldia is occasionally the better option for full-container moves of pallet-deck stock; we work it out per quote.

Picking the right grade for a Kolkata load

The decision rule is usually about the destination, the freight, and the customer of the customer. A few practical patterns:

  • Domestic / regional engineering crate moving from Howrah to North-East or to a project site: MR-grade packing plywood, 12–18 mm, Gurjan face. Cheapest cost per crate where freight isn't the binding constraint.
  • Export crate (Europe, USA, Australia): BWR / BWP plywood with Okoume face. Lower density means lighter crate, which matters when sea-freight is rated on chargeable weight or when an air-freight conversion is on the table.
  • Wet-zone packing (jute mills, tea cartons holding for monsoon): BWR grade — boiling-water resistant glue line. Higher upfront cost, lower failure rate during Bengal's long humid stretch.
  • Shuttering / formwork for construction: a separate film-faced product. Not the same as packing ply. Use it for civil work, not for crates.

Our pricing is structured ex-factory with GST and transport extra. Detail thicknesses, monthly quantity and the delivery zone (Howrah, Khidderpore, Falta, Dankuni, Kalyani etc.) and we will work a landed cost for your gate.

FAQ — what Kolkata buyers usually ask

Do you supply directly to Howrah and Liluah small-unit packers, or only to large exporters?

Both. Our minimum dispatch is typically a part-truck for sheet stock or a single pallet skid for samples. We work with small Howrah engineering packers as readily as with Falta SEZ exporters.

What is your lead time once a PI is confirmed?

Standard packing-grade ply: 4–6 working days for production-ready stock and dispatch from Kochi, then the road or coastal transit on top. Custom-cut crate kits add 2–3 days of cutting.

Can you handle ISPM-15 documentation for our shipping line?

Yes. Every pallet and crate carries the IPPC stamp and we supply the heat-treatment certificate along with the invoice and e-way bill. Most Kolkata CHA offices accept the format directly.

Request a quote

If you are running packing for Howrah engineering exports, tea or jute consignments through Kolkata Port, or a project-cargo move out of Khidderpore, we would like to quote your next month's requirement. Share thickness, sheet size, monthly volume and the delivery zone and we'll come back with an ex-Kochi rate plus a landed price at your gate. Request a quote here, or read the full packing and grade reference in our resource hub before you ask. The Cochin Wood group has been making plywood for the eastern packing trade for forty years; the lane to Kolkata is one we know well.

title: Plywood Supply to Siliguri | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, made-up crates, pallets and container flooring for the tea-export and cross-border cargo trade that moves through.
html:
Why Siliguri sits on a different supply map

Siliguri is not a typical mid-size industrial town. It is the bottleneck of the Siliguri Corridor — the 22-km-wide strip of land that connects mainland India to the entire Northeast — and it doubles as the staging post for four international borders: Nepal at Panitanki, Bhutan via Jaigaon and Phuentsholing, and Bangladesh through Fulbari and Changrabandha. Any business that crates tea, repacks border-trade cargo, ships engineering goods to the Northeast, or moves consolidated freight through NJP rail yard ends up buying plywood, sawn timber, pallets and container-floor boards. We supply that material from Kochi across the country, and Siliguri's mix of tea-export packing and cross-border transshipment is one of the more interesting demand profiles in eastern India.

The Siliguri industrial picture

The packing-grade plywood demand in Siliguri spreads across several distinct belts rather than one large estate. Understanding where your buyer sits often decides the truck's drop point and the size of the order.

Industrial belts and clusters

  • Matigara industrial area — light engineering units, food processing, and a cluster of packing-case workshops that feed tea estates uphill.
  • Dabgram industrial estate (WBIDC) — the largest organised estate in the city, mixed-use with steel fabrication, agri-equipment, and consumer-goods assembly. Pallet and crate consumption here is steady and recurring.
  • Salugara and Fulbari belts — closer to the Bangladesh border crossing, dominated by transshipment yards, jute-and-agri export packers, and timber re-handling operations.
  • Bagdogra and surrounding tea-handling depots — Darjeeling, Dooars and Terai tea moves through Siliguri auction centres and private warehouses before export. Plywood tea chests, shooks, and inner-liner boards are a year-round line item.

Sectors driving plywood demand

  • Tea export packing — chests, cartons-with-ply-base, pallets for container stuffing at Kolkata/Haldia.
  • Border-trade cargo — Bhutan-bound engineering and FMCG goods, Nepal-bound bulk consumer goods, Bangladesh transshipment.
  • Automotive spares and two-wheeler parts repacked for Northeast distribution.
  • Pharma and FMCG warehousing — ISPM-15 pallets, secondary packaging.
  • Construction across Siliguri's high-growth real-estate corridor along NH27 — shuttering plywood for slab and column work.

What we ship into Siliguri

Most Siliguri orders fall into four buckets. The specs below are the standard CWI offer; we tune thickness and face-veneer to the buyer's box-design or load condition.

Packing-grade plywood for crates and cases

Our MR-Packing grade is the workhorse. Sheet size 8x4 ft, thicknesses 6 mm to 18 mm. Face-veneer options include hardwood, Gurjan and Okoume — Gurjan and Okoume are veneer choices on the same packing/BWR substrate, not separate grades. For tea-chest plywood and inner liners, 4 mm and 6 mm move fastest; for one-trip export crates of machinery, 12 mm and 18 mm are the common picks.

Made-up crates and shooks

For buyers who want crate kits rather than raw sheets, we cut and bundle shooks (sides, ends, tops, bottoms, runners) to drawing. This is useful for tea exporters who do not want to run a saw shop on-site and for engineering goods packers handling odd-lot dimensions.

Pallets

Two-way and four-way entry pallets in standard 1200x1000 mm and 1200x800 mm footprints, plus custom sizes for tea-chest stacking. Heat-treated and ISPM-15 stamped on request for export consignments through Kolkata and Haldia.

Container flooring

28 mm BWP container-floor plywood, hardwood face, phenolic film options. Demand in Siliguri comes from container-repair yards and from operators rebuilding the floors of long-haul reefer and dry boxes used on the Bhutan-Bangladesh-NE India circuit.

Kochi-to-Siliguri logistics

The trunk distance from Kochi/Ernakulam to Siliguri is approximately 2,400 km by road. We work two lanes depending on volume and timeline.

ModeRouteTransit (working days)Best for
Direct roadNH-544 / NH-44 / NH-27 via Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Nagpur, Kolkata, NH27 to Siliguri7-10 daysFull-truck orders, urgent crate kits
Rail + last-mile roadErnakulam to New Jalpaiguri (NJP) goods rake, then 12 km road to consignee10-14 daysHeavy bulk orders, cost-sensitive buyers
Coastal + roadCochin Port to Kolkata/Haldia, then ~600 km road to Siliguri via NH12 and NH2712-16 daysContainer-flooring quantities, mixed export loads

Most buyers default to the direct road lane. We quote ex-factory Perumbavoor; freight is billed separately and is the honest variable — we do not bundle a padded freight figure into the sheet rate.

Decision rules — which grade for which Siliguri load

The three grade-and-veneer choices that come up most often:

  • MR-Packing with hardwood face — default for domestic crates, tea-chest secondary packaging, Bhutan-bound consumer goods. Best price-to-performance for single-trip wood boxes.
  • BWR substrate with Okoume face — when the box has to look presentable at the consignee, when the cargo is moisture-sensitive, or when the buyer is invoicing an overseas customer who specifies a uniform light face-veneer. Common in premium tea exports and engineering goods crated for GCC and EU destinations through Kolkata.
  • Shuttering plywood (filmfaced) — only for the construction buyer. The Siliguri real-estate belt along Sevoke Road and Matigara uses 12 mm and 18 mm filmfaced ply for slab and column work. Do not confuse this with packing demand; the buyer profile and reuse expectation are different.

FAQ

Do you supply ISPM-15 heat-treated pallets for tea and engineering exports?

Yes. Pallets are heat-treated and stamped at the contract-factory level. We quote per pallet with the stamp included; the buyer gets the phytosanitary documentation needed for export consignments routed through Kolkata, Haldia or via land borders.

What is the minimum order for a direct shipment to Siliguri?

Full-truck (about 18-22 tonnes / roughly 350-500 sheets depending on thickness) is the cleanest economics. Smaller orders are possible on the rail + last-mile lane but the per-sheet landed cost climbs. We share both options on every quote so the buyer picks.

Can you cut to size or supply only sheets?

Both. Sheets are the cheaper unit. Cut-to-size shooks add a per-sheet conversion charge but eliminate the buyer's saw-shop labour and offcut wastage. For tea-chest shooks and crate kits this almost always pays back.

Request a Siliguri quote

If you are a Siliguri-based crate maker, tea exporter, border-trade packer or container-yard operator, send us a one-line brief — sheet specification or crate drawing, monthly quantity, and the delivery PIN — and we will come back with an ex-factory rate, the freight line for your lane, and the lead time we can hold. The Cochin Wood group has been operating from Perumbavoor since 1986 and ships packing-grade plywood pan-India every week; Siliguri is a regular drop. Request a quote for your next consignment, or browse the resource hub for grade datasheets, ISPM-15 notes and container-floor specifications before you raise the enquiry.

title: Plywood Supply to Patna | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, wooden crates, pallets and container flooring for Bihar's perishables and machinery exporters. Our group has produced.
html:
Patna is not the first city most South Indian plywood mills think about, and that is precisely why the city is interesting. Bihar's capital sits at the intersection of an agri-export economy that is genuinely growing — litchi out of Muzaffarpur, makhana from Darbhanga and Madhubani, mango and vegetables from the Ganga belt — and a slowly expanding engineering and food-processing base around Patliputra, Fatuha and the Bihta-Patna corridor. Every one of those flows eats wooden packaging. The Cochin Wood group has been shipping packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring out of Kerala since 1986, and Patna's specific mix of perishables, machinery and FMCG distribution is exactly the kind of demand profile our material is built for.

The Patna industrial picture

Most buyers we speak to in Bihar are not located inside Patna municipal limits — they are sitting on the industrial belts that ring the city and feed its consumption. Knowing those belts matters, because it changes how a truck is routed and how a packing programme is planned.

  • Patliputra Industrial Area — the older estate inside Patna, dense with food-processing units, FMCG repackers and light engineering. Short hauls from here usually mean smaller crates and reusable pallets.
  • Fatuha Industrial Area — about 25 km east of Patna along NH-31, mid-sized food and chemical units. A common origin point for outbound truckloads going to North-East India.
  • Bihta Industrial Area — the newer, larger belt about 35 km west on NH-922, with a mix of food processing, building materials and increasing pharma activity.
  • Muzaffarpur and Hajipur belts — technically outside Patna district but logistically run through Patna. Litchi processing in Muzaffarpur and the Hajipur food-park cluster are heavy seasonal consumers of export-grade crates and slip-sheet pallets.
  • Darbhanga / Mithilanchal makhana corridor — India's makhana capital is moving from loose-bag distribution to graded, branded cartons inside wooden over-pack for long-distance freight. That over-pack is plywood.

The common thread across these belts is that finished goods rarely stay in Bihar. They move by road to Kolkata for sea export, by rail to Delhi NCR for re-distribution, or by air out of Patna and Kolkata for premium perishables. That long onward journey is what makes the packing decision matter.

What we ship into Patna

Packing-grade plywood (the workhorse)

The bulk of what flows from our Perumbavoor base to Bihar is commercial / rubberwood-core packing-grade plywood. For Patna's load profile we see four thicknesses do the heavy lifting:

  • 8 mm and 10 mm — lids and side panels for litchi and mango export crates, where weight savings during air-freight matter.
  • 12 mm — the default for food-processing equipment crates, FMCG over-pack and most general-purpose boxes leaving Fatuha and Bihta.
  • 18 mm — bases, skids and heavy-duty engineering crates, including transformer parts and large machinery moving out of Patliputra.

Sheets ship in standard 8x4 ft, cut-to-size on request. All rates are quoted EX-FACTORY Perumbavoor; GST and transport are charged separately and quoted live based on the prevailing Kochi-to-Patna lane rate.

Wooden crates and ISPM-15 boxes

For makhana, litchi pulp, mango concentrate and processed-food shipments going onward to the GCC, EU or South-East Asia, we fabricate ISPM-15 compliant crates with heat-treated softwood framing and packing-ply panels. Stencil marks (HT, IPPC code, country code) are applied at the factory so the crate is export-ready when it lands at the consignor's dock.

Pallets

Two pallet patterns cover most Patna requirements:

  • 1200 x 1000 mm — the GMA-style footprint suited to FMCG and food-grade warehouse racking in eastern India.
  • 1200 x 800 mm — the Euro footprint used by exporters shipping into European retail chains, common for processed-food and ready-to-eat lines.

Both are built with hardwood blocks and packing-ply deck boards. Customer logos and one-way vs. returnable markings are stencilled before dispatch.

Container flooring

Patna does not have its own container yard at the scale of a coastal port, but reefer and dry containers are dropped here for stuffing of perishable and engineering cargo before they road-haul to Kolkata or Haldia. We supply 28 mm container-flooring boards in the standard 2440 x 1220 mm format for the few specialist body-builders and reefer-retrofitters operating in the Bihta belt.

Kochi-to-Patna logistics

The straight-line distance from our Perumbavoor base to Patna is around 2,100 km by road. The lane we use most often runs Kochi → Bengaluru → Hyderabad → Nagpur → Varanasi → Patna, a 2,250 km trucking distance that a 32-ft single-axle or multi-axle truck typically covers in 5 to 7 days depending on monsoon conditions and Ghat traffic.

ModeLaneTypical transitBest for
Road FTLKochi → Patna direct5-7 daysMixed sheet + crate loads, single consignee
Road FTL via Nagpur hubKochi → Nagpur → Patna6-8 daysMulti-drop runs picking up other eastern UP / Jharkhand stops
Rail rake (project loads)Ernakulam → Danapur7-10 daysVery large shuttering-ply or packing-ply projects, full rake economics

For most regular orders, road FTL wins on flexibility. We consolidate sheet stock, crate kits and pallet kits into a single truck and quote a delivered-Patna landed cost when the buyer prefers a single invoice.

Decision rules: which grade for which load

The wrong grade is more expensive than the right grade, every time. The simple rules we walk Patna buyers through:

  1. Use packing-grade plywood when the cargo is one-way, the destination is domestic or short-haul export, and the buyer is sensitive to packaging cost per box. This is the right pick for most FMCG over-pack, litchi and mango export crates, and FMCG over-cartons.
  2. Use Okoume plywood when the end customer is in the EU, Japan or the GCC and the consignee will reuse or inspect the crate. Okoume's clean face veneer also helps when the crate carries premium branding — common for high-value processed-food and pharma shipments.
  3. Use shuttering ply only for the construction case — formwork on infrastructure jobs around the new Patna Metro corridor, Ganga bridge approaches, or the Bihta industrial expansion. It is the wrong choice for packing because the film face is heavy and not cost-justified for one-way use.

Our resource hub carries deeper specification notes by grade — see the resource hub for the full reference sheets, including thickness-vs-weight tables and ISPM-15 stencil samples.

FAQ

Do you have a stocking point in Bihar?

No. We ship directly from Perumbavoor on confirmed orders. For repeat buyers running monthly programmes we hold dedicated stock at the factory and dispatch on a fixed weekly truck so the buyer's Patna or Bihta godown sees a predictable inbound rhythm.

What is the minimum order quantity for a Patna delivery?

For straight sheet stock, one full 32-ft truck (roughly 18-20 tonnes) is the economic minimum. For crate and pallet kits we can ship part-loads consolidated with sheet stock. Smaller trial orders are possible but the per-sheet landed cost rises sharply.

How are payment and GST handled for a Bihar delivery?

IGST applies on the inter-state invoice. Standard terms are advance against PI for first-time orders; established buyers move to part-advance, balance-against-LR-copy after the first three clean transactions. The invoice is raised from our Kerala GSTIN against the Bihar consignee's GSTIN.

Get a Patna-landed quote

If you operate a food-processing line in Fatuha, a makhana grading unit in Mithilanchal, a litchi exporter in Muzaffarpur, or an engineering shop anywhere in the Patna industrial belt, send us your thickness, sheet size or crate drawing, monthly quantity, and the exact delivery pincode. We will quote ex-factory plus the live trucking rate for the Kochi-to-Patna lane, plus an indicative transit window. Request a quote with those four data points and we will respond within one working day.

title: Plywood Supply to Guwahati | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture packing-grade plywood, crates, pallets and container flooring for Guwahati's exporters and freight consolidators. Our group has manufactured.
html:
Why Guwahati is a serious packing-plywood market

Guwahati is the freight gateway for the entire North East. Cargo coming out of Assam, Meghalaya, Nagaland, Arunachal, Manipur, Mizoram and Tripura tends to consolidate at LGBI Airport, the Amingaon ICD, or the trucking hubs around the Brahmaputra crossings before moving south. That consolidation pattern is exactly what drives the city's plywood and crate demand. Tea chests for export auctions, oil-field equipment routed out of Numaligarh and Bongaigaon, jute machinery moving between mills, and engineering goods being repacked for line-haul to Kolkata and Mumbai all need predictable, IS-2202 compliant packing material. The Cochin Wood group has been supplying that kind of bulk industrial plywood out of Perumbavoor since 1986, and Guwahati has steadily become one of the more interesting eastern-corridor markets for our packing-grade boards, crates and container flooring.

The Guwahati industrial picture

The buyer base in and around Guwahati is more diverse than people outside the region usually assume. Public-sector heavyweights, mid-size exporters and a long tail of consolidators all pull plywood through the city.

Industrial belts and estates that drive packing demand

  • Amingaon and North Guwahati — the Amingaon ICD/CONCOR terminal is the rail-linked container yard for NE cargo. Most outbound containers are stuffed here, which means crate-makers and packing contractors cluster around it.
  • Bamunimaidam Industrial Estate — older estate with engineering workshops, fabrication units and ancillary packing converters.
  • Brahmaputra Industrial Park (Amingaon) and EPIP Amingaon — export-oriented units that ship finished and semi-finished goods needing ISPM-15 compliant wooden crates.
  • Changsari and Sila — newer warehousing and 3PL clusters along NH-27, taking pallet volumes for FMCG and white-goods distribution.
  • Byrnihat (just across the Meghalaya border) — cement, steel re-rolling, ferro-alloy and chemical units that need heavy-duty crates for machinery dispatch.

Sectors served by Guwahati packing converters

The plywood and crate demand in the city tracks four sectors closely:

  • Tea — Assam supplies roughly half of India's tea output. Auction lots from Guwahati Tea Auction Centre and direct export shipments still rely heavily on plywood-lined chests and corner-reinforced crates.
  • Petroleum and petrochemicals — Numaligarh Refinery, Bongaigaon Refinery and the OIL/ONGC field operations across upper Assam pull drum-packing plywood, gasket crating, and skid-mounted equipment cases.
  • Jute, plywood machinery and engineering — Assam's own plywood and jute mills generate machinery dispatches that need export-grade crating.
  • NE consolidation cargo — handicrafts, agarwood, cane and bamboo products, food processing exports and project cargo all flow through Guwahati packers before line-haul.

What we ship to Guwahati

Most Guwahati orders fall into one of three buckets. The right specification depends on whether the buyer is making the box themselves, buying finished crates, or fitting out containers.

Packing-grade plywood (MR-Packing)

This is the workhorse: BWR-glue rubberwood / hardwood plywood meant for one-trip packing cases, ISPM-15 crates and skids. Common thicknesses we move to Guwahati are 6 mm, 8 mm, 12 mm, 16 mm and 18 mm in 8x4 ft sheets. Buyers ordering for tea-chest reinforcement and small-equipment crating tend to standardize on 6-8 mm; petrochem and machinery exporters typically pick 12-18 mm for sidewalls, skids and base.

Finished crates and ISPM-15 cases

For converters who would rather buy ready-cut and cleated, we ship knock-down plywood crates, cleated boxes and reinforced cases. Heat-treatment and ISPM-15 marking is done at the converter end so the crate clears customs at any port in India or the GCC.

Wooden pallets

Standard sizes that move well into the Guwahati 3PL belt are 1200x1000 mm (Indian standard), 1200x800 mm (Euro) and 1100x1100 mm (Asia). Plywood-top pallets are preferred for FMCG and pharma; plain sawn-timber pallets work for cement, ferro-alloy and heavy industrial use.

Container flooring and lining

For 20-ft and 40-ft container stuffing at Amingaon ICD, 18-21 mm BWR plywood is used as floor protection and as side-lining for tea chests, drum-loads and heavy machinery. We also supply Okoume-faced plywood for buyers who need a cleaner finish on export-visible surfaces.

Kochi to Guwahati: route, distance and lead time

The road distance from Kochi/Perumbavoor to Guwahati is approximately 3,200 km via NH-44 (the Salem-Nagpur-Raipur-Kharagpur-Kolkata-Siliguri-Guwahati corridor). Most of our Guwahati despatches move by full-truckload on 32-ft single-axle and multi-axle trailers, with a normal door-to-door transit of 8-12 days depending on weather and the Siliguri corridor situation. For larger or time-critical loads, rail-on-road through CONCOR's Amingaon terminal is the alternative — typical end-to-end time is 10-14 days but with better predictability during the monsoon.

LaneDistanceTypical transitBest use
Kochi - Guwahati by road FTL~3,200 km8-12 daysStandard plywood, crates, pallets
Kochi - Amingaon ICD via railn/a10-14 daysContainer flooring, large project orders
Kochi port - Kolkata - Guwahaticoastal + road12-16 daysBulk lots when truck capacity is tight

Which grade fits which Guwahati load

A quick decision rule we share with new buyers in the region:

  • MR-Packing rubberwood plywood — one-trip crates, tea chest reinforcement, light machinery, drum packing. Most cost-efficient for the volume Guwahati moves.
  • BWR hardwood plywood — multi-trip pallets, container flooring, monsoon-exposed crates, anything that may sit on a wharf or wait for rail allotment.
  • Okoume-faced plywood — premium export cases for tea, handicrafts, agarwood and any cargo where the box itself is visible to a buyer overseas.
  • Shuttering ply — for the Byrnihat and Changsari construction belt; film-faced 12 mm and 18 mm for column, slab and retaining-wall shutters.
  • Sawn timber (rubberwood, jackwood) — for pallet runners, crate cleats and battens where a converter prefers in-house cutting.

FAQ for Guwahati buyers

Do you supply ISPM-15 heat-treated material?

The plywood we ship is engineered board, which is exempt from ISPM-15 by construction. For solid-wood components (runners, cleats, battens) and finished crates, heat treatment and stamping is done at the converter or at our partner heat-treatment yard before despatch. We can route material through an HT yard on request.

What is the minimum order size for Guwahati?

Given the lane economics, a single 32-ft full-truck load (roughly 600-750 sheets of 18 mm or equivalent mix) is the most cost-efficient drop. Smaller orders can ride as part-loads but the per-sheet freight goes up materially.

Can you quote ex-works versus delivered?

Yes. Most converters in Guwahati prefer a delivered Guwahati city or delivered Amingaon ICD quote so the freight risk sits with us. Ex-Perumbavoor pricing is available if a buyer has their own NE logistics tie-up.

Get a Guwahati quote

If you are sourcing packing plywood, crates, pallets or container flooring into the Guwahati industrial belt, share your sizes, thicknesses, monthly volumes and delivery point and we will turn around a delivered quote with the lane factored in. Request a quote with your specifications, or browse the resource hub for spec sheets, thickness guides and ISPM-15 reference notes before you write to us. The Cochin Wood group has been working with eastern-corridor packing buyers for forty years and the Guwahati lane is one we run regularly — quotations usually come back the same working day.

title: Plywood Supply to Sri City | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: We manufacture the packing-grade plywood, export crates, sea-stuffing pallets and container flooring that Sri City's SEZ and domestic-tariff lines run on.
html:
Sri City sits at the top of Andhra Pradesh's Tirupati district, less than 60 km from Chennai's northern outskirts, and it is one of the densest industrial buildouts in southern India. A 7,500-acre multi-product SEZ with more than 200 operating units, four deep-water ports inside a 100 km radius, and tenants ranging from Foxconn handset assembly to Kia and Isuzu vehicle plants — the cluster generates a steady, predictable demand for industrial packaging timber. This post is a working note on what the Cochin Wood group ships into Sri City, how the lane from Kochi runs, and how buyers in the SEZ should think about grade selection for the loads typically moving out of that belt.

The Sri City industrial picture

Sri City was notified as a multi-product SEZ in 2008 and has since grown into one of the largest planned industrial townships in India. The footprint is split across three zones: a notified SEZ for export-oriented units, a Domestic Tariff Area for units selling primarily into India, and a Free Trade Warehousing Zone for distribution and re-export. Around this core sit several supporting industrial parks in the Satyavedu and Tada mandals — including the older APIIC estates and Sri City's own Sector 1 through Sector 7 layouts. The cluster's tenant mix tells you almost everything about its packaging needs.

  • Automotive and mobility: Kia Motors India, Isuzu Motors, Hero MotoCorp's electric vehicle arm, and a tier of component suppliers feeding CKD (completely knocked down) export programmes.
  • Electronics and EMS: Foxconn, Pegatron, Berger Paints' electronics-grade resins, and a clutch of EMS contractors that ship finished and semi-finished electronics in cushioned, climate-controlled crates.
  • Engineering and capital goods: Alstom rolling stock, Cabot India for industrial carbon, Toppan and several precision-equipment exporters.
  • FMCG and consumer goods: Colgate-Palmolive, Pepsico, Mondelez, Kellogg's — heavy users of pallets and stretch-wrapped block-stacked timber.

The packaging implication is simple. Sri City is not a market for plywood as a building material — that is a Chennai or Bengaluru conversation. It is a market for export-grade industrial packaging: heat-treated crates that satisfy ISPM-15 phytosanitary rules, four-way entry pallets sized for sea-container stuffing, and container flooring for the open-top and flat-rack movements that engineering exporters need.

What the Cochin Wood group ships into Sri City

The product mix below covers roughly 90% of what we move into Andhra Pradesh's SEZ belt. The Cochin Wood group has been in plywood since 1986; CWI Pvt Ltd is the more recent corporate vehicle through which contract-factory manufacturing is now coordinated.

Packing-grade plywood

This is the workhorse for crate walls, lids and bases. Standard thicknesses we move into Sri City are 6 mm, 8 mm, 12 mm and 18 mm in 8x4 ft sheets, with 9 mm and 15 mm available on order. Construction is MR-grade (moisture-resistant) hardwood core with either commercial face veneer or an Okoume face for higher-spec export programmes. The grade is built for one job — surviving a single export journey intact — and is priced accordingly. Typical sectors that pull this from us are wooden packing-case makers, CKD packers for automotive exporters, and engineering-goods crating shops feeding the Krishnapatnam and Chennai port lanes.

ISPM-15 crates and cases

Where buyers want fully built-up crates rather than sheet stock, we supply ISPM-15 heat-treated assemblies — IPPC stamp, treatment code and country mark already burned into each timber member. Common formats: nailed plywood cases for sub-tonne consignments, framed plywood-clad crates for 1-5 tonne assemblies, and skeletal crates with plywood panels for heavy machinery up to container-weight limits. The Sri City automotive and capital-goods sectors are the steadiest pullers here.

Pallets

Four-way entry pallets sized for sea-container stuffing are the bulk of our pallet despatches into Sri City. The two dominant footprints are 1200 x 800 mm (Euro) and 1100 x 1100 mm (CP / Asian standard). Construction can be solid sawn timber, or plywood-deck on a stringer frame for buyers who need a flatter face for stretch-wrap and shrink-sleeve operations. Block-stacking exporters in the FMCG units of Sector 4 and Sector 5 typically draw from this line.

Container flooring and dunnage

For open-top and flat-rack movements — common for over-dimensional engineering exports going out via Krishnapatnam — we ship 28 mm container-floor grade plywood in 2440 x 1220 mm sheets, plus sawn timber dunnage in lengths cut to spec. Apitong-equivalent specifications are available on request for buyers running ISO-container repair or new-build programmes.

Kochi to Sri City: the logistics lane

The road distance from Kochi to Sri City is approximately 750 km, taking 14-16 hours of running time for a single-truck movement. The lane runs NH-544 from Kochi through Coimbatore, then NH-44 north through Salem, Krishnagiri and Chittoor, before turning east at Tirupati onto the Tirupati-Naidupeta corridor that brings trucks into Sri City's gates at Satyavedu.

Two factors keep the lane economically interesting for buyers in the SEZ. First, there is no port stop or sea freight in the chain — packing material arrives by road within 36-48 hours of despatch, so just-in-time call-offs for production-line packing work cleanly. Second, return-load availability on the Andhra-to-Kerala leg is strong (auto components, FMCG, textiles), which means transporters quote the Kochi-to-Sri City direction sharper than the equivalent lane from north Indian supply bases. For SEZ units handling phytosanitary documentation, ISPM-15 IPPC stamping is completed at the contract factory before despatch; the IPPC certificate and treatment paperwork travels with the truck.

Lane factSri City
Distance from Kochi~750 km
Road transit14-16 hours running; 36-48 hours door-to-door
Primary routeNH-544 (Kochi-Coimbatore) → NH-44 (Salem-Krishnagiri-Chittoor) → SH to Satyavedu
Nearest portsKrishnapatnam (~85 km), Kattupalli (~55 km), Chennai (~70 km), Ennore (~65 km)
Typical truck size20 ft / 32 ft single-axle for FTL movements; container stuffing at factory for CFS-bound

Decision rules: which grade for which Sri City load

The grade decision should track three variables — the value of the cargo, whether it is leaving India, and how it will be handled in transit. A simple working framework:

  1. Domestic packing-case work, single trip: commercial-face MR-grade packing plywood in 6-12 mm. This is the volume grade for tier-2 component suppliers and DTA-zone units moving stock to mother plants within India.
  2. Export crates, sea freight, single voyage: Okoume-face packing plywood in 9-12 mm with ISPM-15 timber framing. The Okoume face gives a cleaner appearance and a slightly higher bend strength for crates that may be opened and re-closed at customs.
  3. Heavy-load crates, machinery, multi-handling: 18 mm packing plywood on a sawn-timber framed crate, with ISPM-15 stamps. Container flooring grade is used for the base.
  4. Shuttering plywood: rarely the right answer for Sri City packaging. Shuttering is the right call for civil works inside the SEZ during build-out phases — formwork for concrete pours — not for export crates.

Frequently asked questions

Do you supply ISPM-15 compliant crates direct to SEZ units?

Yes. The contract factories under CWI specifications are IPPC-registered for heat treatment, and the stamp, treatment code and certificate travel with the consignment. Buyers do not need to arrange a separate treatment vendor in Sri City.

What is the minimum order size for a Kochi-to-Sri City movement?

A single 32-ft truck (around 8-9 tonnes of packing plywood, or roughly 800-900 sheets at 12 mm) is the economical floor. Smaller orders can be consolidated with other Andhra-bound consignments on a part-load basis with a longer transit window.

Can you match an existing crate drawing or spec sheet?

Yes. Submit the drawing or specification with the enquiry and the contract factory will quote the build to drawing. For repeat programmes we hold a working spec on file so call-offs go straight to production.

Request a quote

Sri City buyers — packing-case makers, automotive exporters, EMS units, FMCG warehouses — can request a quote with a one-line brief on grade, thickness, sheet size or crate spec, and monthly volume. We will come back with an EX-Kochi rate, a delivered Sri City rate including transport and GST, and an indicative dispatch window. For specification notes on packing plywood thickness selection, ISPM-15 documentation and pallet construction, the resource hub carries the working guides our buyers reference most often.

Questions buyers ask

Okoume plywood, answered.

What is okoume plywood?

Plywood with an okoume (gaboon) face — a light, even-grained African hardwood. It is smooth, low in density and paintable, which makes it a favourite for export packing cases and lightweight panels.

Why use okoume for packing cases?

Because it is light. A lower-density panel cuts the dead weight of the case, which lowers freight on air and volumetric shipments — and it prints and nails cleanly.

Okoume or gurjan — which should I use?

title: Block Board vs Plywood | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Short answer: plywood is built from thin veneers cross-laminated at right angles — it is strong in every direction and the right choice for structural work.
html:
Short answer: plywood is built from thin veneers cross-laminated at right angles — it is strong in every direction and the right choice for structural work, flooring, packing and anywhere moisture is a factor. Block board has a core of solid timber battens glued edge-to-edge between two veneers — it is lighter and resists sagging over long spans, which makes it ideal for doors, long shelves, tabletops and partitions. In one line: plywood for strength and moisture; block board for long, flat, lightweight panels.

What plywood is

Plywood is made from multiple thin wood veneers (plies) bonded with the grain of each layer running at 90° to the next. This cross-lamination gives plywood its defining property: balanced strength in all directions and excellent resistance to splitting. Grade (MR, BWR, BWP/marine) is set by the adhesive — see BWP vs BWR.

What block board is

Block board has a core of solid wood strips (battens), typically 25–30 mm wide, laid edge-to-edge and sandwiched between one or two veneer layers on each face. Because the core runs lengthwise, block board is very stiff along its length and lighter than plywood of the same thickness — but it is weaker across its width and at the edges.

Block board vs plywood at a glance

PropertyPlywoodBlock board
CoreCross-laminated veneersSolid timber battens
Strength directionBalanced, all directionsStrong lengthwise, weaker across
WeightHeavierLighter
Resistance to sagging (long spans)GoodExcellent
Screw-holding at edgesExcellentVariable (depends where the batten falls)
Moisture performanceUp to BWP/marineGenerally interior (MR/BWR)
Best forStructural, flooring, packing, wet areasDoors, long shelves, tabletops, partitions

When to choose block board

Block board shines where you need a long, flat, rigid, lightweight panel: flush doors, wardrobe shutters, long bookshelves and wall units, conference and dining tabletops, and partitions. Its lengthwise stiffness resists the sagging that a long plywood shelf can develop over time. See block board & flush doors.

When to choose plywood

Choose plywood for anything structural, load-bearing in multiple directions, exposed to moisture, or used for packing and flooring: cabinetry carcasses, formwork, container flooring, export crates, marine and exterior applications. Its all-direction strength and the availability of waterproof (BWP) grades make it the more versatile panel. See the full catalogue.

FAQ

Is block board stronger than plywood?

Not overall. Block board is stiffer along its length (better against sagging on long spans), but plywood is stronger in all directions and at the edges. For multi-directional load, plywood wins.

Is block board cheaper than plywood?

Block board is often lighter and can be cost-effective for large flat panels, but pricing depends on grade, core timber and thickness. We confirm the exact price for your spec at quotation.

Which is better for doors — block board or plywood?

Block board, in most cases. Its lengthwise rigidity and lower weight make it the traditional choice for flush doors and tall shutters that must stay flat and not sag on their hinges.

Can block board be used in wet areas?

Generally no — block board is usually an interior-grade panel. For moisture exposure, use BWR or BWP/marine plywood instead.

Which is better for shelves?

For long shelves that must not sag, block board's lengthwise stiffness is excellent. For short or heavily loaded shelves, or where moisture is a factor, plywood is the safer choice.

Related

Block board & flush doors Commercial plywood (BWR & MR) BWP vs BWR plywood Full catalogue

Specifying panels for a project?

Tell us the application and we'll recommend block board or plywood — and quote it FOB Cochin within one business day.

Request a quote

title: IS 710 vs IS 303 Plywood | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Short answer: IS 710 is the standard for BWP (boiling-waterproof) marine plywood — a 100% phenolic-bonded board for constant water and humidity. IS 303.
html:
Short answer: IS 710 is the standard for BWP (boiling-waterproof) marine plywood — a 100% phenolic-bonded board for constant water and humidity. IS 303 covers commercial plywood in two grades: BWR (boiling-water-resistant, for kitchens and humid interiors) and MR (moisture-resistant, for dry furniture and interiors). Use IS 710 where water never leaves; IS 303 BWR where it sometimes does; IS 303 MR where it rarely does.

What IS 710 (BWP marine plywood) is

IS 710 is the Bureau of Indian Standards specification for boiling-waterproof marine plywood. The veneers are bonded with a 100% phenol-formaldehyde (PF) glue line and built on a hardwood core, so the board survives prolonged immersion, steam and humidity without delaminating. It is the grade for boatbuilding, hulls and decks, constantly wet interiors and exterior marine joinery.

What IS 303 (commercial plywood) is

IS 303 is the specification for general-purpose commercial plywood. It is classified into two grades by water resistance:

  • BWR (Boiling Water Resistant) — fortified glue line (melamine-fortified UF or PF), for kitchens, semi-humid and semi-exterior use.
  • MR (Moisture Resistant) — urea-formaldehyde bonded, for dry interiors, furniture and panelling.

The common confusion is BWR vs BWP. BWR resists boiling water; BWP is waterproof. They are not the same grade — BWP (IS 710) is the higher, marine specification.

IS 710 vs IS 303 at a glance

AttributeIS 710 — BWPIS 303 — BWRIS 303 — MR
GradeMarine / boiling-waterproofBoiling-water-resistantMoisture-resistant
Glue line100% phenolic (PF)Fortified UF / PFUrea-formaldehyde (UF)
Water resistanceHighest — withstands immersionHigh — withstands occasional wettingModerate — humidity only
Typical useBoats, decks, constant wetKitchens, bathrooms, humid areasFurniture, dry interiors
Relative costHighestMidLowest

How to choose

Match the grade to how much water the panel actually meets — over-specifying wastes money, under-specifying delaminates:

  • Constant water / marine → IS 710 BWP.
  • Occasional water (kitchen, bathroom, humid climate) → IS 303 BWR.
  • Dry interior furniture and panelling → IS 303 MR.

For an exterior concrete-formwork panel you usually want film-faced shuttering plywood (IS 303 BWR core with a phenolic film), not bare marine ply — see film-faced shuttering plywood.

How to verify a grade

Look for the BIS standard mark and the grade stamp on the panel, ask for the test certificate, and — for marine — confirm the glue line passes the boil test. A genuine IS 710 board holds its bond after hours in boiling water; a mislabelled commercial board will not.

FAQ

Is IS 710 better than IS 303?

For water resistance, yes — IS 710 is boiling-waterproof marine grade, while IS 303 is moisture- or boiling-water-resistant commercial grade. But "better" depends on use: for dry furniture, IS 303 MR is the right, cheaper choice.

Is BWR the same as BWP?

No. BWR is boiling-water-resistant (IS 303); BWP is boiling-waterproof (IS 710, marine). BWP is the higher grade.

Which plywood is best for a kitchen?

IS 303 BWR is the usual choice for kitchens and humid areas. Use IS 710 BWP only where the panel is in constant contact with water.

Which plywood for furniture?

IS 303 MR (moisture-resistant) is standard for dry interior furniture and panelling.

Related

Marine plywood (IS 710) Film-faced shuttering plywood Marine plywood thickness guide Full catalogue

Need a grade quoted?

Tell us the application and we'll recommend the grade and send a written quote within one business day.

Request a quote

title: Do Plywood Boxes Need ISPM-15 | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Short answer: No. Plywood boxes and cases are exempt from ISPM-15 , because plywood is a processed (engineered) wood — the heat and glue used to make it.
html:
Short answer: No. Plywood boxes and cases are exempt from ISPM-15, because plywood is a processed (engineered) wood — the heat and glue used to make it already destroy any pests. The same exemption covers OSB, MDF, particleboard and hardboard. The catch: if your box or crate uses solid-wood parts — skids, runners, blocks, bearers or battens — those components must be heat-treated (HT) and stamped to ISPM-15.

What ISPM-15 actually regulates

ISPM-15 is the international standard for solid wood packaging material — raw, unprocessed wood used as pallets, crates, dunnage, skids and bracing. Because raw wood can carry bark beetles and other pests, it must be treated (usually heat treatment to a 56 °C core for 30 minutes) and marked with the wheat-stamp before it crosses a border.

Why plywood is exempt

Engineered wood is made under heat and pressure with adhesives. That manufacturing process already eliminates pests, so the standard treats it as low-risk and exempts it from treatment and stamping. Exempt materials include:

  • Plywood (all grades)
  • OSB (oriented strand board)
  • MDF and particleboard
  • Hardboard and similar glued/processed panels
  • Veneer and wood wool / sawdust below a thickness threshold

Where a plywood box still needs treatment

Most export boxes are not pure plywood. A typical plywood case sits on a solid-wood skid or runner base and may use solid blocks or framing. Those raw-wood parts are not exempt. So the practical rule is:

Packaging componentISPM-15 needed?
Plywood box / case panelsNo — exempt
OSB / MDF / particleboard panelsNo — exempt
Solid-wood pallet, crate or dunnageYes — HT + stamp
Solid-wood skid, runner, block or batten on a plywood boxYes — that part HT + stamped
All-plywood box on a plywood skidNo — fully exempt

The practical takeaway for exporters

If you want to skip ISPM-15 entirely, specify an all-plywood (or engineered-wood) box and skid — no raw timber. That removes the treatment step, the stamp and the customs risk. If solid wood is needed for strength, keep it to the skid and have just those members heat-treated and stamped. Either way, always confirm the destination country's current rules before shipping.

At Cochin Wood we build both — all-plywood cases and plywood boxes on heat-treated solid skids — and issue the treatment certificate at dispatch. See plywood boxes & crates and plywood pallets.

FAQ

Is plywood ISPM-15 exempt?

Yes. Plywood is a processed/engineered wood and is exempt from ISPM-15 treatment and stamping, along with OSB, MDF and particleboard.

Do plywood pallets need ISPM-15?

The plywood parts do not. Any solid-wood blocks or bearers in the pallet must be heat-treated and stamped. An all-plywood pallet needs no treatment.

Does a plywood crate need a stamp?

Only if it contains solid-wood members. The plywood panels are exempt; a solid-wood skid or frame must carry the ISPM-15 mark.

How can I avoid ISPM-15 altogether?

Use an all-engineered-wood box and skid (plywood, OSB or MDF) with no raw timber. There is then nothing to treat or stamp.

Related

Plywood boxes & crates Plywood & wooden pallets Plywood cable drums Full catalogue

title: How Many Pours Does Film-Faced Plywood Last | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Short answer: a high-density film-faced panel, handled well, gives 20–40+ pours ; a low-quality panel can fail in under 10 . So the figure that decides cost.
html:
Short answer: a high-density film-faced panel, handled well, gives 20–40+ pours; a low-quality panel can fail in under 10. So the figure that decides cost is not the sheet price — it is the cost per pour (sheet price ÷ pours). A panel that lasts four to five times longer works out far cheaper per use, even when it costs more per sheet.

What makes film-faced shuttering plywood last more pours

Reuses climb with film grade and site care.

How the cost-per-pour maths works

Contractors buy on sheet price; site managers pay on cost per pour. The two can point in opposite directions — and the formula is simple: cost per pour = sheet price ÷ realistic pours. Put your own quoted prices against the pours each panel actually delivers:

Panel grade Realistic pours Relative cost per pour
Premium HD film-faced 20–40+ Lowest
Mid-grade film-faced 12–20 Higher
Low-cost panel Under 10 Highest

A panel that lasts several times longer works out far cheaper per pour, even at a higher sheet price — so the cheapest sheet is often the most expensive formwork. We quote current rates against your volume on request.

What decides how many pours you get

  • Film weight (gsm) — a 120–220 gsm phenolic film resists abrasion and alkali far longer than a thin film.
  • Glue line — a true phenolic (WBP) bond keeps the panel intact through repeated wet cycles.
  • Core quality — a dense, gap-free hardwood core holds nails and stays flat.
  • Edge sealing — water enters at the cut edge; a sealed edge is the single biggest life-extender.
  • Site handling — release agent, gentle stripping, cleaning and dry storage routinely double a panel's life.

How to get more pours from every panel

  1. Re-seal any cut edges before first use.
  2. Apply a proper release agent every pour.
  3. Strip with care — no crowbars against the face.
  4. Clean off concrete laitance before it sets.
  5. Stack flat and dry between pours.

Specify the right panel once and the savings compound across a project. See our film-faced shuttering plywood for film weights and thicknesses.

FAQ

How many times can you reuse shuttering plywood?

A good HD film-faced panel gives 20–40+ pours with correct handling; a cheap panel may manage under 10. Edge sealing and care make the biggest difference.

What is cost per pour?

The sheet price divided by the number of usable pours. It is the true cost of formwork — a dearer panel that lasts longer is usually cheaper per pour.

Does the film gsm matter?

Yes — heavier phenolic film (e.g. 220 gsm) resists abrasion and alkali, giving more pours and a finer concrete finish than thin film.

How do I make shuttering plywood last longer?

Seal cut edges, use a release agent every pour, strip gently, clean off laitance, and store flat and dry.

title: IICL TB-001 Container Flooring Plywood | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: What IICL TB-001 specifies for container flooring plywood — thickness, species, bonding and test loads — and how to buy compliant 28 mm floorboards.
html:
Short answer: IICL TB-001 is the technical bulletin published by the Institute of International Container Lessors that defines the floorboard used in shipping containers. It calls for a ~28 mm full-hardwood plywood (or approved bamboo) with a 100% waterproof glue line, an anti-slip face and tightly controlled bending strength, screw-holding and thickness tolerance — so the floor carries cargo and a moving forklift, voyage after voyage.

What the IICL is

The Institute of International Container Lessors is the body whose technical bulletins set the maintenance and repair standards container lessors and depots work to. Its flooring bulletin, TB-001, is the reference container manufacturers and CSC repairers use when they specify or replace a container floor.

What TB-001 covers

The bulletin defines the properties a board must have to serve as a container floor:

PropertyTypical TB-001 requirement
Thickness~28 mm (±0.5 mm tolerance)
Panel size2440 × 1220 mm; 2400 × 1160 mm
ConstructionFull hardwood plywood (keruing/apitong) or approved bamboo
Density700–800 kg/m³
Glue linePhenol-formaldehyde, 100% waterproof (WBP)
SurfaceAnti-slip mesh/wire pattern; phenolic film both faces
PerformanceDefined bending strength (MOR/MOE), screw-holding and forklift load resistance

Why 28 mm hardwood

A container floor is the one surface that takes the entire payload and a 7-tonne forklift moving across it. It needs high stiffness, low deflection and excellent screw-holding to fix down to the steel cross-members — which is why a dense, full-hardwood 28 mm board is the norm, not a softwood or veneer-core panel.

New build vs repair

The same spec applies whether you are flooring new containers on a production line or replacing damaged boards in a repair depot. Repairers usually need boards cut, grooved and drilled to the container model's pattern — which is why TB-001 boards are quoted to drawing.

See our container flooring plywood page for sizes, machining and FOB-Cochin export terms.

FAQ

What is IICL TB-001?

It is the Institute of International Container Lessors' technical bulletin specifying the plywood floorboard for shipping containers — covering thickness, strength, screw-holding and glue bond.

What thickness is container flooring plywood?

The standard is about 28 mm, at 2440 × 1220 mm, in a full-hardwood construction with a 700–800 kg/m³ density.

What wood is used for container floors?

Dense tropical hardwood such as keruing/apitong, or an approved bamboo composite — chosen for stiffness and screw-holding.

Is container flooring the same as shuttering plywood?

No. Container flooring is denser, thicker and anti-slip, graded to IICL TB-001 for rolling loads; shuttering plywood is film-faced formwork for concrete.

Related

Container flooring plywood Film-faced shuttering plywood Marine plywood Full catalogue

Need IICL-grade floorboard?

title: Okoume vs Gurjan Plywood | Cochin Wood Industries
desc: Short answer: choose okoume when weight and a clean face matter — packing cases, lightweight crating and panels — because it is a low-density, smooth African.
html:
Short answer: choose okoume when weight and a clean face matter — packing cases, lightweight crating and panels — because it is a low-density, smooth African hardwood. Choose gurjan (keruing) when strength matters — structural plywood, heavy crates and container flooring — because it is a dense, strong Southeast-Asian hardwood. In one line: okoume for light and smooth, gurjan for heavy and strong.

What okoume is

Okoume (also called gaboon) is a West/Central African hardwood with a pale, even grain. It is light, low in density and very smooth, which makes it easy to nail, easy to print and easy to lift — the reasons it is a favourite face for export packing cases and lightweight panels. It is also used as a core in lightweight marine and aircraft-grade plywood.

What gurjan is

Gurjan (keruing) is a dense Southeast-Asian hardwood. It is heavier, harder and stronger, with excellent screw-holding — the reasons it is used for structural plywood, heavy-duty crates and as the core for container flooring boards.

Okoume vs gurjan at a glance

AttributeOkoume (gaboon)Gurjan (keruing)
OriginWest / Central AfricaSoutheast Asia
DensityLow (~400–450 kg/m³)High (~700–800 kg/m³)
WeightLightHeavy
StrengthModerateHigh
SurfaceSmooth, pale, paintableCoarser, reddish
Best forPacking cases, lightweight panels, marine coresStructural ply, container flooring, heavy crates

For packing cases, weight wins

Packing-case makers and exporters usually favour okoume because a lighter panel cuts the dead weight of every case — which lowers air-freight and volumetric charges — while the smooth face takes branding and stencils cleanly. Where a case must carry heavy machinery and take structural load, a gurjan-based panel or a solid-wood frame does the lifting instead.

See okoume packing plywood for grades and sizes, or container flooring plywood for the gurjan-cored board.

FAQ

Is okoume stronger than gurjan?

No — gurjan is denser and stronger. Okoume's advantage is its low weight and smooth, paintable face, not raw strength.

Which plywood is lighter, okoume or gurjan?

Okoume. Its density is roughly half that of gurjan, which is why it is used to keep packing cases and panels light.

Which is better for packing cases?

Okoume, in most cases — lighter cases mean lower freight, and the smooth face brands well. Use gurjan or a solid frame where the case carries heavy structural load.

Which is used for container flooring?

Gurjan (keruing) — its density and screw-holding suit the IICL TB-001 container-floor specification.

Related

Okoume packing plywood Container flooring plywood Plywood boxes & crates Full catalogue

Not sure which face you need?
